caption:
✅ This has 473 Calories, 52g protein, and 14g net carbs a serving!

I've lost over 150lbs eating food thats easy, tastes so good, and keeps me FULL. Thats been the secret to staying on track without wanting to resort to cheat meals. This is one of those easy meals that feels indulgent but still fits how I like to eat.

Comment "BANGER" below and I'll send you my free mini protein ebook

Here is how to make it:

1 Grab 1.5lb boneless chicken thighs. Trim off any excess fat as you wish and place inside a bowl.

2 Drizzle the chicken with about tsp olive oil, & season with a couple pinches of salt and pepper, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp sweet paprika, 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper, 1/2 tbsp oregano, 1/2 tbsp Aleppo pepper, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/4 tsp cinnamon. To make the chicken extra juicy, I also add 1.5 tbsp FAGE 0% yogurt, 1 tbsp tomato paste, the juice of half large lemon.

3 Mix really well with 1/2 small thinly sliced yellow onion. If you can, let this marinate for 2-3 hrs, but if you can also make it right way. Airfry at 400F for 22-25 mins, or pop in the oven at 400F for 30-35 mins.

4 For the base: I used a box of my high protein lowcarb @kaizenfoodco rice, it has 20g protein on its own with just 6 net carbs per serving, but use any rice you like, quinoa, cauliflower rice. I just prepped according to packaging in salted water.

5 For the creamy cucumber salad: 1/2 English cucumber thinly sliced or 2 Persian cucumbers, 1/4 red onion thinly sliced. 2-3 tbsp finely Chopped fresh dill. 2-3 tbsp FAGE 0% Greek yogurt, juice of half a lemon, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper

6 For the garlicky white sauce: mix 3 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp mayo, 3 tbsp sour cream, juice of ½ lemon, the zest of a lemon, 2 tbsp chopped parsley, salt & lots of fresh cracked pepper. Thin with water if needed.

This makes 4 servings!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!

caption:
High-Protein Greek Bowls (made in 25 minutes)

Comment RECIPE for more high-protein dinner ideas or visit my site https://provecho.co/flavorsbyfrangipane

Comment BOWL for bowl link! 

Greek Chicken
-1 ½ lbs boneless chicken thighs
-¾ cup Greek yogurt
-1 tsp salt
-1 tsp paprika
-1 tsp garlic powder or minced garlic
-1 tsp onion powder
-1 tsp oregano
-½ tsp thyme
-½ tsp black pepper
-Juice of 1 lemon

Bone Broth Garlic Lemon Rice
-1 cup jasmine rice, rinsed
-2 cups bone broth
-2 garlic cloves, minced
-Pinch of salt
-Squeeze of fresh lemon juice
-Fresh dill or parsley, chopped

Tzatziki
-1 cup Greek yogurt
-1 cucumber, grated and squeezed dry (I keep a little of the liquid for flavor)
-1 garlic clove, minced
-Juice of ½ lemon
-1 tbsp olive oil
-Splash of red wine vinegar (optional, to taste)
-1 tbsp fresh dill, chopped
-Pinch of salt + black pepper

Mediterranean Salad
-2 roma tomatoes, diced
-1 cucumber, diced
-2 tbsp olive oil
-Freshly chopped dill
-Lemon juice, to taste
-Salt + pepper, to taste

1. In a large bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, salt, pepper, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, thyme, and lemon juice. Add the chicken thighs and toss until fully coated. Let marinate while you prep everything else, or longer if you have time.
2. Add the rinsed rice, bone broth, garlic, and salt to a pot. Bring to a boil, then cover and reduce to low. Cook for about 15 minutes, or until the rice is tender. Fluff with a fork and finish with fresh lemon juice and herbs.
3. Air fry the chicken thighs at 400°F for 22–25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until cooked through and lightly charred on the edges.
4. In a bowl, stir together yogurt, grated cucumber, garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, red wine vinegar (if using), dill, salt, and pepper. Chill until serving.
5. Combine tomatoes and cucumber in a bowl. Drizzle with olive oil, lemon juice, and season with dill, salt, and pepper. Toss gently.
6. Slice the chicken and serve in bowls with the garlic lemon rice, tzatziki, and Mediterranean salad& enjoy!!
summary: A 25-minute high-protein Greek chicken bowl recipe with marinated air-fried chicken thighs, garlic lemon rice, tzatziki, and Mediterranean salad.

caption:
shrimp noodle bowl 🫑🍤

follow @fridasofiaeats + comment "Noodles" and I'll send you a DM with the recipe!

Every time I make this I wonder why I don't make it more often? It's just a handful ingredients and tastes better than takeout! So fresh yet saucy and delicous! ✨🍽️

Ingredients 
4 packets (servings) of wheat noodles (you will need 2 boxes) I got mine from Trader Joe's 
2 bell peppers, sliced
1 lb shrimp, deveined and peeled
1/2 cup soyaki sauce
1/3 cup reserved noodle water
Salt
Pepper
Olive oil
1 tbsp Butter

Instructions
1. Cook the noodles according to package instructions (they only take about 30 seconds). Reserve 1/3 cup of the cooking water, then drain.

2. Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Add the sliced bell peppers and sauté for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened.

3. Add the shrimp, season with salt and pepper, and cook for about 2 minutes per side until fully cooked.

4. Add the cooked noodles to the pan along with the soyaki sauce and reserved noodle water. Stir in butter if using.

5. Stir everything together until well coated.
summary: A quick shrimp noodle bowl with bell peppers and soy sauce that comes together in minutes.

caption:
26g protein & 450 calories per serving
All the delicious chaos of a Thai street stall in one pan.
This is the kind of noodle bowl I could eat everyday — thick flat noodles, juicy chicken, crunchy veg and a sauce that's umami, sweet, tangy and spicy in the same bite. 

Pad Thai
Makes 4 Servings
Per serving - 
Calories: ~450 kcal | Protein: 26g | Carbs: 48g | Fat: 15g 

Ingredients (serves 4):
For the chicken:
	•	400g boneless chicken thigh strips (or paneer / tofu)
	•	1 tbsp cornflour or arrowroot starch
	•	1 tbsp light soy sauce
	•	Salt and pepper to taste

For the stir fry sauce:
	•	2 tbsp light soy sauce
	•	1.5 tbsp oyster sauce (regular or vegan mushroom oyster)
	•	1 tsp fish sauce (skip for vegetarian)
	•	2 tsp Sriracha or red chilli sauce
	•	1 tbsp chilli crisp (or flakes)
	•	1.5 tbsp tamarind pulp 
	•	2 tsp sugar
	•	Pinch of salt
	•	4-5 tbsp hot water

For the stir fry:
	•	160g flat rice noodles
	•	2 tbsp oil
	•	8-10 cloves garlic, minced
	•	1 medium onion, sliced
	•	1 tsp grated ginger + 1 tsp chopped chilli (optional)
	•	100g oyster mushrooms, torn
	•	1/2 cup carrot strip
	•	1 cup spinach
	•	Large handful of basil leaves
	•	3-4 spring onions, diced
	•	2 tbsp crushed peanuts

Method:
	1.	Soak the flat rice noodles in hot water for 4-5 minutes. Drain and set aside.
	2.	Marinate the chicken strips with cornflour, soy sauce, salt and pepper. Rest for 10 minutes.
	3.	Whisk all stir fry sauce ingredients together until smooth.
	4.	Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large pan or wok. Fry the chicken on high heat until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
	5.	In the same pan (no extra oil), add minced garlic, sliced onion and grated ginger. Cook for a minute.
	6.	Add veg & stir fry on high flame for 2 minutes.
	7.	Return the chicken to the pan along with the drained noodles and stir fry sauce. Toss everything together on high heat.
	8.	Finish with basil leaves, spring onion and crushed peanuts. Serve hot.
.
(Pad thai noodles, chicken noodle recipe, high protein noodle bowl, easy Thai recipe)

#Noodles #padthai #HighProteinMeals
summary: High-protein pad thai recipe with chicken, flat rice noodles, and vegetables, 26g protein per serving.

caption:
✅SAVORY Breakfast Waffles

These come out to  290 calories, 15g protein, and so good. You can load them up with more protein with more eggs, or swap out the avocado for something else. The fiber from the sweet potato and the healthy fats from the avocado do serious work in keeping me full

They're good enough to prep in advance, so if you want a healthy meal prep, they're a great candidate so you have healthy breakfasts ready to go.

Ingredients

2-3 tbsp mashed sweet potato
1 serving shredded cheddar cheese
1/2 small avocado, mashed
1 egg
Salt, pepper
Optional: everything bagel seasoning, chile lime seasoning, or any seasoning you like.

Here is how I made it:

1.I used my mini waffle maker here. Preheat it. You want it hot.

2.Add half a serving cheese at the base, 2-3 tbsp mashed sweet potato, and top with another half serving of cheese to the waffle maker. I used cheddar, but use whatever cheese you prefer.

3.If you dont have leftover sweet potatoes and  prepping the sweet potatoes from the beginning, just place 4 medium sweet potatoes (about 125g each) in the oven at 350°F for 70 to 80 minutes. Let them cool slightly, open them, and mash the insides with a fork.

4.Close the waffle maker and press down gently - let it do its thing until it becomes crispy on both sides.

5.While the waffle cooks, mash half an avocado with a little salt and pepper and a touch of lime juice or use guacamole.

6.Top the waffle with an egg, some everything bagel seasoning and chile lime seasoning.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

ENJOY
.
.
.
.
#healthyrecipes #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyfoodshare #goodmoodfood
summary: A savory breakfast waffle recipe made with sweet potato, cheese, and egg, topped with avocado and an egg.

caption:
Trader Joe's Masala Burrata Sandwich 🥪🌿
A flavorful fusion sandwich with crispy toasted bread, spicy masala burger patties, creamy burrata, and tangy pickled onions!
Ingredients
* Trader Joe's Sandwich Starter bread
* Trader Joe's Vegan Pesto
* Trader Joe's Masala Burger Patties
* Trader Joe's Burrata Filling
* Tomato slices
* Pickled onions
* Fresh spinach
* Salt & black pepper
* Butter or oil for toasting (optional)
Instructions
1. Toast the Trader Joe's Sandwich Starter bread on a pan or toaster until golden and crispy. You can lightly butter the bread for extra crunch.
2. Cook the masala burger patties according to package instructions until hot and slightly crispy on the outside.
3. Spread a generous layer of Trader Joe's Vegan Pesto on one side of the toasted bread.
4. Place the cooked masala burger patty on the bread. Top with tomato slices, burrata filling, pickled onions, and fresh spinach.
5. Sprinkle salt and black pepper to taste.
6. Close the sandwich with the second slice of toasted bread, slice in half, and serve warm.
Perfect for a quick lunch, picnic, or easy high-flavor meal 🌱✨

caption:
~280 cal | 30g protein per serving
Ever heard of a paneer based prawn curry? 😛
This is what I make when I want a creamy curry except with no cream and more protein. Perfectly cooked prawns finished with a flavourful blended paneer velvety gravy. All the richness, none of the heaviness — and 30g of protein per bowl. Save this one.

Creamy Spicy Paneer Prawn Curry
Per serving (makes 2):
Cal: ~280 | Protein: ~30g | Carbs: ~10g | Fat: ~13g | Fibre: ~2g
Ingredients:
	•	200g prawns, cleaned and deveined
	•	1.5 tbsp mustard oil or ghee (half for gravy, half for tadka)
	•	6–8 garlic cloves, finely chopped
	•	1 medium onion, finely chopped
	•	1 tsp turmeric powder (+ a pinch for tadka)
	•	1 tsp red chilli powder (+ 1 tsp for tadka)
	•	1 tsp black pepper powder
	•	1 tsp garam masala
	•	½ tsp chilli flakes
	•	1 tbsp tomato paste (or 2 tbsp tomato puree)
	•	Salt, to taste
	•	½ cup water or no-salt chicken stock
	•	100g low-fat paneer, blended smooth with 50ml water
	•	Fresh coriander leaves, chopped
	•	To serve: rice or roti, sliced onion, green chilli, lemon wedge

Method:
	1.	Heat half the mustard oil in a pan till it lightly smokes, then lower the flame.
	2.	Add the chopped garlic and onion. Sauté till soft and golden.
	3.	Tip in the prawns and cook for a minute, just till they start turning pink.
	4.	Add turmeric, chilli powder, pepper, garam masala, chilli flakes, and salt. Mix well.
	5.	Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.
	6.	Pour in the water or stock and simmer for 3–4 minutes.
	7.	Add the blended paneer and stir gently. Simmer for 2 minutes till the gravy turns creamy.
	8.	For the tadka: heat remaining mustard oil in a small pan till smoking. Take off the flame, add a pinch each of turmeric and chilli powder, and pour straight over the curry.
	9.	Finish with chopped coriander.
	10.	Serve hot with rice or roti, sliced onion, green chilli, and a wedge of lemon.
.
high protein prawn curry, high protein recipe, creamy prawn curry without cream, low calorie prawn curry, paneer prawn curry, Indian prawn recipe, healthy seafood curry

#creamyprawncurry #prawncurry #highproteinrecipe
summary: A creamy paneer-based prawn curry with 30g protein and 280 calories per serving, using blended paneer instead of cream.

caption:
HIGH PROTEIN CHICKEN CAESAR PASTA SALAD

This comes out to 400 calories, 46g protein, 11 net carbs a serving and one of those incredible combinations that takes a calorie heavy restaurant menu item and makes it into a healthy pasta salad - this is a great, substantial meal on its own, and is so good! 

It's also one of my favorite ways to use my high protein lowcarb kaizen pasta. Kaizen has just just 6 net carbs a serving, packs 20 grams of protein, and its gluten free. 

✅Comment "PROTEINPASTA" and I'll DM you how to grab my kaizen pasta!

As always, use whatever pasta you like!

INGREDIENTS:

CHICKEN: 
1 lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs
1/2 tsp avocado oil 
Juice of 1 lemon 
1 tbsp smoked paprika
1 tsp oregano 
1 tsp onion powder
1/2 tsp chili flakes

DRESSING:
2 tbsp avocado oil mayo
4 tbsp Greek yogurt
1 tbsp Dijon mustard
1.5 tbsp apple cider vinegar 
Juice of 1 lemon
1 garlic clove, finely minced
3 anchovies, finely diced (optional, but so good!)
1/4 cup fresh grated Parmesan
Salt + LOTS of cracked pepper

SALAD:
2 heads of romaine, finely chopped 
8oz protein pasta (I use kaizen)

Here's how I made it:

1. Drizzle 1 lb chicken thighs with 1/2 tsp avocado oil, then add the juice of 1 lemon, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tsp oregano, 1 tsp onion powder, and 1/2 tsp chili flakes. Mix well, then roast at 425F for 35 mins, remove then dice it up into cubes.

2. For the dressing: mix 2 tbsp avocado oil mayo with 4 tbsp plain Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp Dijon mustard, 1.5 tbsp apple cider vinegar, juice of a lemon, 1 minced garlic cloves, 3 finely diced anchovy filets (optional), 1/4 cup shredded parmesan, salt, and lots of fresh cracked pepper. Mix well, think out with water or lemon juice as preferred.

3. Finely chop 2 heads of romaine.

4.Cook 8oz of your favorite high protein or lowcarb pasta. I used my @kaizenfoodco pasta because its both high protein (20g) and low carb (6 net carbs).

5. Assemble in a jar or bowl: Layer cubed chicken, romaine, then the pasta, then flip, drizzle with dressing, top with extra parmesan on top, and ENJOY!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS

ENJOY!
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #highprotein
summary: A high-protein, low-carb chicken Caesar pasta salad with 400 calories and 46g protein per serving.

caption:
✅This is such a good sandwich trick - and honestly, tastes so good, I dont miss the bread.

Im down 150lbs and it's been all about not only eating more protein and fewer carbs, but food thats exciting and I look forward to eating. This a great way to get some more veggies in, keep carbs low, and instead of adding cheese to the sandwich, adding into inside the "bread" :)

You can fill these up however way you want!

Here is how I made it:

1.Grab 2 large zucchini. Shred them inside a large bowl over a towel, then squeeze out the excess water really well… like REALLY well. Until you are leftover with zucchini pulp in the bowl. Discard the water or use it to feed plants.

2.To the zucchini pulp, add two to three pinches salt, 1tsp garlic powder, and 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese and mix really well.

3.Line a baking tray with parchment paper, spray a bit of avocado oil, then divide up the zucchini into 4 buns and press down to make it even height. You can always top this with a little more cheese on top if you'd like.

4.Pop the tray in the oven at 400F for 20-22 mins, until fully set. Remove and let cool for a couple minutes.

5.Fill these with whatever fillings you want. I spread the base with 2tbsp of my protein mayo (to make this I just mix 2 tbsp mayo with 4 tbsp yogurt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, a couple pinches salt, with lots of fresh cracked pepper). Then add whatever toppings you like. For me it was tomato, lettuce, some red onion, and ham.

Put the top side on, cut in half, and ENJOY!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!
.
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #highproteinmeals #healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare

caption:
Mom's sheet pan Benihana fried rice✨

My debut cookbook is officially out now! Order with the link in my bio or search for Maxi's Kitchen wherever you like to buy books.

Fried Rice:▪️3 Cups Cooked, Cooled Jasmine Rice (from 1 cup uncooked rice)▪️1/3 Cup Finely Chopped Yellow Onion▪️1/4 Cup Finely Chopped Carrot▪️2 Scallions, sliced▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Pepper▪️1 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️1 Tbsp Sesame Seeds▪️2 Tbsp Toasted Sesame Oil▪️2 Eggs▪️

Garlic Butter:▪️1-2 Tbsp Butter▪️1 Garlic Clove, finely chopped▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F and line a sheet pan with parchment paper.
2️⃣ Spread the rice in an even layer and top with the onion, carrot, salt, pepper, soy sauce, sesame seeds, and sesame oil. (Make sure to drizzle some of the sesame oil around the edges of the rice for extra crispiness.)
3️⃣ In a small bowl, combine the garlic and butter. Microwave until melted (or melt in a pan), stir, and drizzle evenly over the rice.
4️⃣ Use two spoons to make two small wells in the rice, then crack an egg into each one.
5️⃣ Bake for 15 mins, until the rice is crisp at the edges and the eggs are cooked through. Slice the eggs into small pieces with two knives, then transfer everything to a serving bowl and toss until evenly combined. Enjoy!

⏲10 minute prep time + 15 minute cook time

Recipe serves 4 as a side dish

caption:
WATERMELON SALAD with berries & feta 

This is one of my fav watermelon salad combinations. If you need a healthy summer salad recipe with all the right textures and flavors, check this out. The watermelon, feta, mint combo is such a classic on its own, but when you add berries, some lime, and pistachios into the equation, it takes it to the top

Feel free to adjust the ratios below however way you wish depending on what you like - and you can always swap out the pistachios for walnuts, cashews, or pine nuts.

Here is how I made it:

1.Start with 4-5 cups of watermelon chunks at the base base - I cut these in small chunks, about 1/4 inch on each side.

2.Add 4oz blueberries or blackberries & 4oz of raspberries.

3.Add 1/3 cup fresh finely chopped mint. This part is important, trust me!

4.Add the zest and juice of 1 large lime.

5.Top with 4-5oz of feta and a couple drizzles of high quality olive oil, and a couple pinches of salt (but not too much because the feta has some salt already!)

6.Sprinkle 1-2 tbsp chile lime seasoning or tajin.

7.Toss gently but well, & right before serving, crown it all off with 1/3-1/2 cup freshly toasted pistachios.

This is best served IMMEDIATELY 😎

ENJOY!!
.
.
.
.
#salads #healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #mediterraneanfood #lowcarb
summary: A fresh watermelon salad recipe with berries, feta, mint, lime, and toasted pistachios.

caption:
✅CRISPY lowcarb MEDITERRANEAN CHICKEN - topped with burrata and a fresh tomato salad

This is better than takeout crispy Mediterranean chicken that will completely blow you away. I used lowcarb breadcrumbs here and popped these in the oven for a healthier spin - all the crispy, without the extra oil! It's packed with flavor, SO good, and you can also airfry it if you wish.

Here is how I made it:

1.Grab 2lbs of chicken - you can use tenderloins or boneless skinless chicken thighs. Add to a bowl. 

2.Season chicken with 1 tsp olive oil, Salt + Pepper, 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp oregano, 1 tbsp garlic powder or granulated garlic, 1/2 tbsp chili flakes

3.Crack 3 eggs into a bowl. In a separate bowl, add 1 cup of regular breadcrumbs, lowcarb breadcrumbs (I used Real Phat Foods lowcarb crumbs), or crushed up pork rinds.

4.Dip the chicken into the egg wash, then into the crumbs you're using. Then lay out on a grated oven safe tray so the air can circulate.

5.Pop into the oven at 400F for 30-35 mins.

6.While thats going, make a beautiful tomato salad. I cut a pint of cherry tomatoes in half, season with salt, fresh cracked pepper, and drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tbsp balsamic glaze. Let it sit for a while.

7.When the chicken is done, remove it, top with fresh mozarella or burrata, top with a spoonful or two of the tomato salad, top with fresh basil, and enjoy!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #ketorecipes
summary: A low-carb baked Mediterranean chicken recipe topped with burrata and fresh tomato salad, using low-carb breadcrumbs for a healthier crispy coating.

caption:
460 cal | 32g protein per serving

Red sauce pasta, but make it Asian inspired, umami packed and high in protein. Soy, oyster sauce, and a teaspoon of chilli crisp tucked into the tomatoes, basil, blended paneer — high protein, fully creamy, no compromise. Save this one for your next pasta night.

Asian inspired Tomato Basil Pasta

Per serving: 460 cal | 32g protein | 55g carbs | 13g fat | 9g fibre
Makes 3 Servings
Ingredients:

	•	High-protein pasta, 180g dry
	•	Extra virgin olive oil, 1 tbsp
	•	Garlic paste, 1 tbsp
	•	Onion, 1 medium, chopped
	•	Bell pepper, 1 large, chopped
	•	Cherry tomatoes, 300g, halved
	•	Soy sauce, 1.5 tbsp
	•	Oyster sauce, 1 tbsp
	•	Chilli crisp, 2 tsp
	•	Honey, 1 tsp
	•	Kashmiri chilli powder, ¾ tsp
	•	Black pepper powder, ½ tsp
	•	Salt, a pinch
	•	Low-fat paneer or tofu or cottage cheese , 300g, blended with ¾ cup water into a smooth purée
	•	Fresh basil, a generous handful, torn
	•	Grated parmesan, 15g, to finish

Method:

	1.	Cook the pasta in well-salted water till al dente. Reserve a little pasta water before draining.
	2.	Heat the olive oil in a pan on medium. Add the garlic paste and sauté till fragrant.
	3.	Toss in the onions and bell peppers and cook till softened with a little colour at the edges.
	4.	Add the halved cherry tomatoes, soy sauce, oyster sauce, chilli crisp, honey, Kashmiri chilli powder, pepper and salt. Cook on medium-low till the tomatoes break down completely and the mixture turns jammy.
	5.	Pour in the paneer purée and stir through — this is your creamy base. Let it bubble for a minute.
	6.	Add the torn basil and the cooked pasta. Toss to coat, loosening with reserved pasta water if needed.
	7.	Plate, finish with grated parmesan, an extra drizzle of chilli crisp, and a few more basil leaves on top.

.
(umami tomato basil pasta, pasta, high protein pasta, fusion pasta recipe, red sauce pasta, pomodoro)
.

#umamitomatobasilpasta #pasta #highproteinpasta
summary: Asian-inspired high-protein tomato basil pasta with soy, oyster sauce, and blended paneer for a creamy umami sauce.

caption:
Meals for Weight-loss: Ep 3
Chicken Souvlaki Bowl

If you want all my recipes with macros 
Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the link!!

Ingredients: 

Chicken Marinade
* Chicken breast, 800g
* 0% Greek yogurt, 0.5 cups
* Lemon juice, 2 tbsp
* Olive oil, 1 tbsp
* Garlic, 4 cloves minced
* Dried oregano, 2 tsp
* Paprika, 2 tsp
* Salt, 1.5 tsp
* Black pepper, 1 tsp

Potatoes
* Potatoes, 1200g diced
* Olive oil spray, few sprays
* Dried oregano, 2 tsp
* Garlic powder, 1 tbsp
* Paprika, 2 tsp
* Salt, 1 tsp
* Black pepper, 0.5 tsp
* Lemon juice, 1 tbsp

Greek Salad
* Cucumber, 1 large diced
* Tomatoes, 2 medium diced
* Red onion, 0.5 medium sliced
* Parsley, 2 tbsp chopped
* Lemon juice, 1 tbsp
* Salt, pinch

Tzatziki
* Greek yogurt (0%), 1.5 cups
* Cucumber, 0.5 grated and squeezed dry
* Garlic, 2 cloves grated
* Lemon juice, 1 tbsp
* Salt, 0.5 tsp
* Black pepper, pinch

Instructions
1. Add the chicken, yogurt, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper to a bowl. Mix well and marinate for at least 30 minutes. Tread the chicken onto skewers if available. 
2. Dice the potatoes into small cubes, then season with oregano, garlic powder, paprika, salt, pepper, and olive oil spray. Air fry at 400°F for 20 minutes, shaking halfway through, then finish with lemon juice.
3. Air fry the chicken at 400°F for 12 minutes or until fully cooked and lightly charred on the outside.
4. Chop the cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, and parsley, then add them to a bowl with lemon juice and a small pinch of salt. Mix well.
5. Grate the cucumber for the tzatziki and squeeze out as much water as possible using paper towels or a clean kitchen towel. Add it to a bowl with Greek yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, dill, salt, and pepper, then mix until smooth.
6. Divide the potatoes, chicken, salad, and tzatziki evenly into 4 bowls and serve.

The goal is 40lbs in 90 days

caption:
🏃 18g of protein Tupperware frittatas that are perfect for on-the-go.

💾 Save this one for when you know you've got a busy week ahead.

🥪 These go down a treat in my household, especially as my wife and I are super busy during the week. Enjoy one on its own as a light meal, or use it to bulk out a wrap, sandwich, or salad bowl. We never get bored of them!

💡 Top tip: make sure to dry your greens completely before adding to avoid excess water.

For plenty more prep-ahead breakfast ideas ➡️ simply download The Doctor's Kitchen app and search "breakfast".

Ingredients (serves 3)
6 eggs
50g peas (frozen and thawed)
50g spring onion
50g spinach (frozen and thawed)
50g sun-dried tomatoes
150g cottage cheese

Method
1. Gather and prepare your ingredients. Preheat the oven to 200°C/180°C fan. Line a small glass ovenproof dish (8x12cm) with parchment paper.
2. Crack the eggs into a bowl and whisk to break up.
3. Add all remaining ingredients and stir to combine.
4. Pour the mixture into your prepared baking dish and bake for 16–18 minutes, until mostly set. A slight jiggle is fine, as they will continue to cook further in the residual heat. If any parts look very runny, use a knife to gently cut through the frittata so the uncooked mixture can mingle with the cooked, then return to the oven for 30 seconds to 1 minute.
summary: High-protein baked frittata recipe with eggs, cottage cheese, and vegetables for meal-prepped breakfasts.

caption:
The first time I tried this was at a restaurant near me called Woodstack, and I've done a lot of trial and error since them to get it right. These are the 3 steps that I landed on to make them taste perfect everytime 😋

WHAT YOU NEED

25 Brussel Sprouts
Large pinch of Salt

TO SEASON

1 tbsp Olive Oil
1/2 tsp garlic powder
1/4 tsp oregano
1/2 tsp red chili powder
Pinch of Salt

FOR THE GLAZE

1/3 cup maple syrup (the real stuff)
1-2 tsp red chili flakes
1/2 tsp garlic powder
1/2 tsp Italian seasoning
2 tbsp butter

HOW TO MAKE IT

1. Coat the Brussel sprouts in 2 large pinches of salt and toss. Let it sit for 5 minute while you bring a pot of water to boil. Drop the salted Brussel sprouts the boiling water and boil for 5 minutes.
2. Strain the Brussel sprouts and toss them into iced cold water. Slice each one into two halves.
3. Add the brussel sprout halves to a bowl with 1-2 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, oregano, red chilli powder and salt.
4. Spread them on a baking tray with no parchment paper, with the center FACE DOWN. Bake or airfry at 400F for about 20-25 minutes. Keep an eye on them  to make sure they don't burn but don't move the brussel sprouts while they bake.
5. In a bowl, add the maple syrup, red chili flakes, garlic powder, Italian seasoning and butter. Microwave until the butter melts completely and stir together. Toss the crispy brussel sprouts in & it's ready to go!

#bakedmacandcheese #recipe #recipes #thanksgiving #foodporn #foodie #cooking #reels #holidayrecipes #thanksgivingsides #sides #appetizers #snacks #brusselsprouts #easyrecipes #holidayrecipes #macandcheese
summary: Maple-glazed roasted Brussels sprouts recipe with three-step method for crispy caramelized results.

caption:
🥘 Shredded Tofu Quesadillas

Vegan • Easy • Oven-Baked

Crispy on the outside, cheesy on the inside, and loaded with smoky, saucy tofu 😋these quesadillas are a total weeknight win 😍

INGREDIENTS 💚
• 1 block extra-firm tofu, shredded
• 1–2 tbsp olive oil
• 1 tsp chili powder
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp paprika
• Salt to taste
• 1 cup your favorite salsa
• Tortillas
• Vegan cheese

DIRECTIONS ⭐️

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a sheet pan with parchment.
2. Shred tofu and toss with olive oil, chili powder, onion powder, garlic powder, paprika, and salt.
3. Spread evenly on the sheet pan and bake 12–15 minutes, mixing halfway, until lightly crispy.
4. Transfer tofu to a bowl and mix with 1 cup salsa.
5. Prepare a second sheet pan. Lay tortillas flat, add vegan cheese to one half and tofu mixture on the other. Fold closed.
6. Lightly brush tops with olive oil.
7. Bake until golden, crispy, and melty.

Serve hot with salsa, cashew cheeze sauce, or your favourite toppings 🌿

➡️ Go to blog for full recipe 💛

#veganrecipes #easyvegan #plantbased #quesadillas #tofurecipes weeknightdinner
summary: Oven-baked shredded tofu quesadillas with smoky spiced filling and vegan cheese.

caption:
160 calories. 12g protein. All the cosy comfort of restaurant-style corn palak — minus the cream, plus a serious protein punch. 🌽🌿
This is one of those throw-it-together weeknight meals when you want something warm, indulgent, and actually filling. The blended high-protein paneer turns into a silky sauce that coats every kernel of sweet corn and every wilted spinach leaf. Finish with a drizzle of chilli oil and it tastes far more decadent than it is.
Creamy No Cream Corn & Spinach 
160 cal | 12g protein | 7g fat | 14g carbs (per serving)
INGREDIENTS (Serves 2)
	•	1 tsp butter
	•	2-3 cloves garlic, chopped
	•	½ cup boiled sweet corn
	•	1 cup chopped spinach
	•	Salt, to taste
	•	Chilli flakes, to taste
	•	½ tsp peri peri seasoning
	•	½ tsp Italian seasoning (optional)
	•	100g high-protein low-fat paneer
	•	3-4 tbsp water (for blending)
	•	1 tsp chilli oil (to finish)
METHOD
	1.	Heat butter in a pan. Add chopped garlic and sauté till fragrant.
	2.	Add boiled sweet corn and chopped spinach. Cook for 2-3 minutes till the spinach wilts.
	3.	Season with salt, chilli flakes, peri peri seasoning, and Italian seasoning.
	4.	Blend the paneer with water till smooth and creamy.
	5.	Pour into the pan and cook for another 2-3 minutes till it comes together into a creamy sauce.
	6.	Finish with a drizzle of chilli oil. Serve hot with rice.
.

caption:
💥CHICKEN SHAWARMA LOAF PAN CHICKEN

This truly is the secret to making the easiest, best chicken right at home without turning on a grill.

The chicken is spiced so ridiculously well, the chicken comes out so flavorful and juicy, and you can make a ton of easy meals throughout the week. Use it alongside some roasted vegetables, inside some pita, or you can make high protein bowls with some rice. I used my @kaizenfoodco high protein low carb rice, but use whatever rice you like depending on your goals!

Here is how to make it:

1 Start with 2.5 lbs boneless chicken thighs. Trim off the excess fat as you wish and place inside a large bowl.

2 Drizzle the chicken with about 1 tbsp of olive oil, then season with a couple pinches of salt and pepper, and some spices. I used 1.5 tbsp garlic powder, 1.5 tbsp sweet paprika, 1/4 to 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper, 1 tbsp oregano, 1 tbsp Aleppo pepper, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp cinnamon. To make the chicken extra juicy, I also add 2 tbsp yogurt, 1.5 tbsp tomato paste, the juice of half large lemon.

3 Mix it super well, then thinly slice 1/2 large yellow onion and mix it in with the chicken. If you can, let this marinate for 2-3 hrs or overnight, but if you want to make it right away, you can.

4 When you're ready to eat, let the chicken come up to room temp while you pack it inside a loaf pan (the size of my loaf pan is 8.5x4.5x2.5). Place it in the oven for 50-55 mins at 425F.

5 Remove your chicken from the oven, tilt the pan over a glass container to pour out the excess juices. I usually pour this over the chicken before serving, or sometimes use it as a base for soups.

6 Flip the loafpan over a cutting board and cut chicken into strips, and serve it up!

I made an amazing high protein lowcarb chicken shawarma and rice bowl with 55g protein with my @kaizenfoodco lowcarb rice, a fresh garden salad, and my garlicky white sauce. So easy, and so good!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS. I share easy high protein recipes, Mediterranean food, and more!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #chickenrecipes #keto #mediterraneanfood #lowcarbrecipes
summary: A baked chicken shawarma loaf pan recipe with Mediterranean spices, yogurt marinade, and serving suggestions for meal prep.

caption:
Chickpea, Beet, Feta & Roasted Squash Salad

 Ingredients

* 2 cups butternut squash, cubed
* 1 tbsp olive oil
* 1 tsp paprika
* Salt & black pepper, to taste
* 1 can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
* 2 cooked beets, diced
* 1/2 cup feta cheese, crumbled
* 2 cups mixed greens
* 2 tbsp pumpkin seeds (optional)

Dressing

* 2 tbsp olive oil
* 1 tbsp lemon juice
* 1 tsp honey
* 1/2 tsp Dijon mustard
* Salt & black pepper, to taste

 Instructions

1. Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
2. Toss squash with olive oil, paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast for 25 minutes until tender.
3. In a small bowl, whisk dressing ingredients together.
4. In a large bowl, combine mixed greens, chickpeas, beets, roasted squash, and feta.
5. Drizzle with dressing and toss gently.
6. Top with pumpkin seeds and serve.
.

caption:
Baked Parmesan Zucchini Rounds

Ingredients
2 medium zucchinis, sliced into 1/4-inch rounds

1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese

1.5 tbsp Extra Virgin Olive Oil

1/2 tsp garlic powder

Salt and black pepper to taste

Optional: 1/4 cup Panko breadcrumbs (mix with cheese)

Garnish: Freshly chopped parsley

Instructions
Preheat: Set oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a tray with parchment.

Prep: Pat zucchini slices dry with paper towels.

Season: Toss zucchini with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper in a bowl.

Arrange: Place in a single layer on the tray. Press a small amount of Parmesan onto each round.

Bake: Roast for 15-20 mins until tender.

Broil: Optional 1 minute for a deeper golden crust.

Serve: Garnish with parsley and enjoy hot.

caption:
💥PROTEIN BABA

This babaghanoush packs 60 grams of protein for the whole thing - break it up into 4 or 6 servings as an appetizer, or use it as a spread for your favorite sandwich.

To boost up the protein and make it more creamy, I added some thick greek yogurt in, but you can always skip the yogurt or add half.

Usually, you'll want to grill the eggplant for maximum smoky flavor (ideally on a charcoal grill), but if you dont have a grill handy like me, this method in the oven will be just as good! 

Here is how I made it:

1.Grab 2 medium eggplant, cut them in half, score them diagonally, then drizzle liberally with olive oil and season liberally with sea salt. Roast in the oven at 425F for 55-60 minutes. Time can vary by oven, so keep an eye on it. If you're grilling, just make sure these are as mushy as possible, which means they are done!

2.Remove the cooked eggplant, carefully peel the skin, then put on top of a strainer to remove any excess liquid while it cools. You can also remove majorly excess seeds here if you prefer. Mine didn't really have too many seeds, but if I come across an eggplant that's too seedy, I'll pick out extra seeds. Again, optional.

3.While thats going, grab a food processor, add in 3 large garlic cloves, the juice of 1 large lemon, 1/3 cup of a velvety tahini, a couple generous pinches salt (salt is KEY!). And to add some protein, add 1.5 cups of a thick greek yogurt-  I used fage 0%. Whip these together without the eggplant and let sit in the food processor.

4.Add the eggplant into the food processor, and gently process, pulsing a few times until very well combined,

5.Then plate it up! I spread the baba at the bottom of a plate, then topped with some olive oil, fresh chopped mint and parsley, Aleppo pepper flakes, and pine nuts.

6.You can use this as a spread, as a dip, or as a topping. Always great with some lowcarb pita, crackers, or cucumber/carrots.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS! If you like Mediterranean food or middle eastern food, follow along!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #highprotein
summary: High-protein baba ghanoush recipe made with roasted eggplant, Greek yogurt, and tahini, yielding 60g protein per batch.

caption:
✅EASY high protein chicken salad (Mediterranean inspired)

This has 154g protein and 1300 calories for the whole bowl! Divide into 3 huge servings at just 430 calories, 
 51g protein, and 10 net carbs a serving.

If you love mediterranean food, and want to get your protein in, this is such a great healthy high protein mediterranean inspired chicken salad.

➡️Comment "BANGER" and I'll send you my free mini protein ebook.

Its got briny olives, sundried tomato, a creamy lemony garlicky dressing,  plus over 140g protein in total. Split this into 3 super satisfying servings for around 45g protein each.
The lemony dressing softens the onions slightly, the herbs wake everything up, and the olives + sundried tomatoes bring so much flavor to every bite.

Eat it as is, over lettuce wraps or sourdough, or scoop it up with lowcarb crackers.

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Add 3 cups cooked shredded or diced chicken brst to a large bowl. Add 1/2 finely diced red onion, 1/3 cup finely chopped Kalamata olives, 1/4 cup chopped sundried tomatoes (the ones marinated in olive oil), 2 thinly sliced scallions, and a generous handful of fresh cilantro and mint.

2️⃣ In a blender or food processor, add 1 cup plain Greek yogurt, 3 tbsp mayo, 2 garlic cloves, zest and juice of 1 large lemon, 1 tsp oregano, 1 tsp chili pepper flakes, salt, and black pepper. Process until smooth and creamy.

3️⃣ Pour the dressing over the salad and toss everything together really well. Taste and adjust lemon, salt, or herbs however you like. I almost always add a touch more lemon juice and salt.

4️⃣ Let it sit for 10-15 mins before serving if you can, because it gets even better

caption:
Would you make this EASY CHOPPED EGG SALAD??

I always meal prep eggs on sundays to make simple healthy meals like this throughout the week This lunch as it as easy as it gets, and so good every time. I feel like egg salads dont get as much love as they should, but this is such a great way to keep things lower calorie and lower carb, and sneak a ton of veggies in.

This makes 2 servings, but as always, adjust however way you wish. I scoop these up over lowcarb crackers, or you can eat them over lettuce as boats or even bell peppers, and of course, sourdough or pita would be amazing, too.

Here is how I made it:

1.You can chop this up on a cutting board or grab a bowl and mash it, it's up to you.

2.Start with the eggs, I boiled 5 hard boil eggs. I bring water to a boil, grab my eggs from the fridge and drop them in for 8.5 minutes. When theyre done, drop them into an ice bath for about 15-20 seconds to stop the cookign, then they easily peel.  Cut them in half and add them to the board.

3.Add 2 scallions, half of a small shallot, half a jalapeno (de-seed if you don't want it spicy), half or a quarter of an avocado, and fresh herbs like cilantro, parsley, or dill. I usually use cilantro. 

4.Dress it up with 1.5 tbsp greek yogurt, 1 tbsp mayo, 1 tbsp mustard, a squeeze or two of fresh lemon juice, and a couple pinches of salt and pepper.

5. Chop everything up with a knife to your desired consistency (I like to get a bit of everything in each bite and keep it somewhat chunky)

Then just serve it over your favorite vessel and enjoy. If you're eating keto, grab some lowcarb crackers, lettuce, or red bell peppers, if you're eating lowcarb sourdough or your favorite lowcarb bread, and if you're just looking to eat healthy, enjoy it with whatever  you like!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
.
#eggsalad #healthyrecipes #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #ketorecipes
summary: Easy chopped egg salad recipe with boiled eggs, avocado, scallions, and Greek yogurt dressing, customizable for keto or low-carb diets.

caption:
Chilli Lime Roasted Cauliflower 💚

Ingredients:

-1 medium cauliflower, cut into florets
-1 tablespoon extra virgin olive oil
-1 teaspoon paprika
-1 teaspoon garlic powder
-1/2 teaspoon salt
-black pepper

Sauce
-2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
-zest from 1 lime
-juice from 1/2 lime
-1 tablespoon honey
-1 teaspoon chilli flakes 
-fresh parsley, finely chopped 

Instructions:

1. Preheat your oven to 200 degrees C.
2. In a large bowl toss together the cauliflower florets, olive oil, paprika, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
3. Like a baking tray with parchment paper and add the seasoned florets in a single layer. Roast for 20-30 minutes, or until the edges are browned and the insides are tender.
4. For the sauce, add all the ingredients to a small pan and cook on low heat for about 1 minute.
5. Spoon sauce over cauliflower and serve. Enjoy!

caption:
I might get into trouble sharing this… 👀
but these are that famous restaurant(s) green beans everyone orders every single time.

Perfectly blistered, glossy, salty-sweet, and somehow better than the main dish.
If you've eaten there, you already know exactly what I'm talking about.

Same flavor. Same drama.
Zero reservation required.

Save this for the next time you want a side dish that steals the show 💚
Recipe below ⬇️

Ingredients
1-2 Lbs  fresh green beans trimmed

2 tbsp. avocado oil 
8-10 @kosher_taste garlic cloves

For the glaze: 
3 tbsp. Sesame oil
1/4 cup  soy sauce or tamari
2 tbsp sugar or honey 
2 small lemons zested and juiced (can use only 1 lemon if you don't like it too tangy) 
Salt 
Optional sesame seeds or chili flakes

Instructions
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil Add green beans and cook 5-7 minutes until bright green and crisp Drain and immediately transfer to ice water

Process the garlic cloves with the oil till spoonable. 

Heat a wide skillet or wok over high heat 
Add oil to coat bottom of pan. 
Once shimmering add green beans in a single layer 
Let sit undisturbed 1 to 2 minutes until blistered 
Toss and repeat once more

Add garlic directly to the pan and cook and toss for 1-2 minutes until fragrant 
Remove green beans to serving bowl. 

In same dirty pan , add soy sauce coconut sugar and sesame oil . Toss quickly until glossy and sugar is melted 

Pour over the green beans. Add the lemon zest and juice and generously salt to taste. 

serve immediately

Episode No. 11 NO BORING BEANS 🫘 Black Bean Enchilada Skillet 🫘 Be sure to follow @dietitiandebbie for more bean recipes that are anything BUT boring.😉

This black bean enchilada skillet is one of my top recipes for a reason! A one pot meal that takes 40 minutes or less from pantry staples like beans, enchilada sauce, and tortillas.

INGREDIENTS
1 tablespoon olive oil
1/2 yellow onion, chopped (about 1 cup) chopped
1 bell pepper, chopped
2 zucchini or summer squash, sliced and quartered (about 2 cups)
1/4 cup chopped jalapeno
1 tablespoon chili powder
2 teaspoons garlic powder
1 1/2 teaspoons cumin
1 1/2 cups red enchilada sauce
1 (15 oz.) can black beans
5 corn tortillas cut into strips
1 1/4 cups shredded vegan Mexican blend cheese

DIRECTIONS
In a large skillet with tall sides add the olive oil, onion, bell pepper, and zucchini/squash. Cook until onion is translucent, about 5-7 minutes. �
Stir in the jalapeño, chili powder, garlic powder, and cumin. Cook for another 1-2 minutes. �

Stir in the black beans and tortillas. Taste and add salt if needed. �
Sprinkle with the cheese and bake under the broiler to melt cheese on top. (Only takes a minute or two!)�
Serve with chopped avocado, cilantro, hot sauce, vegan sour cream.

caption:
248 cal & 23g protein per Serving
Sweet from ripe mango, smoky from soaked dry red chillies, salty-umami from soy, all melting into a creamy coconut sauce. The paneer is grilled till charred for that lovely smoky bite, and the crushed peanuts on top? Non-negotiable. All this goodness — and a proper restaurant-style meal — for under 250 cal a serving, with 23g of protein doing the heavy lifting.
Creamy Mango Chilli Paneer
Macros per serving: 248 cal | 23g protein | 17g carbs | 10g fat | 2g fibre
Ingredients (serves 2):
For the sauce:
	•	6 dry red chillies (soaked in hot water for 10 mins, drained)
	•	50g ripe mango
	•	5 garlic cloves
	•	1 stalk spring onion
	•	½ tsp honey
	•	1½ tbsp light soy sauce
	•	2 tsp lemon juice
	•	Salt to taste
	•	2–3 tbsp water (to blend)
To cook:
	•	200g low-fat, high-protein paneer, cubed
	•	60ml light coconut milk
	•	Salt, black pepper, and chilli flakes (for the paneer)
To finish:
	•	1 tbsp crushed roasted peanuts
	•	Fresh coriander, chopped
	•	Steamed rice, to serve
Method:
	1.	Soak the dry red chillies in hot water for 10 minutes until soft. Drain.
	2.	Blend the soaked chillies with mango, garlic, spring onion, honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, salt, and water until smooth.
	3.	Pour the sauce into a non-stick pan and cook on medium heat for 4–5 minutes, until the raw smell of garlic and onion disappears and it thickens slightly. Splash in a little water if it sticks.
	4.	Stir in the coconut milk and simmer for 2 more minutes until creamy and glossy.
	5.	Meanwhile, season the paneer cubes with salt, pepper, and chilli flakes. Dry-grill on a hot non-stick pan until charred on the edges.
	6.	Add the grilled paneer to the sauce and toss to coat.
	7.	Finish with crushed peanuts and coriander. Serve hot with steamed rice.
creamy mango chilli paneer, mango coconut paneer recipe, high protein paneer recipe, low calorie paneer recipe, indian fusion paneer dish, macro friendly paneer recipe, paneer in mango sauce.

#creamymangochillipaneer #paneerrecipes #highproteinrecipes​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
summary: Creamy mango chilli paneer recipe with grilled paneer, mango-coconut sauce, and 23g protein per 248-calorie serving.

caption:
Chopped salad tostadas with avocado and dill✨

My debut cookbook is officially out now! Order with the link in my bio or search for Maxi's Kitchen wherever you like to buy books.

Tostadas: ▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️6 Corn Tortillas (I use Guerrero brand)▪️Kosher Salt▪️

Dressing:▪️1 Tbsp + 1 tsp Fresh Lemon Juice▪️1 Garlic Clove, grated or minced▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️ 1/4 tsp Pepper▪️

Salad:▪️1 Cup Diced Cucumber▪️1 Cup Diced Red Cabbage▪️1/2 Cup Quartered Cherry Tomatoes▪️1/4 Cup Diced Red Onion▪️1 Avocado, diced▪️3 Tbsp Chopped Fresh Dill, plus more for garnish▪️Flaky Salt▪️

1️⃣ Make the tostadas: Preheat the oven to 400F on convection mode (or 425F regular bake) and drizzle the olive oil on a large baking sheet.
2️⃣ Rub both sides of each tortilla in the oil and sprinkle with salt. Bake for 6 minutes on the first side, flip and bake for 3-5 more minutes until deep golden brown and crispy.
3️⃣ Meanwhile, make the dressing:  In a small bowl, add the lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Whisk until combined. 
4️⃣ Make the salad: In a medium bowl, add the cucumber, cabbage, cherry tomatoes, red onion, avocado, and fresh dill. Pour on the dressing and toss until all ingredients are evenly distributed. 
5️⃣ Spoon the salad onto the tostadas and sprinkle with flaky salt. Garnish with a sprig of fresh dill on each and enjoy!

⏲15 minute prep time + 10 minute cook time

Recipe serves 2 as a light meal or 6 as an appetizer

For all recipes delivered to your inbox weekly (with printable PDFs) + access to the searchable recipe archive, subscribe to my newsletter via the link in my bio 😘

Ingredients

Sprinkle mozzarella, parmesan, oregano, salt, and pepper over the top.

Bake 25–30 minutes until bubbly and golden.

caption:
Desi Eggs Recipe 👇🏼

Ingredients:
• 2 tbsp oil, butter, or ghee (I use @nanak_foods ghee)
• 1 large white/yellow onion, chopped
• 1–1.5 tomatoes, chopped
• Thai green chilies (as many or as few as you like)
• ½ tbsp red chili powder (adjust to taste)
• ½ tbsp cumin seeds
• 1 tsp turmeric
• Salt to taste
• 4 eggs
• Cilantro for garnish
• Optional: cheese on top

Instructions:

1. Place a skillet or cast iron pan on medium-high heat.
2. Add 2 tbsp oil/butter/ghee.
3. Once the oil is hot, add the chopped onions and sauté until they turn translucent and lightly golden.
4. Add in your tomatoes and Thai green chilies.
5. Lower the heat slightly and cook for a few minutes until the tomatoes start to break down and soften.
6. Add your spices: red chili powder, cumin seeds, turmeric, and salt.
7. Keep the heat low and sauté until the oil separates from the masala.
8. Crack in 4 eggs. Cook for 20–30 seconds max so they stay soft and silky (not rubbery!).
9. Optional: sprinkle on some cheese and let it melt.
10. Transfer to a plate, top with fresh cilantro, and enjoy with bread, sourdough, or paratha, plus a cup of chai or coffee. 🫶🏽✨

#DesiEggs #Recipe #PakistaniFood #IndianFood #Deevaandfood
summary: A desi-style scrambled eggs recipe with tomatoes, chilies, and spices cooked in ghee or oil.

caption:
This simple quiche studded with creamy, tangy goat cheese and a mix of sautéed spinach and leeks for a deliciously savory bite. To access the complete recipe online, visit our profile and click the "likeshop" link, then click on the image that corresponds to this video. To save the recipe with MyRecipes, simply tap the heart icon. 🩷

Ingredients
- 2 tablespoons extra-virgin olive oil
- 1 medium leek, trimmed and thinly sliced into half-moons (about 1 cup)
- 1 1/2 tablespoons thinly sliced garlic
- 1 (5-ounce) package baby spinach, coarsely chopped (about 8 cups)
- 6 large eggs
- 1/4 cup whole milk
- 1/4 cup half-and-half
- 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard
- 1/4 teaspoon salt
- 1/4 teaspoon ground pepper, plus more for garnish
- 1/3 cup coarsely crumbled goat cheese

Directions
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Coat a 9-inch pie pan with cooking spray.
2. Heat oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat; swirl to coat. Add leek; cook, stirring often, until softened and starting to caramelize, about 5 minutes. Add garlic; cook, stirring constantly, until softened and fragrant, about 1 minute. Add spinach; cook, stirring constantly, until wilted, 1 to 2 minutes. Transfer to the prepared pie pan.
3. Whisk eggs, milk, half-and-half, mustard, salt and pepper in a medium bowl until smooth. Pour over the spinach mixture. Sprinkle with cheese. Bake until set and golden brown, about 30 minutes. Let stand for 10 minutes before slicing. Garnish with additional pepper, if desired.
summary: A savory quiche with goat cheese, sautéed spinach, and leeks baked until golden.

caption:
GROUND CHICKEN FAJITA SKILLET✨A one pan meal thats packed with protein, veggies, fiber, and soooo much flavor. This type of recipe is the weeknight warrior of my kitchen. Its crowd-pleasing, easy for me to throw together, and tastes great the next day so its suitable for meal prep. 

Ingredients:
1 tablespoon avocado oil 
1 lb ground chicken 
1 small yellow onion, chopped
2 bell peppers (red, yellow, or orange), thinly sliced
3 garlic cloves, minced 
2 tbsp tomato paste 
1 tbsp fajita seasoning 
1 (15 oz) can black beans, drained and rinsed 
1 cup fresh or frozen corn 
1/2 cup white rice 
1 cup chicken bone broth 
Salt and black pepper, to taste*
juice of 1 lime 
Top with: pickled red onion, cilantro, sliced avocado, crushed tortilla chips 

Instructions:
In a large oven-safe skillet, heat oil over medium heat. Add the chicken and onion and cook 5-7 minutes, breaking it up as you go. 
Stir in garlic, bell pepper, and fajita seasoning. Cook for 6–8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until peppers are tender.
Add the tomato paste and stir to incorporate. Stir in black beans, corn, rice, and bone broth.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for 18-20 minutes. Remove the lid, add the lime juice, and stir. If the mixture is still quite wet, simmer for a few minutes uncovered. 
Remove from heart, taste and season with salt and pepper as needed. Garnish with pickled red onion, cilantro, sliced avocado, and crushed tortilla chips. Serve straight from the skillet. Enjoy!
summary: One-pan ground chicken fajita skillet with rice, beans, peppers, and corn.

caption:
56g protein, 13g net carbs, 480 calories a serving!

I've lost over 150lbs eating food thats easy, tastes so good, and keeps me FULL. Thats been the secret to staying on track without wanting to cheat. This is one of those easy meals that feels indulgent but still fits how I like to eat.

Comment "RECIPE" below and I'll send you the full printable recipe.

Ingredients:
3 chicken brsts
8 oz enchilada sauce
2 tbsp tomato paste
1/2 cup chicken broth
1 bell pepper, diced
1/2 large onion, diced
2–3 garlic cloves (or 1tsp garlic powder)
1/2 block cream cheese (4 oz)
1–1.5 cups shredded cheese (Mexican blend or cheddar)
1 8oz box @kaizenfoodco high protein low carb pasta or any pasta you like, cooked for half the time on packaging

Spices:
1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp oregano, salt & pepper
Optional: pinch cayenne or chipotle powder

Optional toppings:
Juice of 1/2 lime
2–3 tbsp sour cream, Greek yogurt
Cilantro, scallions, and/or or jalapeño
Hot sauce

Here is how I made it (makes 5 servings)

1️⃣ Add the raw chicken, enchilada sauce, tomato paste, chicken broth, diced bell pepper, onion, garlic, and all spices to a slow cooker, then mix gently to combine.

2️⃣ Cover and cook on low for 4–5 hours or high for about 3 hours, until the chicken is fully cooked and easy to shred.

3️⃣ Shred the chicken directly in the slow cooker using two forks until it's fully pulled apart.

4️⃣ Add the half block of cream cheese and stir until completely melted and incorporated
into the sauce.

5️⃣ Stir in the shredded cheese and let it melt.

6️⃣ Boil the Kaizen pasta ( I used Lumache) for 3 minutes  and gently fold everything together, adding a splash of broth if needed to loosen the sauce.

7️⃣ Plate it up, add your optional toppings, and enjoy! 

If you make it let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!
.
.
.
.
.
#lowcarbrecipes #lowcarb #healthyrecipes #slowcookerrecipes #ketorecipes
summary: Low-carb slow cooker chicken enchilada pasta with 56g protein and 13g net carbs per serving.

caption:
~160 cal per serving
A guilt-free version of the viral Korean Hwachae — fruity, fizzy, and the prettiest bowl of refreshment you'll make all summer. No ice cream, no condensed milk, no sugar overload. Just fresh fruit, soaked sabza, a splash of milk and soda, and a dessert you can dig into without a second thought.
Healthier Korean Fruit Punch Bowl
~160 cal | 3g protein | 18g carbs | 8g fat | 4g fibre
Ingredients (Makes 1 serving):
	•	1/2 cup watermelon, diced
	•	1/4 cup strawberries, sliced
	•	1/4 cup lychee or longan, peeled and halved
	•	2 tbsp pomegranate pearls
	•	(Or any fruits of your choice — pineapple, grapes, apple, coconut malai all work beautifully)
	•	1/2 cup coconut milk (light)
	•	1/2 cup almond or skim milk (unsweetened)
	•	1 tsp sabza/basil seeds, soaked (or chia seeds)
	•	1/3 cup soda/sparkling water 
	•	Stevia or any sweetener of your choice 
	•	Ice cubes
Method:
	1.	In a serving bowl, mix all the diced fruits together.
	2.	Stir in the soaked sabza seeds.
	3.	Pour in the coconut milk and almond milk.
	4.	Add a splash of soda, ice cubes, and stevia to taste.
	5.	Mix gently and serve chilled.

(hwachae recipe, korean hwachae, healthy hwachae, fruit punch bowl, korean fruit dessert, sabza recipe, basil seeds dessert, summer fruit bowl, low calorie korean dessert, viral korean dessert)
.
#hwachae #healthydessert #koreanrecipes​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
summary: A lighter version of Korean hwachae (fruit punch bowl) made with fresh fruit, soaked basil seeds, coconut and almond milk, and sparkling water at 160 calories per serving.

caption:
Mediterranean Cauliflower Parmesan Steaks

Ingredients
1 large head of cauliflower

3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil

1 cup Mediterranean-style marinara sauce

1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese

1/4 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese

1 tsp Italian seasoning

Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

Garnish: Fresh parsley, basil, and red pepper flakes

Instructions
Prep: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Slice cauliflower into 1-inch thick steaks, keeping the core intact.

Season: Place on a baking sheet. Brush with olive oil and sprinkle with Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.

Roast: Bake for 15 minutes, flip, and bake for another 15–20 minutes until tender.

Top: Spread marinara and cheeses over each steak.

Broil: Broil for 3–5 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbly. Garnish and serve.
summary: Mediterranean cauliflower parmesan steaks recipe with roasted cauliflower slices topped with marinara and melted cheese.

caption:
Brown butter banana bread pancakes✨

My debut cookbook is officially out now! Order with the link in my bio or search for Maxi's Kitchen wherever you like to buy books.

Pancakes:▪️3 Tbsp Butter▪️2 Cups Flour▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️2 tsp Baking Powder▪️1 tsp Baking Soda▪️1/2 tsp Ground Cinnamon▪️2 Ripe Bananas, mashed▪️2 Eggs▪️1+3/4 Cups Buttermilk▪️1 tsp Vanilla Extract▪️2 Tbsp Light Brown Sugar▪️1/2 Cup Chopped Walnuts (optional)▪️Olive Oil, for cooking▪️

Serving:▪️1 Banana, sliced in thin rounds▪️Butter, slightly softened▪️Warm Maple Syrup, for serving▪️

1️⃣ In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon. 
2️⃣ In a separate bowl, mash the bananas until mostly smooth. Whisk in the eggs, buttermilk, vanilla, and light brown sugar.
3️⃣ In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Continue cooking, swirling occasionally, until it smells nutty and the milk solids turn golden brown, 3-5 mins. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
4️⃣ Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until just combined. Do not over-mix. The batter should still be a little lumpy. Fold in the brown butter and walnuts.
5️⃣ Heat a thin layer of oil in a large nonstick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Scoop about 1/3 cup batter per pancake. Cook until bubbles form and pop on the surface and the underside is golden, about 2–3 min. Flip and cook another 1–2 min, until cooked through.
6️⃣ Serve warm with sliced bananas, butter, and maple syrup. Enjoy!

⏲15 min prep time / 25 min total time

Serves 4-6 (10-12 pancakes)

For all recipes delivered to your inbox weekly (with printable PDFs) + access to the searchable recipe archive, subscribe to my newsletter via the link in my bio 😘
summary: A detailed recipe for brown butter banana bread pancakes with step-by-step instructions and serving suggestions.

caption:
Garlic Herb Roasted Cauliflower and Mushrooms
Prep time: 15 minutes | Cook time: 25–30 minutes | Oven Temp: 425°F (220°C)

Ingredients
The Vegetables

1 medium head Cauliflower, cut into bite-sized florets

8–16 oz Mushrooms (cremini, button, or baby bella), halved or quartered

1 small Red or yellow onion, sliced

Aromatics & Fat

3–4 cloves Garlic, minced

3 tbsp Extra virgin olive oil (or a mix with 2 tbsp melted butter)

Seasoning & Herbs

1 tsp Kosher salt

½ tsp Ground black pepper

½ tsp Smoked paprika

1 tbsp Fresh thyme and rosemary, chopped

Garnish

Handful Fresh parsley, chopped

Optional: Squeeze of fresh lemon juice

Instructions
Prep the Oven: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a large rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.

Season the Veggies: In a large bowl, toss the cauliflower and mushrooms with the olive oil, garlic, onion, and all seasonings and herbs until evenly coated.

Arrange: Spread the vegetables in a single layer on the baking sheet. Use two pans if necessary to avoid overcrowding, which causes steaming instead of roasting.

Roast: Bake for 25–30 minutes. Stir the vegetables halfway through to ensure even browning.

Finish: Remove from the oven when the cauliflower is tender and the edges are golden brown.

Serve: Garnish with fresh parsley and an optional drizzle of lemon juice before serving warm.

Quick Customizations
Cheesy Version: Sprinkle ½ cup of grated Parmesan over the vegetables during the final 5 minutes of roasting.

Spicy Kick: Add ¼ tsp of red pepper flakes to the initial seasoning mix.

Air Fryer Method: Cook at 400°F for 12–15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.

#mediterraneanrecipes #mediterraneandiet #usafoods
summary: A garlic herb roasted cauliflower and mushroom recipe with Mediterranean seasonings and cooking method variations.

caption:
In loving memory of Aunty Sheila. The lady who taught me everything in the kitchen and beyond ❤️ 

Aunty Sheila's Famous Mac & Cheese Recipe

Ingredients:

-4 tbsp butter
-4 tbsp all purpose flour
-2 cups of 2% milk (500 ml)
-250 grams of "the best old white cheddar you can find", grated
-Black pepper, to taste
-18 ounces of elbow macaroni (500 grams)

Instructions:

1. Cook your macaroni according to the box. Once done, drain then rinse the macaroni under cold water to prevent clumping. This was aunty sheila's favorite trick.
2. Melt the butter and as soon as it is melted, add the flour. You "mustn't brown or burn the butter", aunty sheila says.
3. Then you add the flour, add it quickly and "beat it up quickly until there are no lumps".
4. Start adding your milk in small amounts at a time, giving it a "good whisk" each time. 
5. Add the grated cheese and mix until melted. Add in the cooked macaroni noodles. Season with black pepper if you wish.
6. That's it… then the only thing that can go wrong is you don't have enough 🤷‍♂️ 

Pro tip: Aunty Sheila always recommended serving her mac and cheese with a warm, fresh slice of buttered toast.
summary: Aunty Sheila's mac and cheese recipe with step-by-step instructions and serving tips.

caption:
The Best Cheesy Garlic Bread (ft: ZWILLING Knives)

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!

The only thing sharper than my ZWILLING knives? My craving for this cheesy garlic bread. One swift slice and BOOM—crispy edges, melty cheese, and a dangerously addictive aroma. Honestly, the real challenge isn't cutting it… it's not eating the whole loaf in one sitting.

Recipe inspo via the best @erinscozykitchen 🤠

Ingredients:

-1/2 cup salted butter (113 g), room temperature 
-2 garlic heads
-2 shallots 
-¼ cup Parmesan cheese, grated 
-2 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped 
-10 oz mozzarella cheese, grated (280 g)
-1 tbsp honey, optional
-1 small Focaccia or any Italian bread
-1 tsp paprika
-Olive oil 
-Salt and pepper, to taste 

Instructions: 

1. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. Slice the tops off the garlic bulbs and shallots. Drizzle them with olive oil, wrap in foil, and roast in the oven for about 1 hour, until softened and aromatic. 
3. Once roasted, let them cool slightly. Squeeze the softened garlic and shallots into a bowl, discarding the skins. Add the softened butter, grated Parmesan, paprika, salt, pepper and chopped parsley. Season with salt and pepper, then mix until well combined. 
4. Slice the bread in half lengthwise. Spread the garlic butter mixture evenly over both halves or you can do just one half with a thicker layer of butter & cheese! Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella evenly over the buttered bread halves. 
5. Place the bread on a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 15–20 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and golden brown. 
6. Once out of the oven, optionally sprinkle additional parsley on top and optionally drizzle with honey. Slice and serve warm.
summary: A recipe for roasted garlic and mozzarella cheese bread baked until golden and melty.

caption:
SIMPLE CILANTRO LIME CHICKEN SKEWERS 🍋‍🟩

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!"

Juicy, citrusy, and packed with bold flavours - these chicken skewers come together in no time! This recipe is ridiculously easy and ridiculously good 🔥

Ingredients:
-2 cups fresh cilantro, stems & leaves
-6 to 8 garlic cloves
-1 tbsp lime zest
-2 tbsp avocado oil @chosenfoods 
-5 tbsp mayonnaise 
-3 limes, juiced
-1.5 lbs (680g) chicken thighs, boneless & skinless
-2 tsp chili powder
-2 tsp black pepper
-1 tsp salt
-1 tsp cumin 
-1/2 tsp cayenne (optional)
-1 tbsp honey

For the Cilantro Butter:

-6 tbsp butter, melted
-2 tbsp reserved marinade 

Instructions:
1️⃣ Cut your chicken thighs into large bite-sized pieces. Place the cilantro, garlic, lime zest, mayonnaise, chili powder, pepper, salt, cumin, cayenne, avocado oil, and honey into a blender or food processor. 
2️⃣ Blend until a paste is formed and reserve two tablespoons for the butter. Mix the remaining marinade with the chicken and lime juice. Toss to coat.  Marinate for as long as your patience can handle - you can do one minute but you can also do overnight (the longer the better the flavor).
3️⃣ Preheat the air fryer to 375°F (190°C). Skewer the chicken and cook for 17-20 minutes until fully cooked through. For extra crispy edges, increase the temperature to 400°F (205°C) for the last 2-3 minutes.
4️⃣ While the chicken cooks, mix the melted butter with the reserved marinade. 
5️⃣ Let the chicken rest for 5-10 minutes then baste with the cilantro butter, more fresh cilantro, lime juice and a hot honey drizzle.
summary: A cilantro lime chicken skewer recipe with a homemade marinade and cilantro butter, cooked in an air fryer.

caption:
the BEST Creamy Chicken Noodle Soup 🤗

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!!

One pot. Endless flavor. Pure coziness. This is best chicken noodle soup you'll ever have…

Recipe inspo from the best: heresyourbite and @eatwithalyssa 

Ingredients:
-2 large chicken breasts 
-Chicken seasonings: 3 tsp paprika, 1 tsp cayenne, 2 tsp italian seasoning, 2 tsp onion powder, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper
-10 cloves fresh garlic, minced
-1 1/2 cups yellow onion, diced
-1 1/2 cups large carrots, diced
-1 1/2 cups celery, diced
-3 cups yukon gold potatoes, diced
-3 tbsp olive oil and 3 tbsp butter
-Veggie seasonings: 1 tbsp italian seasoning, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp chili flakes
-6 cups chicken broth + 1 tbsp chicken bouillon @betterthanbouillon 
-1 cup heavy cream, optional
-1 parmesan rind, optional
-1 bay leaf
-Fresh herbs: 2 tbsp fresh dill (chopped), 3 tbsp fresh parsley (chopped) & 1 tbsp fresh thyme
-1 lemon, juiced
-1lb (450g) egg noodles
-Salt & pepper, to taste

1. In a large pot, heat 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tbsp butter over medium-high heat. Once hot, sear the chicken breasts for 3 to 5 minutes until golden on each side (they will finish cooking in the soup). Remove & set aside.
2. Reduce the heat to medium, add 2 tbsp of olive oil & 2 tbsp of butter, then add the onion, carrot and celery along with the veggie seasonings listed above. Sauté for 7-9 minutes. Add the minced garlic, cook for one more minute.
3. Add the chicken broth along with more salt & pepper, the chicken bouillon, diced potatoes, parmesan rind, bay leaf, and return the seared chicken.
4. Bring to a boil then simmer for 18-20 minutes.
5. Cook your egg noodles in salted water according to the package. Drain and set aside.
6. Remove the chicken and shred it (you can use two forks or an electric mixer).
7. Remove the parmesan rind and bay leaf then add in the heavy cream, lemon juice, and fresh herbs. Add the shredded chicken back in and stir.
8. Adjust the seasonings as you wish. Serve over the egg noodles topped with fresh parmesan, olive oil, black pepper, chili flakes, fresh dill and lemon juice.

Pot: @lecreuset
summary: A creamy chicken noodle soup recipe with fresh herbs, vegetables, and optional heavy cream and parmesan.

caption:
Butter Chicken 🧈🐔

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!

Low effort, high comfort. A one-pan butter chicken that delivers big flavor and an easy cleanup with my stainless steel @madein pan

If you want cookware that will actually last, check out the full @madein Stainless Collection in my bio.

For the Marinade.
-2 lb boneless skinless chicken thighs, cubed
-Seasonings: 1 tbsp red chili powder (Kashmiri if you have it), 1 tbsp garam masala, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp cayenne (optional)
-1/2 cup Greek yogurt
-Juice of 1/2 lemon
-2 tsp fresh ginger paste
-5 garlic cloves, minced
For the Sauce:
-6 tbsp butter, divided
-2 tbsp olive oil, divided
-1 large or 2 small yellow onions, finely diced
-5 garlic cloves, minced
-2 tsp fresh ginger paste
-Seasonings: 2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp garam masala, 2 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri if you have), 1 tsp ground coriander, 1 tsp salt, 1 tbsp white sugar
-1 1/4 cups passata (tomato sauce)
-1 cup heavy cream, room temp (add more if you like it extra creamy)
-25 cashews (optional, but very good)
-1/4 cup hot water, optional for thinning
To Finish: 
-Lime wedges & fresh cilantro
-Basmati rice
-Warm naan (full recipe in my bio)

1. Add the chicken to a bowl with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes, or ideally overnight.
2. Heat 2 tbsp butter + 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Cook the chicken until golden. Remove and set aside.
3. In the same pan, add 2 tbsp butter + 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Add the onions & cook until soft, about 5 mins. Add the garlic, ginger, and sauce spices. Stir & cook for 1–2 mins.
4. Add the passata & cashews if using. Gently simmer for 5 mins. Stir in the cream. 
5. Using a handheld blender or transferring to a blender, carefully blend the sauce. Optionally add hot water to adjust consistency.
6. Add the blended sauce back to the pan along with the reserved chicken. Keep the heat low & simmer for 10–15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through. Add the remaining 2 tbsp of butter.
7. Finish with a squeeze of lime and lots of cilantro.

#madeinpartner #ad
summary: A one-pan butter chicken recipe with yogurt-marinated chicken thighs and a creamy tomato-based sauce.

caption:
Sheet Pan Beef Kefta Wraps 🔥

All the flavor, none of the fuss… juicy spiced beef, roasted garlic sauce, & a zesty salad 

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!!

For ALL of you asking for the app: search "osta recipes" on the app or google play store 🫶

For the Beef Kefta:
-1.5-2lbs lbs lean ground beef (700-900 g)
-8 garlic cloves, minced
-2 tsp smoked paprika, 2 tsp cumin, 1 tsp allspice, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp cinnamon 
-1/3 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1/3 cup fresh cilantro, chopped (optional)
-1 large or 2 medium yellow onions, grated + squeezed dry*
-On top before baking: 4 tbsp olive oil
For the Roasted Garlic:
-2 garlic heads, 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp salt
For the Fresh Salad:
-1/2 large red onion, thinly sliced
-1 cup cherry tomatoes, quartered
-1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1 tsp salt, 1 tsp pepper & 2 tsp sumac
-Juice of 1/2 lemon
Roasted Garlic Paprika Sauce:
-Roasted garlic cloves, 1/2 cup Greek yogurt, juice of 1/2 lemon, 3 tbsp tahini, 2 heads of roasted garlic, 2 tbsp ice water, 1 tsp each: paprika, cumin, salt, pepper. Optionally add fresh chopped parlsey/cilantro.

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Slice the tops off two garlic heads, drizzle with olive oil and salt, wrap in foil, & roast for 45–60 mins until golden and soft. While the garlic roasts, prep the kefta.
2. Grate the onion, squeeze out all liquid with paper towel or cheesecloth (prevents watery kefta.) In a large bowl, combine beef, garlic, spices, parsley, cilantro (if using), and the grated onion. Mix with your hands for 2–3 minutes until sticky & well combined.
3. Spread onto the sheet pan in a 1/2-inch (1.25 cm) thick rectangle. With a knife, score into 1-inch strips (2.5 cm). Drizzle evenly with 4 tbsp olive oil. Bake for 12–15 minutes at 400°F, then broil 2–5 minutes until golden & crisp on top. Careful removing!
4. Toss onion, tomatoes, parsley, salt, pepper, sumac, & lemon juice in a bowl.
5. Squeeze roasted garlic cloves into a bowl, mash, mix with yogurt, lemon juice, tahini, water & spices. On lavash/pita: add sauce, kefta, & salad. Roll tight. Crisp the wrap in a lightly oiled skillet.
summary: Sheet pan beef kefta wraps with roasted garlic sauce and fresh salad.

caption:
Korean Popcorn Chicken 🍿🍗

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!!

For all of you asking about the app, search "Osta" on the app or Google play store ‼️🫶

The crispiest korean porocrn chicken… sweet, spicy and so simple. You can also make this in the air fryer!!🔥

For the Chicken:
-2 lb chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1 inch cubes
-2 eggs
-2 tbsp rice vinegar
-6 garlic cloves, minced
-1 tbsp fresh ginger, grated
-Seasonings: 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp onion powder, 2 tsp salt, 1 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp gochugaru (optional)
-1/4 cup cornstarch
-1/2 cup all-purpose flour
-Neutral oil, for frying

For the Sauce:
-1.5 tbsp gochujang
-2 tsp sesame seeds
-3 tbsp ketchup
-4 garlic cloves, minced
-1.5 tbsp honey
-3 tbsp butter
-1 tbsp rice vinegar
-1 tbsp soy sauce
-2 tsp sesame oil
-1 tsp gochugaru (optional)
Garnish:
-Sesame seeds
-Green onions, sliced

Instructions:
1. In a bowl, combine chicken with eggs, vinegar, garlic, ginger, & seasonings. Mix well & marinate as long as you wish (1 hour ideal, 1 min works).

→For frying: add the cornstarch & flour directly into the same bowl and mix until evenly coated & sticky.
→For air frying: do NOT add flour yet. You'll coat it separately in step 2.

2. For frying: skip this step & go to step 3. For air frying: in a new bowl, mix cornstarch & flour. Remove chicken from marinade then toss in dry ingredients until fully coated. Press lightly so it sticks.
3. For frying: heat neutral oil to 350°F / 175°C. Fry in batches for 7–8 mins until golden and crispy, transfer to a rack.
4. For air frying: oil the basket & spray/brush the chicken with oil. Cook at 375°F for 8 mins, flip, cook for 6–8 mins more until crispy & cooked through. Work in batches.
5. In a pan over medium-low heat, combine gochujang, ketchup, sesame seeds, garlic, honey, rice vinegar, soy sauce, sesame oil, & optional gochugaru. Bring to a simmer, add butter, stir until melted, remove from heat.
6. Add the chicken directly to the sauce & toss OR in a mixing bowl, combine your desired amount of sauce with the chicken to control how saucy it gets.
7. Serve over jasmine rice, garnish with sesame seeds & green onions. Enjoy :)
summary: Korean popcorn chicken recipe with sweet and spicy gochujang sauce, cookable via frying or air fryer.

caption:
💾 Save this recipe for later HERE ⤵️

———
🥘 High protein roasted veg + chicken kale salad 

Recipe (4 servings):

🍠 500g Sweet Potato
🥬 300g Beetroot
🫘 200g Chickpeas
🐔 300g Rotisserie Chicken Breast
🥬 150g Kale
🧀 80g Feta Cheese
🫒 15g Olive Oil

🌶️ 1 Tbsp Paprika, 1 Tbsp Cumin
🧂 Pinch of salt and pepper
———
Dressing:

🫒 1 tsp Olive Oil
🍯 1 tsp honey
🍎 60ml Apple Cider Vinegar
🥄 1 tsp Mustard
🍇 1 tsp Balsamic Vinegar
🧄 1 tsp Grated Garlic
🍋 1 Lemon (squeezed)
🧂 Pinch of salt

1. Add sweet potato, beetroot, and chickpeas onto a tray bake and add paprika, cumin, salt, pepper, and a spray of olive oil
2. Add into a preheated oven at 180°C for approximately 35 minutes, until soft and crispy
3. Massage the kale with the olive oil, a pinch of salt, and a squeeze of lemon
4. Add the roasted tray into the kale and mix together
5. Add feta cheese and chicken breast, then mix everything together
6. Make your dressing, shake well, pour over, and enjoy

Enjoy!

➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖➖

Each serving (1 serving):

Calories: 450 cals
Protein: 35g
Carbs: 49g
Fat: 14g
Fibre: 11g

caption:
ROASTED RED CABBAGE AND QUINOA SALAD!💜
Roasted cabbage and red onion salad with fluffy quinoa - the combo you needed! Perfect for lunch, hosting, or an easy healthy dinner. You can also turn it into a full meal by adding feta, grilled fish, or grilled chicken, and it tastes even better the next day.
Follow @ruhamasfood for more
You can shop my WOW apron 👩‍🍳 at my website: RuhamasFood.com.
.
סלט כרוב צלוי וקינואה!
מתכון בעברית בתגובות 🥗
.
Recipe:
Ingredients: 
For roasting:
1 red cabbage, cut in half and thinly sliced
1 red onion, cut in half and thinly sliced
1/2 teaspoon flaky maldon salt
4 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil

For the salad:
Handful parsley, finely chopped
Handful mint leaves, finely chopped
Handful chives, finely chopped
1 cup cooked quinoa
5 pitted dates, sliced
Handful roasted nuts, roughly chopped
1 teaspoon salt
1/2 teaspoon ground black pepper
1 teaspoon sumac
5 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil
Juice of 1 large lemon
1 teaspoon Dijon mustard
1 teaspoon date syrup or honey
2 garlic cloves, minced

Optional (you can choose from): 
Feta cheese, grilled salmon, or grilled chicken

Method: 
1. Preheat the oven to convection broil at 450F.
2. Place the sliced cabbage and onion on a baking pan. Season with flaky salt and olive oil.
3. Mix well and massage with your hands until slightly softened.
4. Spread evenly and roast for about 20 minutes, mixing a few times during roasting so the vegetables brown evenly.
5. In a large bowl, combine the chopped herbs, quinoa, dates, and nuts.
6. Add the roasted cabbage and onions.
7. Season with salt, black pepper, sumac, olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, Dijon mustard and date syrup.
8. Mix everything well and serve. 
Enjoy! Ruhama🤍
.
#redcabbage #roasted #salad #vegan #ruhamasfood
summary: Roasted red cabbage and quinoa salad with herbs, dates, and nuts, optionally topped with feta or grilled protein.
