caption:
✅ HEALTHY LUNCH IDEA, PART 19

I've lost 150+lbs and this is one of my favorite healthy meal prep options. It's keeps me insanely full with the slow digesting carbs, protein, health fats, and fiber. Plus the healthier homemade sugar free hot honey sauce is SO good.

If you are watching carbs, feel free to sub spaghetti squash, eggplant, or even zucchini instead of butternut squash.

Here is how I made it (makes 4 servings)

1.Finely dice 1 medium butternut squash. You'll want this finely diced so it cooks faster. Drizzle with 1 tsp olive oil, season with salt, pepper, 1 tsp oregano, 1 tsp smoked paprika, and 1 tsp Chile lime seasoning (or tajin). Add to a tray, pop in the oven for 27-30 mins at 400F.

2.Do the same thing with another lower sugar, high fiber veggie. I used 2 cups of broccoli florets . Same thing - drizzle with olive oil, season with salt, pepper, and the same spices. Pop in the oven until nice and crispy.

3.Cook 1 lb lean ground beef (I used 93/7)) on medium high heat in 1 tsp olive oil. Season your beef with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp onion powder, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano, 1.5 tbsp smoked paprika, and 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper. Use a spoon or a spatula to mince it down to small pieces. I like to get meat browned really well, almost crispy, so this should take about 15-17 mins.

4.When your butternut squash and broccoli are roasted, divide it up in to 4 different serving bowls or meal prep containers. Add 4oz of the seasoned beef, a quarter of an avocado cubed, 3tbsp thick greek yogurt, and any sauce you like. I like using a low sugar hot honey sauce here (step 5 below) but you can use a bottled hot honey, or any sauce you like.

5.For the sugar free hot honey sauce, in a small pan over low heat, stir together 5 tbsp sugar-free (or regular) honey, 2 tbsp sriracha, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and 1/2 to 1 tsp chili flakes for about a minute. I used about 2 tbsp of sauce for each.

This is a great healthy lunch recipe, and perfect for a healthy meal prep. Per serving 510 cals, 30g prot, 18 net carbs.

If you try it, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!
.
.
.
.
.
#highprotein #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #lowcarbrecipes #healthyfoodshare

caption:
215 cal | 18g protein per serving
The easiest dumplings you'll ever make — and the filling is built with watermelon rind which actually gives you that juicy water chestnut crunch you get in restaurant dim sum. Mixed in with grated low-fat paneer and finished with a chilli crisp soy drizzle.

WATERMELON RIND & PANEER DUMPLINGS
215 cal | 18g protein | 24g carbs | 5g fat per serving (One serving = 6 dumplings)
Ingredients (makes 24 dumplings)
For the filling:
	•	300g watermelon rind
	•	200g low-fat paneer,  grated
	•	2 tsp ginger garlic paste
	•	2 tbsp coriander leaves, finely chopped
	•	4 spring onions, whites and greens separated
	•	1 tbsp chilli crisp or chilli garlic crisp (or 1 tsp chilli flakes)
	•	1 tbsp light soy sauce
	•	½ tsp salt
	•	½ tsp white pepper
For the assembly:
	•	24 dumpling wrappers OR 12 rice paper sheets cut into quarters
	•	1.5 tsp neutral oil
	•	 Hot water for steaming

For the chilli crisp soy glaze:
	•	2 tbsp light soy sauce
	•	1 chilli crisp
	•	3 tbsp hot water
	•	spring onion greens
Method
	1.	Peel the green skin off the rind and trim away any pink flesh. Finely chop/grate the white part, toss with 1 tsp salt, and rest for 10 minutes. Squeeze bone-dry in a muslin cloth.
	2.	Combine the squeezed rind with grated paneer, ginger garlic paste, coriander, spring onion whites, chilli crisp, soy sauce, salt, and pepper. Mix gently till evenly combined.
	3.	Heat oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat. Drop tablespoon mounds of filling. Aim for 6-8 mounds per batch.
	4.	If using dumpling wrappers, place one over each mound. If using rice paper, dip 2 quarters in warm water for 5 seconds and drape over each mound.
	5.	Pour hot water around the dumplings, cover with a tight lid, and steam for 5 minutes till wrappers turn glossy and translucent.
	6.	Uncover, let any remaining water evaporate, and crisp the bottoms for another 2-3 minutes.
	7.	Whisk soy sauce with chilli crisp and dilute with hot water. Drizzle over the dumplings, scatter with spring onion, and enjoy.

.
(watermelon rind dumplings, no fold dumplings, high protein vegetarian dumplings, low calorie momos).

#healthymomos
summary: High-protein steamed dumplings made with watermelon rind and paneer filling, 215 calories and 18g protein per serving.

caption:
Mexican Crunch Wrap 🌯✨
If you love layers, textures, and bold flavors—this crunch wrap is that girl. Crispy on the outside, creamy and cheesy inside, with a satisfying crunch in every bite. It's quick, customizable, and honestly feels like a homemade upgrade of your favorite fast-food wrap.

Ingredients
* 1 large tortilla
* 1 small tortilla
* 1 tostada (crispy corn shell)
* ½ cup refried beans
* ½ cup Mexican shredded cheese blend
* 2–3 tbsp queso
* 2–3 tbsp sour cream
* ¼ cup chopped bell peppers
* ¼ cup chopped tomatoes
* ¼ cup shredded lettuce
* Butter or oil for cooking

Instructions
1. Assemble the Base
* Lay the large tortilla flat.
* Spread refried beans evenly in the center.
* Sprinkle Mexican cheese on top.
* Drizzle queso over it.
2. Add the Crunch Layer
* Place the tostada on top of the cheesy layer.
3. Add Fresh Toppings
* Spread sour cream over the tostada.
* Add bell peppers, tomatoes, and lettuce.
4. Seal the Wrap
* Place the small tortilla on top of the fillings.
* Fold the edges of the large tortilla inward, creating a sealed wrap (pleated style).
5. Cook
* Heat a pan or griddle with a little butter/oil.
* Place the wrap seam-side down first.
* Cook on medium heat for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.

Serve
Slice in half and enjoy that perfect cheesy + crunchy combo. Pair with salsa, guac, or extra queso if you're feeling extra 😌

#CrunchWrapSupreme #viral #EasyMeals #mexicanfood #explore
summary: A homemade Mexican crunch wrap recipe featuring layered tortillas, beans, cheese, tostada, and fresh toppings, pan-fried until crispy.

caption:
Is this really the 4th best spread or dip in the world?

Guacamole was recently ranked as the 4th best sauce in the world, in my book, it's a very close battle with hummus for that number one spot. Let me know which you would pick!

I like to make my guac a mix of creamy but chunky, and also left out tomatos, which are typically in guac, so feel free to include them, and also make any substitutions you like to make your own version. This is usually the way I do it for an easy appetizer that i know people will like, especially in the summer.

Another preference is temperature! I like my guacamole on the colder side, so make it with avocados straight out of the fridge and serve right away for best results.

Ingredients:

✅3 large avocados
✅1 large handful fresh cilantro (about 1/3 cup finely chopped)
✅1/2 large red onion, finely diced (finely diced is key!)
✅1 jalapeno, finely diced (remove seeds if you dont want it spicy)
✅ 2 garlic cloves, minced (use fresh but jarred works, too)
✅1 lime
✅1/2 tsp cumin
✅2-3generous pinches salt

Here is how I made it:

1.I prefer cold avocados so store them in the fridge for at least 2 hours prior to starting.

2.Grab a board or a bowl - i did this right on the board. Finely chop 1 large handful fresh cilantro which yields about 1/3 cup.

3.To the cilantro, add 1/2 finely diced large red onion 1 finely diced jalapeño (remove the seeds if you dont like it spicy!), and 2 freshly minced garlic cloves.

4.Grab your 3 large avocados, remove the skin and pit and add to the rest.

5.Squeeze the juice of 1 small lime. If you want more lime-y you can add more lime after you taste it later.

6.Add 1/2 tsp cumin, a couple generous pinches of salt, then mash using a fork or utensil. I like my guac on the chunkier side so mash lightly, but mash longer or shorter depending on your preference.

7.Enjoy immediately, with your favorite vessel

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

caption:
Follow me @kalejunkie for recipes like my JUICY SHEET PAN MIDDLE EASTERN BEEF KEBABS, seasoned just like my dad used to make when I was growing up! These kebabs are so juicy and flavorful, you will be obsessed! You can serve however you like—in lavash, pita, over rice, etc.

Makes 6-8 servings
2 lbs ground beef (I like 85/15 for juicer kebabs, but leaner works too!)
1/2 white onion
1 red bell pepper
4 cloves garlic
1/4 cup parsley, chopped
2 tb tomato paste
2 tsp cumin
2 tsp paprika
2 tsp aleppo pepper (optional but recommend)
1 tsp ground black pepper 
1 1/2 tsp kosher salt

Salad
2 persian cucumbers, chopped
1/2 red onion, sliced
2 roma tomatoes, chopped
2 tb sumac
2 tb red wine vinegar
2 tb avocado oil
1 tsp kosher salt

Yogurt sauce
1 cup greek yogurt
1 Persian cucumber, grated, liquid pressed out
1/2 lemon
1/2 tsp kosher salt

Lavash

Preheat oven to 375F. First, make the kebabs. In a small food processor, blend the onion, bell pepper and garlic. It will be liquidy. Transfer to a bowl and use a paper towel to remove as much moisture as possible. Next, add the beef to a large bowl along with the onion/bell pepper/garlic mixture, parsley, and seasonings. Mix very well until incorporated—I like to use my hands. Spread the beef mixture evenly in a flat layer on a greased quarter sheet pan. Make cuts horizontally and vertically just like kebabs you see at a restaurant! Bake for 20 minutes, then broil for an additional 4-5 minutes until golden on top. Remove from oven, cut beef along the horizontal lines you previously cut, to separate the kebabs. To make the salad, chop the veggies and toss with the vinegar, avocado oil, sumac and salt. Make the yogurt sauce and serve as desired. On a lavash, spread yogurt mixture, add th
summary: Sheet pan Middle Eastern beef kebab recipe with yogurt sauce, salad, and instructions for 6-8 servings.

caption:
Chicken Fajitas with Cheesy Charred Tortillas 

1. 2 chicken breast cut into strips 
2. Small Red, yellow, orange , green bell peppers cut into strips . 1/2 onion cut into strips 
3. Fajita tortillas 
4. 2 cups shredded cheese 
5. Garnishes - guacamole, jalapeño slices, sour cream , hot sauce , chipotle mayo , cilantro 

Chicken and peppers seasoning: 
1. Juice of one lime 
2. 2 tablespoons cumin 
3. 1 teaspoon onion powder, paprika, oregano, salt 
4. 1/2 teaspoons cayenne 
5. 2 tablespoons water 
6. 2 tablespoons olive oil 

1. Cut peppers, onion into slices
2. Cut chicken breast lengthwise, then into strips, not too thick, not too thin. 
3. Mix seasoning in a bowl
4. Season chicken with 2 to 3 tablespoons of the seasoning reserve some seasoning for the peppers
5. In a skillet add 2 tablespoon of olive oil and 1 tablespoon butter and cook and seer the chicken 3 to 4 minutes without over cooking the chicken. Remove to a bowl.
6. In the same pan, sauté peppers and onion . Add 1-2 tablespoons of the reserved seasoning. Cook about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove 
7. Char the tortilla. Melt cheese in a pan top with the tortilla while turning so that the cheese sticks to the tortilla.
8. Once the cheese is melted, flip the tortilla over.
9. Assemble your fajita with chicken peppers, guacamole, sour cream, jalapeños, and cilantro. I added chipotle, mayo, and hot sauce.

caption:
"Mediterranean Lentil Salad"

Ingredients:

- 1 cup dry brown/green lentils, rinsed
- 1 cup cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1 medium cucumber, sliced
- ½ small red onion, thinly sliced
- ¼ cup Kalamata olives, pitted
- ¼ cup green olives, pitted
- 60 g feta cheese, crumbled
- ½ cup fresh parsley, chopped
- 3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil
- 1½ tbsp red wine vinegar or lemon juice
- 1 tsp dried oregano
- Salt & black pepper to taste

Method:

1. Simmer lentils 18-20 min until tender. Drain, rinse cold, cool.
2. Whisk olive oil, vinegar/lemon, oregano, salt, and pepper for dressing.
3. Combine lentils, tomatoes, cucumber, onion, olives, and parsley in a bowl.
4. Toss with dressing, then fold in feta.
5. Chill 15 min and serve cold.
summary: A Mediterranean lentil salad with tomatoes, cucumber, olives, feta, and herb dressing.

caption:
260 calories and 28g of protein per serving — done in 20 minutes, one pan, zero fuss. Plump prawns, brown onions, fiery chilli garlic paste, all tossed through cooked rice to get a desi spicy fried rice feel. 🔥❤️

Chilli Garlic Prawn Rice
📊 ~260 kcal | ~28g Protein | ~20g Carbs | ~9.5g Fat per serving (with shirataki rice)
📊 ~467 kcal | ~31g Protein | ~65g Carbs | ~9.5g Fat per serving (with regular cooked rice)
Makes 2 Servings of 350 g each

Ingredients
FOR THE PASTE
•	10–12 garlic cloves
•	1 tbsp Kashmiri chilli powder
•	2 tsp coriander powder
•	Salt to taste
•	Splash of water
FOR THE STIR FRY
•	1 tbsp oil
•	1 tsp mustard seeds
•	2 sprigs curry leaves
•	1 medium onion, sliced
•	250g prawns, diced
•	350g cooked shirataki rice (or 350g cooked white/basmati rice — approx. 130g raw)
•	Salt to taste
•	Fresh coriander leaves, chopped (to finish)
•	1.5 tbsp lemon juice (to finish)
QUICK TIP — HOW TO COOK DRY SHIRATAKI RICE (I've used Urban Platter)
Mix 100g dry shirataki rice with equal parts water (100ml) and microwave on high for 2 minutes.

METHOD
•	Blitz or pound the garlic, Kashmiri powder, coriander powder, salt, and a splash of water into a smooth paste.
•	Heat oil in a pan, splutter the mustard seeds, then add curry leaves and sliced onion. Sauté till the onion softens and turns translucent.
•	Add the spice paste and cook on medium for 2–3 minutes till the raw smell disappears and the oil separates.
•	Add the diced prawns, mix well, and cook for about a minute till they just turn opaque — don't overdo it or they'll go rubbery.
•	Add the cooked shirataki rice (or regular cooked rice), toss everything together, and let it warm through for another minute so the masala coats every grain.
•	Check salt, finish with chopped coriander leaves and a generous squeeze of lemon. Divide between 2 plates and serve hot.

.

summary: A 20-minute one-pan chilli garlic prawn fried rice with 28g protein per serving, available in low-carb and regular rice versions.

caption:
Mom's chicken salad✨

This chicken salad is creamy, tangy, and fresh, and pairs so well with @crunchmastercrackers for the most satisfying crunch in every bite. They're gluten-free and made with simple ingredients like olive oil and seeds. Find Crunchmaster at your nearest store and try this combo for yourself! #ad #SatisfyYourCrunch #Crunchmaster #HealthySnacking 

▪️2 Boneless Skinless Chicken Breasts (1 lb.)▪️Kosher Salt & Pepper▪️2 Small Stalks Celery, diced small▪️1/2 of a Small Sweet Yellow Onion, diced small▪️1/2 of a Honey Crisp or Pink Lady Apple, diced small▪️1/4 Cup Finely Chopped Parsley▪️1 Lemon, juice (or more to taste)▪️1/4 Cup Plain Greek Yogurt▪️1/4 Cup Mayo▪️1 Tbsp Dijon Mustard▪️Crunchmaster Multi-Seed Crackers, for serving▪️Microgreens, for serving (optional)▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F. Place the chicken on a baking sheet, season generously with salt and pepper, and roast for 20–25 mins until internal temp reaches 165F. Let cool slightly, then dice small.
2️⃣ In a large bowl, mix together the chicken, celery, onion, apple, parsley, lemon juice, 3/4 tsp kosher salt, 1/2 tsp freshly ground black pepper, Greek yogurt, mayo, and dijon until well combined. Taste and add more salt, pepper, or lemon juice if desired. 
3️⃣ Top with microgreens, if using, and serve with Crunchmaster crackers. Enjoy!

⏲15 min prep time / 30 min total time

Serves 3-4
summary: A creamy chicken salad recipe with roasted chicken, fresh vegetables, and Greek yogurt served with gluten-free crackers.

caption:
What I make when I don't really feel like cooking (but still want to eat well)! Sheet Pan Roasted Cauliflower, Chickpeas, and Dates—my latest recipe for @nytcooking. 

Comment "RECIPE" and I'll DM you a gift link so you can grab the recipe for free 🥳 

Sheet Pan Dinner Formula ⬇️

1️⃣ Pick a protein and a vegetable you can roast together at the same temp, ideally for the same amount of time for ease. 

Here, cauliflower and chickpeas are roasted at 425ºF / 220ºC for ~30 minutes with great results.

2️⃣ If you want to use 2 vegetables, make sure they have similar textures & water content. Cauliflower + broccoli ✅ Cauliflower + zucchini 🚫

3️⃣ Alliums make a great complementary vegetable: smashed garlic cloves as in this recipe, or onion wedges, sliced leeks, etc. 

4️⃣ If cooking for 3-4 people, use two sheet pans: one for the protein, one for the vegetable. That way, each piece of food comes into contact with the pan. If you crowd the pan = steamed & less flavorful. 

If cooking for 1-2, you can halve the recipe and use one pan: put the veg on one half of the pan, protein on the other half. 

If roasting 2 pans, consider swapping the rack locations halfway through for even cooking/browning. 

5️⃣ While everything roasts, make your sauce. This is the flavor powerhouse to bring the meal together. For easy cleanup, pick a sauce that can be made in a bowl / doesn't require a food processor, blender, etc. 

6️⃣ Don't forget to layer something fun into the recipe! This can be a flavor booster (e.g., preserved lemons), a crunchy element (e.g., toasted panko), a spicy flair (fresh chiles or chili crisp), or anything that gives it pizzazz (e.g., the charred dates in this recipe). 

⭐️ Save this post so you have the formula handy for dinnertime! 

Cover photo: Ryan Liebe for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Barrett Washburne

#easyrecipes #veganrecipes #vegancooking #weeknightdinner #healthyrecipes
summary: Sheet pan roasted cauliflower, chickpeas, and dates recipe with a reusable dinner formula for beginner-friendly vegan cooking.

caption:
Spiced Chicken Koftas 🌶️🌱

Serves: 4
Ingredients
500g chicken mince
1 small red onion, very finely chopped or grated
3 garlic cloves, crushed
1 tbsp ginger, finely grated
1 green chilli, finely chopped (optional)
¼ cup fresh coriander, finely chopped
¼ cup fresh mint, finely chopped

1 tsp cumin powder
2 tsp coriander powder
1 tsp garam masala
1 tsp Kashmiri chilli powder (for colour + mild heat)
Salt to taste
2 tbsp besan (gram flour) or breadcrumbs
1 egg (slightly beaten)
1 tbsp lemon juice
2 tbsp oil (for cooking)
1 tbsp soy sauce 
Method
In a large bowl, combine the chicken mince with onion, garlic, ginger, chilli, herbs, spices, besan/breadcrumbs, egg, and lemon juice. Mix really well your hands are best here to help everything bind together.
Cover and rest the mixture in the fridge for at least 20–30 minutes. This helps the flavours deepen and makes shaping easier.
With slightly damp hands, shape the mixture into small round koftas, similar to meatballs.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Cook the koftas in batches, turning gently, until golden brown on all sides and cooked through (about 8–10 minutes).
You can also Air-fry these at 180C for 10-12 mins. Turning them over halfway.
Rest for a couple of minutes before serving this keeps them juicy.

PS: if you skip chilli, this is superrrr kid friendly, my little ones love it ❤️✌🏼

#chicken #indianfood #delicious #foodie #instafood
summary: Recipe for spiced chicken koftas with cumin, coriander, and garam masala, served with cooking and air-frying instructions.

caption:
Here is the detailed recipe for 🥦 Creamy Broccoli Soup (with Cashews):
Serve 3–4
Ingredients
• Broccoli florets – 2 cups
• Broccoli stem – ½ cup, peeled and chopped
• Onion – 1 medium, chopped
• Garlic – 3 cloves
• Cumin seeds – ¼ tsp
• Cashews – ¼ cup
• Olive oil – 1 Tbsp (or butter)
• Pepper powder – to taste
• Salt – to taste
• Water – about 2 to 2½ cups
Method
Wash the broccoli florets thoroughly and soak them in lightly salted water for 10–15 minutes to remove any impurities. Rinse well and drain.

Heat olive oil or butter in a pan. Add cumin seeds and allow them to splutter.

Add garlic and chopped onion. Sauté until the onion turns soft and slightly translucent.

Add the broccoli florets, chopped broccoli stems, and cashews. Sauté for 2 minutes to enhance the flavor.

Add salt and 2 to 2½ cups water. Cover and cook for 8–10 minutes until the broccoli becomes tender.

Allow the mixture to cool slightly, then blend until smooth and creamy.

Pour the blended soup back into the pan. Adjust the consistency with water if required and check the salt.

Add freshly crushed pepper and simmer for 1–2 minutes.

Serve hot and comforting.

✨ Tips
• Cashews give a natural creaminess without adding cream.
• A small squeeze of lemon at the end enhances the flavor.
• You can garnish with roasted broccoli bits or toasted seeds.
#InstaFoodie #AuthenticFlavors #HomeChefCreations #vegetarian #mangalorecooking
summary: Detailed recipe for creamy broccoli soup made with cashews, cumin, and garlic.

caption:
335 Kcal & 36 g Protein per Serving 💪🏼

The easiest, cheesiest spicy chicken dish you'll make this week. Bhutan's national dish with a protein upgrade — tender thigh cubes simmered with onions, green chillies, and melted cheese until the whole pan goes glossy and gorgeous. Pairs beautifully with steamed rice, hot rotis, soft buns or crusty bread.
Inspired by @withanushkarawat 's yummm and easy Egg Ema Datshi ❤️

Chicken Ema Datshi
Makes 3 serving of ~185 g each
Per serving: ~ 335 kcal | 36g protein | 6g carbs | 18g fat​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

Ingredients 
For the chicken:
	•	1 tsp mustard oil (any oil works)
	•	500g boneless chicken thighs, cubed
	•	Salt, to taste
	•	¼ tsp turmeric
	•	½ tsp black pepper powder
For the gravy:
	•	1 medium onion, thinly sliced (~100g)
	•	3–4 green chillies, sliced
	•	1 tsp chilli flakes (adjust to heat tolerance)
	•	1 chicken stock cube (optional- I've used Knorr Zero Salt, available on Amazon)
	•	½ cup water
	•	3 cheese slices (used a mix of melty mozzarella + cheddar)
	•	1 tsp cornstarch + 2 tsp water, slurry (optional, for a thicker gravy)
	•	Coriander leaves / spring onion greens, to finish (optional)
Method
	1.	Heat mustard oil in a pan until it just starts to smoke, then bring the flame down. Add the chicken cubes, salt, turmeric, and black pepper. Cook on high for 2–3 minutes until the edges colour.
	2.	Add the sliced onion, green chillies, and chilli flakes. Toss for a minute.
	3.	Crumble in the stock cube (if using), pour in the water, and bring to a gentle simmer.
	4.	Once it's simmering, drop in the cheese slices. Stir continuously until the cheese melts into a glossy sauce that clings to every cube.
	5.	For a thicker gravy, stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook for another 30 seconds.
	6.	Finish with chopped coriander and spring onion greens. Serve hot with rice, roti, or bread.

.

caption:
Episode 3 of 7 Days, 7 Sandwiches 🌶️🧀

Chilli cheese toast isn't just a snack, it's a whole mood. This is my non-negotiable comfort bite for every craving that needs a little kick.

Crispy bread, spicy heat, cheesy pull… simple, satisfying, and always hitting the spot ❤️

Ingredients 
🌶️4 bread slices 
🌶️4 tbsp butter, softened 
🌶️2 cups cheddar and pizza cheese mix, grated 
🌶️4 tbsp capsicum, finely chopped 
🌶️2 tbsp onion, finely chopped 
🌶️2–3 green chillies, finely chopped 
🌶️4 tbsp fresh cream 
🌶️1 tsp oregano 
🌶️½ tsp black pepper 
🌶️Salt, to taste 

Method:
🌶️In a bowl, mix the grated cheese, capsicum, onion, green chillies, cream, oregano, pepper, and a little salt to form a creamy filling. 
🌶️Spread butter on the bread slices, then generously spread the cheese mixture on top. 
🌶️Place in a preheated air fryer at 180°C and cook for 5–7 minutes until the cheese melts, turns slightly golden, and the bread becomes crisp. 
🌶️Serve hot.

caption:
Does it Slap or Is it CHEEKS (Ep. 32) 

SUPREME SLAPPER 👋🏽👋🏽

So insanely tasty, easy, and macro friendly 👀

You HAVE to give this one a try 👇🏽

Ground Chicken Seekh Kebabs 
(with Lachha Pyaaz)

Ingredients (Kebabs)
•	1 lb ground chicken
•	½ medium red onion, finely diced
•	2 tsp garlic paste
•	1 tsp ginger paste
•	1 tsp minced green chilies
•	1 tbsp coriander seeds, coarsely ground
•	1 tsp garam masala
•	½ tsp ground cumin
•	½ tsp Kashmiri red chili powder
•	½ cup cilantro, chopped
•	1½ tsp kosher salt
•	1 tbsp olive oil

Lachha Pyaaz (Spiced Onions)
Ingredients
•	1 medium red onion
•	1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder
•	1 tsp kosher salt
•	½ tsp chaat masala
•	2 tbsp cilantro, finely chopped
•	Juice of 1 lemon or lime (I did lemon)

Instructions 
Lachha Pyaaz 
1.	Slice onion into medium-thick pieces (not too thin).
2.	Soak in ice-cold water for 5–10 minutes, then drain completely and pat dry.
3.	Add chili powder, salt, chaat masala, cilantro, and lemon/lime juice.
4.	Toss (or gently massage) until evenly coated.
5.	Serve immediately or refrigerate (up to 2 days).

Chicken Kebab (Air Fryer Instructions)
1.	Mix all kebab ingredients in a bowl until fully combined.
2.	Spread mixture evenly onto parchment paper into an 8x8-inch square.
3.	Cut in half, then divide each half lengthwise to create 4 strips. 
4.	Use a spatula to make horizontal indentations (~½ inch apart) along each strip. 
5.	Air fry at 380°F for 10 minutes.
6.	Increase to 400°F for 2–3 minutes until golden with slight char.
7.	Enjoy with rice and Lachha Pyaaz

Macros 
(Per kebab, recipe makes 4)
~168 cals | 22.5 pro | 3.5 carb | 9.6 fat

caption:
Bold, smoky, spicy, and packed with flavor — this Mexican quinoa skillet is the kind of meal that makes you genuinely forget there's no meat in it. 🌶️

One pan, real ingredients, and it comes together in about 30 minutes. The enchilada sauce and fire roasted tomatoes do all the heavy lifting on flavor so you don't have to.

This is the dish I make when I want something that feels indulgent but leaves me feeling incredible after.

⬇️ Full recipe below — save this for your next taco night craving.

Mexican Quinoa Skillet

1 tbsp avocado oil
½ onion, diced
3 garlic cloves, minced
1 red pepper and 1 green pepper, diced
1 jalapeño, diced
1 cup uncooked quinoa
2½ cups vegetable broth
1 tsp each: chili powder, cumin
½ tsp each: salt, smoked paprika
¼ tsp pepper
1 cup enchilada sauce
1 can fire roasted diced tomatoes
1 can black beans, drained
1 cup corn
Juice of one lime
Toppings: avocado, cilantro, jalapeños, lime wedges
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium high. Sauté onion, garlic, peppers, and jalapeño for 8 minutes. Add quinoa, broth, and spices and cook 20-30 minutes until quinoa is done. Stir in enchilada sauce, tomatoes, black beans, corn, and lime juice. Cook 5 more minutes on medium low. Top with avocado, cilantro, jalapeños, and lime wedges.

Follow @yourcareself care for easy meals that actually make you feel incredible — no meat required. 🌿

caption:
💥SHEET PAN KAFTAS

These are such a great high protein meal prep option, and can be used with any kind of rice as your base. If you're high protein and watching carbs, you can always use my @kaizenfoodco lowcarb rice like I did.

This is one of those meals that feels like it took way more effort than it actually did. If you love mediterranean food or middle eastern food, this is as easy as it gets and so good! I've lost over 150bs eating meals like this. Simple, high-protein, low-carb, and something you'll actually look forward to eating.

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Add 2 lb ground meat to a large bowl. I like using a mix of 1lb (90/10) ground beef and 1 lb ground lamb. The lamb adds a ton of flavor.

2️⃣ In a small food processor, add 1 large shallot (or 1/2 small red onion), 3 garlic cloves, 2-3 tbsp pine nuts (or walnuts), and a large handful of parsley. Pulse until finely minced, then add it straight into the meat.

3️⃣ Add 2 tbsp tomato paste, then season with 1 tbsp paprika, 1 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp sumac, 1 tbsp oregano, 1/4 tsp cayenne, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp Aleppo pepper flakes, and a generous pinch of salt+pepper. Mix well.

4️⃣ Divide and shape into oval logs, about 2 oz each. This should give you ~18 kaftas.

5️⃣ Place on a sheet pan. Add veggies around them - I used cherry tomatoes, onion wedges so they cook faster, and a few jalapeños. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt.

6️⃣ Bake at 400°F for 28-30 mins, until the kaftas are cooked through and the veggies are roasted.

7️⃣ Make your herby yogurt sauce. Mix 1 cup plain yogurt with 3 tbsp finely chopped parsley, 1 large minced garlic clove, a squeeze of lemon, and a pinch of salt + pepper. Finish with a drizzle of olive oil and sprinkle of Aleppo pepper.

8️⃣ Eat this as is, wrap it up, or serve it over rice. If you're high-protein or low-carb, this goes well over my Kaizen lowcarb high protein rice. It's got just 6 net carbs, and packs 20g protein in a single serving. Plate it up with kaftas, veggies, 2tbsp of the yogurt sauce and fresh parsley.

If you make it, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS 🙌

ENJOY!
.
.
.
#highproteinmeals #lowcarb #keto

caption:
Crispy Roasted Cauliflower Recipe

Ingredients
1 medium head of cauliflower (approx. 2 lbs), cut into florets

3 tbsp Extra Virgin Olive Oil

1 tsp Garlic Powder

1 tsp Smoked Paprika

1/2 tsp Salt (to taste)

1/4 tsp Black Pepper

Optional: Fresh parsley and lemon wedges for serving

Instructions
Preheat: Set oven to 425°F (220°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.

Prep: Wash and completely dry cauliflower florets.

Season: In a bowl, toss florets with olive oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.

Arrange: Spread on the baking sheet in a single layer. Ensure space between florets.

Roast: Bake for 20-25 minutes. Flip halfway through.

Finish: Optional: Broil for 2 minutes for extra char. Garnish with parsley and a squeeze of lemon.

caption:
🥒🍅 Crustless Zucchini Tomato Quiche

Light, fluffy, and naturally low-carb—this easy quiche is perfect for breakfast, brunch, or a quick dinner.

🧾 Ingredients:
2 medium zucchini (thinly sliced)
1 cup cherry tomatoes (halved)
4 large eggs
1 cup milk or heavy cream
¾ cup shredded mozzarella cheese
¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese
2 cloves garlic (minced)
1 tbsp olive oil
1 tsp dried oregano or Italian seasoning
Salt & black pepper (to taste)
Optional: fresh basil or parsley (for garnish)
👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1. Preheat Oven

Preheat to 180°C (350°F).
Grease a baking dish or pie pan.

2. Cook Vegetables

Heat olive oil in a pan.
Sauté zucchini for 3–4 minutes to remove excess moisture.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Remove from heat.

3. Prepare Egg Mixture

In a bowl, whisk eggs with milk/cream.
Add salt, pepper, and oregano.

4. Assemble

Spread zucchini evenly in the dish.
Add cherry tomatoes on top.
Sprinkle mozzarella and Parmesan.
Pour egg mixture over everything.

5. Bake

Bake for 30–35 minutes until set in the center and lightly golden on top.
🍽️ Serve:
Let cool for 5–10 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with fresh herbs and serve warm.
💡 Tips:
Sautéing zucchini prevents a watery quiche.
Use cream instead of milk for a richer texture.
Add cooked chicken or turkey for extra protein.
❓ Q&A:

Q: Is this keto-friendly?
Yes, especially if you use heavy cream.

Q: Can I make it ahead?
Yes! Store in fridge and reheat when needed.

Q: Can I freeze it?
Yes, slice and freeze portions for up to 1 month.

Q: What other veggies can I add?
Spinach, mushrooms, or bell peppers work great.

Q: How do I know it's done?
Center should be set and not jiggly.
#EasyMediterraneanRecipes #mediterraneanrecipes #mediterranean #egg #crustlesszucchini  #tomatoquiche
summary: A crustless zucchini and tomato quiche recipe with eggs, mozzarella, and Mediterranean seasonings.

caption:
Save this one for your next family night 💾 

Loaded Sweet Potato & Black Bean Nachos

I never thought I'd be posting nachos on here, but I made these for the family last night and they were gone in about six minutes.

Between the sweet potato and the black beans, you're getting a massive hit of fibre and solid protein from something that feels like pure comfort food. 

Serves 4
Macros (per portion):
~580 kcal · 27g protein · 70g carbs · 22g fat · 9g fibre

Tray (first roast):
• 350g sweet potato, skin on, diced small
• 1 red onion, diced
• 1 tbsp olive oil
• 1 tsp smoked paprika
• 1 tsp cumin
• Salt & black pepper

After first roast:
• 1 bell pepper, diced
• 1 tin black beans (240g drained)
• 1 × 400g tin chopped tomatoes
• 3 garlic clove, crushed
• 1 tbsp soy sauce
• Juice of lime

To Serve (per plate):
• 50g Tortilla chips (you can get high protein chips which work well here)
• 25g Grated mature cheddar
• 110g 0% Greek yoghurt
• Fresh coriander
• Jalapeños
(Guacamole not included in the macros, so adding that would bump things a little - worth it in my opinion 😉)

Method:
1. Preheat oven to 200°C (fan 180°C). Toss sweet potato and red onion with oil and spices. Roast 20 minutes until caramelised
2. Add pepper, black beans, tinned tomatoes, garlic, soy sauce and lime. Stir through. Back in uncovered for 15–20 minutes until reduced into a thick chunky sauce
3. Tortilla chips on a plate. Spoon the hot mixture over. Scatter cheddar on immediately so it melts in
Top with Greek yoghurt, jalapenos, coriander (and guacamole if you are having it)

Never thought I'd be posting nachos. But so glad I have 💛
owner_name: Alex Hall | Women's Health & Menopause Coach
summary: A loaded sweet potato and black bean nachos recipe with macros and step-by-step instructions.

caption:
Prawns Starter For IPL Nights!

Ingredients
For marination
Tiger prawns – 1 kg, shelled and deveined
Fennel powder – 1 tbsp
Mustard powder – 1 tbsp
Turmeric powder – 1 tsp
Red chilli powder – 1 tbsp
Garlic paste – 1 tbsp
Lime juice – juice of ½ lime
Salt to taste
Coconut oil – as required
For the gravy
Oil – 1 tbsp
Garlic paste – 1 tsp
Ginger paste – 1 tsp
Mustard powder – 1 tsp
Fennel powder – 1 tsp
Thick coconut milk – 1 cup
Turmeric powder – ½ tsp
Red chilli powder – 1 tsp
Fresh coriander – chopped
Lime juice – 1 tsp
Salt to taste
For garnish
Onion – 1, sliced and fried golden
Fresh coriander – chopped

Method

-Marinate the prawns with fennel powder, mustard powder, turmeric, chilli powder, garlic paste, lime juice and salt. 
-Mix well and let them rest for 5 to 10 minutes.
Heat coconut oil in a pan. Once hot, pan fry the prawns on a medium to low flame for about 2 minutes. Flip them and cook the other side until done. Remove and keep aside.
-In the same pan, heat 1 tablespoon oil. Add garlic paste, ginger paste, mustard powder, fennel powder, thick coconut milk, turmeric powder, chilli powder and fresh coriander. Mix everything well.
-Add a little salt and drizzle in lime juice. Now add the fried prawns back into the gravy and mix gently.
-Top it with golden fried onions and fresh coriander. Cook for another minute and serve hot.
-This is the kind of snack that makes IPL watching even better.
-For a vegetarian version, you can use potatoes, paneer or mushrooms instead of prawns.

caption:
What a phenomenal discovery! When I was diagnosed with cancer back in December 2023, I was juicing copious amounts of carrots, beets, and apples. My mom didn't have access to a juicer at the time, so she decided to eat 1 carrot, 1/2 beet and 1/2 apple every day alongside me after watching a Facebook reel and being encouraged to eat these 3 foods to lower her cholesterol.

Comment SALAD for the recipe!

My mom had been struggling with her cholesterol for years, and didn't think much of this being any benefit. However, without any additional changes she went in for her annual check up, and her cholesterol had dropped 50 points. My mom is a very healthy person, you would never think she would have high cholesterol, but she did. 

Now, after doing this for several months, we have seen that it truly is this combination, as we recently learned that her eye pressure is back in the normal range! What a relief this has given my mom.

We eat the carrot raw, apple raw, and the beets cooked. All 3 of these foods can be found at Walmart, and even Costco.

If you're ready to take your kitchen management skills to the next level, and learn how I manage my kitchen to feed my family of 6: 3 meals per day, keep a stocked pantry, and have meals prepared in the freezer, then comment GUIDE and I will show you how.

*disclaimer: I am not a doctor, nor am I trying to be one. I am sharing a testimony of my mother and her outstanding results from eating these 3 foods. Please consult your doctor for medical advice.

#eatforhealth #healthylifestyle #changeyourhabits
summary: Mom's daily intake of one carrot, half beet, and half apple reportedly lowered her cholesterol by 50 points and normalized her eye pressure.

caption:
Baked Chicken and Peppers

Ingredients (Serves 3–4)
4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (or thighs)
2 bell peppers (red, yellow, or orange), sliced
1 green bell pepper, sliced
1 large onion, sliced
3 cloves garlic, minced
3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil
1 tsp dried oregano
1 tsp paprika
½ tsp thyme
½ tsp chili flakes (optional)
Salt & black pepper, to taste
Juice of ½ lemon
1 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped (for garnish)

Instructions
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
In a large baking dish, add sliced peppers, onion, and garlic. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil and toss lightly.
Place the chicken on top of the vegetables.
In a small bowl, mix remaining olive oil, oregano, paprika, thyme, chili flakes, salt, and pepper. Rub this mixture all over the chicken.
Drizzle lemon juice over everything.
Bake for 25–35 minutes, or until chicken is fully cooked and juicy (internal temp ~75°C / 165°F).
Broil for 2–3 minutes at the end for a slightly charred, roasted finish.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

Pro Tips
Use chicken thighs if you want extra juicy, flavorful results
Slice peppers thick so they don't get too soft
Let the chicken rest for 5 minutes before serving
Serving Ideas
Serve with rice, quinoa, or couscous
Pair with a side of Greek salad for a complete Mediterranean meal
Wrap in pita bread with yogurt sauce for a quick sandwich
.
.
.
#mediterraneandiet #usafoodie #bakedchickenpeppers
summary: A baked chicken and bell peppers recipe with Mediterranean herbs and seasonings.

caption:
SMASHED ZUCCHINI & CHICKPEA SALAD WITH AVOCADO &  PISTACHIOS! Zucchini doesn't need saving—it just needs this.

Smash it, crisp it, and layer it with avocado, herbs, and pistachios for something that hits way harder than it should. Textures and flavors 10/10.
•
For the salad: 
3 large zucchini 
Salt
2 tbsp olive oil 
1 tsp paprika
½ tsp garlic powder 
¼ cup chopped cilantro 
⅓ cup finely chopped red onion
1 avocado, finely chopped
¼ cup pistachios
Tahini, optional for topping
•
For the chickpeas: 
1 15-oz can chickpeas, rinsed and drained
½ tbsp olive oil
1 tsp paprika
¼ tsp salt
½ tsp garlic powder
•
1️⃣Preheat the oven to 425. 2️⃣Place the zucchini down onto a cutting board or the counter. Use a rolling pin or a heavy pan to smash the zucchini into medium sized pieces. Repeat with the remaining zucchini. 3️⃣Next, place the zucchini into a colander then sprinkle liberally with salt. Let the zucchini sit for 10 minutes then use a paper towel or a rag to wipe and gently squeeze the moisture from the zucchini. Do not rinse with water. 4️⃣Place the zucchini onto a baking sheet, add the oil, paprika and garlic powder and toss to coat. Spread the zucchini out, face the skin side up, then place it in the oven for 30-35 minutes flipping halfway.5️⃣Next, add the chickpeas to a separate sheet pan and place them in the oven for 10 minutes, then remove them from the oven and drizzle with the olive oil, paprika, salt and garlic powder. Toss to coat, then place them in the oven for 10 more minutes. Remove them from the oven and allow them to cool. 6️⃣Place the zucchini into a bowl, then add the cilantro, onion, avocado and pistachios. Toss to combine. Add the chickpeas and lightly toss, then plate the salad. Drizzle with tahini, if using. 
•
#zucchinirecipes
#healthyrecipes
#foodreels
#easyrecipes
#homecooking
summary: A smashed and roasted zucchini salad with chickpeas, avocado, and pistachios.

caption:
🥧 Irresistibly Easy Ina Garten–Style Crustless Quiche
📝 Ingredients
6 large eggs
1 cup heavy cream (or half-and-half for lighter option)
1 cup milk
1½ cups shredded Gruyère or Swiss cheese
½ cup grated Parmesan cheese
1 cup cooked spinach (well-drained)
½ cup cooked bacon or ham (optional)
1 small onion (finely chopped)
2 cloves garlic (minced)
1 tbsp butter or olive oil
½ tsp nutmeg (optional, but classic)
Salt & black pepper (to taste)
👩‍🍳 Instructions
1. Preheat oven

Set to 180°C (350°F) and grease a pie dish or baking pan.

2. Cook the filling
Heat butter or oil in a pan
Sauté onion and garlic until soft
Add spinach and cook off excess moisture
Stir in cooked bacon/ham if using
3. Make the custard

In a bowl, whisk together:

Eggs
Cream
Milk
Nutmeg, salt, and pepper
4. Assemble
Spread the veggie mixture evenly in the dish
Sprinkle cheeses on top
Pour egg mixture over everything
5. Bake

Bake for 35–40 minutes until set in the center and lightly golden

6. Rest & serve

Let cool for 5–10 minutes before slicing
Serve warm or at room temperature 😍

💡 Tips (Ina-style perfection 😉)
Always drain spinach well to avoid watery quiche
Use high-quality cheese for rich flavor
Let it rest before cutting—it sets beautifully
Add fresh herbs like dill or chives for a gourmet touch
🍽️ Serving Ideas
Fresh green salad 🥗

Fresh fruit platter 🍓

caption:
This is the secret to the juiciest, tastiest chicken you've ever made it home. It's amazing on its own, but even better with my spicy shred sauce.

Comment "BANGER" and I'll send you my free mini protein ebook.

Here's how I made it (makes 4 servings)

1.Grab a bowl. Add 1.5lb skin-on bone-in thighs to a bowl. You want to use bone in skin on chicken thighs here to keep them juicy, and you can always remove the skin later.

2.Drizzle the chicken with 1 tsp olive oil and season it with salt & pepper, 1.5 tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1 tbsp oregano, and 1/4 to 1/2 tsp cayenne pepper. 

3.Now, to make the skin crispy, the secret hack is to add 1 tbsp baking powder. Make sure you mix this really well, and ideally let sit so the chicken comes up to room temp before you pop it into the oven.

4.Lay this out on a cooking tray (lined, so the air goes underneath the chicken) and pop into the oven at 400F.

5.For the sauce, use whatever you like. I personally LOVE this spicy shred sauce. Grab a small food processor, and you can use all mayo, or a mix of greek yogurt and mayo like I did for a healthier lower calorie sauce. Just process 1/2 cup Greek yogurt, 2 tbsp mayo, 2 scallions, 2 large handful fresh cilantro, 1/2 jalapeño (seeds removed if preferred), juice of 1.5 limes, some paprika or chipotle powder & couple pinches salt. Taste it & adjust as you wish!

You can serve this with roasted vegetables, some regular rice or quinoa, or if you're high protein or lowcarb, my high protein lowcarb Kaizen rice!

If you make this, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #ketorecipes #healthyfoodshare
summary: Recipe for crispy skin-on chicken thighs with spicy cilantro-lime sauce, designed for low-carb and keto diets.

caption:
Black Bean Quesadillas

BIG BATCH Meal Prep, Episode 3

Per Quesadilla:
270 Calories
20g Protein
24g Carbs (19g Fiber)
9g Fat

Full recipe below!

—

Beans:
30g (2 Tbsp) olive oil
10 cloves garlic, finely chopped
1800g (4 cans) black beans
30g (1 packet) taco seasoning
800g (2 jars) red enchilada sauce
10g (1 Tbsp) nutritional yeast
1 bunch cilantro stems, finely chopped

Cheese:
360g (1 1/2 cups) cottage cheese
200g (2 cups) fat free mozzarella, shredded
200g (2 cups) sharp cheddar, shredded
1 bunch cilantro leaves, finely chopped

Tortillas:
20 low carb taco-size tortillas

Low-Calorie Chipotle Sauce:
200g (3/4 cup) diced chipotles
200g (3/4 cup) 0% Greek yogurt
20g (1 Tbsp) honey
10g (2 tsp) avocado oil
Salt to taste

—

Instructions

- Heat a large pot over medium heat. Add the olive oil and garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant

- Add the black beans, taco seasoning, enchilada sauce, nutritional yeast, and chopped cilantro stems. Stir well and bring to a simmer. Cook over medium-low heat for about 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until thickened

- Mash the beans directly in the pot until the mixture is thick and cohesive but still has some texture. Add the cottage cheese, mozzarella, cheddar, and chopped cilantro leaves. Stir until melted and fully combined. Transfer the filling to a large bowl and let cool for 15–20 minutes.

- Lay out the tortillas and divide the filling evenly. Spread onto one half of each tortilla, then fold closed. Optionally toast in a skillet or on a griddle until lightly golden on both sides. Let cool completely, then freeze.

REHEAT INSTRUCTIONS:

Air fry at 375°F for 6–8 minutes from frozen
OR
Microwave for about 2 1/2 minutes from frozen

Enjoy! 

#stealthhealth #mealprep #bigbatch
summary: Big batch black bean quesadillas with cottage cheese and enchilada sauce, 270 calories and 20g protein per quesadilla, with air fryer reheating instructions.

caption:
Crispy Breaded Cauliflower Bites
Ingredients
The Veggie: 1 large head cauliflower, cut into bite-sized florets

Wet Batter: 1 cup buttermilk, 1 large egg, and 1 cup all-purpose flour

Crispy Coating: 1.5–2 cups panko breadcrumbs

Seasoning: 1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp paprika, salt, and black pepper

Creamy Dipping Sauce: ½ cup mayonnaise, ¼ cup sour cream (or Greek yogurt), 1–2 tbsp sriracha, 1 tsp honey, and 1 tsp lime juice

Garnish: Fresh parsley or chives

Instructions
Prep: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) or air fryer to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Dredge Station: Whisk the buttermilk, egg, and flour into a thick batter. In a separate bowl, combine the panko with the seasonings.

Coat: Dip each floret into the wet batter, then roll in the panko mixture, pressing gently to ensure an even, thick crust.

Cook:

Oven: Arrange florets in a single layer. Spray with oil. Bake for 22–25 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crisp.

Air Fryer: Place in the basket (do not overcrowd). Spray with oil and air fry for 12–15 minutes, shaking the basket halfway through.

Whisk Sauce: Combine all sauce ingredients in a small bowl until smooth.

Serve: Garnish the hot cauliflower with fresh herbs and serve immediately with the spicy-sweet dipping sauce.
summary: Recipe for crispy breaded cauliflower bites with a spicy sriracha-lime dipping sauce, cooked via air fryer or oven.

caption:
Chickpea, Beet & Feta Salad with Lemon Garlic Vinaigrette 🥗✨

Ingredients:

For the salad:

1 ½ cups cooked chickpeas (or 1 can, drained & rinsed)
2 medium beets (roasted or boiled, peeled & diced)
1/2 cup feta cheese (crumbled)
1/4 red onion (thinly sliced)
1/4 cup fresh parsley (chopped)
1/4 cup cucumber (diced, optional)
2 tbsp toasted walnuts or almonds (optional)

For the lemon garlic vinaigrette:

3 tbsp olive oil
2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
1–2 garlic cloves (minced)
1 tsp honey or maple syrup
Salt and black pepper to taste

Instructions:

If using fresh beets, roast them at 200°C (400°F) for 35–40 minutes wrapped in foil, or boil until fork-tender. Let cool, peel, and dice.
In a large bowl, combine chickpeas, diced beets, red onion, cucumber, and parsley.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, honey, salt, and pepper until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat everything evenly.
Add crumbled feta and toasted nuts on top. Toss lightly again or leave as topping for presentation.
Let the salad sit for 10–15 minutes before serving so flavors meld beautifully.

Serving tip:
Serve slightly chilled or at room temperature with warm pita or grilled chicken for a complete Mediterranean-style meal 😍

#mediterraneandiet #usafoodie #fetasalad
summary: A Mediterranean chickpea and beet salad with feta cheese and lemon garlic vinaigrette.

caption:
Punjabi Chicken Karahi is a true North Indian classic, rooted in the kitchens of Punjab where bold flavours and fast cooking define the cuisine. Traditionally made in a heavy iron karahi over high heat, this dish is all about intensity, simplicity, and letting the ingredients speak for themselves.

The texture is what sets it apart - a thick, glossy masala that coats every piece of chicken without being overly saucy. The chicken stays juicy and tender, while the gravy delivers a punchy balance of heat, tang, and deep, comforting richness.

It's rustic, vibrant, and full of character - a dish that captures the heart of Punjabi cooking in every bite.

#indianfood #chicken #chickenkarahi #recipe #punjab

caption:
240 calories and 28g of protein per serving — and the flavour is absolutely unreal. Golden crispy chicken, charred broccoli, juicy mushrooms, all tossed in a sticky-spicy soy-honey-chilli crisp glaze. Done in under 20 minutes. 🔥
The secret? A tiny pinch of baking soda in the marinade — it raises the pH of the chicken, which stops the proteins from bonding too tightly when they hit the pan. Translation: juicy, silky chicken inside, golden and crispy outside. Chinese restaurant trick, zero effort. 

Chicken, Mushroom & Broccoli Stir Fry
📊 ~240 kcal | ~28g Protein | ~12g Carbs | ~8g Fat per serving
Makes 2 Servings | Serving size: ~220g
Ingredients
FOR THE CHICKEN
	•	1 large chicken breast (~250g), sliced into strips
	•	1 tbsp arrowroot or corn flour
	•	¼ tsp baking soda
	•	1 tsp soy sauce
	•	Salt to taste
	•	Pepper to taste
	•	1 tsp neutral oil of choice - I've used light olive oil
FOR THE STIR FRY
	•	1 tsp oil
	•	1.5 tbsp crushed ginger and garlic (combined)
	•	¼ cup chopped onion or spring onion whites
	•	1 cup sliced mushrooms
	•	1 cup broccoli florets
	•	Salt to taste
	•	1.5 tsp soy sauce
	•	1 tsp chilli crisp
	•	1 tsp honey
	•	2 tbsp spring onion greens (to finish)
METHOD
	•	Marinate chicken strips with arrowroot, baking soda, soy sauce, salt, and pepper. Rest 10–15 minutes.
	•	Pan-fry on high heat in a little oil until golden brown (max 2 mins on each side). Set aside.
	•	In the same pan, add a little oil. Sauté crushed ginger and garlic, then add onion/spring onion whites.
	•	Add mushrooms, broccoli, and salt. Stir-fry on high flame until broccoli is crisp-tender.
	•	Add soy sauce and toss. Return chicken to the pan with chilli crisp and honey. Toss until glossy for another 1-2 mins.
	•	Finish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

.
#HighProteinMeals #ChickenStirFry #HealthyDinner #MacroFriendly​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
summary: A 20-minute chicken, mushroom, and broccoli stir fry with a sticky soy-honey-chilli glaze, delivering 28g protein and 240 calories per serving.

caption:
This easy Tandoori Chicken Tikka made in an air fryer or grilled on a pan is a high protein recipe. If prepped in advance, your dinners like tikka kebabs or kathi rolls can be ready in just a few minutes.

• 1 kg boneless chicken thigh (only)
• 1 cup hung curd
• Salt to taste
• 1 tbsp lemon juice 
• 1 tsp ginger garlic paste each
• 1 tbsp tandoori masala
• 1/2 tsp garam masala
• 1/2 tsp turmeric powder (for color)
• 1/2 tsp Kashmiri mirch powder
• 2 tbsp mustard oil
• 2 tbsp pre roasted besan

First marinate the chicken with lemon juice and salt so it releases maximum water.

After 30 minutes, prepare the marinade and add the chicken pieces. Mix well.

For best flavor absorption, leave it overnight. It works perfectly as part of weekly meal prep.

Once marinated, cook in an air fryer or preheated oven for 20 minutes at 180 degrees.

No oven? Just cook it on a pan and enjoy with green chutney.

#chickentikka #chickendinner #chickentikkamasala #tikkamasala

COUVE FLOR GRATINADA AO FORNO

Tem legumes que surpreendem quando viram prato principal. A couve flor fica macia o refogado dá mais sabor e a cobertura douradinha de queijo deixa tudo ainda mais apetitoso. Uma forma simples e gostosa de variar as refeições.

Modo de preparo.
Coloque a couve flor em pedaços em um refratário e reserve.
Em uma panela aqueça azeite e refogue a cebola.
Acrescente a cenoura o sal e a pimenta do reino e refogue até a cenoura amolecer.
Espalhe esse refogado sobre a couve flor.
Em outro recipiente misture os ovos o leite os temperos e a cebolinha.
Bata bem e despeje sobre a couve flor.
Leve ao forno preaquecido a 180 graus por 20 minutos.
Retire cubra com a mussarela e volte ao forno por mais 10 minutos para gratinar.

caption:
Copycat California Pizza Kitchen Thai Crunch Salad (with crispy quinoa)!

Recipe

Cook 4 cups of quinoa and set half aside for the salad. 

Spread the other half on a baking sheet with a little olive oil and salt and bake at 375° for 25 to 30 minutes. This will be the gluten free crispy salad topping.

The Salad 

2 cups quinoa 
4 mini cucumbers, diced
4 to 5 green onions, chopped
3 cups shredded romaine 
6 oz coleslaw mix 
2 avocados, diced
1 cup shredded carrots
1 bunch of cilantro, chopped up
1 red bell pepper, diced 
1 cup peanuts
1 cup cooked edamame 
salt and pepper to taste
2 cups crispy quinoa 

The Dressing

1/3 cup peanut butter
1/3 cup water 
2 tablespoons brown sugar 
3 tablespoons avocado oil
3 tablespoons rice vinegar
2 tablespoons soy sauce
2 teaspoons sesame seed oil
1/2 teaspoon garlic granules or fresh garlic
juice of one lime
1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes

Combine all of the salad ingredients in a big bowl, top with dressing and crispy quinoa. Enjoy!
#dinner #dinnerideas #glutenfree #dairyfree #easyrecipes food blogger mom life healthy recipe healthy lunch healthy dinner nutrient dense salad high protein vegetables vegetarian yum

caption:
Just 230 calories per serving — but absolutely no compromise on flavour, richness, or that slow-cooked depth you expect from a good dal makhani. Smoky, creamy, dreamy. 🤌
Special shout out and thanks to my friend @mrsyumtum for giving me the chana dal hack - makes it so creamy! 😋🫶🏼❤️

Healthier Dal Makhani
🥣 Serves 4 (roughly 225 g per serving)
📊 ~230 kcal | ~13g Protein | ~25g Carbs | ~7g Fat per serving

FOR PRESSURE COOKING
	•	150g whole black urad dal
	•	30g rajma
	•	20g chana dal
	•	1 green chili, 1 bay leaf
	•	½ tsp turmeric, 1 tsp grated ginger, salt
FOR THE BASE
	•	2 tsp ghee
	•	1 tsp cumin seeds
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	1.5 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
	•	¾ cup tomato puree (two medium sized tomatoes puréed)
	•	1 tsp garam masala, salt to taste
INSTEAD OF CREAM
	•	100g low-fat high-protein paneer, blended with 3–4 tbsp warm water into a silky puree
FOR THE TADKA
	•	2 tsp ghee
	•	1 tsp kasuri methi (lightly toasted and powdered)
	•	1.5 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
METHOD
	•	Soak all dals overnight (if you forgot, don't worry, soak it in hot water for 3-4 hours). Drain, add fresh water, and pressure cook with chili, bay leaf, turmeric, ginger, and salt for 6–7 whistles until completely soft. Remove bay leaf and chilli.
	•	Heat ghee, add cumin seeds. Add Kashmiri chili powder and ginger-garlic paste, cook until fragrant.
	•	Add tomato puree, cook until oil separates. Add garam masala, fold in the cooked dals, add hot water, season with salt, cover and simmer 8–10 minutes.
	•	Blend paneer with warm water until completely smooth. Pour into the dal, simmer 2–3 minutes.
	•	For tadka: heat ghee, switch off flame, add powdered kasuri methi and Kashmiri chili powder. Pour straight over the dal.

[dal makhani, healthy dal makhani, high protein Indian food, Indian food for weight loss, no cream dal makhani, healthy Indian dinner, paneer instead of cream, macro friendly Indian recipes, easy high protein meals]

#DalMakhani #HealthyIndianFood #HighProteinMeals
summary: A 230-calorie, high-protein dal makhani recipe using blended paneer instead of cream for richness.

caption:
Zucchini Breadsticks

Ingredients
2 cups zucchini, grated
1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
¼ cup grated parmesan cheese
1 egg
2 cloves garlic, minced
½ tsp salt
½ tsp black pepper
½ tsp Italian seasoning
½ cup breadcrumbs (or almond flour for low-carb)
1–2 tbsp olive oil

Instructions

Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking tray with parchment paper.
Grate the zucchini and squeeze out as much moisture as possible using a clean cloth (this step is crucial).
In a bowl, mix zucchini, mozzarella, parmesan, egg, garlic, salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning.
Add breadcrumbs and mix until a dough-like consistency forms.
Transfer the mixture onto the baking tray and press it into a rectangular shape (about ½ inch thick).
Brush lightly with olive oil for a golden crust.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until set and lightly golden.
Remove from oven, cut into breadstick shapes, and return to bake for another 5–10 minutes for extra crispiness.
Serve warm with marinara or garlic yogurt dip.

Tips
• The drier the zucchini, the crispier the breadsticks.
• Add chili flakes for a spicy kick.
• Sprinkle extra cheese on top before the second bake for a cheesy crust.

#mediterraneandiet #usafoodie #zuchhinibreadsticks
summary: Baked zucchini and cheese breadsticks with Italian seasonings, served with marinara or garlic yogurt dip.

caption:
Tandoori tofu 👀 

(1) tear the extra firm tofu into a regular pieces, and boil in heavily salt, salted water for five minutes.
(2) set aside the tofu on a paper towel to let it cool.
(3) make your tandoori marinade - I used turmeric, coriander powder, kashmiri chili powder, fenugreek leaves, lemon juice, ginger and garlic paste, and oil. (Full recipe in my cookbook "Make it Meatless!"
(4) coat the tofu in the marinade and lightly spray with oil before putting it into the air fryer at 400° for 15 to 20 minutes.
(5) make a cilantro lime chutney with one bunch of cilantro, lots of mint, a Serrano chili, lemon, a big dollop of Greek yogurt, and salt!

caption:
Prawns masala 🦐

 INGREDIENTS 
* Prawns-300 gms
* oil-20 ml
* Mustard seeds -1 tsp
* Dried red chilli-2 nos
* Chopped green chilli -1 nos
* Sliced shallots (small onions-15 nos
* Chopped tomato 🍅-1 small sized
* Curry leaves 🍃 -1 sprig
* Ginger garlic paste-1 tsp
* Salt 🧂 
* Turmeric powder -1/4 tsp
* Chilli 🌶️ powder -1 tsp 
* Coriander powder-1/2 tsp
* Crushed pepper-1/2 tsp
* Chopped coriander leaves 

METHOD 
* Marinate the prawns with chilli and turmeric powder
* Heat oil in a kadai crackle mustard seeds and dried red chilli then add curry leaves and green chilli and saute
*  Now Add sliced shallots and Sauté till shallots turns golden brown then add ginger garlic paste and sauté now add chopped tomatoes and cook till the tomatoes are mashed nicely 
* Start adding dry powders turmeric/chilli powder/coriander powder sauté till the raw flavours disappears and add salt 
* Now bring the marinated prawns to the masala and cook for 4-5 minutes when the prawn is cooked increase the flame and Start drying up the masala
* To finishing and crushed pepper and chopped coriander leaves 
* Serve it with steamed rice 

Always remember this "everybody can cook and everybody has to cook"

Happy cooking 😊

caption:
CRISPY VIRAL HIGH PROTEIN TORTILLA BAKE

This VIRAL crispy tortilla bake is my new obsession. Literally the easiest dinner ever… and the best part? You can fill it with anything you have in the fridge.

Save the recipe below, tag a friend and let me know in the comments if you're going to make it🙌

Ingredients:
For the base:
3 large tortilla wraps
Water (for softening)
For the filling:
1 cup broccoli florets, finely chopped
1 cup corn kernels
1 cup chickpeas (drained and rinsed)
1 cup cooked quinoa
½ cup Greek yoghurt (use dairy-free, if vegan)
½ cup shredded cheese (use dairy-free, if vegan)
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp dried oregano
½ tsp garlic powder
½ tsp onion powder
Salt and pepper, to taste
To finish:
Extra shredded cheese
Fresh parsley, chopped
Method:
Lightly wet each tortilla with water to soften, then cut each one in half to create 6 semicircles. Heat a pan over medium heat. Add chopped broccoli and cook for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened. Stir in corn, chickpeas and cooked quinoa. Cook for another 2–3 minutes. Add Greek yoghurt, shredded cheese and spices. Mix well until everything is combined and creamy. Remove from heat. Place a spoonful of filling onto each tortilla half. Fold into a triangle by bringing the edges together. Place folded tortillas into a round baking dish, arranging them in a circle so they fit snugly together. Sprinkle with extra cheese and a little parsley. Bake in a preheated oven at 180°C (fan-forced) for 10 minutes or until golden and crispy. Let it cool slightly, then serve warm perfect on its own or with extra yoghurt on the side. Enjoy and follow for more healthy recipes!

#vegetarian #highprotein #tortilla #easyrecipes #healthyfood
summary: Crispy baked tortilla triangles filled with broccoli, corn, chickpeas, quinoa, and yogurt—a high-protein vegetarian dinner.

caption:
🥚 Crustless Quiche

⏱ Prep Time: 10 minutes
🔥 Cook Time: 30–35 minutes
🍽 Servings: 4–6

🛒 Ingredients

Egg Base:

6 large eggs

½ cup milk or cream

½ tsp salt

¼ tsp black pepper

½ tsp garlic powder (optional)

½ tsp dried herbs (thyme, oregano, or parsley)

Filling (choose your favorites):

1 cup chopped vegetables (spinach, broccoli, bell peppers, mushrooms, etc.)

½ cup cooked protein (bacon, ham, or chicken, optional)

½ cup shredded cheese (cheddar, feta, or mozzarella)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a 9-inch pie dish or oven-safe baking dish.

2️⃣ Prepare filling:
If using vegetables like broccoli or mushrooms, sauté lightly until tender. Cook any raw proteins beforehand.

3️⃣ Mix eggs:
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and dried herbs.

4️⃣ Assemble quiche:
Spread vegetables and proteins evenly in the prepared dish. Pour the egg mixture over the top. Sprinkle cheese evenly.

5️⃣ Bake:
Bake for 30–35 minutes, or until the center is set and the top is lightly golden. A knife inserted in the center should come out clean.

6️⃣ Serve:
Allow to cool 5–10 minutes before slicing. Serve warm or at room temperature.

💡 Tips

Add fresh herbs like chives or parsley for extra flavor

Use a muffin tin for mini crustless quiches—bake 15–20 minutes

Can be stored in the fridge for 3 days and reheated

Perfect for breakfast, brunch, or a high-protein snack 🥚🧀🥦
#crustlessquich #usafoodie #mediterraneandiet
summary: A crustless quiche recipe with customizable vegetable and protein fillings, baked for 30-35 minutes.

caption:
Roasted Veggie Chickpea Bowl with Creamy Dressing 🥗✨

🧾 Ingredients

For the roasted veggies & chickpeas:

1 can (240g drained) chickpeas
1 zucchini (chopped)
1 bell pepper (chopped)
1 cup broccoli florets
1 carrot (sliced)
1 small red onion (chunked)
2 tbsp olive oil
1 tsp paprika
½ tsp cumin
½ tsp garlic powder
Salt & pepper (to taste)

For the creamy dressing:

½ cup Greek yogurt
2 tbsp tahini
1 tbsp lemon juice
1 clove garlic (minced)
1–2 tbsp water (to thin)
Salt (to taste)

For assembling:

1–2 cups cooked quinoa, brown rice, or couscous
Fresh parsley or cilantro (chopped)
Optional: avocado slices, feta cheese, chili flakes
👨‍🍳 Instructions
1. Roast the veggies & chickpeas
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Pat chickpeas dry for extra crispiness.
Toss chickpeas and veggies with olive oil, paprika, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Spread on a baking tray in a single layer.
Roast for 20–25 minutes until golden and slightly crispy.
2. Make the creamy dressing
In a bowl, whisk together Greek yogurt, tahini, lemon juice, and garlic.
Add water gradually until smooth and pourable.
Adjust salt to taste.
3. Assemble the bowl
Add a base of quinoa, rice, or couscous.
Top with roasted veggies and chickpeas.
Drizzle generously with creamy dressing.
Garnish with fresh herbs and optional toppings.
🔥 Pro Tips
Roast at high heat for caramelized edges and deeper flavor.
Add a pinch of smoked paprika for a smoky twist.
Make it vegan by swapping yogurt with plant-based yogurt.

#mediterraneandiet #usafoodie #chickpeabowl

*Veggie & Black‑Bean Quesadilla Recipe
1. *Ingredients* (for 4 quesadillas):
- 8 large flour tortillas
- 2 cups shredded cheese (cheddar or Mexican blend)
- 1 cup cooked black beans, drained
- 1 avocado, sliced or mashed
- 1 bell pepper (yellow & red), diced
- ½ red onion, finely chopped
- 2 tbsp fresh cilantro, chopped
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp chili powder (optional)
- 2 tbsp olive oil or butter
- Salt & pepper to taste
2. *Method*:
- *Mix filling*: combine beans, bell pepper, onion, cumin, chili powder, salt & pepper.
- *Assemble*: lay a tortilla, sprinkle cheese, add filling, avocado slices, cilantro, then another tortilla on top.
- *Cook*: heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium‑high heat. Place quesadilla in pan, press gently, cook 2‑3 min per side until golden & cheese melts.
- *Finish*: remove, let rest 1 min, then cut into wedges. Garnish with extra cilantro.
3. *Serve*: with salsa, sour cream, or guacamole on the side.

caption:
Would you try this stuffed sweet potato??

Forget the sweet potato… the creamy sriracha sauce is SO good you'll want to put it on everything.

I've lost over 150lbs and this is one of my favorite healthy lunch meal prep option. It's such an easy tasty option and great way to keep full and satiated with slow digesting carbs, protein, health fats, and fiber.

If you are watching your carb intake, sub in spaghetti squash, eggplant, or even zucchini instead!

Here is how I made it (makes 6):

1.Slow roast 6 small sweet potatoes at 350°F for 75 mins. Time depends on thickness. You can always roast on higher heat (400F for 50 mins) but I prefer slow roasting.

2.Cook 1 lb lean ground beef (I used 93/7 to be more calorically friendly) in 1 tsp oil. Season beef with salt, pepper, 1 tbsp chipotle powder, 1tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp thyme, and 1 tbsp sweet paprika. Mince it down finely with a spatul. I like to get these browned up really nicely, almost crispy in a sense.

3.When the sweet potato are done, cool for a minute or two, then fill each with 4oz of cooked beef, avocado, diced red onion,  chopped cilantro, and sauce. You can use hot sauce, or my creamy sriracha sauce  in step 4 below.

4.For the sauce (this normally makes 4 servings, but I stretched it to all 6 sweet potato)… mix  2 tbsp mayo with 1/4 cup sriracha, 2 tbsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp soy sauce, 2 tsp powdered monk fruit sweetener, the juice of 1 solid lime, 1 grated garlic clove, and 1 thinly sliced scallion or a handful of chopped cilantro. Season with salt and pepper, mix it up, taste and adjust.

5.Top with any veggies you like, chili flakes, sesame seeds, or herbs.

This is a great healthy lunch recipe, and perfect for meal prep. If you want to make it higher protein, add ccottage cheese or greek yogurt. It makes a great healthy lunch.

If you try it, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!
.
.
.
.
.
#healthyrecipes #healthylifestyle #healthyfoodshare #goodmoodfood
summary: Stuffed sweet potato with seasoned ground beef and creamy sriracha sauce, designed as a high-protein, low-calorie meal prep option.

caption:
🍯 HIGH PROTEIN BAJA HOT HONEY CHICKEN BOWL 🍚

485 calories • 47g protein • 8 net carbs

This is a super high protein meal that hits all the right spots. It's a little sweet, a little spicy with all the right creamy and crunchy textures.

Here's what you'll need:
1lb (~2 ½ cups) grilled or air-fried chicken, diced up
1/4 cup sugar-free honey (or use regular honey) 
2 tbsp sriracha sauce
1 tbsp rice vinegar
1 tbsp soy sauce

1 cup thinly shredded green cabbage
½  red onion, super thinly sliced
2-3 tbsp chopped cilantro
1 small avocado, sliced

1 carton (8oz) Kaizen lowcarb rice

Chipotle Lime Protein Crema:
½ cup greek yogurt
2 tbsp mayo
2 limes, juiced
½ tbsp chili lime seasoning
½ tsp chipotle powder
Salt & pepper, to taste

Here's how to make it:
1️⃣ For the hot honey: Use a bottled one, or make my macro-friendly one: Add ¼ cup sugar-free honey, 2 tbsp sriracha, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, and 1 tbsp soy sauce to a pan on low heat. Stir or about a min until combined.

2️⃣ Add 2 cups cooked chicken (or rotisserie) to a bowl and dice. Toss your diced chicken in the sauce until fully coated.

3️⃣ Make the slaw: combine shredded cabbage, red onion, and chopped cilantro in a bowl. Make sure its super thinly sliced, this is important.

4️⃣ Make the protein crema: Mix the yogurt, mayo, lime juice, seasonings, and spices until smooth. Add half of it to the slaw and toss. We'll use the other half to drizzle later.

5️⃣ Cook up your Kaizen high protein + low carb rice!

6️⃣ Then build your bowl with rice, chicken, slaw, avocado, and any toppings you like.

7️⃣ Drizzle some more crema, sprinkle sesame seeds, top with extra herbs

ENJOY!

#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #highproteinmeals #healthyrecipes #proteindinner
summary: High-protein Baja hot honey chicken bowl with chipotle lime crema, cabbage slaw, and low-carb rice.

Ground Chicken (96/4 or similar, not ground breast) or ground meat of choice▪️1 Large Clove Garlic, finely chopped▪️1/4 Cup Finely Chopped Fresh Parsley▪️1/4 Cup Grated Parmesan Cheese▪️1 Egg▪️3/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/2 tsp Freshly Ground Pepper▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️

Sauce:▪️2 Tbsp Butter▪️8 oz. Cremini Mushrooms or White Button Mushrooms, sliced 1/4-inch thick▪️1/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Freshly Ground Pepper▪️1 Shallot (3 oz.), finely chopped▪️3 Cloves Garlic, finely chopped▪️1 tsp Flour▪️1 Cup Chicken Stock▪️1/4 Cup Cream Cheese (2 oz.), cut into small pieces▪️2 tsp Balsamic Vinegar▪️1 tsp Fresh Thyme Leaves, finely chopped▪️

Serving:▪️Mashed Potatoes▪️Fresh Parsley (optional)▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 400F convection (or 425F regular bake). Drizzle olive oil onto a parchment lined baking sheet. Combine the panko and milk in a bowl and let sit until absorbed.
2️⃣ In a medium bowl, add the ground chicken, garlic, parsley, parmesan, egg, soaked panko, salt, and pepper. Mix until combined. Form slightly heaping 1 Tbsp meatballs and place on the baking sheet, rolling in the oil as you go.
3️⃣ Bake for 12-15 mins, until the internal temp reaches 165F and bottoms are browned. Broil 2-3 mins for extra color if desired.
4️⃣ While the meatballs bake, make the sauce. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the mushrooms, salt, and pepper and cook, stirring occasionally, until golden and tender, 3-5 mins.
5️⃣ Add the shallot and cook 1 min, then add garlic and flour and cook 1 min more, until fragrant.
6️⃣ Pour in chicken stock and add the cream cheese. Stir, scraping up any bits, and bring to a simmer. Cook 3-4 mins, until smooth and slightly thickened.
7️⃣ Stir in balsamic vinegar and thyme. Taste and add salt and pepper if desired.
8️⃣ Add the meatballs to the sauce and toss to coat. Simmer 1 min.
9️⃣ Serve over mashed potatoes. Top with parsley if desired and enjoy!

⏲20 min prep time / 40 min total time

Serves 4
summary: Step-by-step recipe for creamy mushroom meatballs served over mashed potatoes, made with ground chicken and a balsamic-thyme cream sauce.

caption:
Genius lowcarb bread hack that actually works - would you try this lowcarb bread?

If you are looking for EASY lowcarb recipes, this is a simple bread option that works, and it's SO good. This was one of the best lowcarb bread recipes I've made, and truly, I didn't miss real bread.

You can fill these up however way you want!

Here is how I made it:

1.Start with a bowl and 2 large zucchini. Shred them, add them to a towel, and squeeze out the excess liquid well, then add the shredded zucchini to a bowl

2.Add a couple pinches salt, 1tsp garlic powder, and 1 cup shredded mozarella, and mix well.

3.Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper, spray with avocado oil, divide it up into 4 buns and press down. You can top with a little more shredded cheese on top if you want, but you dont have to.

4.Pop it into your oven at 400F for 20-22 mins, until fully set and let cool for a couple minutes.

5.Fill these with whatever fillings you like. I spread the base with two tablespoons of my protein mayo (mixed 2 tbsp mayo with 4 tbsp yogurt, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, a couple pinches salt, and lots of fresh cracked pepper), tomato slices, romaine lettuce, red onion, ham. Put the other side of the bun on, and ENJOY!!

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarbrecipes #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #keto #healthyfoodshare
summary: A low-carb zucchini bread recipe that can be filled like sandwich buns.

caption:
Sweet Chili Chicken #sweetchili  #garlicbutter #chickenrecipes #dinnerideas #easyrecipe

Sauce:
1/4 cup Sweet red chilli sauce by Thai Kitchen
1/2 Lime (juiced)
1/2 tsp Lime zest
1 tsp Soy sauce
1 tsp Sesame oil
1 tsp Sriracha

Instructions
- Cut your chicken in half
- Season with salt, pepper, and paprika on both sides.
- In a large pan, heat up some olive oil.
- Cook for 4-5 min on each side on med/high heat.
- Lower heat to med/low and melt butter and add minced garlic. Let garlic infuse (1min)

- In a small bowl, whisk together the sweet chili sauce, lime juice, lime zest, soy sauce, sesame oil, and sriracha (if using) until well combined.
- Serve chicken over a bed of jasmine rice and top with pan drippings and sweet chili lime sauce.
- Garnish with fresh parsley. Enjoy!

caption:
I feel so proud when Indian regional food is being loved globally.
Made brinji with a global creator @plantfuture who appreciates the depth of our cuisine.
Food really is the easiest way to bring cultures together…

Brinji Rice

Ingredients
To grind
• Ginger – 2-inch piece
• Garlic – 12 small cloves
• Green chilli – 2
• Fennel seeds – 1 tsp

Other ingredients
• Oil – 1 tbsp
• Ghee – 1½ tbsp
• Bay leaf – 2
• Stone flower (kalpasi) – 2 pieces
• Star anise – 1
• Cinnamon – 1-inch piece
• Onion (thinly sliced) – 1
• Tomato (sliced) – 1 small
• Mixed vegetables – potato, carrot & beans (cubed)
• Seeraga Samba Kaima Rice – 1 cup (soaked 30 mins)
• Coconut milk – 1 cup
• Water – 1 cup
• Curry leaves – a few
• Mint leaves – a handful
• Salt – to taste

Method
	1. In a thick-bottomed pan, heat oil and ghee. Add all the whole spices and let them release their aroma.
	2. Add sliced onions and sauté till light golden. Mix in the ground paste and cook till the raw smell goes away.
	3. Add tomato, chopped veggies, coriander and mint leaves. Sauté for a minute.
	4. Add soaked rice, coconut milk, water, and salt. Mix gently.
	5. Cover and cook on low flame for 15–20 minutes, till all the liquid is absorbed and rice is fluffy.
	6. Let it rest for 5 minutes before serving. Serve hot with Onion Raita . 

Tips
• Use Seeraga Samba rice for its unique aroma and grainy texture.
• Don't stir the rice too often while cooking — it helps retain the grainy texture.

[Brinji, regional cuisine, Brinjirice, South Indian cuisine, Traditional recipes, Indian flavours, Ellai saapadu]
summary: A brinji rice recipe featuring south Indian spices, coconut milk, and vegetables, made in collaboration with a global creator to celebrate Indian regional cuisine.

caption:
Hot Cabbage Pesto Salad

Cabbage Love is real in my house. We truly love it in every form and one of my favorite way of eating it , is this hot cabbage pesto salad.

Very easy to make, and yes, you can totally use pre checked bagged cabbage for this. 

Works fantastic as a side dish or just straight up as a salad. Add some shredded chicken and you have an amazing meal. 

RECIPE
For the cabbage: 
1 head red cabbage, sliced thin 
1 head green cabbage, sliced thin 
1/3 cup oil 
3 tsp. Salt
2 tsp. Black pepper 
2 tbsp. Garlic powder 
2 tbsp. Minced onion 

For the Pesto
Bunch fresh parsley
3 garlic cloves 
1 cup sliced almonds 
2 tsp. Salt 
1 tsp. Black pepper 
2 tsp. Smoked paprika 
3 tbsp. Oil 

1/2 lemon juiced 
Raisins 
2 tbsp. Honey 

Mix your cabbage with the oil and spices well. Throw onto 2 parchment lined baking sheets and spread out well. Bake on 350F for 25-30 minutes till cooked and slightly crispy on the edges. 

Place into your salad bowl.

Make your pesto by throwing all the ingredients into a blender and processing until you have a somewhat smooth and chunky pesto.

Add the lemon juice to cabbage and mix. Then add the pesto, raisins and honey and mix. 
Serve and eat warm. 

#kosherrecipes #passover #koshercooking #saladsofinstagram #viralrecipes chagwithsarah
owner_name: Sarah M Lasry - EASY RECIPES  & TRAVEL
summary: A hot cabbage pesto salad recipe with roasted red and green cabbage tossed with a parsley-almond pesto, raisins, and honey.

caption:
Crispy Cauliflower

*Sometimes the simplest vegetables turn into something surprisingly special in the oven. This crispy cauliflower develops golden edges and warm spices, making it a wonderful side dish that's both light and full of flavor.*

What You'll Need
1 large head cauliflower, cut into florets
2–3 tbsp avocado oil, divided
1 tsp sea salt
1 tbsp curry powder
½ tsp maple syrup (optional)

Optional Garnishes
Fresh cilantro
Toasted almonds
Tahini drizzle

How to Make It

1. Preheat your oven to 450°F (232°C).
2. Cut the cauliflower into evenly sized florets so they cook at the same rate.
3. In a bowl, toss the cauliflower with 1 tablespoon of avocado oil, sea salt, curry powder, and maple syrup if using.
4. Heat the remaining oil in an oven-safe skillet over high heat until very hot.
5. Add the cauliflower and sear for about 5 minutes, letting it develop golden, crispy edges.
6. Transfer the skillet to the oven and roast for 10–15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes, until tender and nicely browned.
7. Remove from the oven and garnish with fresh cilantro, toasted almonds, or a drizzle of tahini if desired. Serve warm.

Prep Time: 10 minutes | Cook Time: 20 minutes | Total Time: 30 minutes | Serves: 4
Calories: 119 per serving | Protein: 4 g per serving
summary: A recipe for crispy roasted cauliflower with curry powder and optional garnishes like cilantro and tahini.
