caption:
Grilled Zucchini & Feta Chickpea Wrap with Herbed Yogurt

Fresh, protein-packed, and Mediterranean-inspired—perfect for lunch, meal prep, or a light dinner.

Ingredients (Serves 3–4)

Grilled Zucchini

2 medium zucchini, sliced lengthwise

1 tbsp olive oil

½ tsp salt

¼ tsp black pepper

Chickpea Filling

1½ cups cooked chickpeas (or 1 can, drained & rinsed – 400 g)

1 tbsp olive oil

½ tsp smoked paprika

½ tsp cumin

¼ tsp garlic powder

Salt & black pepper, to taste

Herbed Yogurt Sauce

¾ cup Greek yogurt (180 g)

1 tbsp lemon juice

1 tbsp olive oil

1 small garlic clove, grated

2 tbsp fresh dill or parsley, finely chopped

Salt, to taste

Wrap Assembly

4 whole wheat wraps or flatbreads

½ cup crumbled feta cheese (75 g)

Optional: sliced cucumbers, cherry tomatoes, red onion, or mixed greens

Instructions

Grill the zucchini
Toss zucchini with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Grill on a hot grill pan or outdoor grill for 2–3 minutes per side until tender with light char marks. Set aside.

Season the chickpeas
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add chickpeas, smoked paprika, cumin, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sauté for 5–7 minutes until warmed and lightly crisped.

Make the herbed yogurt
In a bowl, mix Greek yogurt, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, herbs, and salt until smooth. Adjust seasoning to taste.

Warm the wraps
Heat wraps briefly on a dry pan or in the microwave to make them pliable.

Assemble the wraps
Spread herbed yogurt sauce on each wrap. Add grilled zucchini, spiced chickpeas, crumbled feta, and any optional veggies.

Wrap & serve
Fold tightly and serve immediately, or wrap in parchment for meal prep.

🍽️ Serving & Storage Tips

Great warm or cold

Keeps well refrigerated for 24–36 hours (store components separately for best texture)

Serve with olives, hummus, or a simple Greek salad

#grilledzucchini #mediterraneandiet #usafoodie
summary: A grilled zucchini and feta chickpea wrap with herbed yogurt sauce, designed for lunch or meal prep.

caption:
Sheet pan chicken adana kababs🔥 

Meet your new favorite sheet pan meal 🤝 These kababs are healthy, easy, and INCREDIBLY delicious. Definitely one of the best recipes I've made in a while. I hope you enjoy 💟

Ingredients 

- 1 lb ground chicken thighs/breast
- 1 small onion + green or red bell pepper, finely chopped
- 1/2 cup fresh parsley, finely chopped
- 4-5 cloves of garlic, chopped
- 1 tspn salt
- 1 tspn fresh cracked black pepper
- 1 tbspn coriander powder
- 1 tspn sumac powder
- 1 tspn crushed red chili flakes
- 1 tspn allepo pepper
- 1 tbspn harissa paste (or chili paste)
- 1 tbspn lemon juice
- 1 tbspn olive oil

Method

- Chop onions, parsley, and garlic using a chopper or food processor. Remove
- Pulse chicken in the same chopper (do not over mix)
- Combine the above ingredients until well incorporated. Refrigerate for at least one hour
- Add oil to a sheet pan. Add the chicken mix, smooth outC and make 2 inch kabab shapes. Add sliced onions, tomatoes, and jalapeños on the side. 
- Bake at 380F for 20-25 minutes, flip halfway through. You make need to brush on more oil as they bake.
- Broil for the last 2-3 minutes. 
- Serve with naan, rice, salad, white sauce, or harissa

caption:
Copy-cat Nordstrom Tomato Soup & Parmesan Crisps 
Ingredients:
2 tbsp olive oil 
2 large yellow onions, chopped
6 large carrots, grated
2 tbsp dried basil
6 (28 oz) cans whole peeled tomatoes (with purée)
6-8 cups chicken broth
1–2 Parmesan rinds (optional, adds rich umami)
1 cup heavy cream
Salt and black pepper, to taste
Fresh basil, for garnish (optional)

Instructions:
1.	Sauté base:
In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add chopped onions, grated carrots, and dried basil. Sauté for about 10 minutes, until vegetables are soft and fragrant.
2.	Add tomatoes, broth, and Parmesan rinds:
Stir in all tomatoes (with purée) and chicken broth.
If using, drop in 1–2 Parmesan rinds at this stage to infuse flavor.
3.	Simmer:
Bring to a boil, then lower heat and simmer uncovered for 25–30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4.	Remove rinds & blend:
Take out Parmesan rinds, then blend the soup with an immersion blender until smooth (or transfer in batches to a regular blender).
5.	Finish:
Stir in the heavy cream. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Simmer another 5 minutes.
6.	Serve:
Garnish with fresh basil and pair with crostini (below).

Parmesan Crostini

Ingredients:
•	1 baguette, sliced into ½-inch rounds
•	Salted butter, softened (for spreading)
•	3 cloves garlic, pressed or finely minced
•	1 cup Parmesan cheese, finely grated
•	3 tbsp fresh chives & 3 tbsp parsley minced

Instructions:
1.	Prep crostini:
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lay baguette slices on a baking sheet.
2.	Butter & garlic:
Spread each slice with a thin layer of salted butter. Press or mince garlic and spread a little onto each piece.
3.	Top with cheese & herbs:
Sprinkle generously with Parmesan and minced chives. Add parsley or thyme if desired.
4.	Bake:
Bake for 8–10 minutes, until golden and cheese is melted. Serve warm with the soup. #tomatosoup #copycatrecipe #parmesan #husbandwife  #vegetarian  @lecreuset
summary: Copycat Nordstrom tomato soup with parmesan crostini recipe featuring roasted vegetables, cream, and garlic-herb cheese toasts.

caption:
A simple curry that tastes like childhood

Ingredients
1 cup black eyed beans (alsande), soaked and boiled with salt
1.5 cups freshly grated coconut
1 tsp coriander seeds
1 cherry sized tamarind
6–7 bedgi red chillies
0.5 tsp turmeric powder
1 cup water (for grinding)

For tadka
2 tbsp coconut oil
5–6 large garlic cloves, crushed

Method
Boil the black eyed beans with water and salt till soft and keep aside.
Grind coconut, coriander seeds, tamarind, bedgi chillies and turmeric with water to a smooth paste.
Add this masala to the boiled beans and let the curry simmer for about 5 minutes.
Heat coconut oil, fry crushed garlic till golden and pour this tadka over the curry.
Cover and let it rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot with steamed rice, pickle and papad.

caption:
Two ingredient cheesecake cups ✨

Recipe inspired by the viral trend in Japan 🥰

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Cheesecake Cups:▪️6 Full Graham Cracker Sheets▪️1+1/2 Cups Full-fat Plain Greek Yogurt▪️

Optional Toppings:▪️Fresh Berries▪️Honey or Maple Syrup▪️

1️⃣ Crush 2 full sheets of graham crackers and divide evenly between the bottoms of two small bowls or jars, pressing lightly to form the base.
2️⃣ Spoon the yogurt over the graham cracker base and spread into an even layer.
3️⃣ Break the remaining 4 graham cracker sheets into rectangles. Gently press them into the yogurt so they're mostly submerged, leaving a little space between each piece so there's a thin layer of yogurt in between.
4️⃣ Cover and refrigerate overnight to allow the crackers to soften and set into a cake-like texture.
5️⃣ Enjoy straight from the fridge. Add berries if using, and drizzle with a little honey or maple syrup if you prefer a sweeter finish (although we liked them as is!).

Recipe serves 2

⏲10 min prep time + overnight rest
summary: A two-ingredient no-bake cheesecake cups recipe made with graham crackers and Greek yogurt, inspired by a Japanese viral trend.

caption:
Crispy Chipotle Paneer Tacos 🌮🔥
Macros per taco: 

 •	Calories: ≈135 kcal
	•	Protein: ≈15 g
	•	Carbs: ≈13 g
	•	Fats: ≈4 g

There's something about cooking on the right cookware that completely changes the outcome—and these tacos are proof. I made them on my @embercookware ceramic pan, which heats evenly, gives that beautiful crisp without burning, and holds heat long enough to get that perfect golden crust ✨

The smoky chipotle-style sauce comes from charring the bell peppers and garlic, and Ember cookware really helps lock in that flavour while keeping the cooking oil minimal—exactly how I like my everyday meals.

Recipe ⬇️

Chipotle Sauce:
Char 1 red bell pepper and 7–8 garlic cloves in 2 tsp oil (covered) until soft and smoky.
Cool & blend with 100g low fat paneer, 3 tbsp Greek yogurt / hung curd, salt & a pinch of sugar.

Paneer Filling:
Mix 200g crumbled low fat paneer, 1 grated carrot, 1 chopped capsicum,
2 chopped green chillies, salt, black pepper & some cheese.

To Assemble:
Stuff the filling into leftover roti or paratha, press gently.
Cook on a lightly oiled Ember ceramic pan until crisp and evenly browned on both sides.
Spread chipotle sauce, add lettuce & serve fresh 🌮
#easy #healthy #protein #taco #weightloss
summary: High-protein crispy chipotle paneer tacos with charred pepper sauce and nutritional macros.

caption:
Chicken Mexican Pizza (Taco Bell–Style)
Ingredients (Makes 6 mini pizzas)
For the base
6 small flour tortillas
Cooking spray or oil
For the chicken
1½ cups cooked chicken, shredded or diced
1 tbsp taco seasoning
2 tbsp water
For assembly
1 cup refried beans
1 cup enchilada sauce or mild taco sauce
1½ cups shredded Mexican-blend cheese (cheddar & Monterey Jack)
For topping
1 cup diced tomatoes
½ cup chopped green onions
👩‍🍳 Instructions
Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C).
Lightly spray tortillas with oil and bake directly on the rack for 6–8 minutes, flipping once, until lightly crispy.
In a skillet, warm chicken with taco seasoning and water until coated and heated through.
Spread a thin layer of refried beans on each tortilla.
Add seasoned chicken, then spoon enchilada sauce over the top.
Sprinkle generously with shredded cheese.
Bake on a baking sheet for 8–10 minutes, until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from oven and immediately top with diced tomatoes and green onions.
Serve warm and enjoy!
🍽️ Serving Suggestions
Serve with sour cream, salsa, or guacamole on the side. These are perfect for family dinners, game nights, or Taco Tuesday at home.
summary: Recipe for homemade Taco Bell-style chicken Mexican pizzas made with tortillas, seasoned chicken, beans, enchilada sauce, and cheese.

caption:
Korean-style beef pockets - quick, easy and full of flavour. Thanks for the inspo @alexcookjoy 

250 g extra lean beef mince
1/4 cup soy sauce
1 tbsp brown sugar
1 tbsp sesame oil
1 tbsp rice wine vinegar
1 tsp grated ginger
1 clove garlic, minced
1/2 tbsp gochujang
3 large tortillas
200 g mozzarella, grated
1 spring onion, finely sliced
2 tsp chilli oil (optional)

1. Heat a pan over medium-high heat. Add beef mince and cook, breaking it up, until browned.
2. Add soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, rice wine vinegar, ginger, garlic, and gochujang. Stir and simmer for 3–5 minutes until glossy and slightly sticky. Remove from heat.
3. Preheat oven to 180C. Line a baking tray with baking paper.
4. Lightly dip tortillas in water if stiff, then cut each tortilla in half.
5. Spoon beef mixture into the centre of each tortilla half. Top with mozzarella and fold into triangles.
6. Arrange on tray, sprinkle with a little extra mozzarella, and bake for 20–25 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
7. Finish with spring onion and a drizzle of chilli oil before serving.
#easydinner #tortilla #koreanbeef

caption:
Crispy Tandoori Chicken & Chips🍗🍟🔥

Pure comfort in each bite - crispy juicy chicken paired with salt n pepper fries and a creamy homemade coleslaw. An incredible combo you have to try!🤌🏽

Macros Per Serving (5 Total)

572 Calories | 52g Protein | 54g Carbs | 16g Fat

Ingredients (Serves 5)

Tandoori Chicken
- 1100g Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, trim excess fat
- 2 Tsp Tandoori Masala (salt included)
- 1 Tsp Black Pepper
- 2 Tsp Smoked Paprika
- 2 Tsp Garlic Powder
- 1.5 Tsp Cumin
- 1.5 Tsp Coriander Powder
- 4 Tsp Olive Oil
- 80g Fat Free Yogurt
- 3/4 Lemon Juice

• Spread chicken thighs evenly on sheet pan, oven bake or air fry for 20-25 mins at 200C / 400F until golden brown and juicy
• Once cooked rest for 5 minutes before slicing into delicious cubes. Mix in all the resting juices! No flavor gets wasted🤌🏽

Crispy Fries
- 1100g Uncooked White or Yellow Potatoes, cut into fries
- 1.5 Tsp Black Pepper
- 1.5 Tsp Garlic Powder
- Avocado Oil Spray
- Salt to taste after cooking

• Season with garlic and pepper only then air fry for 22-27 mins until golden and crispy
• Once cooked sprinkle salt to taste. Best to add salt later to prevent moisture release while cooking

Creamy Homemade Coleslaw
- 300-400g Green Cabbage, thinly sliced
- 250g Carrots, grated
- 150g Red Onion, thinly sliced
- 2-3 Tsp Fresh Coriander, finely chopped
- 1/2 Tsp each Salt & Pepper
- 100g Fat Free Yogurt
- 80g Light Mayo
- 20g Honey
- 3/4 Lemon Juice

• Make this in batches, store separately and refrigerate to keep fresh and crisp
• Makes reheating easier. Reheat chicken and fries in air fryer for 5 mins at 190C

The creamiest chicken sandwich you'll ever try.

Ingredients:
• 2 tbsp oil
•	2 onions, finely chopped
•	Salt to taste
• ⁠½ tsp ginger paste
• ½ tsp garlic paste
•	3–4 green chillies, finely chopped
•	2 tomatoes, finely chopped

•	Panini bread loaf (or sandwich bread)

Method:
1.	Heat oil, sauté onions till golden with salt.
2.	Add ginger paste, garlic paste, and green chillies.
3.	Add tomatoes, turmeric, coriander powder, and chilli powder. Cook well.
4.	Add chicken, mix, then stir in coconut milk till creamy.
5.	Slice and butter the panini loaf. Spread the creamy chicken, top with lettuce, onions, and chaat masala.
6.	Cover with another slice, butter the outside, and grill on a butter-smeared tawa till golden.
7.	Serve hot with crispy chips.

caption:
Southwest chicken taco bowl meal prep🍴

If you know me, you know I could eat Mexican-inspired food every single day—and this week's meal prep did not disappoint. These Southwest chicken taco bowls are simple, so good, and the sauce is everything.

For the chicken & veggies:
•	Chicken thighs
•	Taco seasoning (I used store-bought from Trader Joe's)
•	Oil
•	Red onion
•	Red and green bell peppers

For the sauce:
•	1 cup Greek yogurt (or sour cream if you prefer)
•	1 cup salsa verde
•	1 tbsp lime juice
•	Handful of fresh cilantro

For assembling the bowls:
•	Jasmine rice
•	Mexican-style shredded cheese (or whatever cheese you like)

Instructions
1.	Preheat oven to 425°F. Season chicken thighs with taco seasoning and a drizzle of oil. Bake for ~25 minutes.
2.	Chop red onion and peppers. Toss with a little oil and taco seasoning. Roast in the air fryer at 375°F for 15–20 minutes (or until they're as crispy as you like).
3.	Make the sauce: blend Greek yogurt, salsa verde, lime juice, and cilantro until smooth.
4.	Assemble bowls: add jasmine rice, chicken, roasted veggies, shredded cheese, and drizzle (or drench) with the sauce.

Note: In this video, I only show three lunches for the aesthetic, but this meal actually made about eight lunches for my husband and me. How you portion it out totally depends on your own nutrition goals—this is just what works for us.

#mealpreprecipes #mealprepping #mealprepmonday #easyrecipe #easyrecipeideas #recipeideas
summary: Southwest chicken taco bowl meal prep recipe with seasoned baked chicken, roasted veggies, rice, and cilantro-lime yogurt sauce.

caption:
Loaded Paneer Sandwich 🥪 

Ingredients: ( makes 3-4 sandwiches )
6-8 slices of sourdough bread
Butter/olive oil for toasting
2-3 tbsp mustard sauce 
150 grams fresh malai paneer chopped into cubes
12-15 lettuce leaves
cheese slices ( as many as you like )
100 grams or 3/4 cup Greek yogurt 
3 heap tbsp fresh pesto
1 medium onion thinly sliced
1 avocado chopped
1/2 cup green olives sliced 
2 tsp pepper
Salt as per taste 
2-3 tsp lemon juice 

1. In a large mixing bowl, simply mix the yogurt, pesto and lemon juice. Then add the paneer, avocado, onions, and olives, along with salt and pepper and mix well until combined.

2. Toast the sourdough bread in butter or olive oil, then spread a generous layer of mustard on both the slices. Place a thick layer of lettuce leaves and cheese over the sourdough, followed by a generous amount of filling. Sandwich with the other slice of bread, and slice through the centre. Enjoy!

caption:
🔥 GREEK CHICKEN SALAD

Per serving: 295 calories, 44g protein, 3.5g net carbs
Total (5) servings: 1,470 calories, 221g protein, 18 net carbs

I've lost 140lbs+ eating high protein, lowcarb meals that taste incredible, and things like this easy chicken salad are a go-to.

This is best with pita bread or lavash, but I made it low carb over lettuce and it really hits the spot.
�Salad:
1.5 lb cooked diced chicken (rotisserie, leftover, are ok!)
1/2 English cucumber
1/2 red onion
1 bell pepper�Large handful fresh dill
Optional: black olives

Dressing:�1/2 cup thick Greek yogurt�2-4 tbsp mayo�Zest of 1 lemon�Juice of 1/2 lemon�2 tbsp fresh mint�2 tbsp fresh dill�1 garlic clove�Salt and pepper

To serve: lettuce leaves, bread, or crackers

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Add 1.5lb finely diced cooked chicken to a bowl. If you want to make chicken from scratch, grab 2lb chicken, drizzle with 1 tbsp avocado oil, the juice of half a lemon, season with 1 tbsp garlic powder,  1 tbsp sweet paprika, 1 tbsp oregano, and a couple pinches salt and pepper. Mix really well, & bake in the oven at 400F for 40 mins, or air fry it.

2️⃣ While the chicken is going, dice up your veggies. I finely chopped half an English cucumber, finely diced 1/2 red onion and 1 bell pepper, a large handful of fresh dill. I would have normally added black olives, but was out, but feel free to add if you like. 

3️⃣ Now, the best part, the sauce! In a small food processor process 1/2 cup of a thick Greek yogurt, 2-3 tbsp mayo, zest of a full lemon, the juice of half a lemon, 2 tbsp fresh mint, 2 tbsp fresh dill, 1 garlic clove and a couple pinches of salt and pepper.

4️⃣ Once the chicken is done & cooled, finely dice it, then fold it into the sauce along with the rest of the ingredients.

5️⃣ Serve it however you like!

✅Comment "PLAN" and I'll message you how to grab my high protein meal plan.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.

.

caption:
Growing up, none of us really thought about supplements.
But as adults, with changing lifestyles, stress, indoor living and nutrient gaps, the conversation naturally shifts.
That's exactly what this episode of Live Your Best Life, Unscripted reminded me of — food is always the foundation of good health, and supplementation simply helps fill in the gaps.

Full episode of Live Your Best Life, Unscripted on Herbalife India's YouTube channel - link pinned in comments. 

Recipe ⬇️
Low-Carb Chicken Noodle Soup

Serves: 2
Macros (per serving, approx):
~280 kcal | Protein ~32 g | Carbs ~14 g | Fat ~9 g

Ingredients
	•	1 tsp oil
	•	1 tbsp chopped ginger
	•	1 tbsp chopped garlic
	•	½ cup chopped onions
	•	300 g boneless chicken thigh
	•	½ cup shredded carrot
	•	1 small potato, finely chopped
	•	1–1½ tbsp soy sauce
	•	1 tsp chilli crisp / chilli oil / chilli flakes (to taste)
	•	Salt & black pepper, to taste
	•	1 chicken stock cube
	•	1 tsp vinegar
	•	Water, as needed
	•	1 pack shirataki noodles, washed & drained
	•	Spring onion greens, for garnish

Method
	1.	Heat oil in a pan. Add ginger, garlic and onions; sauté until fragrant.
	2.	Add chicken thigh and sear on both sides for a few minutes.
	3.	Add carrot, potato, soy sauce, chilli, salt, pepper, stock cube and vinegar.
	4.	Add water to cover, cover and cook until the chicken is tender.
	5.	Remove chicken, slice it, and return it to the soup if needed.
	6.	Place shirataki noodles in a bowl, pour hot soup over, top with sliced chicken.
	7.	Garnish with spring onion greens and serve hot.

⸻

#TheMindShift #LiveYourBestLifeUnscripted #herbalifeindia
summary: Low-carb chicken noodle soup recipe with nutritional macros and discussion about supplementation and health.

caption:
Chicken Shawarma sheet pan with tzatziki and tomato salad 

Marinate the chicken: 
1. 5-6 chicken thighs, boneless skin sliced thin 
2. 2 tablespoons plain Greek yogurt
3. 5-6 garlic minced 
4. 1 tablespoon tomato paste 
5. A tablespoon of olive oil
6. Half a lemon squeezed
7. 1 teaspoon chili powder, paprika or cayenne salt, black pepper, coriander powder, cumin powder, half a teaspoon turmeric
8. 1/2 onion sliced 
9. 1/2 red pepper sliced 

Simple tzatziki: mix 
1. 1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt 
2. Small cucumber shredded- remove water 
3. 1/2 teaspoon salt
4. Squeeze of lemon 
5. 1 tablespoon olive oil 

Tomato onion salad : mix 
1. 2 tomatoes cut into bite size pieces 
2. 1/2 onion sliced 
3. Parsley 
4. Optional if you have it : Heaping teaspoon sumac 
5. Salt , lemon juice and olive oil 

1. Cut chicken thighs into thin slices and seasoned with the marinate listed above. 
2. Leave the chicken out in a greased pan add some chopped onions and peppers if you have it. 
3. Drizzle olive oil all over the chicken and bake at 425° for 20 minutes flipping halfway through. 
4. Make your Taziki refrigerate until ready to use. 
5. Make your tomato onion salad.

caption:
One Pan Harissa Meatballs & Coconut Rice Recipe

An easy weeknight meal perfect for the whole family, all made in one pan! Scroll down for nutritional info. This recipe serves 4. 

For the meatballs:
* 500g chicken mince
* 1 red onion, grated and excess liquid squeezed out
* 2 tbsp chopped coriander or parsley
* 2 tbsp harissa paste (adjust to taste)
* 2 tsp harissa seasoning (or substitute: 1 tsp paprika + ½ tsp cumin + 1 tsp garlic granules)
* 1 tsp salt, or to taste
* 2 tbsp breadcrumbs

For the rice:
* 2 cups (380-400g) basmati rice, washed and soaked for 30 minutes
* 3 tbsp olive oil
* 1 white onion, finely chopped
* 1 tbsp chopped coriander stalks
* 1 tbsp harissa paste
* 1 tsp salt, or to taste
* Zest and juice of 1 lime
* 1 red pepper, diced
* 400ml (1 can) coconut milk
* 100-125ml (½ cup) chicken stock

Method:
1. Add the grated and squeezed red onion to the chicken mince along with chopped coriander leaves, harissa paste, seasoning, salt, and breadcrumbs. Mix well, then cook a small piece to taste for seasoning and adjust if necessary. Form the mixture into 10-12 meatballs and set aside.
2. Wash and soak the basmati rice for 30 minutes before cooking, then drain.
3. In a large pan, heat the oil and sear the meatballs on all sides until golden. Remove and set aside.
4. In the same pan, add the chopped white onion and sauté until slightly translucent. Add the coriander stalks, harissa paste and lime zest. Sauté for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
5. Add the drained rice and diced red pepper to the pan and stir to combine. Toast the rice for 2-3 minutes over the heat. [method continued in the comments] 

Approx per serving(4): 530 cals, 25g protein, 4g fibre 
How you can make this meal more nutritious - add a can of drained chickpeas for an extra 6g fibre per serving, serve with a side salad and a dollop of Greek yoghurt for even more fibre and protein.
summary: One-pan harissa chicken meatballs with coconut basmati rice for four servings.

caption:
GRANDMA'S LONGEVITY SOUP ✨ my grandma is 82 and THRIVINNNG and this is one of her go to soup recipes that she loves to meal prep for longevity and optimal wellbeing 🤩follow @lainiecooks_ for more recipes like this! 

Ingredients: 
* 2 cups carrots, chopped
* 2 cups celery ribs, thinly sliced
* 2 zucchini, diced 
* 1 white onion, chopped 
* 3 garlic cloves, grated
* 1 15 oz can fire roasted diced tomatoes 
* 1 can cannellini white beans or kidney beans, drained and rinsed
* 2 cups chopped green beans
* 4 cups vegetable broth (or chicken broth) 
* 2 bay leaves
* 1/2 tbsp dried oregano & dried basil
* 1/2 cup small pasta, elbows, shells, orecchiette
* 1 tbsp dried or fresh parsley
* Red pepper flakes
* 1 teaspoon sea salt, plus more to taste
* Freshly ground black pepper
* Grated Parmesan cheese, optional, for serving

Instructions: 
* Heat the oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the onion, big pinch of salt and saute one minute. Add carrots, celery, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 3 minutes, until the vegetables begin to soften.

* Add the garlic, tomatoes, beans, green beans and zucchini and cook for 3-5 minutes. Add in broth, bay leaves, oregano, and dried parsley or basil. Cover and simmer for 20 minutes.

* Stir in the pasta and cook, uncovered, for 10 more minutes, until the pasta is cooked through.

* Season to taste and serve with parsley, red pepper flakes, and grated parmesan.
summary: A vegetable and pasta soup recipe featuring carrots, celery, zucchini, beans, and tomatoes, credited to the poster's 82-year-old grandmother.

caption:
Full recipe below! You can never go wrong making these White Chicken Enchiladas for dinner🤤 Can't wait for you guys to give these a try! 

#chickenenchiladas #texmex #dinnerideas #easyrecipe #cookingathome 

Ingredients
1 tbsp olive oil 
3 bell peppers, diced
1 onion, diced 
2 ½ cups chicken broth 
1 ½ cups heavy cream
2 cups shredded chicken
4 tbsp butter
4 tbsp flour
3 cups shredded Monterey cheese
¾ cup sour cream
8-12 tortillas 
1 tbsp paprika
1 tbsp chili powder
Salt & pepper
1/2 cup cilantro

Instructions
Add peppers and onions to a large oiled skillet. Cook until softened.
Add in 1 cup chicken broth & 1 cup heavy cream. Season with pepper and chili powder. Add in shredded chicken, stir, and set to the side.
Grab same pan for the sauce. Melt butter, add in flour, and cook down for a couple of minutes. Slowly add in remaining chicken broth and heavy cream. Once well mixed, add in 1 ½ cups of cheese & let completely melt in sauce. Season with paprika, salt and pepper.
Grab a casserole dish. To each tortilla, add the chicken and pepper mixture. Roll up & place each one into the casserole dish.
Pour the sauce over the top of the enchiladas. Sprinkle remaining cheese on top along with fresh cilantro. 
Bake on 350° for 30 minutes & broil last minute.
summary: White chicken enchiladas with creamy cheese sauce, sautéed peppers and onions, baked until golden.

caption:
High Protein Broccoli Feta Soup 🥦

If you thought soup couldn't be high protein and taste incredible, you've got to try this!

Full recipe: 985 calories and 79g protein
Each serving (4): 246 calories, 19.5g protein, 10g carbs

✅Comment "SLURP" and I'll send you a free ebook with my 3 favorite high protein soups.

This protein packed broccoli feta soup is as good as it gets. It's got roasted broccoli and feta two of my favorite protein hacks: some creamy cottage cheese and bone broth.

Its great for an easy chilly night, a great healthy meal prep option, and a lunch or dinner that doesn't leave you feeling heavy.

Soup Ingredients:

5 cups (450g) broccoli florets, cut small so they roast evenly
2 cups of baby spinach
1 small quartered yellow onion
4 garlic cloves
1-2 tbsp olive oil
3 cups bone broth
1/2 cup cottage cheese
6oz feta cheese
The zest and juice of 1/2 lemon 
1 tsp dried oregano
1 tsp chili flakes
Salt and pepper to taste
Garnish: Fresh dill & finely shredded manchego cheese

Here is how I made t:

1️⃣ Preheat your oven to 415°F.

2️⃣ Cut your broccoli into smaller florets so they cook faster and roast up evenly. Dont use large chunks.

3️⃣ Toss your broccoli into an oven safe dish along with your quarterly onion, and fresh garlic. Drizzle with olive oil, add your oregano, chili flakes, salt, and pepper. Place your feta in the corner. Roast for 30 mins until roasted and slightly crispy. Time varies by oven, so keep an eye!

4️⃣ Add your roasted veggies to a blender with the bone broth, cottage cheese, baby spinach, and lemon zest, and juice. Blend everything until smooth and creamy.

5️⃣ I its cooled down, warm it up in in a pot, then taste and adjust to make sure it has enough salt and lemon juice.

Plate it up, and garnish with fresh dill, chili flakes, and finely shredded manchego or parmesan cheese to bring out the flavors.

>Comment "SLURP" and I'll send you a free ebook with my 3 favorite high protein soups.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS! 

Enjoy!

caption:
Mom's shrimp fried rice✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

▪️4 tsp Avocado Oil▪️8 oz. Small or Medium Shrimp, peeled and deveined▪️4 Eggs▪️Kosher Salt & Pepper▪️1 Medium Carrot, peeled and diced small▪️2 Cups Napa Cabbage, sliced in thin 1" strips▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️4 Cups Cooked Jasmine Rice (day old rice is best!)▪️2 Tbsp Sesame Seeds▪️1 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️4 Tbsp Butter, divided▪️1 Tablespoon Sesame Oil▪️3 Scallions, chopped▪️

1️⃣ Season both sides of the shrimp with salt and pepper.
2️⃣ In a small bowl, whisk the eggs with salt and pepper.
3️⃣ In a wok or large pan over medium-high heat, add 2 tsp avocado oil and the shrimp. Spread the shrimp into one layer and cook for about 2 mins per side until pink and golden brown around the edges. Remove from the wok. 
4️⃣ Add 2 tsp more oil and the eggs. Cook, stirring, for 1-2 mins until scrambled, and then remove from the wok and set aside. 
5️⃣ Add 2 tbsp of the butter and the carrots. Cook for 1-2 mins until they're starting to become tender, then add the cabbage and garlic. Season with 1 tsp kosher salt and 1 tsp pepper and cook, stirring, for 3-4 mins until almost tender. Add the rice and mix it with the veggies. Add the soy sauce and sesame seeds and continue cooking until everything is combined (2-3 more mins). 
6️⃣ Create a small well in the middle of the rice and add the remaining 2 Tbsp of butter in the well. Drizzle the sesame oil around the outer edge of the rice, and let the rice fry, undisturbed, until golden brown and crispy, 2 to 3 mins. Stir the rice to mix up the buttery pieces. Cook, tossing once or twice, until there are more crispy bits, about 2 minutes.
7️⃣ Add the scrambled eggs and mix, breaking up into smaller pieces with a spatula. Add the scallions and shrimp and cook, tossing two or three times until combined, 1-2 mins. Taste and adjust seasoning if desired (we added 1/2 tsp more kosher salt, so 1+1/2 tsp total). Enjoy!

⏲15 min prep time / 40 min total time

Recipe serves 6 as a side dish or 4 as a main dish
summary: Step-by-step recipe for mom's shrimp fried rice with shrimp, eggs, vegetables, and crispy rice.

caption:
505 Kcal with 47 g of Protein 💪🏼🔥
Juicy chicken cooked till golden and finished in a light, spiced cream sauce — creamy, spicy, comforting and restaurant-style without being heavy.

Creamy Afghani-Alfredo Style Chicken Skillet
Makes 4 Servings. Macros (per serving) ~505 kcal | Protein ~47 g | Carbs ~15 g | Fat ~30 g
Ingredients 
Chicken
	•	Chicken drumsticks + thighs (bone-in) – 1 kg
	•	Thick curd (yogurt) – 200 g
	•	Ginger-garlic paste – 1 tbsp
	•	Coriander stems, finely chopped – 2 tbsp
	•	Chilli flakes – 1 tsp
	•	Cumin powder – 1 tsp
	•	Coriander powder – 1½ tsp
	•	Garam masala – 1 tsp
	•	Black pepper – ¼ tsp
	•	Salt – 1½ tsp (or to taste)
	•	Vinegar – 1 tbsp
	•	Neutral oil – 1 tbsp
White sauce
	•	Light butter – 1 tbsp
	•	Onion, finely chopped – 1 medium
	•	Green chillies, finely chopped – 1–2
	•	Whole wheat atta or maida – 1 tbsp
	•	Cold skim milk – ¾ cup (180 ml)
	•	Processed cheese slice – ½ slice (≈10 g)
	•	Chilli flakes – ½ tsp
	•	Black pepper – ¼ tsp
	•	Salt – ¼ tsp (adjust to taste)
To finish
	•	coriander leaves, chopped – 1–2 tbsp
METHOD
1. Marinate the chicken
In a bowl, mid all ingredients for the marinade. Add chicken and mix well. Marinate for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
2. Cook the chicken -
	•	Pan: Heat oil in a wide pan, add chicken, cover and cook on medium heat, turning occasionally, until well browned and fully cooked; the curd marinade will reduce and form thick pan drippings.
	•	Air fryer: Air fry at 180°C for 18–22 minutes, flipping halfway.
	•	Oven: Bake at 200°C for 35–40 minutes, turning once.
Remove cooked chicken and keep aside.
3. Make the sauce
In the same pan, melt butter. Add onion and green chillies; sauté till soft and lightly golden. Add atta/maida and cook for 1 minute. Slowly whisk in cold milk till smooth. Add cheese, chilli flakes, pepper and salt; cook till lightly thick.
4. Finish
Add chicken back to the pan, coat well with the sauce and simmer for 2 minutes. Finish with chopped coriander.
Serve with roti, rice or salad.

.
(creamy chicken recipe, yoghurt chicken, white sauce chicken Indian style, high protein chicken, Afghani chicken, one pot chicken, high protein recipe, healthy comfort food)
.
#afghanichickenskillet
summary: A high-protein creamy Afghani-style chicken skillet recipe with yogurt-marinated chicken and light spiced cream sauce, 505 kcal and 47g protein per serving.

caption:
🥘 Mexican Black Bean Dump & Bake

Vegan • Gluten-Free • One Dish

This bold, saucy, and ridiculously easy dish is a busy weeknight dream come true!! Everything bakes together in one dish, then gets drizzled with a garlicky cashew cheeze that makes it taste better than takeout 😍

INGREDIENTS 💚
• 2 tbsp olive oil
• ¼ onion, diced
• 2–3 garlic cloves, minced
• 2 bell peppers, chopped
• 1 can black beans, drained
• 1 cup corn
• 1 cup rice, rinsed
• 2 tbsp chili powder
• 1 tsp paprika
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp onion powder
• 1 tsp oregano
• 3 tbsp tomato paste
• 1 cup salsa
• 2 cups veggie broth
• salt & pepper to taste

Cashew Cheeze
• 1 cup cashews, soaked in hot water for 10 minutes
• ½ cup fresh water
• 1 tbsp olive oil
• 3 tbsp nutritional yeast
• 1 tsp garlic powder
• 1 tsp onion powder
• Pinch of salt
.
DIRECTIONS ⭐️
1. Preheat oven to 375°F and grease a 9×13 dish.
2. Add everything for the dump & bake directly into the dish and stir well.
3. Cover tightly with foil and bake 45–65 minutes, until the rice is tender.
4. Blend all Cashew Cheeze ingredients until smooth.
5. Drizzle over the bake and serve hot. Enjoy!
.

caption:
💥VIRAL COSTCO CHICKEN

Would you try this??

This is how you can make the Viral Costco Rotisserie Chicken in a bag. I'll be honest, its actually an incredible easy meal, super delicious, and something you can prep in less than 5 minutes if you have cooked rice handy. Cooking the rice or chopping up the veggies is the hardest part. 

I made two substitutions to add protein and for ingredient quality: first, the Costco Rotisserie chicken has some less than ideal ingredients, so I went with a Whole Foods rotisserie chicken. Use what you like here. Next, to make this higher protein and low carb, I used my @kaizenfoodco high protein rice, but you can use regular rice, brown rice, quinoa or even couscous here.

Comment PLAN and I'll message you how to grab my 7 day high protein meal plan for the new year!

Here is how I made it:

1.Start with a Rotisserie chicken. Even though this is the Costco Rotisserie chicken, I went with a Whole Foods one for a better quality chicken. But use whatever rotisserie chicken you prefer. Remove from the bone, and dice up the chicken. Add it to the Rotisserie chicken bag.

2.Next, are the veggies. I finely diced 2 Persian cucumbers, 3 scallions (the white and green parts), and a large handful of cilantro. I like lots of cilantro. Add it on top of the chicken.

3.Next is your base. I cooked up a bag of my high protein lowcarb Kaizen rice, but use 2 cups of cooked rice or quinoa of your choice. Add cooked rice to the chicken and veggies.

4.Add 1 to 2 tbsp of soy sauce or Tamari, 2 tbsp of garlic chili crunch, and lots of toasted sesame seeds.

5.Close the top of the Rotisserie bag, give it a good shake, then give it a taste and adjust with more sauce, sesame seeds, or anything else you like.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS! This was inspired by @tiffy.cooks but if you are the original creator let me know so I can share credit!

As always, you can add any other veggies you like, like edamame, or avocado, so make any substitutions you like to make it how you like it.

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #highprotein #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #lowcarbrecipes
summary: A viral Costco rotisserie chicken bowl recipe made in a bag with rice, vegetables, and Asian-inspired seasonings.

caption:
The best recipes of 2025 - 5/10! 💫✨
These honey & lime chicken burrito bowls are so so nice & feel so fresh such a perfect meal to get back in to eating some healthier meals! They take just 20 minutes to make (yes actually) & would also be perfect for meal prep!
To make 2 bowls you will need:
4 boneless & skinless chicken thighs
Juice 1 lime
2 tsp minced garlic
3 tbsp honey
1 tsp smoked paprika
1/2 tsp cayenne
1 tsp chilli flakes
1 tsp coriander
Pinch salt & pepper
Add all the ingredients to a bowl & stir well until all the chicken is fully coated.
Fry off the chicken on a high heat for 5 mins then turn down the heat to medium turn over & cook for another 7 mins until cooked through.
I served mine alongside some rice, salad, guacamole, salsa, sour cream & tortilla chips.

caption:
Best Bean Burger 🍔 
The best burger you'll ever have is VEGAN and very very easy to make, so give it a try! 👇😋
RECIPE (3 big patties, 45min prep time):
Caramelized onions:
-1 big onion
Cut into thin slices and cook on medium heat in a pan with some oil until brown and caramelised (I often add a pinch of sugar to help that process).
Patties:
-1 can (220g) black beans (or kidney beans), drained and washed
-1 Tsp cumin
-1/2 Tsp smoked paprika
-1 Tsp dried oregano
-2 Tbsp soy sauce
-5 Tbsp breadcrumbs or chickpea flour
(-3 to 4 Tbsp water if the mix feels too dry)
Mash with a fork until it's a chunky mix and refrigerate while making the sauce.
Then shape into 3 big patties and fry in oil until golden brown.
Sauce:
-6 Tbsp vegan mayo
-2 Tbsp ketchup
-1 Tap mustard
-1 Tsp chopped pickles 
-salt to taste
Mix well.
Assemble everything on burger buns and enjoy.
-
Bester Bohnen-Burger 🍔
Der beste Burger, den du je essen wirst, ist VEGAN und super super einfach zu machen, also probier ihn unbedingt aus! 👇😋
REZEPT (3 große Patties, 45 Min. Vorbereitungszeit):
Karamellisierte Zwiebeln:
-1 große Zwiebel
In dünne Scheiben schneiden und bei mittlerer Hitze in einer Pfanne mit etwas Öl braten, bis sie braun und karamellisiert ist (ich gebe oft eine Prise Zucker dazu, um den Prozess zu unterstützen).
Patties:
-1 Dose (220 g) schwarze Bohnen (oder Kidneybohnen), abgetropft und gewaschen
-1 TL Kreuzkümmel
-1/2 TL geräuchertes Paprikapulver
-1 TL getrockneter Oregano
-2 EL Sojasauce
-5 EL Semmelbrösel oder Kichererbsenmehl
(-3 bis 4 EL Wasser, falls die Mischung zu trocken ist)
Mit einer Gabel zerdrücken, bis eine stückige Masse entsteht, und während der Zubereitung der Sauce kalt stellen.
Dann zu 3 großen Patties formen und in Öl goldbraun braten.
Sauce:
-6 EL vegane Mayo
-2 EL Ketchup
-1 TL Senf
-1 TL gehackte Gewürzgurken
-Salz nach Geschmack
Gut vermischen.
Alles auf Burger-Brötchen zusammensetzen und genießen. 😋
summary: A vegan black bean burger recipe with caramelized onions, homemade sauce, and detailed instructions for three patties.

caption:
High-Protein Chicken Tikka Masala 🔥

Makes 5 servings. Per serving:

430 calories, 57g protein with my @kaizenfoodco high-protein rice

OR 486 calories, 44g protein with regular rice

This healthier chicken tikka masala proves you can eat incredible food while still hitting your protein and eating healthy. I swapped in Greek yogurt for part of the cream, cut down on the oil, and its still so good. Perfect for high-protein meal prep or an easy healthy weeknight dinner.

➡️➡️Comment PLAN and I'll message you how to grab my 7-day high-protein meal plan.

INGREDIENTS

Chicken Marinade:
2 lb boneless skinless chicken 
1 tsp oil
1/2 cup nonfat Greek yogurt
Juice of 1/2 lemon
1 tbsp garam masala
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp ground cumin
1/2 tsp turmeric
1/2 tsp chili powder
1 tsp salt
1 tsp grated garlic
1 tsp grated ginger

Masala Sauce:
1 tsp oil
1 small to medium onion, finely diced
4 cloves garlic, minced
1 tbsp grated ginger
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 cup tomato sauce or crushed tomatoes
1 cup chicken stock
1 tbsp garam masala
1 tsp ground cumin
1 tsp paprika
1/2 tsp turmeric
1/2 tsp chili powder
Salt to taste

Creamy Finish
1/2 cup Greek yogurt
1/4 cup half and half or cream

To Serve:
1 bag Kaizen protein rice, cooked, OR 4 cups regular rice
Fresh cilantro & lemon juice

DIRECTIONS

1.Mix all marinade ingredients, coat chicken well, and marinate at least 4 hrs or overnight.

2.Heat a pan over medium heat, add chicken, and cook 12-14 mins until cooked through. Remove and set aside.

3.In the same pan, add onion and cook 3 to 5 minutes until soft and lightly golden. Add spices, then tomato paste, garlic, and ginger. Stir 1 to 2 mins.

4.Add the broth and scrape the bottom, add tomato sauce and simmer until thickened. Lower heat.

5.Slowly stir in yogurt and half and half on low heat, then return chicken to the pan. Cover and simmer 5-6mins.

6.Finish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lemon.

I served this over my Kaizen protein rice, but it's great with regular rice, quinoa, cauliflower rice, or high-protein naan.

➡️Comment PLAN for my high protein meal plan!

#highprotein #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes healthyrecipes
summary: High-protein chicken tikka masala recipe with 57g protein per serving using Greek yogurt instead of cream.

caption:
Kurkure Karele | Crispy Karela | A wonderful tea time snack 

These are so delicious and very simple to make. Just cut up karela and mix with spices, fry and it's done! 

Ingredients  
* 4 Karelas/bitter gourd (wash, dry and cut into 12 long pieces per karela) 
* 1t Salt  
* 2 Tbsp Cornstarch  
* 2 Tbsp Besan(gram flour) 
* 2 Tbsp Rice Flour  
* 1/2 tsp Cayenne Pepper  
* 1 tsp Dhania powder  
* 1/2 tsp Turmeric powder  
* 1/2 tsp Garam masala  
* 1/2 tsp Mango powder (amchur) 
* 1/2 tsp Kashmiri mirch  
* 1/2 tsp Chaat masala  
* 1/2 tsp Sugar  
* 1/2 tsp Black pepper  
* Salt to Taste  
* 1 tsp Ginger paste  
* Oil to fry  
Garnish lime juice and cilantro  
 
Method  
1) Mix salt to karela pieces nicely and keep it aside for 10 to 15 minutes.  
2) Squeeze the water out.  
3) Wash it nicely thrice and again squeeze the water out.  
4) Mix corn starch, besan, rice flour and the spices together.  
5) Add the mixture to Karela and add ginger paste too.  
6)Make sure that the dry mixture sticks to Karela pieces. Can add 2T of water if needed.  
7)Heat oil on medium high and fry Karela pieces a few at a time until light brown and crisp.  
8) Serve hot and squeeze lime juice and sprinkle chat masala and  cilantro.  
* Can reheat it in Air-fryer.
owner_name: From Ma's Kitchen - Authentic Indian Recipes
summary: Recipe for crispy fried bitter gourd (karela) seasoned with Indian spices, served as a tea-time snack.

caption:
High-Protein Tandoori Paneer Rice Bowl 🔥

Ingredients (1 Serving)

Tandoori Paneer
	•	Milky Mist High Protein Paneer – 200 g
	•	Thick curd – ½ cup
	•	Ginger-garlic paste – 1 tbsp
	•	Tandoori masala – 1½ tbsp
	•	Kashmiri red chilli powder – 1 tsp
	•	Turmeric – ¼ tsp
	•	Garam masala – ½ tsp
	•	Lemon juice – 1 tbsp
	•	Salt – to taste
	•	Mustard oil – 1 tsp (optional)

Seasoned Rice
	•	Steamed basmati rice – 100 g (cooked)
	•	Chilli flakes – to taste
	•	Oregano – to taste
	•	Fresh coriander – chopped

Hung Curd Side
	•	Hung curd – ½ cup
	•	Cucumber – chopped
	•	Chilli flakes – a pinch
	•	Lemon juice – to taste
	•	Salt – to taste

⸻

Method
	1.	Mix curd, spices, lemon juice, mustard oil, and salt. Add paneer and marinate for 30 minutes.
	2.	Cook on a gas stove using a griller, medium flame, turning until evenly charred.
*Alternatively: Air fryer at 180°C for 10–12 min or oven at *200°C for 15–18 min (grill mode at end).
	3.	Toss cooked rice with chilli flakes, oregano, and coriander.
	4.	Mix hung curd with cucumber, chilli flakes, lemon, and salt.
	5.	Assemble the bowl and serve hot.

Total Meal Macros
	•	Calories: ~520 kcal
	•	Protein: ~48–49 g
	•	Carbs: ~42–44 g
	•	Fat: ~15 g
summary: High-protein tandoori paneer rice bowl recipe with marinated paneer, seasoned basmati rice, and hung curd side.

caption:
Winter Wellness 🍅
There's something deeply comforting about soup in the wintertime. When the days are colder and the body craves warmth, soups become one of the easiest and most powerful ways to support gut health and overall wellness. They're gentle, hydrating, grounding, and full of nutrients your body can actually absorb.
This simple tomato soup is a gut-loving staple made with real, whole ingredients that work together to nourish digestion and support immunity during the colder months.
✨ Gut-Healthy Ingredients:
• Tomatoes – rich in antioxidants like lycopene that support inflammation balance
• Garlic – a natural prebiotic that feeds beneficial gut bacteria
• Onions – support digestion and gut diversity
• Bell peppers – vitamin C–rich and supportive for immune health
• Olive oil – healthy fats that help reduce inflammation and aid nutrient absorption
Slow-simmered soups are easier on the digestive system, making them perfect when your gut needs extra care. Warm foods help stimulate digestion, support the gut lining, and create a sense of comfort that's just as healing as the nutrients themselves. Recipe: 15 pear tomatoes, halved + 1 yellow onion, quartered + 1.5 bell peppers (red & yellow) + 2 whole garlic heads, halved + salt + EVOO
—-> Bake 35-40 minutes until golden. @ 200C/400F —> add 6-8: leaves fresh basil + squeeze out garlic —-> Blend —-> mix in 1/4-1/2 cup cream. Place in a pot and simmer on low for 20 minutes until warm. 
✨ Winter reminder: healing doesn't have to be complicated. Sometimes it's as simple as a warm bowl, real food, and slowing down.
👉 Gut health resources and wellness tools are linked in my bio and highlights to support your gut health and wellness journey. #TomatoSoup #soups #FoodAsMedicine #DigestiveHealth #guthealth
summary: A gut-friendly tomato soup recipe made with roasted tomatoes, garlic, peppers, and cream, designed to support digestion and wellness.

caption:
Without a doubt the most useful & delicious thing in my freezer 

Spinach & Feta Rolls

Makes 12 to 24 depending how you cut. I make a variety of sizes, smaller snack size and larger for light meals. Freezing & cooking instructions at the bottom. 

2 Sheets puff pastry 

Filling:
2 onion chopped 
350g frozen spinach or 400- 500g fresh 
1 tsp oregano & or mint 
Salt & Pepper 

400g Feta cheese 
120g grated Mozzarella 

2 x egg separated (white mixed into filling and yolk mixed with 1 sp milk for brushing

Sesame seeds

If you're cooking some immediately Preheat oven to 180C. 

Sauté onion for a few min, throw in frozen spinach plus 1/2 cup of water (helps the spinach defrost, cook & caramelise the onion rather then burning) (if you're using fresh spinach you can skip the water). With the pan on a high heat move spinach around while water evaporates until spinach is defrosted, sautéed and no liquid is left. Cool down while you prepare the cheese and egg (on a large plate is quickest) 
Crumble the feta really well, mix with grated mozzarella & mix in the egg whites. Add the spinach mixture & mix again. Roughly split in the mix into 4. 

Put the egg yolk in another little bowl for brushing. 

Start with one pastry sheet & cut it lengthways in half. Add 1/4 of the filling onto the pastry about an inch away from the edge. Roll the pastry over into a log tuck in any filling and then fully roll the pastry over so the seam is at the bottom. Use your hand to make it a cylinder shape. cut in half and then each half into 3, 4, 5 or 6 depending what size you like. Repeat with the other half of the pastry and do the same with the second sheet. If you're cooking some straight away add to a baking dish with parchment paper. Brush egg wash all the way down the sides. Sprinkle with sesame seeds. bake on a low shelf 180C 32-35 min.

For freezing add to a tray with parchment paper brush with the egg, add sesame seeds. (You can double layer them just put parchment between layers. Freeze for 2 hours till hard & in a sealed bag. 

From frozen: bake 200C for 5 min turn it down 180C bake another 28-32 min 

Enjoy🫶

#puffpastry #batchcooking #spinachrolls #feta #easyrecipes
summary: A recipe for spinach and feta cheese rolls made with puff pastry that can be batch-cooked and frozen for later.

caption:
310 Kcal, spicy, tangy, creamy — this Chatkara Chicken is ridiculously easy to make. And the flavour explosion when green chillies, curd and desi masalas come together! 🤌🏼🤌🏼🤌🏼Perfect with flaky parathas or even jeera rice.

Chatkara Chicken Recipe 
Macros (per serving | serves 4 | approx.):
~310 kcal | 28 g protein | 6 g carbs | 19 g fat

Ingredients

For marinating the chicken
	•	Boneless chicken thigh – 500 g, cubed
	•	Ginger-garlic paste – 1 tbsp
	•	Salt – to taste
	•	Black pepper – ½ tsp
	•	Lemon juice – 1 tbsp

For the curd masala
	•	Thick curd – ¾ cup
	•	Red chilli powder – 1 tsp
	•	Turmeric powder – ¼ tsp
	•	Coriander powder – 1½ tsp
	•	Black pepper powder – ½ tsp
	•	Garam masala – ¾ tsp
	•	Chilli flakes – ½ tsp
	•	Salt – to taste

For cooking
	•	Oil – 1 tbsp
	•	Lite butter – 1 tbsp
	•	Ginger juliennes – 1 tbsp
	•	Garlic juliennes – 1 tbsp
	•	Fresh green chillies – 6–8, thinly sliced

To finish
	•	Sugar – a small pinch
	•	Lemon juice – 1 tsp
	•	Chaat masala – ½ tsp
	•	Fresh coriander leaves – chopped
	•	Extra green chillies & ginger juliennes – for garnish

⸻

Method
	1.	Marinate chicken with ginger-garlic paste, salt, pepper and lemon juice for 10 minutes.
	2.	Whisk curd with all spice powders, chilli flakes and salt until smooth.
	3.	Heat oil and lite butter in a pan. Add ginger and garlic juliennes, sauté briefly.
	4.	Add marinated chicken and cook on medium-high heat for 3–4 minutes.
	5.	Add sliced green chillies, lower flame and mix in the whisked curd.
	6.	Cover and cook on low until oil separates, then uncover and cook till gravy thickens.
	7.	Adjust salt, add a tiny pinch of sugar. Crush the chicken lightly with the spatula. Finish with lemon juice, chaat masala, coriander, extra green chillies and ginger juliennes.
	8.	Serve hot with flaky paratha, naan or jeera rice.

⸻

(Chatkara Chicken, spicy chicken curry, green chilli chicken, curd based chicken gravy, dhaba style chicken, boneless chicken recipe, high protein Indian dinner, weeknight chicken recipe)

⸻

#ChatkaraChicken #SpicyChicken #IndianChickenRecipes
summary: Chatkara Chicken recipe with curd, green chillies, and desi masalas—spicy, tangy, and creamy at 310 kcal per serving.

caption:
PERSIAN CUCUMBER SALAD

This is without a doubt one of my favorite light & healthy salads - it's the perfect combo in each bite. A little crunchy, salty, creamy and it's SO good.

➡️✅Comment "CUCUMBER" below and I'll send you my free ebook with 3 of my favorite cucumber salads!

Here is how I made it:

1.Thinly slice or dice 5-6 Persian cucumbers. Add them to a mason jar or salad bowl.

2.Cube up a small avocado and add to the jar/bowl. You can always add more avocado, too.

3.Thinly slice 1-2 scallions (you'll both the white and green parts of this).

4.Finely chop a large handful fresh dill & fresh mint (this yield get you about 1/3 cup of each herb once chopped)

5.Add 2oz toasted and cooled walnuts (pecans or pistachios would be great too), 1.5 oz feta cheese, and 2 tbsp sumac for a nice citrusy touch.

6.Dress it up with the zest of a full lemon, the juice of 1/2 lemon, 3-4 tbsp of a GOOD olive oil, a couple pinches salt & pepper.

Give it a really good shake (or toss!) and enjoy.

If you try it, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

ENJOY.
.
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #ketorecipes #mediterraneanfood #healthyrecipes
summary: Persian cucumber salad recipe with avocado, feta, walnuts, fresh herbs, and lemon-olive oil dressing.

caption:
Sticky Korean bbq skewers & fried rice 
Chicken skewers

Can either comment "recipe" for it to be sent directly to you or save to try tomorrow for dinner! 

Skewers ingredients
- 2.5lbs chicken breast 
- 1/3 cup soy sauce
- 1/4 cup brown sugar
- 3 tablespoons rice vinegar
- 3 tbsp sesame oil
- 2 tsp freshly grated ginger
- 4 garlic cloves minced
- 2 tsp onion powder
- 1 tbsp gochujang
- 1 tsp black pepper

Skewer glaze 
- 3 tbsp honey
- 2 tbsp brown sugar
- 2 tbsp low sodium soy sauce 
- 1 tbsp gochujang
- 1/2 tsp ginger, mustard, white pepper
- 1 tsp sesame seeds and garlic powder 

Fried rice:
- 2 tbsp salted butter
- 2 tbsp sesame oil
- 1 c diced carrots
- 3/4 c diced onion
- 1/3-1/2 c diced green onion just green parts
- 3/4 c green peas
- 2 eggs
- 1/3 c egg whites
- 4-5 c day old cooked white rice
- Salt to taste 
- 1 tsp white pepper
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 3-4tbsp low sodium soy sauce 

Instructions: 
1. Finley slice chicken into one to 2 inch pieces. Add chicken and all marinated ingredients to a Ziploc bag and shake to cover completely. Let marinade in fridge for minimum of two hours but best overnight.
2. Skewer chicken tightly. Heat 3 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat in a large pan. Sear skewers on both sides for 5-7 minutes until carmelized and golden. Set aside
3. While cooking the skewers make the skewer glaze. Mix all ingredients together in a small bowl. 
4. In the same pan used to cook the skewers add butter and sesame oil. Add carrots and onion and cook until fragrant and softened about 7 to 10 minutes. 
5. Push vegetables to the side of your pan and pour in your whisked eggs and egg whites to fry. This will happen quickly.
6. Add peas. Stir all together. Add rice and mix together. Then press everything down in the pan and let crisp for 30-60s, stir, press again and repeat to fry and crisp the rice for 4-5 minutes. 
7. Add seasonings, soy sauce, and green onions and fry for 2-3 more minutes and it's done. 
8. Glaze skewers and serve hot with some lettuce leaves and or an Asian cabbage slaw 🤤
summary: Korean BBQ chicken skewers with sticky glaze and sesame fried rice recipe

caption:
Dal like you've never had before. 🍲 
This dal is inspired by savory mung bean stews from Indonesia and the Philippines — specifically Sayur Kacang Hijau (Indonesia) and Monggo Guisado (the Philippines).
Creamy coconut, deeply comforting, loaded with umami and perfect with rice 🍚

RECIPE

Southeast Asian–Inspired Coconut Dal
Macros (per serving, without rice | serves 4): ~230 kcal | 12 g protein | 30 g carbs | 8.5 g fat | 7–8 g fibre

Ingredients
	•	1 tsp neutral oil
	•	1 bay leaf
	•	1 tsp crushed ginger & 1 tbsp crushed garlic
	•	3 spring onions (whites), finely chopped
	•	1 medium onion, finely chopped
	•	1 medium tomato, finely chopped
	•	¾ cup masoor dal (washed & soaked 20–30 mins)
	•	2.5 tsp soy sauce or 1 tsp soy sauce + 1 tsp oyster sauce + 1/2 tap fish sauce
	•	½ cup coconut milk + extra for finishing
	•	1 stock cube (chicken / veg /mushroom/ zero-salt)
 • Chilli flakes/chilli Oil for spice 
	•	Water, as needed
	•	Salt, if required
	•	2 packed cups fresh spinach, chopped
	•	1/2 cup Spring onion greens, finely chopped
	•	Chilli oil, to finish

Method
	1.	Heat oil in a pot. Add bay leaf, ginger, garlic, spring onion whites, and onion.
Sauté for 2–3 minutes until soft and fragrant.
	2.	Add chopped tomatoes and cook until slightly jammy.
	3.	Add soaked masoor dal, soy sauce, fish sauce, oyster sauce and chilli flakes/oil. Mix well.
	4.	Add coconut milk, crumble in the stock cube, and pour in enough water to fully cover the dal.
	5.	Mix, cover, and cook on a low flame for ~15 minutes, until the dal is completely cooked.
	6.	Check seasoning. Add salt if necessary. Add spinach and spring onion greens, cook for 1 minute until just wilted.
	7.	Switch off the flame. Finish with a little more coconut milk and a drizzle of chilli oil.

Serve hot with steamed rice and a simple salad.

⸻

(Southeast Asian dal, coconut dal, lentil stew, masoor dal recipe, Sayur Kacang Hijau, Monggo Guisado, Filipino inspired, Indonesian inspired, umami dal, comfort food)

⸻

#CoconutDal #ComfortFood #RiceBowl
summary: A Southeast Asian-inspired coconut dal recipe combining Indonesian and Filipino flavors with creamy coconut milk and umami seasonings.

caption:
Chicken kofta with tzatziki and lemon dill rice✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Tzatziki: ▪️1/2 a Large Cucumber, grated▪️2 Cups 2% Greek Yogurt▪️1 Clove Garlic, grated or minced▪️1 Lemon, juice▪️1 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️

Chicken Kofta:▪️1 lb. Ground Chicken (96% lean, not ground breast)▪️1/2 Cup Yellow Onion, finely chopped▪️1/4 Cup Parsley, finely chopped▪️2 Tbsp Mint, finely chopped▪️3 Cloves Garlic, minced▪️1 Egg▪️1 Tbsp Coriander▪️1+1/2 tsp Cumin▪️1/2 tsp Cinnamon▪️1/4 tsp Cayenne Pepper▪️1 Pinch Cloves▪️1/4 tsp Black Pepper▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️Olive Oil, for cooking▪️

Lemon Dill Rice:▪️1 Cup Long Grain White Rice, uncooked▪️1+1/2 Cups Chicken Stock▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1 Clove Garlic, finely chopped▪️1 Medium Lemon, zest (~1 tsp)▪️2 Tbsp Fresh Dill, chopped▪️2 Tbsp Butter▪️

Veggies (for serving):▪️1/2 a Cucumber, sliced▪️1/4 of a Yellow Onion, sliced in strips▪️1 Tomato, sliced in thin wedges▪️ 

1️⃣ Mix together ingredients for the tzatziki and set aside in the fridge.
2️⃣ Wash the rice until the water runs clear, and then drain it with a fine mesh sieve. Transfer to a medium pot and add the chicken stock, salt, and garlic. Bring to a boil over high heat, then cover and simmer on low heat for 15 mins. Remove from heat and let rest, covered, for 10 mins. Stir in the lemon zest, dill, and butter.
3️⃣ Make the chicken kofta: Combine the ground chicken, onion, parsley, mint, garlic, egg, coriander, cumin, cinnamon, cayenne, cloves, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
4️⃣ Preheat a large nonstick skillet over medium heat and add 1 Tbsp olive oil. Place dollops of the chicken mixture (~1 heaping Tbsp each) into the pan and cook for 2-3 mins until browned. Flip and flatten slightly with a spatula, then cook another 2-3 mins until the internal temperature reaches 165F. Repeat with remaining chicken, adding more oil as needed.
5️⃣ Serve chicken kofta over lemon dill rice with tzatziki, and sliced veggies. Enjoy!

🕒 20 min prep time / 45 min total time

Serves 4
summary: A complete recipe for chicken kofta with tzatziki sauce and lemon dill rice, serving four in 45 minutes.

Cheesy Green Garlic Bread 

This is hands down the best garlic bread! Loaded with cheese and that bold green garlic flavour — perfect for sharing and guaranteed to disappear fast at any gathering😋

- ½ cup Green Garlic Bulb
- ½ cup Softened Butter
- ½ cup Green Garlic finely chopped
- 2 tbsp Green Chillies finely chopped
- 2 tbsp Coriander finely chopped
- Salt to taste
- 1 Subway Loaf Bread
- 1 cup Mixed Cheese
- Garnish with coriander

Method:

caption:
In Italy, lentils are eaten on New Year's Eve as they are considered a symbol of good fortune for the coming year 🍀

But I also love this comforting vegan dish all year round as it is a true feel-good recipe that's both delicious and very good for you. It's a great way to introduce more legumes into your diet and lentils are so nutritional, packed with plant-based protein, fibre, iron and minerals! 

I usually soak the lentils the night before or for at least 3-4 hours prior to cooking. It's not necessary as they cook relatively quickly but it makes them easier to digest. I recommend using brown or green lentils as I found them perfect for this dish and they cook in about 40 minutes. 

I have served the ragu with pasta but you can also serve it on its own as a lentil soup with some toasted garlic bread! 

Here are the ingredients 👇 Or you can find the full written recipe on my website (link in bio)

Makes 2 portions

1 large carrot
2 sticks of celery
1 large brown onion
1 tbsp tomato paste
400g tin of crushed peeled tomatoes or chopped tomatoes
3 sun-dried tomatoes
1 large garlic clove
1 sprig of rosemary
1 sprig of sage
2 bay leaves
100ml white wine
10g dry porcini mushroom
200g green or brown lentils
200g of linguine

#vegan #veganfood  #meatfree  #italianfood #lentils
summary: Italian vegan lentil ragù recipe served with pasta, highlighting lentils as a nutritious New Year's Eve tradition.

caption:
Mediterranean Chicken & Rice Bowl

I've lost over 140lbs and one of the things thats helped me best was eating satiating, high protein meals that are packed with flavor and adventure in each bite. I used my @kaizenfoodco high protein lowcarb rice here for 20grams of protein a serving, but you can use regular rice, quinoa, or cauliflower rice too.

✅Comment "POWER" if you want to grab my ebook with 10 high protein meals with 35g+ protein.

Here's how I made it:

1.Place 4 bone-in skin-on chicken thighs in a bowl. I like to keep the skin on during the cooking process to keep things juicy, then remove it before eating.

2.Drizzle with 1tsp olive oil, season with a couple generous pinches of salt and pepper.

3.In a small food processor, process 1 large handful of basil, 5 garlic cloves, the juice of 1 large lemon, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 tbsp dijon mustard, a couple generous pinches of salt,& pepper.

4.Add this mixture to the chicken, toss really well, then for best results let this sit for 2-6hrs to marinate. Or you can proceed to next step if you want to eat right away.

5.Preheat oven to 425F. Bring chicken out of the fridge and let sit for 30 mins to come up to room temp. Grab a cast iron pan, spray with avocado oil, place the chicken at the base along with some red onion cut into eighths. You can add any other veggie here, too

6.Pop into the oven for 37-40 mins. Time varies by oven and thickness of your chicken.

7.Make a simple salad by combining 1/2 super finely diced red onion, 2-3 diced Persian cucumbers, 1 diced green pepper, 3-4 tbsp freshly chopped parsley, 1oz feta, 1 tbsp olive oil, the juice of 1/2 lemon, 1.5 tsp sumac, a generous pinch of salt and pepper. Give it a good mix, taste, and adjust.

8.Make your favorite rice according to instructions. To keep this lowcarb and high protein, I used my kaizen low carb rice. Currently 25% off on the Kaizen site.

9.Remove chicken, top with 4 servings of rice, the salad, 4-5 tbsp thick yogurt, and top it all with sumac.

Comment "POWER" for my protein ebook. And if you make this, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!

.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #highprotein #healthyrecipes
summary: A high-protein Mediterranean chicken and rice bowl recipe with marinated bone-in thighs, fresh salad, and yogurt topping.

caption:
This palak bhuji is a simple vegetable to add to any meal. This is a perfect recipe for any beginner! 

Ingredients  
8 oz. Potatoes, peeled and cubed  
4 oz. Fresh spinach, cut up  
2 Tbsp Canola oil  
1/2 tsp Cumin seeds  
1/2 tsp Mustard seeds  
Pinch Hing 
1/2 cup Onions, finely chopped  
1 tsp Green Chillies  
1 tsp Ginger, chopped  
1/4 tsp Turmeric powder  
1/2 tsp Cumin powder  
1/2 tsp Coriander powder  
Salt to taste  
Pinch of sugar 
1/4 tsp Mango powder  
1/4 tsp Chat masala  
 
Method  
 
* Heat oil on medium heat, add cumin seeds, mustard seeds & Hing.  
* Add onions, ginger, green chilies and stir until little limp.  
* Add turmeric, cumin powder and coriander powder.  
* Add potatoes, stir, cover and cook until potatoes are 3/4 done. Keep stirring in between.  
* Add spinach and salt and a pinch of sugar. Cover and cook until done.  
* Sprinkle chat masala and mango powder & mix.

caption:
41 g Protein & 305 KCal per Serving. 💪🏼
Iron-rich, antioxidant-packed red amaranth paired with high-quality protein from prawns — a simple, seasonal combo that's light, nourishing, and full of flavour.

This Bengali-style Prawn Lal Shak uses minimal mustard oil, gentle spices, and lots of greens — proof that traditional food can be both comforting and macro-friendly.

Chingri Lal Shak Recipe

Servings: 2
Macros (per serving): ~305 kcal | 41 g protein | 10–11 g carbs | 14–15 g fat | 3.5–4 g fibre

Ingredients
	•	Cleaned prawns – 400 g
	•	Mustard oil – 1.5 tbsp (total)
	•	Lal shak (red amaranth leaves) – ~200 g, cleaned & chopped
	•	Onion or shallots – 1 small, sliced
	•	Garlic – 6–8 cloves, sliced
	•	Dry red chilli – 1–2, sliced
	•	Green chilli – 1, slit
	•	Kalonji (nigella seeds) – ½ tsp
	•	Turmeric powder – ½ tsp + a pinch for prawns
	•	Coriander powder – ½ tsp
	•	Red chilli powder – to taste
	•	Salt & pepper – to taste
	•	Grated coconut – 2 tbsp
	•	Fresh coriander leaves – for finishing

Method
	1.	Marinate prawns with turmeric, coriander powder, salt, and pepper.
	2.	Heat a little mustard oil and quickly cook the prawns until just done. Remove and set aside.
	3.	In the same pan, add remaining mustard oil. Add kalonji seeds.
	4.	Add garlic, dry red chilli, and green chilli; sauté until aromatic.
	5.	Add sliced onion/shallots and cook until soft.
	6.	Add chopped lal shak, turmeric, chilli powder, and salt. Cook until wilted.
	7.	Add cooked prawns back in, mix gently, and cook for 1–2 minutes.
	8.	Finish with grated coconut and fresh coriander. 
OPTIONAL - you can also mix in a spoon of Kasundi or mustard sauce at the end for some extra oomph. 

⸻

(Bengali prawn recipe, lal shak recipe, red amaranth leaves, mustard oil cooking, high protein Indian meals, seasonal vegetables, gut friendly, healthy Indian recipes, local food)

.
#indianrecipes #seasonalvegetables #healthyindianfood
summary: Bengali-style prawn and red amaranth curry (Chingri Lal Shak) with 41g protein per serving, using mustard oil and traditional spices.

caption:
These seekh kebabs are so simple and delicious and I loved making them with my grandson🤍 He is so excited to make them for his college roommates! 

Watch to the end to see the taste tester sneak a bite😉

Ingredients  
* 1 lb. Ground chicken  
* 2 Tbsp Onion, finely chopped  
* 1 Tbsp Ginger and Garlic  finely chopped 
* 2 Green chilies, finely chopped  
* 1 tsp Mint, finely chopped  
* 2 Tbsp Cilantro, finely chopped  
* 1 tsp Ground cashews  
* 1 tsp Gram flour  
* 1 tsp Black pepper  
* 1/2 tsp Kasoori methi( Fenugreek leaves, dry) 
* 2 Tbsp Green cardamom, ground  
* 1 tsp Garam Masala  
* 1 Tbsp Shan Seikh kabab masala 
* 1 tsp Lime Juice  
* 1 Tbsp Oil or butter or ghee  
* 1 1/2 tsp Salt  
* 1/4 cup Bread crumbs  
 
Method  
* Soak wooden skewers in water for 15 minutes.  
* Garnish - 1 slices red onion mixed with 1/2 tsp lime juice + 1 Tbsp cilantro, pinch of salt  
* Mix everything together and keep it in the fridge for 30 mins.  
* Put it on skewers  
* Heat 1T oil in a frying pan on medium heat. Put the kebab skewers on it and cook it on each side until light brown.

Ingredients:

	•	Salt to taste

	•	1 tsp coriander powder

	•	1 tsp kasuri methi (crushed)
	•	1 tbsp green chillies, finely chopped
	•	2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves
	•	Juice of ½ lemon slice
	•	3 tbsp oil

Method:
	1.	Add all the ingredients to a bowl and mix well.
	2.	Add chicken pieces and coat evenly.
	3.	Cover and marinate for 2–3 hours (overnight for best flavour).
	4.	Give dhungar (smoke) using hot charcoal and ghee.
	5.	Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers.
	6.	Grill in a preheated oven or airfryer at 160°C for 25–30 minutes, turning once in between.
	7.	Serve hot with mint chutney and lemon wedges.

caption:
tofu manchurian 🌶️🥡😋 let me know what else can i "manchurianize"? find the recipe for this dish below!👇🏽 

[INGREDIENTS]
14 oz extra firm tofu, pressed and cubed 
½ cup cornstarch 
½ cup rice flour 
1 teaspoon garlic powder 
½ teaspoon ground black pepper
1 teaspoon red chili powder 
1 teaspoon salt 
Oil for frying 

Sauce
1 tablespoon cooking oil 
4-5 cloves of garlic, minced
2 teaspoons chopped green onion 
1 teaspoon minced green chili
2 teaspoons finely grated ginger 
2 tablespoons tomato ketchup
2 teaspoons red chili sauce 
1 teaspoon soy sauce
1 teaspoon white vinegar 
1 teaspoon sugar 
Salt to taste 
Cornstarch slurry: 2 teaspoons cornstarch + ½ cup water 

[DIRECTIONS]
1. Prepare the tofu: wrap the block of tofu in a bundle of paper towels and place something heavy on top for about 10 minutes to press out all the excess water. Cube in equal small pieces and set aside. 
2. Make the coating: mix together the cornstarch, rice flour, and spices. Toss in the cubed tofu and mix until all the pieces of tofu are completely and evenly coated with the flour/spice mixture. 
3. Drop 7-8 tofu pieces into hot oil and fry until golden brown. Take out of the oil and place on a paper towel lined plate to drain any excess oil. 
4. Make the sauce: In a large pan, heat up one tablespoon of oil. Add in the garlic, chili, ginger and sizzle for a minute. Add in the rest of the ingredients for the sauce and let the sauce simmer and slightly thicken. 
5. Toss in the fried pieces of tofu and coat thoroughly. 
6. Topped with chopped green onions and/or cilantro and sesame seeds, and enjoy!!

#tofumanchurian #indochinese #homecooking #vegetariancooking
summary: Full recipe and instructions for making crispy fried tofu manchurian with a savory garlic-ginger sauce.

caption:
Sheet Pan Chicken Kabob Wraps

Forget diet food that tastes like sacrifice! ➡️➡️Comment "POWER" & I'll send you the link to grab my protein book.

These are so easy and so good, the perfect easy weeknight meal and perfect for a healthy meal prep.

Ingredients:

4-6 Lowcarb Lavash, Pita, or Bread of choice
2 lbs ground chicken
1 small red onion
3-4 garlic cloves
Large handful fresh parsley
1 jalapeño or green chili
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp sumac
1 tsp chili flakes
1/2 tsp ground cumin
1 tbsp smoked paprika
1/4 tsp turmeric
Salt & Pepper
1 tbsp olive oil

Here is how I made it:

1️⃣ Preheat your oven to 425°F.

2️⃣ Grab a small food processor and process 1 small red onion, 3-4 garlic cloves, and a handful of fresh parsley.

3️⃣ Add 2lbs ground chicken to a bowl, the onion/garlic/parsley mixture, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 finely chopped deseeded chili pepper, and these spices: 1 tbsp sumac, 1 tsp Aleppo pepper or chili flakes, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1/4 tsp turmeric, and season with salt and pepper. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil. 

4️⃣ Mix until evenly mixed. Add to a sheet pan, and spread out evenly across the bottom.

5️⃣ Run a knife along the edges to smooth out the edges, and cut long vertical lines through the chicken to make single kabobs with space between them.

6️⃣ Bake for 18-20 mins. Then Broil for 3 mins.

7️⃣For the yogurt spread/sauce: mix ½ cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1-2 small grated garlic clove,  1 tbsp fresh dill, and a pinch of salt. Stir until smooth & creamy.

8️⃣ For the salad: Combine 1 diced cucumber, 1 chopped tomato, thinly sliced red onion, a handful of chopped parsley or dill, the juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 tsp sumac, salt, and pepper mix well.

9️⃣I used a lowcarb wrap, you can use lavash, pita, tortilla, or any regular type of bread depending on your goals. You can also use my high protein lowcarb Kaizen rice or regular rice, too!

Assembled with your bread of choice, spread some spread, add lettuce, 2 chicken skewers, salad, and top with more sumac and fresh parsley.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS

ENJOY!
.
.
.
#lowcarb #highproteinmeals #lowcarbrecipes #mediterraneanfood #healthyrecipes
summary: Sheet pan chicken kabob wraps with Mediterranean spices, yogurt sauce, and fresh salad.

caption:
New York Times Top 50 Recipes of 2025 | Chicken Stew by @nytcooking @grossypelosi

Ingredients:

• 2 tbsp each butter & olive oil

• 1 medium onion, diced

• 2 carrots, diced

• 3 celery stalks, diced

• 4 garlic cloves, minced

• ½ tsp sweet paprika

• Salt & black pepper

• 3 tbsp all-purpose flour

• 4 cups chicken broth

• 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar

• 1½ lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs

• 1 lb baby potatoes, quartered

• 1 cup green beans

• ½ cup heavy cream

• ½ tsp each dried sage, thyme & oregano

• Parsley & lemon wedges, to serve

Heat butter and olive oil in a large pot over medium, then cook the onion, carrots, celery, garlic, paprika, and a big pinch of salt until softened. Stir in the flour and cook briefly, then add the chicken broth and vinegar, stirring until smooth. Add the chicken thighs, potatoes, green beans, cream, sage, thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook partially covered until the chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes. Remove and shred the chicken, fully cover the pot and cook the vegetables until tender, then return the chicken, season to taste, and serve warm with parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
summary: New York Times Top 50 Recipes of 2025 featuring a creamy chicken stew with potatoes, vegetables, and herbs.

caption:
Top 3 Recipes of 2025: Sheet Pan Chicken Kefta Wraps 🔥

For all of you asking - the app link for Osta is in my bio or you can search "Osta Recipes" on the App Store or Google Play store ‼️🫶

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the  full recipe card for free!!

For the Chicken Kefta:
-1.5–2 lbs ground chicken (700-900 g)
-2 tbsp tomato paste
-8 garlic cloves, minced
-2 tsp smoked paprika, 2 tsp cumin, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp garlic powder
-1/3 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1/3 cup fresh cilantro, chopped (optional)
-1 large or 2 medium yellow onions, grated + squeezed dry*
-On top before baking: 4 tbsp olive oil
For the Roasted Garlic:
-2 garlic heads, 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp salt
For the Fresh Salad:
-1/2 large red onion, thinly sliced
-1 cup cherry tomatoes, quartered
-1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1 tsp salt, 1 tsp pepper & 2 tsp sumac
-Juice of 1/2 lemon
Roasted Garlic Paprika Sauce:
-Roasted garlic cloves, 1/4 cup mayo, 1/4 cup Greek yogurt, juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 tsp paprika + 1 tsp pepper

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Slice the tops off two garlic heads, drizzle with olive oil and salt, wrap in foil, and roast for 45–60 minutes until golden and soft. While the garlic roasts, prep the kefta.
2. Grate the onion, squeeze out all liquid with paper towel or cheesecloth. (This prevents watery kefta.) In a large bowl, combine chicken, tomato paste, minced garlic, spices, parsley, cilantro (if using), and the grated onion. Mix with your hands for 2–3 minutes until sticky and well combined.
3. Spread onto the sheet pan in a 1/2-inch (1.25 cm) thick rectangle. With a knife, score into 1-inch strips (2.5 cm). Drizzle evenly with 4 tbsp olive oil.
4. Bake for 12–15 minutes, then broil 2–5 minutes until golden and slightly crisp on top.
5. While baking, toss onion, tomatoes, parsley, salt, pepper, sumac, and lemon juice in a bowl.
6. Squeeze roasted garlic cloves into a bowl, mash, then mix with mayo, yogurt, lemon juice, paprika, and pepper until smooth.
7. On lavash or pita, slather sauce, add 1–2 strips of chicken kefta, top with salad. Roll tight. For extra magic, crisp the wrap in a lightly oiled skillet for 1-2 mins per side.
summary: Sheet pan chicken kefta wraps with roasted garlic sauce and fresh salad.

caption:
Best dish of 2025? 🤯

This dish just got voted the WORLD'S BEST DISH of this year… so surely I gotta try it!😱
It's called "vori vori" a Paraguayan cornmeal-dumpling soup and I have to say especially for the simplicity of this dish, it's REALLY GOOD and I'm definitely gonna make it again!🤞
RECIPE vori vori-vegan version (3 servings, 45min prep time):
-1 big carrot
-300g pumpkin
-3 potatoes
-1 leek
Chop everything and sauté in oil on medium heat for 5-10mins.
-1 Tsp ground coriander
-1 Tsp ground cumin
-1 Tsp dried oregano
-2 to 3 bay leaves
-salt to taste
-600ml water 
Add everything and let it simmer for 15-20mins.
Cornmeal dumplings:
-90g corn flour (NOT STARCH)
-70-80ml of the broth
Mix to combine until it forms a dough.
Shape into small balls.
Add the dumplings to the soup (add some more water if needed).
Let them simmer on medium low heat for 10mins.
Season the soup on more time to taste.
Serve with fresh lime, cilantro and chili flakes. Yummmm 😋 
-
Bestes Gericht 2025? 🤯
Dieses Gericht wurde gerade zum BESTEN GERICHT DER WELT dieses Jahres gewählt… also musste ich es natürlich probieren!😱
Es heißt "vori vori", eine paraguayische Maismehl-Klößchensuppe, und ich muss sagen: Gerade für die Einfachheit dieses Gerichts ist es WIRKLICH RICHTIG GUT und ich werde es definitiv wieder machen!🤞
REZEPT vori vori – vegane Version (3 Portionen, 45 Min. Vorbereitungszeit):
-1 große Karotte
-300g Kürbis
-3 Kartoffeln
-1 Lauch
Alles klein schneiden und in Öl bei mittlerer Hitze 5–10 Min. anbraten.
-1 TL gemahlener Koriander
-1 TL gemahlener Kreuzkümmel
-1 TL getrockneter Oregano
-2 bis 3 Lorbeerblätter
-Salz nach Geschmack
-600ml Wasser
Alles dazugeben und 15–20 Min. köcheln lassen.
Maismehl-Klößchen:
-90g Maismehl (KEINE STÄRKE)
-70–80ml von der Brühe
Alles vermengen, bis ein Teig entsteht.
Zu kleinen Kugeln formen.
Die Klößchen in die Suppe geben (bei Bedarf noch etwas Wasser hinzufügen).
Bei niedriger bis mittlerer Hitze 10 Min. ziehen lassen.
Die Suppe ein letztes Mal abschmecken.
Mit frischer Limette, Koriander und Chiliflocken servieren. Yummmm 😋
summary: Vegan recipe for vori vori, a Paraguayan cornmeal-dumpling soup with vegetables, described as the world's best dish of 2025.

caption:
Comment "Recipe" and I'll send the recipe right to your DMs! 😊🙌

$0.80 for a side of Creamy Jalapeño Sauce from @tacobell ? Not in my house! 😂 Now you can make your favorite quesadillas at home for a fraction of the cost, enjoyyyyy! ❤️

Full recipe is below or you can check it out on my profile! 

🧾 Ingredients 🧾

One 12 ounce jar pickled jalapeño slices (I am using Mt. Olive)
1 teaspoon paprika
1/2 teaspoon ground cumin
1 teaspoon garlic powder
1 teaspoon onion powder
1 teaspoon chili powder
1 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper, or 1/2 teaspoon for a spicier sauce
1/2 cup mayonnaise (I am using @dukes_mayonnaise , of course)
1/2 cup sour cream

👨‍🍳 Instructions 👨‍🍳

1) Add the entire jar of pickled jalapeño slices, including the liquid, to a blender. Blend until completely smooth, scraping down the sides as needed. The mixture should look like a thin, pale green puree with no visible chunks.
2) In a mixing bowl, combine the mayonnaise, sour cream, paprika, cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, chili powder, cayenne pepper, and salt. Stir until the mixture is completely smooth and evenly seasoned.
3) Add seven tablespoons of the blended jalapeño puree to the bowl and mix until fully incorporated. Start with seven tablespoons, then taste and add more puree if desired. Adding too much can make the sauce overly vinegary or too spicy, so increase gradually.
4) Transfer the sauce to an airtight container and refrigerate for at least one hour before using. This resting time allows the flavors to fully develop. The sauce will keep in the refrigerator for up to four days.

#TacoBellDupe #DishwithDrew #CopycatRecipe #FastFoodHack #CreamyJalapenoSauce
summary: Homemade copycat recipe for Taco Bell's Creamy Jalapeño Sauce made with pickled jalapeños, mayo, and sour cream.

caption:
ven pongal (recipe below)👇🏻 

Ingredients

1 cup moong dal
1 cup rice
Salt
Water

3 tbsp ghee
1 tsp cumin seeds
1 tsp whole black peppercorns
1/4 tsp hing
1 tbsp finely minced ginger
5–6 curry leaves
2 tbsp cashews

Method

1. Dry toast the moong dal in a pan over medium heat, stirring constantly, until fragrant and lightly golden. Remove from the heat.

2. Mix the toasted dal with the rice. Wash, rinse, and drain together until the water runs mostly clear.

3. Transfer the rice and dal to a pressure cooker. Add salt and enough water to fully cover the grains generously. Pressure cook until the rice and dal are completely broken down and very soft.

4. Allow the pressure to release naturally. Open and stir gently to even out the texture.

5. Heat the ghee in a small pan over medium heat. Add the cashews and fry until golden.

6. Add the cumin seeds and peppercorns and let them crackle.

7. Add the hing, ginger, and curry leaves. Cook briefly until aromatic and the curry leaves crisp.

8. Pour the hot tadka over the pongal, mix well, and adjust salt if needed.
summary: Step-by-step recipe for ven pongal, a South Indian rice and lentil dish with ghee and spice tempering.
