caption:
Christmas is when my kitchen smells the best and this year, it looks its best too.
Baking my healthier Red Velvet Cake in my dream @sleekkitchens , where cooking feels effortless, organized, and inspiring every single day.
Because good food always tastes better when made in a space you love. 🤍

Healthier Red Velvet Cake

Servings: 6 slices

Macros (per slice):
~185 kcal | 7g protein | 24g carbs | 6g fat

⸻

🧂 Ingredients

For the cake:
	•	1 cup cooked beetroot, blended to a smooth purée
	•	½ cup thick yogurt (hung curd or Greek-style)
	•	¼ cup neutral oil (or melted butter)
	•	½ cup sugar substitute or powdered jaggery
	•	1 tsp vanilla extract
	•	1 cup almond flout (or half almond flour + half oats flour)
	•	2 tbsp cocoa powder
	•	1 tsp baking powder
	•	½ tsp baking soda
	•	A pinch of salt
	•	½ tsp vinegar or lemon juice

For lighter frosting (optional):
	•	½ cup light cream cheese or thick Greek yogurt
	•	1–2 tbsp powdered sweetener
	•	½ tsp vanilla extract

⸻

👩‍🍳 Method
	1.	Preheat oven to 170°C and line a 6-inch cake tin.
	2.	In a bowl, whisk beetroot purée, yogurt, oil, sweetener, and vanilla until smooth.
	3.	Sift in flour, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, and salt.
	4.	Add vinegar, gently fold till just combined.
	5.	Pour batter into tin and bake for 30–35 minutes or until a toothpick comes out clean.
	6.	Cool completely. Spread frosting if using. Slice and serve.

⸻

caption:
Shaved Brussel Sprouts Salad

✅Comment "SALADS" and I'll send you a link on how you can grab my top 10 salads ebook!

#If you're looking for an amazing winter or holiday salad, this is a simple but ridiculously good combo. It's got crunchy, sweet, tangy, and savory, and all brought together with a homemade maple vinaigrette that takes everything to the next level.

Ingredients:
-12 oz super thinly shredded Brussel sprouts (thin is key!)
-1 large Honeycrisp apple, cubed
-1 large pomegranate (just the seeds)
-3 scallions, thinly sliced
-1/3 to 1/2 cup toasted pecans, roughly chopped
-4-5 oz freshly shredded Manchego cheese

Dressing:
-5 tbsp olive oil
-3 tbsp apple cider vinegar
-2 tbsp Dijon mustard
-2 tbsp maple syrup
-2 minced garlic cloves (or 1/2 tbsp garlic powder)
-Generous pinch salt & fresh cracked pepper

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Grab a large salad bowl and add 12 oz super thinly shredded Brussel sprouts. They need to be shaved really thin.

2️⃣ Thinly cube 1 large Honeycrisp apple and toss in the bowl with the seeds from 1 large pomegranate.

3️⃣ Thinly slice 3 scallions (use both the green and white parts) and mix them in.

4️⃣ Roughly chop 1/3 to 1/2 cup toasted pecans and toss them in. Then, shred 4-5 oz of fresh Manchego cheese and add that too.

5️⃣ For the dressing, in a small jar mix together 5 tbsp olive oil, 3 tbsp apple cider vinegar, 2 tbsp Dijon mustard, 2 tbsp maple syrup, 2 minced garlic cloves (or 1/2 tbsp garlic powder), and a pinch of salt and pepper. Taste and adjust to your liking.

6️⃣ Pour the dressing over the salad and toss until everything is evenly coated.

7️⃣ Top with a little more freshly grated Manchego cheese before serving for that final touch.

Enjoy!

#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #salads
summary: A low-carb shaved Brussels sprouts salad with apples, pomegranate, pecans, and Manchego cheese dressed in maple-Dijon vinaigrette.

This Christmas gift match game is so easy yet still so fun! My tips for making it ever better below! 🎁🎁🎁

Comment GAME and I'll send you details plus more easy Christmas party games! 

🎁 Make them all gift boxes so you can't hear the bags rustle 

🎁 Make a rule that there's no talking in line for pre-planning 

🎁 Make one line longer than the other so you're mixing up the pairs each time 

🎁 Make sure one gift doesn't stick out so everyone doesn't just choose that one (aka green vs everything else is red) 

🎁 Matches win a separate prize, not what's in that gift, so you can keep the same number of gifts on the table! 

🎁 Foam board makes a great divider! Get it at dollar store!

caption:
Sheet-Pan Chicken Fajitas with Roasted Garlic Chipotle Sauce 🔥

Comment "Recipe" and I'll send you the full recipe card for free!

All the flavor, one pan… juicy spiced chicken loaded with garlicky goodness 🧄

Chicken marinade
-2 lb (0.8 kg) boneless skinless chicken thighs, sliced
-3 tbsp mayonnaise
-2 tbsp @primalkitchenfoods avocado oil
-2 tbsp honey + Juice of 1 lime
-7 garlic cloves, minced
-Seasonings: 2 tsp paprika, 1 tbsp chili powder, 1 tbsp cumin, 1 tsp cayenne (optional), 2 tsp oregano, 2 tsp onion powder, 2 tsp garlic powder, 2 tsp salt & 2 tsp  pepper

Veggies:
-1/2 red onion, sliced
-1/2 each of a red, yellow, orange and green bell pepper, sliced
-2 tbsp avocado oil @primalkitchenfoods 
-Veggie seasonings (1 tsp each): garlic powder, cumin, chili powder, paprika, oregano, onion powder, salt, pepper

For the Roasted garlic:
-1 head of garlic, top 1/4 sliced off + 1 tbsp oil of choice + 1 tsp salt

Roasted garlic chipotle sauce:
-1/2 cup sour cream + 1/4 cup mayonnaise
-Roasted garlic (the whole head)
-2 chipotle pepper + 1 tbsp honey + Juice of 1/2 lime

1. Add the sliced chicken to a bowl with mayo, avocado oil, honey, lime juice, garlic, and all chicken seasonings. Toss until everything is coated. Marinate for at least a few mins, or up to overnight. Longer = better flavor.
2. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Drizzle the garlic head with oil and salt, wrap it in foil like a little flavor gift, and place on a tray. Roast for 45–60 minutes, until soft & golden.
3. Add all sliced veggies to a large sheet pan. Drizzle with avocado oil, sprinkle with veggie seasonings, and toss to coat. Add the marinated chicken directly onto the pan with the veggies. Toss everything together so it plays nice. Bake for 20–25 minutes, until the chicken is fully cooked, lightly charred, and the veggies are tender.
4. Once the garlic is cool, squeeze the roasted cloves into a blender or food processor. Add sour cream, mayo, chipotle peppers, honey, and lime juice. Blend until smooth.
5. Spoon pan juices back over the chicken. Top with cilantro, extra lime, and dollops of the chipotle sauce. I love turning this into wraps. Load up tortillas and crisp them in a buttered pan.
summary: Sheet-pan chicken fajitas with roasted garlic chipotle sauce recipe.

caption:
Mexican Pizza 🌮🍕
A homemade version of the famous Taco Bell Mexican Pizza, only better!
Super easy to make and a fun dinner the whole family will love.

This recipe comes together in just a few minutes with simple ingredients.

How to make it:
	•Prepare your refried beans.
	•Bake the tortillas until crispy, or fry them lightly in oil.
	•Chop veggies like capsicum, tomato, and lettuce for garnish.

Assembly:
	1.Spread a layer of refried beans on one crispy tortilla.
	2.Top with taco sauce or enchilada sauce.
	3.Place a second tortilla on top and add another light layer of beans and sauce (don't overdo it or it'll get soggy).
	4.Add diced tomatoes, capsicum, and an even layer of cheese.

Bake until the cheese is melted, the tortillas are crispy, and everything is hot and delicious.

Serve with lettuce on top and enjoy your homemade Mexican Pizza! 😍

#mexicanpizza #mexicanpizzarecipe #mexicanrecipes #mammidadhaba
summary: A homemade Mexican pizza recipe with crispy tortillas, refried beans, cheese, and fresh toppings.

caption:
Chicken you'll actually make steak house style chicken 🍗 🥩 

Recipe:

🍗 Crispy Steak-Spiced Chicken on Rice
No skin. No bone. Big flavor. 🔥
Ingredients:
• 600 g boneless, skinless chicken thighs
• 2–3 tsp homemade steak seasoning
• 1 tbsp oil
• Rice (jasmine or basmati)
Homemade Steak Seasoning:
• 2 tbsp smoked paprika
• 1 tbsp garlic powder
• 1 tbsp onion powder
• 1 tbsp black pepper
• 1 tbsp coarse salt
• 2 tsp brown sugar (optional)
• 1 tsp chili flakes or cayenne (optional)
• 1 tsp dried thyme or rosemary (optional)
Method:
1️⃣ Rub chicken with seasoning + oil. Marinate 30 min (or longer).
2️⃣ Hot pan, little oil. Sear 3–4 min per side until deep golden and cooked through.
3️⃣ Rest briefly, serve over rice.
Juicy chicken, smoky steakhouse crust, super simple.

caption:
Weekday comfort food done right. 🤌🏽
Garlicky, spicy, tangy from the curd, finished with kasuri methi and just a touch of cream — all cooked in mustard oil with juicy boneless chicken thigh.
Feels indulgent but still hits your protein goals beautifully. Save this one 🤍

Lasooni Dahi Chicken Curry (लसूनी दही चिकन करी)
Macros (per serving): ~380 kcal | ~43 g protein | ~7 g carbs | ~26 g fat

Serves: 1

Ingredients
	•	180 g boneless chicken thigh, cut into cubes
	•	6–8 garlic cloves
	•	2 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	½ tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp coriander powder
	•	Salt to taste
	•	1 tsp mustard oil
	•	½ tsp mustard seeds
	•	½ tsp cumin seeds
	•	8–10 curry leaves
	•	½ medium onion, sliced
	•	½ cup whisked curd (thick curd)
	•	1 tsp fresh cream
	•	1 tsp kasuri methi, crushed
	•	Chopped coriander leaves to finish

Method

1️⃣ Blend garlic, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, and salt into a smooth paste.
2️⃣ Marinate the chicken with this paste for about 10 minutes.
3️⃣ Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, and curry leaves and let them splutter.
4️⃣ Add sliced onions and sauté for 2–3 minutes until slightly softened.
5️⃣ Add the marinated chicken and cook on medium heat until it releases moisture.
6️⃣ Add the whisked curd, mix well, cover, and cook until the chicken is tender and the oil begins to separate.
7️⃣ Finish with crushed kasuri methi, chopped coriander leaves, and 1 tsp fresh cream.
8️⃣ Serve hot with rice or rotis.

⸻

(lasooni dahi chicken curry, garlic yogurt chicken curry, high protein chicken recipe, boneless chicken recipe, healthy Indian chicken curry)
.

#HighProteinMeals #lasoonidahichicken #comfortfood
summary: High-protein garlic and yogurt chicken curry made with boneless chicken thigh, kasuri methi, and cream.

The BEST Brussels sprouts 🥬 
Here's how you go from baaad tasting Brussels sprouts to GREAT Brussels sprouts (it's actually quite easy)…🤫
RECIPE (3-4 servings, 45min prep time):
Preheat the oven to 220C WITH a baking try inside.

Parboil in salted water for 3-5mins.
Drain and transfer to a bowl.

This is a perfect side dish for Christmas dinner.😋

caption:
JUICY ROASTED CHICKEN & SPICY CILANTRO SAUCE

Forget diet food that tastes like sacrifice! ➡️➡️Comment "POWER" & I'll send you the link to grab my protein book.

This is one of my most favorite easy healthy recipes. I used the oven for these, but they're perfect and super crispy in the air fryer if you have one.

And this is one of my all time favorite sauces, perfect any protein, power bowls, tacos, you name it. Its in my Shredhappens cookbook if you have it, so may sure you make it if you've got my book!

Here is how I made it:

1.Start with 1.5 lbs of bone-in skin-on chicken thighs. I like to cook these up with the skin and bone to add maximum flavor and make it "juicy". If you prefer to go leaner, you can use skinless or go with your favorite cut. But trust me, this makes a difference. You can always remove the skin before eating.

2.Add chicken to a bowl, drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, add the juice of 1.5 limes, 3-4 finely minced fresh garlic cloves. 

3.Then add a couple pinches salt, fresh ground pepper, 1.5 tbsp paprika, 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper, 1/2 tbsp onion power, and 1/2 tsp cumin, give everything a good mix to make sure the chicken is evenly coated.

4.Cover the chicken and let sit for 1-2 hours before making it. If refrigerated, I like to take it out about 30 mins before cooking so the chicken comes up to room temp.

5.I recommend airfrying these at 425F for 18-20 mins. Time varies by airfryer, so just keep an eye on it. If using an oven, I go 415F for 30-35 mins -  again time varies by oven & you want to get to 165F internal temp.

6.I topped it with my spicy cilantro sauce. In a food processor mix a large handful fresh cilantro, 2-3 cloves garlic, 1 jalapeño (with seeds removed all the way or slightly to control spice level), juice of 1.5 limes, 2 tbsp sour cream, 1 tbsp olive oil, a couple pinches sea salt, 1 tbsp chile lime seasoning. Process well, then adjust to taste. Salt is key in this recipe.

You can meal prep more of this for easy healthy meals, but keep sauce separate.

If you make it, let me know what you think on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #chickenrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #keto #ketorecipes
summary: Roasted chicken thighs with spicy cilantro sauce, optimized for air fryer or oven cooking.

caption:
[RECIPE] Protein-Packed Loaded Sweet Potato 🍠🔥

[RECIPE BELOW]

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 425°F. Grab 2 large sweet potatoes, poke holes all over with a fork, and lightly coat them in olive oil.

2️⃣ Bake for 45–60 min. While they cook, brown 1 lb ground meat (I used buffalo).

3️⃣ Season with curry powder, chili powder, turmeric, garlic seasoning, salt, pepper, and chili flakes (about 1 tsp each).
Add chopped bell peppers, jalapeños, red onion, and 4 cloves minced garlic. Mix well.

4️⃣ When the potatoes are done, slice them open and fluff the insides.

5️⃣ Load in the cooked meat + veggie mix.

6️⃣ Top with smoked Gouda (or your preferred cheese) and bake at 350°F until melty.

7️⃣ Finish with cilantro + a drizzle of honey. 🤤🔥

caption:
Most buyers walk through a home looking at countertops and paint colors.
Smart buyers look at the stuff that actually costs money.

Here's my checklist:
1. The water heater. Look at the label on the tank. Find the manufacture date. Water heaters last 10-12 years. If it's older, that's $1,500-3,000 coming out of your pocket soon. Use it in negotiations.
2. The electrical panel. Open it up or ask to see inside. If you see Federal Pacific, Zinsco, or fuses instead of breakers, budget $2,500-4,500 for an upgrade. Some insurance companies won't even cover these panels.
3. Ceiling stains. Look up. Brown or yellow stains mean water. Could be a roof leak. Could be plumbing. Either way, you're looking at $3,000-15,000 depending on the source. Ask when it happened and if it was fixed.
4. The HVAC unit. Find the manufacture date on the unit outside. HVAC systems last 15-20 years. Replacement runs $8,000-15,000. If it's on borrowed time, negotiate a credit or home warranty.
5. The windows. Single pane or double pane? Foggy between the glass means the seal is broken. Full window replacement runs $15,000-30,000 for an average home. Even replacing a few adds up fast.
6. Foundation cracks. Check the basement or crawl space. Hairline cracks are normal. Horizontal cracks or stair-step patterns in brick? That's structural. Repairs range from $5,000-25,000 depending on severity.
7. The sewer line. You can't see it but you can ask about it. Homes built before 1980 often have clay or cast iron pipes that deteriorate. A sewer scope costs $250-400 and can save you from a $10,000-20,000 nightmare.
8. Water pressure. Turn on the shower and flush the toilet at the same time. Weak pressure could mean galvanized pipes that need replacing. Budget $8,000-15,000 for a repipe.
9. Grading around the foundation. Walk the exterior. The ground should slope away from the house. If it slopes toward it, water is getting in somewhere. Drainage fixes run $2,000-8,000.

Every single one of these is leverage at the negotiating table.

The pretty stuff is easy to see. The expensive stuff is hiding in plain sight. 🏠

Save this list for when you go view a house.
summary: A nine-point checklist of major home systems and structural issues to inspect when buying a house, with typical repair costs for negotiation leverage.

caption:
When Mexican street-style elote meets Indori bhutte ka kees, this is what you get 🤌🏽🌽
Creamy, garlicky, mildly spicy, finished with salty cheese and a squeeze of lemon — this Mexican Elote Kees is winter comfort in a bowl.

Mexican Elote Kees
Macros (per serving): ~285 kcal | ~12 g protein | ~38 g carbs | ~9 g fat

Serves: 2
Ingredients
	•	2 whole sweet corn on the cob, kernels removed and finely chopped / grated
	•	1 tbsp light butter
	•	6–8 garlic cloves, finely minced
	•	½ tsp pepper powder
	•	½ tsp chili powder
	•	Salt to taste
	•	1½ cups skim milk
	•	2 tbsp chopped spring onion greens
	•	2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves

To finish
	•	30–40 g feta cheese, crumbled (or soft paneer / any cheese of choice)
	•	A pinch of chili powder
	•	Extra chopped spring onion greens
	•	Extra chopped coriander leaves
	•	Fresh lemon juice, to taste

⸻

Method

1️⃣ Remove kernels from the corn cobs and finely chop, grate, or pulse them in a chopper.
2️⃣ Heat light butter in a pan. Add minced garlic and cook for 1–2 minutes until lightly golden.
3️⃣ Add the sweet corn along with pepper powder, chili powder, and salt. Cook for 2–3 minutes.
4️⃣ Pour in the skim milk and mix well. Let it simmer on medium heat until thick, creamy, and the corn is fully cooked.
5️⃣ Add spring onion greens and coriander leaves. Cook for 30 seconds and switch off the flame.
6️⃣ Transfer to a bowl, crumble feta cheese on top, sprinkle chili powder, add more spring onion greens and coriander.
7️⃣ Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, mix gently, and serve warm.

⸻

(mexican elote, bhutte ka kees, winter comfort food, sweet corn recipes, healthy Indian fusion recipes)

#WinterRecipes #ComfortFood #elotekees
summary: A Mexican Elote Kees recipe that combines Mexican street corn with Indian bhutte ka kees, made with sweet corn, garlic, milk, and finished with feta cheese and lemon.

caption:
New York Times Top 50 Recipes of 2025 | Chicken Stew by @nytcooking @grossypelosi

Ingredients: 

• 2 tbsp each butter & olive oil

• 1 medium onion, diced

• 2 carrots, diced

• 3 celery stalks, diced

• 4 garlic cloves, minced

• ½ tsp sweet paprika

• Salt & black pepper

• 3 tbsp all-purpose flour

• 4 cups chicken broth

• 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar

• 1½ lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs

• 1 lb baby potatoes, quartered

• 1 cup green beans

• ½ cup heavy cream

• ½ tsp each dried sage, thyme & oregano

• Parsley & lemon wedges, to serve

Heat butter and olive oil in a large pot over medium, then cook the onion, carrots, celery, garlic, paprika, and a big pinch of salt until softened. Stir in the flour and cook briefly, then add the chicken broth and vinegar, stirring until smooth. Add the chicken thighs, potatoes, green beans, cream, sage, thyme, oregano, salt, and pepper, bring to a gentle boil, then reduce to a simmer and cook partially covered until the chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes. Remove and shred the chicken, fully cover the pot and cook the vegetables until tender, then return the chicken, season to taste, and serve warm with parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
summary: New York Times top 50 recipe for a creamy chicken stew with vegetables, potatoes, and herbs.

caption:
Marry Me Chicken Pie Recipe 

Still one of my favourite pies I've ever made - Marry Me Chicken Pie - delicious and comforting, perfect for this cold weather!! 

Recipe: 

- 1 kg chicken mince
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 onion
- 3 cloves garlic
- 2 tsp paprika
- 1 tsp oregano
- 1 tsp thyme
- 1 tsp salt (or to taste)
- 1 tsp black pepper
- 1 tsp red chilli flakes (optional)
- 1 tbsp tomato purée
- 3 tbsp sun dried tomato pesto
- 250 ml / 1 cup chicken stock
- 1.5 tsp corn flour
- 4 tbsp double cream or creme fraiche
- 250g baby spinach

For the Mashed Potato:
- 1 kg potatoes 
- 250 ml milk
- 75g butter
- 3 garlic cloves
- Salt + pepper to taste

Method:

1. Heat oil over low heat and sauté onion and garlic until soft. Add chicken mince, spices and optional chilli; cook until chicken changes colour, breaking it up.
2. Stir in tomato purée and sundried pesto; cook 5-6 min. Add vegetables and cook until moisture evaporates and mixture is fairly dry.
3. Add chicken stock, cornflour slurry and cream (or crème fraîche). Simmer 6-7 min until thickened, then stir in spinach and remove from heat.
4. Boil potatoes until tender. Meanwhile, gently heat milk, butter, and garlic (or use roasted garlic).
5. Mash potatoes with the garlic milk; season to taste.
6. Spoon chicken filling into a pie dish, top with mash (and optional cheese and parsley).
7. Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 20–25 min until golden. Serve hot

#pie #marryme #chickenpie #easyrecipe #comfortfood
summary: A complete recipe for Marry Me Chicken Pie with creamy chicken mince filling and garlic mashed potato topping.

caption:
Cheat's Homity Pies
If you've never made homity pie before, you are going to have to take this as a sign to try it this week

Ready-made mash with soft, buttery fried onions, melted cheddar and fresh herbs in a pastry case...all topped with gooey, golden cheese

When I was a kid this was one of my favourite dinners. My mum used to make mini versions - think jam tarts but with mashed potato. 

I'm not going to lie, these pies are ENORMOUS!! If you don't want to make them quite as big, you can always freeze any leftover filling, or just make one big pie!

I have written up the recipe below, let me know if you'll be making these over Christmas! A x

Make 4 individual pies

2 x sheets ready rolled shortcrust pastry
2 x 400g ready made mashed potato
Oil for frying
2 large onions finely chopped
100g grated cheddar cheese (plus extra for the top)
50g fresh spinach finely sliced
50ml milk
2 tbsp finely chopped fresh herbs (I used Thyme, Rosemary and Sage)

Heat the oven to 180C
Grease the side of the pie dishes (11cm wide x 5cm deep) with butter.
Cut out 4 x 20cm circles from the two sheets of pastry. Gently push the pastry into the pie dishes, making sure it goes into the corners and sticks to the sides.
Prick holes in the bottom of the pastry using the prongs of a fork.
Cut out 4 x 20cm circles from baking paper. Push each one into the pie dish and tuck it over the edge so the pastry is covered.
Fill each one with around 2-3cm baking beans
Bake in the oven for 10 minutes. Lift the paper and beans from the pie cases and bake for another 5 minutes.
Whilst the pie crust is baking, heat a drizzle of oil in a large non-stick pan. Add the onion and fry for 5-7 minutes until soft.
In the meantime, Place the mashed potato  into a mixing bowl. Gently warm through in the microwave so that it's easier to stir. Mash it with a fork.
Add 100g grated cheese, chopped fresh herbs, fried onions, chopped spinach and milk to the bowl. Season with salt and pepper. Give it a good stir until all the ingredients are combined.
Fill the pie cases with the mash until filled to the top. Cover in grated cheese and return to the oven for 20 minutes until golden brown.

#christmasidea #pie #baking
summary: Recipe for homemade homity pies with mashed potato, fried onions, cheddar cheese, and herbs in a pastry case.

caption:
270 KCal & 26 g per Serving. 💪🏼
This version of crispy fish cutlet is a total game-changer — great as a snack or weeknight dinner, and perfect for a macro friendly Christmas appetizer. 

Tuna & Sweet Potato Cutlets
Servings: 2
Yield: ~8 medium cutlets
Macros:
	•	Per serving (4 cutlets): ~270 kcal | Protein ~26 g | Carbs ~20 g | Fat ~8 g
	•	Per cutlet: ~68 kcal | Protein ~6.5 g | Carbs ~5 g | Fat ~2 g

Ingredients
	•	Yellowfin tuna (boneless) – 250 g, cubed
	•	Sweet potato – 200 g, boiled & mashed
	•	Onion – 1 medium (100 g), finely chopped
	•	Green chillies – 1–2, finely chopped
	•	Ginger-garlic paste – 1 tsp
	•	Light butter or oil – 1 tsp
	•	Red chilli powder – 1 tsp
	•	Chilli flakes – ½ tsp
	•	Turmeric powder – ¼ tsp
	•	Coriander powder – 1 tsp
	•	Garam masala – 1 tsp
	•	Garlic granules – ½ tsp (this takes the dish to a whole new level - I used the one from Snappin)
	•	Black pepper powder – ½ tsp
	•	Salt – to taste
	•	Water – 2–3 tbsp, just to prevent spices from burning
	•	Fresh coriander leaves – 2 tbsp, chopped

Method
	1.	Heat butter/oil in a pan; sauté onions, green chillies, and ginger-garlic paste until soft and aromatic.
	2.	Add tuna, spices, salt, and a splash of water; cook on high flame for 3–4 minutes.
	3.	Using a spatula, break and smash the tuna until fully shredded and mince-like.
	4.	Add mashed sweet potato and coriander; mix well.
	5.	Switch off the flame and let the mixture cool slightly.
	6.	Shape into 8 equal cutlets.
Cooking options 
	•	Pan-fry: Shallow-fry on a non-stick pan with a light oil spray on medium heat, 3–4 minutes per side, until deep golden and crisp.
	•	Air-fry: Air-fry at 180°C for 10–12 minutes, flipping halfway and lightly spraying oil.
	•	Bake: Bake in a preheated oven at 200°C for 18–20 minutes, flipping once halfway, until crisp outside.

Serve hot with any dip of choice and enjoy.

.

(Tuna cutlets, healthy fish cutlets, high protein snack, air fryer fish cutlets, tuna recipes, gluten-free cutlets, sweet potato cutlets, easy Indian fish snack, fish cakes, Christmas recipes, party appetisers, Kerala recipes)

.

Bold, slow cooked and unapologetically rich. This Punjabi style chicken masala leans more towards the dry side, where the flavours cling to every piece instead of swimming in sauce. Deep, roasted notes, warming spice and that classic dhaba style intensity that hits hard and lingers. Each bite is packed, smoky and satisfying, the kind of dish that fills the room with aroma and keeps you going back for more without needing a heavy gravy.

Recipe Ingredients 
Serves 3-4

caption:
GREEN CHILI CHICKEN ENCHILADAS – a cozy high protein dinner and the first recipe I posted on Well Plated 14 years ago! My Grammy made these every year for our Christmas dinner. It might sound odd but they hit the spot!💃🏻

Grammy passed away last year, and I've been coming back to this recipe more than ever.🩷 It's comforting, simple, and one of the reasons I started my blog: to preserve her recipes in a healthier way. I swap her sour cream for nonfat Greek yogurt, load them up with veggies, and use rotisserie chicken to make them even easier.

Comment "SEND" for the recipe!

Be sure to SAVE this recipe for your festive season, or any night you want comfort food with a story.

YOU'LL NEED
-2 c shredded chicken
-1½ tsp kosher salt, divided
-1 T extra-virgin olive oil
-2 small red, yellow, or orange bell peppers, diced
-1 medium yellow onion, diced
-1 tsp chili powder
-¾ tsp garlic powder
-½ tsp cumin
-½ tsp ground black pepper
-2 c shredded Monterey jack or Mexican blend cheese, divided
-1½ c plain nonfat Greek yogurt
-2 (4-oz) cans diced green chiles, drained
-8 whole wheat 7-inch flour tortillas
-1 (15-oz) can green enchilada sauce
-For serving: diced tomato, cilantro, avocado, red onion

Sauté the peppers and onions in olive oil until soft, then stir in the chicken and seasonings. In a bowl, mix the yogurt, green chiles, 1 cup cheese, and remaining salt. Add the chicken and veggie mixture once cooled and stir to combine.

Roll into tortillas, place seam side down in a greased 9x13-inch dish, and cover with enchilada sauce. Sprinkle with remaining cheese, cover, then bake at 350°F for 30 minutes. Uncover, bake 5 to 10 more minutes, then broil the top if desired. Add your toppings and DEVOUR!

Comment "SEND" and I'll DM you link to the recipe, or find it at the link in my @wellplated bio or at wellplated.com.

caption:
210 Kcal per Serving 🫶🏼
If salads ever felt boring, this Broccoli Pomegranate Salad will definitely will change your mind. 🥑
Fresh, crunchy, tangy, lightly sweet, and ridiculously addictive — great as a side for your meals or a satisfying meal on its own. This is also a perfect side for your Christmas dinner btw. ❤️

Recipe
Servings: 2
Macros (per serving): ~210 kcal | Protein: ~6 g | Carbs: ~15 g | Fat: ~15 g

Ingredients

For the dressing
	•	½ medium avocado (or ½ cup hung curd / Greek yogurt)
	•	1 - 2 small green chilli
	•	½ tsp ginger-garlic paste
	•	3 tbsp fresh mint leaves
	•	2 tbsp fresh coriander leaves
	•	Salt, to taste
	•	½ tsp sugar (or sweetener of choice)
	•	1 tsp olive oil
	•	1½ tbsp lemon juice

For the salad
	•	2 cups broccoli florets, steamed and chopped
	•	2 tbsp flaked almonds (or nuts of choice), lightly toasted
	•	¼ cup pomegranate seeds

Method
	1.	Blend all the dressing ingredients into a smooth, creamy sauce. Adjust salt, lemon, and sweetness to taste.
	2.	In a bowl, add the steamed broccoli, toasted nuts, and pomegranate seeds.
	3.	Pour the dressing over the salad and mix well until everything is evenly coated.
	4.	Serve immediately as a side dish or enjoy it as a refreshing standalone salad.

caption:
Mediterranean Spinach Ricotta Bites 🇬🇷✨ 
Protein | 7g per bite

Golden, cheesy, and loaded with Mediterranean flavor — these soft, savory bites are perfect as a snack, appetizer, or protein-packed meal prep favorite!

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 1 cup ricotta cheese 
✦ 1/2 cup grated parmesan 
✦ 1 cup cooked spinach (well-drained & chopped) 
✦ 1/2 cup almond flour 
✦ 2 eggs 
✦ 1 garlic clove (minced) 
✦ 1/2 tsp oregano 
✦ 1/2 tsp basil 
✦ Salt & pepper to taste 
✦ Chopped chives or parsley for garnish 

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣ - Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking tray with parchment paper. 

2️⃣ - Mix ricotta, parmesan, spinach, garlic, eggs, almond flour, and spices in a bowl until thick and scoopable. 

3️⃣ - Form small round balls and place them on the baking tray. 

4️⃣ - Bake for 20–22 minutes or until golden on top. 

5️⃣ - Sprinkle with parmesan and herbs before serving. 

✯ Protein: 7g 
✯ Carbs: 3g 
✯ Fat: 6g 
✯ Calories: 105

caption:
The secret to the best roasted potatoes 🥔🔥
⠀
This is the only recipe for roasted potatoes you'll ever need! Credits to @kenjilopezalt who has an in-depth article about the science behind this recipe on his blog @seriouseats
⠀
RECIPE
- about 2 kg potatoes
- salt
- 1/2 tsp baking soda
- 4 tbsp oil (neutral vegetable oil or olive oil)
- 1 sprig rosemary
- 3 garlic cloves
⠀
❶ Preheat oven to 230°C/450°F.
❷ To a large pot add 3 liters of water, salt and baking soda and bring to a boil.
❸ Peel and chop potatoes, then boil for 10-15 minutes until fork tender. Boiling the potatoes with baking soda helps breaking down the pectin, creating a rough surface and thus more crispiness. 🔥
❹ Strain the potatoes, then shake to roughen the surface.
❺ Infuse the oil with garlic and rosemary. Fry until the garlic just begins to turn golden, then strain and set garlic/rosemary mixture aside, so it doesn't burn in the oven.
❻ Add the potatoes to a baking sheet, drizzle with the infused oil, spread out evenly and bake for 50-60 minutes, turning them 2-3 times, every 20 minutes.
❼ Transfer to a large bowl to add the garlic/rosemary mixture and a pinch of salt, then toss to coat.
⠀
Please tag me if you make it, I love seeing your remakes!
⠀
#easyrecipes #potatoes #homecooking #veganfood #cooking
summary: A detailed recipe for crispy roasted potatoes using baking soda and parboiling to achieve maximum crispiness.

caption:
Shrimp sushi bites✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

▪️1 lb. Shrimp, peeled and deveined▪️Kosher Salt▪️3 Tbsp Mayonnaise▪️1 Tbsp Sriracha▪️2 Scallions, sliced▪️1/2 tsp Soy Sauce▪️1/4 tsp Sesame Seeds▪️3-4 Packs Roasted Salted Seaweed Snacks▪️1 Cucumber, sliced into thin 2" strips▪️

1️⃣ Bring a pot of generously salted water to a boil. Fill a medium bowl with ice water.
2️⃣ Boil the shrimp for 2-3 minutes, until pink and opaque, then drain and transfer to the ice bath for a few minutes to prevent overcooking.
3️⃣ Thoroughly dry the shrimp with paper towels, then roughly chop and place in a bowl. Add the scallions, mayonnaise, sriracha, soy sauce, and a pinch of salt. Mix until evenly combined, then taste and adjust seasoning if needed.
4️⃣ Transfer to a small serving bowl and sprinkle with sesame seeds. Serve with sliced cucumber and seaweed. To eat, spoon a dollop of the shrimp mixture onto a piece of seaweed, top with a couple cucumber slices, and enjoy!

⏲️ 25 minute total prep + cook time

Serves 4-6 as an appetizer
summary: Easy shrimp sushi bites made with boiled shrimp, sriracha mayo, and seaweed snacks, ready in 25 minutes.

caption:
Crunchy parmesan potato wedges✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Recipe inspired by @unpezzoditalia 🥰

▪️3 lb. Yukon Gold Potatoes, peeled & cut into bite-sized wedges▪️Kosher Salt▪️5 Tbsp Olive Oil, divided▪️1/2 Cup Italian Breadcrumbs▪️1/4 Cup Panko Breadcrumbs▪️1/2 Cup Grated Parmesan Cheese▪️1/2 tsp Garlic Powder▪️1/2 tsp Freshly Ground Black Pepper▪️2 Tbsp Chopped Fresh Parsley▪️Zest of 1 Lemon▪️Sour Cream, for serving (optional)▪️

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 400F convection (or 425F regular bake) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. 
2️⃣ Place potato wedges in a large pot of cold, generously salted water and bring to a boil. Simmer until just tender when pierced with a knife (about 8 mins). Drain and transfer to a large bowl. 
3️⃣ Drizzle on 3 Tbsp of the olive oil and toss to coat, then add the breadcrumbs, panko, Parmesan, garlic powder, pepper, and 1/2 tsp kosher salt. Toss until evenly coated.
4️⃣ Spread in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet and drizzle on the remaining 2 Tbsp of olive oil. Bake for 30-35 mins, tossing the potatoes halfway through, until deeply golden and crisp.
5️⃣ Transfer to a serving platter and sprinkle with parsley and lemon zest. Serve with sour cream for dipping if desired, and enjoy! 

⏲ 15 min prep time / 1 hour total time

Serves 8 

Leftovers reheat well! Pop them back in a 375F convection oven for 10–15 mins, or in the air fryer for a few mins less, until hot and crisp.
summary: Recipe for crunchy parmesan potato wedges baked in the oven with breadcrumbs and lemon zest.

caption:
New York Times Top 50 Recipes of 2025 | Hoisin Garlic Noodles by @nytcooking @hettymckinnon

Ingredients

• 14 oz dried wheat or egg noodles

• ¼ cup Hoisin

• 2 tbsp Soy sauce 

• 1 tbsp Sesame oil

• 2 tsp Maple syrup or honey 

• Vegetable oil

• 6 garlic cloves, finely chopped

• 6 scallions, white & green parts sliced

• 4 tbsp toasted sesame seeds

• Salt & pepper

Cook the noodles in salted boiling water until al dente, then drain and rinse to cool. Mix hoisin, soy sauce, sesame oil, and maple syrup in a small bowl. Heat oil in a large skillet, add the garlic and white scallion parts, and cook until fragrant, then quickly pour in the sauce and add the noodles, tossing to coat. Let the noodles cook undisturbed until the bottom turns crispy, 2 to 3 minutes, then season to taste. Serve topped with sesame seeds and the green scallion parts.
summary: NYT Top 50 recipe for hoisin garlic noodles with sesame seeds and crispy bottom.

caption:
ONE BOWL GLUTEN FREE CRISPY CAULIFLOWER 🤍🥦✨

Dump everything in, mix it up, bake on high DONE.

The easiest crispy cauliflower recipe ever.
So flavorful, crunchy, and the perfect quick side dish.
Serve it as a snack, toss it into a salad as cauliflower croutons, plate it next to dinner…it works with everything.

I made mine gluten-free using GF breadcrumbs, but you can use any kind you like.
And if you need it vegan, just swap the mayo for vegan mayo! It works perfectly.

This is the kind of weeknight vegetable recipe that goes viral because it's:
✔️ one bowl
✔️ super crispy
✔️ loaded with flavor
✔️ ready fast
✔️ gluten-free + vegan optional
✔️ kid approved + dinner friendly

SAVE + SHARE this crispy cauliflower recipe so you don't lose it! it's one of those you'll make on repeat.
Who wants the full recipe? 👇🔥

Recipe:
3/4 cup mayonnaise 
Juice of half a lemon
1&1/2  tsp kosher salt 
2 tsp granulated onion
2 tsp granulated garlic
2 tsp mustard powder 
1 tsp dried parsley 
1 tsp dried cilantro 
1 bag (600 grams) frozen cauliflower 
2 cups breadcrumbs (I used a mix of whatever gf bread crumbs I had, use any you have!)
Remove bag of frozen cauliflower from the freezer 1 hour before starting. 
Preheat oven to 210ºc (410ºf) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper. Spray liberally with non stick spray. Set aside.
To a large bowl add mayonnaise, lemon and spices. Mix well to combine. Add in cauliflower (will still be frozen just not as frozen) and mix well to coat. Pour breadcrumbs over the cauliflower and use your gander to gently toss so that the breadcrumbs fully coat each piece of cauliflower. Pour onto the sheet pan and spread into a single layer. Place in the oven and bake for 40-45 minutes until each floret is golden and crisp! Serve and enjoy!
summary: One-bowl gluten-free crispy baked cauliflower with mayo-based coating and breadcrumb crust.

caption:
24 Days of Christmas Appetizers Pt 16 🎅🎄

Spinach & Feta Pies

Advance prep at the bottom ⬇️🫶
1 sheet puff pastry

Filling:
150g fresh spinach chopped 
1 onion chopped small
150g Feta Cheese
100g grated Mozzarella 
1/2 tsp oregano, mint or dill 

1 egg for brushing 

Sesame seeds
Honey 

Saute your onion and spinach  with salt pepper and oregano. Let it cool on a plate for 5 min and then mix in  with well crumbled feta and the mozzarella.

Preheat your oven to 180C 

Cut the pastry into 12 pieces by creating 3 rows lengthways and 4 rows widthways. Slice each row in half widthways so your 12 pieces are cut in half to create 24 (1 will be the lid) and start filling up the bottom half of each piece with some of the filling, 

Cut diagonal slits into the top piece of pastry being careful to not cut all the way through. LIGHTLY brush  all the exposed pastry with egg wash (too much will be hard to handle) gently lift the lid onto each pastry but don't press it down yet. 

Transfer to dish lined with parchment paper and then press down all the edges well to seal it closed. Brush the top with egg wash and sprinkle sesame seeds and bake for 28-33 minutes. my oven is strong so I normally turn it down to 170C after 10 min. Squeeze some honey over the top as soon as they come out.

For advance prep after egg wash flash freeze on the tray till hard and then into a zip lock bag. Cook straight from frozen when you're ready 

Enjoy D 🫶

#appetizers  #feta  #partyfood  #puffpastry  #spinach  #christmas #christmasfood
summary: Spinach and feta puff pastry pies with sesame seeds and honey, part of a 24-day Christmas appetizer series.

caption:
Thai basil chicken fried rice✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Sauce: ▪️2 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️1 Tbsp Fish Sauce▪️1 tsp Sugar▪️

Fried rice:▪️2 Tbsp Avocado Oil (or other neutral oil), divided▪️4 Eggs▪️Kosher Salt & Freshly Ground Pepper▪️1 Chicken Breast (8 oz.), cut in 1/2" pieces▪️1 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️2 Tbsp Butter, divided▪️1 Large Shallot, diced small▪️1 Red Fresno Chili, seeds removed and thinly sliced▪️2 Cloves Garlic, finely chopped▪️4 Cups Cooked Jasmine Rice▪️3 Scallions, sliced▪️1 Cup Thai Basil Leaves, thinly sliced▪️

1️⃣ In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar until the sugar dissolves.
2️⃣ In a wok or large nonstick skillet, heat 1 Tbsp of the avocado oil over medium heat. Crack in the eggs (you may have to do this in two batches if using a wok). Sprinkle with salt and pepper and cook 1-2 mins until set, then flip and cook 30-60 secs until the whites are fully set but the yolks are still runny. Remove from the wok and set aside.
3️⃣ Add the remaining 1 Tbsp oil, the chicken, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper. Spread the chicken into an even layer and let it sear, undisturbed, for 2 mins until golden brown. Cook, stirring, for 1 more min until just cooked through, then stir in the 1 Tbsp soy sauce.
4️⃣ Add 1 Tbsp butter, the shallot, red chili, and garlic, and cook until the shallot softens and becomes translucent, about 3 mins.
5️⃣ Add the rice and the sauce mixture along with 1 tsp freshly ground pepper and 1/2 tsp kosher salt. Cook, stirring, until everything is combined, about 2 mins.
6️⃣ Stir in the scallions, then create a small well in the middle of the rice and add the remaining 1 Tbsp butter. Let the rice fry in the melted butter, undisturbed, until crispy, 2-3 mins. Stir the rice to mix up the buttery pieces throughout, then cook, tossing once or twice, until more crispy bits form, about 2 mins.
7️⃣ Add the eggs, breaking them into pieces. Add the Thai basil and cook, tossing, until everything is combined, about 1 min. Serve in bowls and enjoy!

⏲ 10 minute prep time / 30 minute total time

Recipe serves 4 as a main or 6 as a side
summary: Thai basil chicken fried rice recipe with step-by-step instructions and ingredient list.

caption:
This ramen was so easy and it came together so quickly! Here is how I made it.

Eggs:
5 eggs
1/4 cup soy sauce
1/4 cup water
1 tsp sugar

Add eggs to boiling water and cook for 7 minutes. Drain and place them in a bowl of ice water. Peel and add to a container. Pour in the soy sauce, water, and sugar. Add a lid and gently shake. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Chicken:
1 lb chicken thighs
2 tbs oil
3 tbs soy sauce
Pepper
Paprika

Add all ingredients to a bowl and mix. Marinate for 30 minutes. Preheat a skillet and cook until golden and crisp on both sides. Set aside.

Soup:
2 tbs oil
4 cups chicken broth
3 cups water
1 shallot, chopped
5 cloves garlic, grated
3 cups bok choy
4 tbs miso paste
8 oz noodles
2 tbs soy sauce
Green onion for topping

Add oil, garlic, and shallot to a pan. Cook for 2 minutes over medium heat or until fragrant. Add water and chicken broth and bring to a boil. Add the noodles and cook until tender. Reduce heat to low. Add bok choy and stir. Mix in the miso paste and remove from heat once it is all mixed in. Add in soy sauce and stir. Serve the soup in bowls and top with the chicken, an egg, and green onion.

#husbandwife #dinnerideas #marriedcouples #couplescomedy #momhumor #homemade #homemadecooking #dinnerrecipes #easyrecipes #soup #cozydinner
summary: Easy homemade ramen recipe with marinated chicken, soft-boiled eggs, and miso broth.

caption:
90 Calorie Shrimp Summer Rolls

I dont know how to make a lot of Vietnamese food, but I absolutely love it every time I had it eating out. The flavors are fresh, it feels light yet filling, and its so good.I had some spring and summer rolls a few weeks ago and wanted to make them more at home.

These are super light, fresh, crunchy, and ridiculously satisfying. Just 90 calories each (without sauce) but so much better with just a teaspoon or so of the peanut sauce below.

If you are prioritizing protein, as always, you can double up on the protein 

Here is how I made them:

Ingredients (Makes 10–12 Rolls)
-3/4 lb wild shrimp, boiled or precooked (then cut in half)
-Rice paper sheets (35 cals each, grab them at any Asian grocery near you or online)
-Lettuce leaves (I used gem lettuce)
-2 thinly Shredded carrots
-1/2 cup thinly cut red cabbage
-4 Scallions, thinly sliced lengthwise
-Large handful of fresh mint + lemon basil or regular basil
-1 thinly sliced jalapeño 
-1 large avocado thinly sliced

The rolls:
1.Dip rice paper in warm water (just quick dunk!).
2.Add lettuce, then your veggies and herbs, leaving space on the edges 
3.Fold bottom of rice paper up, pull tight and tuck it in.
4.Add a couple jalapeño slices, 2-3 shrimp cut in half, and 2-3 thin avocado slices
5.Fold in the sides, then roll tight, mini burrito-style.
6.Repeat until you are satisfied. This makes about a dozen rolls depending on how much you fill them up.

🥜 Optional Peanut Sauce (but lets be honest, its the best part)
-2 tbsp crunchy PB
-1 tbsp gochujang
-1.5 tbsp soy sauce
-1 tbsp maple syrup
-1–2 tbsp hot sauce
-1/4–1/3 cup water to thin

💡 Save this for an easy meal prep, lighter lunch, or snack to have in the fridge anytime.

And if you make them, let me know on SHREDHAPPENS. ENJOY!
.
.
.
#lowcalorie #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare
summary: A 90-calorie Vietnamese shrimp summer roll recipe with fresh vegetables, herbs, and optional peanut sauce.

caption:
Beet Salad / Ensalada de Betabeles–Remolachas

Ingredients / Ingredientes

• 3 large beets / 3 betabeles o remolachas grandes
• A splash of vinegar for cooking (red wine, white, or apple cider) / Un chorrito de vinagre para cocción (vino tinto, blanco o manzana)
• 3 tbsp extra virgin olive oil / 3 cucharadas de aceite de oliva extra virgen
• 2–3 tbsp apple cider vinegar (or any vinegar you have) / 2–3 cucharadas de vinagre de manzana o el que tengas
• 1 garlic clove, minced / 1 diente de ajo picado
• Juice of 1 fresh lemon / Jugo de 1 limón fresco
• 1 bunch fresh cilantro or parsley, chopped / 1 manojo de cilantro o perejil fresco picado
• Salt and pepper to taste / Sal y pimienta al gusto

Directions / Preparación
 1. Wash beets thoroughly, cut stems, and place them in a pot with water and a splash of vinegar. / Lava bien los betabeles, corta los tallos y colócalos en una olla con agua y un chorrito de vinagre.
 2. Cook until fork tender. / Cocina hasta que estén tiernos al pinchar con un tenedor.
 3. Peel using a paper towel and cut into cubes or slices. / Pela usando una toalla de papel y córtalos en cubos o rodajas.
 4. In a bowl, mix olive oil, vinegar, garlic, lemon juice, herbs, salt, and pepper. / En un bowl mezcla el aceite de oliva, vinagre, ajo, limón, hierbas, sal y pimienta.
 5. Add beets while still warm so they absorb the vinaigrette better. / Añade los betabeles aún calientes para que absorban mejor la vinagreta.
 6. Refrigerate 1–2 hours before serving. / Refrigera 1–2 horas antes de servir.

#carolistermomandchef #beetsalad #beets
summary: A bilingual beet salad recipe with olive oil vinaigrette, garlic, lemon, and fresh herbs.

caption:
EASY & DELICIOUS CHICKEN SATAY // Let me show you how to make a crowd-pleasing dish that's minimal effort but maximum flavour. You just can't go wrong with these delicious tender and marinated chicken skewers that are packed with umami flavour, plenty of spice and fragrant coconut.

Ingredients:
750g chicken breast or thighs, cut into cubes
MARINADE
2 tbsp peanut butter
1 tbsp Thai red curry paste
100ml good quality coconut milk 
1 tbsp brown sugar
1 clove garlic, minced
1 tsp grated ginger
Squeeze of lime juice
Salt to taste

Julienned carrots and cucumber, for optional quick pickle
Steamed rice, coriander leaves and red chilli, to serve

1. In a bowl whisk together all of the marinade ingredients until smooth. Set aside a few spoons of the sauce for drizzling later and marinate the chicken with the rest of the marinade, coating well. Marinate for at least 20 minutes, or up to overnight for deeper flavour. 
2. Skewer the chicken pieces onto sticks ready for cooking. Heat a drizzle of oil in a pan over medium-high heat. Cook the skewered chicken for 6–8 minutes until browned and cooked through. Alternatively you can also grill until charred on the edges.
3. Make your quick pickle by combining the julienned vegetables, some coriander, a squeeze of lime and a pinch of salt. Reserve for later.
4. Serve the skewers with a squeeze of lime alongside rice and fresh vegetables or the quick pickle.
summary: Recipe for easy Thai chicken satay skewers with peanut-coconut marinade, served with rice and quick pickled vegetables.

caption:
WHY BMI IS A LIE FOR INDIANS. 🧬
You go to the doctor. They check your weight. They check your BMI. They say, "You are in the normal range."
Do not believe them.
Standard health guidelines are built for Western bodies, not Indian ones.
We have a unique genetic legacy called the "Thin-Fat" Phenotype.
Because our ancestors survived centuries of famine, our bodies are genetically wired to hoard energy.
We store fat differently—not just under the skin, but deep inside, wrapping around our liver and pancreas (Visceral Fat).
This means an Indian man with a BMI of 23 has the same diabetes risk as a European man with a BMI of 30.
You can be thin on the outside and metabolically sick on the inside.
Stop relying on the scale. Start looking at your metabolic markers.

In Sick Nation, I explain exactly how to turn off this "famine switch" in a world of abundance.

Comment "BOOK" for early release copy. 

#SickNation #ThinFatIndian #AsianIndianParadox #VisceralFat #IndianHealth #Genetics #sweetreactions
summary: Explains why standard BMI measurements don't account for genetic differences in how Indian bodies store fat and metabolize energy.

caption:
Mom's shrimp and chive gyoza✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Gyoza:▪️1 lb. Peeled and Deveined Raw Shrimp, cut into small pieces then rough chopped▪️1 Garlic Clove, finely chopped▪️3/4 Cup Chopped Chinese Chives, also known as garlic chives (or 1/2 cup chopped scallions + 1/4 cup chopped regular chives)▪️2 Tbsp Finely Chopped Fresh Ginger▪️1 Tbsp Cornstarch▪️1/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Pepper▪️1 Tbsp Sesame Oil▪️1 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️1 Package Gyoza Skins▪️

Sauce:▪️3 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️2 Tbsp Rice Vinegar▪️2 Tbsp Sesame Oil▪️1/2 tsp Sugar▪️1 tsp Sesame Seeds▪️1 Tbsp Chili Oil Crisp or Chili Garlic Sauce, optional▪️1 Tbsp Sliced Scallions▪️

For Cooking:▪️4 Tbsp Avocado Oil, divided▪️1 Cup Water, divided▪️

1️⃣ Make the filling: In a medium bowl, add the shrimp, garlic, Chinese chives, ginger, cornstarch, salt, pepper, sesame oil, and soy sauce. Mix until all ingredients are evenly combined.
2️⃣ Fold the gyoza: Fill a small bowl with water. Moisten the edge of a wrapper with your finger and place about 1 tsp filling in the center. Fold the wrapper in half, pinching to seal at the top. Starting from the midpoint, make two small pleats on each side, folding the edges toward the center and pressing firmly to secure. Repeat with the remaining filling.
3️⃣ Make the sauce: In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sugar, sesame seeds, scallions, and chili oil crisp (if using). Stir until the sugar dissolves.
4️⃣ Cook the gyoza: Add 2 Tbsp oil to a cold large nonstick skillet with a lid. Add half the gyoza seam-side up, rubbing them lightly in the oil and leaving space between each. Add 1/2 cup water, cover, and place over medium heat. Cook until the water is absorbed, 7–9 mins. Uncover, reduce heat to medium low, and cook undisturbed until the bottoms are golden brown, 3–4 mins. Flip so the pleated side is down and cook 3–4 mins more until golden. Transfer to a plate and repeat with the remaining oil, water, and gyoza.
5️⃣ Serve the gyoza hot with the dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy!

⏲️ 30 min prep time / 45 min total time

Serves 4
summary: Shrimp and chive gyoza recipe with step-by-step folding and pan-frying instructions, plus dipping sauce.

caption:
Certain $2 bills in circulation today could be worth thousands of dollars to collectors, depending on their age, condition, and unique features. Bills printed before 1976, those with specific serial numbers, or ones with special seals can command significantly higher prices at auctions or through private sales. While most $2 bills are worth their face value, rare editions can fetch anywhere from a few hundred to several thousand dollars.

Collectors and enthusiasts often look for star notes, uncirculated bills, or bills from limited printing runs, which tend to be more valuable. Experts recommend checking the bill's year, seal, serial numbers, and overall condition to estimate its worth. This has led to a surge of interest, with some holders discovering that a bill they thought was ordinary could be a hidden treasure.
summary: Information about which $2 bills can be worth significantly more than face value based on age, condition, and features.

caption:
SHEET PAN CHICKEN SHAWARMA 🍗🍋🌿 38g of protein and only 320 cals per serving!

This is one of my favorite cozy winter dinners because it's warm, flavorful, and so easy to make on a busy night. This recipe makes a big batch so you have plenty leftover for dinner or lunch the next day. It stores perfectly in the fridge so it's a great way to meal prep chicken for the week.

This serves about 6 people. You can serve it over rice with tzatziki and hummus, in pita bread, or over a salad.

The full recipe is below or comment recipe and I will send you a printable link.

Ingredients: 
- 3 lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs
- 1/2 cup plain greek yogurt 
- 2 tbsp tomato paste
- Zest and juice of 1/2 a lemon
- 1 tbsp minced garlic 
- 1 tbsp dried oregano 
- 1 tbsp paprika
- 2 tsp cumin 
- 1/4 tsp cinnamon 
- 1 tsp salt
- 1/2 tsp black pepper 
- 1 large red onion
- Olive oil spray 

How to: 
- Preheat oven to 425F. 
- Dice chicken thighs into 1 inch cubes. Add to a large mixing bowl with all of the other ingredients, except for the red onion.
- Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and marinate for at least 20 minutes on the counter, or place in the fridge for up to 12 hours for even more flavor!
- Line a baking tray with parchment paper and spread the chicken into an even layer. Slice the red onion and sprinkle it over the top of the chicken, then lightly spray everything with olive oil spray.
- Bake for 35-40 minutes, then I like to turn the oven to broil for 3-5 minutes until the tops of the chicken get a little char. 
- You can serve this over rice, in a pita, or on a salad! Enjoy! ☺️

#healthyrecipes #sheetpan #sheetpandinner #easydinner #dinnerrecipe #dinneridea #mealinspo #healthyeating #healthycooking #highprotein #chickenrecipe #mealprep #chickenshawarma #sheetpanchickenshawarma

caption:
This is finger-licking good! 

Herb-Roasted Chicken Thighs with Cucumber Avocado Salad + Yogurt Herb Dip

Chicken:
Season 4 chicken thighs in a bowl with sliced red onion, juice of 1 large lemon, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 2 tbsp olive oil, salt + pepper, 1 tbsp Hungarian sweet paprika, 1 tbsp garlic powder, and 1/2 tbsp dried oregano. Mix until fully coated so the marinade turns into a thick, rich paste that clings to the chicken. Lay the sliced onions in a baking dish, place the chicken right on top, and make sure any extra marinade is spread over the thighs. Roast at 425°F (220°C) for 35 minutes until browned and juicy, then broil 2–3 minutes if you want extra color. Let it rest so the juices settle and the onions soften underneath.

Cucumber Avocado Salad:
Finely dice 2 Persian cucumbers, cube 1 small avocado, and add 1/4 small red onion, 2 tbsp cilantro, 1/2 tsp oregano, 1 tbsp olive oil, salt + pepper, and a squeeze of lemon. Toss gently so the avocado holds its shape.

Yogurt Herb Dip:
Blend 
1 shallot, 1 garlic clove, parsley, dill (or mint), optional basil, juice of 1/2 lemon, Greek yogurt, olive oil, salt + pepper until smooth and creamy. 
Chill to let the flavors come together.
.#cookwithme #dadlife #easydinnerideas #chickenrecipe #athlete #teenagers #cookwithme #simplerecipe #onepanmeal #mediterranean
summary: Herb-roasted chicken thighs with cucumber avocado salad and yogurt herb dip.

caption:
Share this Red Curry Sweet Potato Dal with someone who loves delicious healthy food & save the recipe below! 🍠😋🍛

Ingredients 

3 tablespoons olive oil 
1 lb sweet potatoes, cubed 
1 medium yellow onion, chopped 
3 tbsp Thai red curry paste 
3 garlic cloves, minced (about 1 tablespoon) 
1 (1-inch) piece fresh ginger, peeled and grated (about 1 tablespoon) 
1 tsp ground turmeric 
1 cup red lentils, rinsed 
3 cups low-sodium vegetable stock 
2 tsp sea salt 
1 (13-ounce) can full-fat coconut milk 
1 (5oz) bag baby spinach
1/2 lime, juiced 
Cilantro, for serving

Method 
1. In a Dutch oven or pot, heat 2 tablespoons olive oil over medium-high. Add the sweet potatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned all over, 5 to 7 minutes. Transfer the browned sweet potatoes to a plate and set aside. 

2. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil to the pot and set the heat to medium-low. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until translucent, 4 to 6 minutes. Add the curry paste, garlic, ginger, and turmeric, and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. 

3. Add the lentils, stock, salt, and browned sweet potatoes to the pot and bring to a boil over high heat. Lower the heat and simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the lentils are just tender, 7 to 13 minutes. 

4. Add the coconut milk and simmer, stirring occasionally, until the liquid has reduced, and the lentils are creamy and falling apart, 15 to 20 minutes. Stir in spinach and cook until wilted. Remove from heat, stir in the lime juice, and top with cilantro. Enjoy!

Browning the sweet potatoes before cooking them with the lentils brings out their sweetness, balancing the heat from the chile and curry paste, while baby spinach tossed in just before serving adds fresh flavor. Serve over steamed white or brown rice, or with toasted flatbread on the side.

#curryrecipe  #dal  #curry  #thaicurry  #redcurry  #dairyfree  #marrymechicken  #marrymelentils  #plantbaseddinner  #meatlessmonday  #highprotein  #vegan  #easyrecipes  #fallrecipes  #recipeshare  #plantbased  #cozyvibes #onepotmeal #onepan
summary: A dairy-free Thai red curry sweet potato dal recipe made in one pot with red lentils, coconut milk, and spinach.

caption:
20 MIN GREEK LEMON CHICKEN SOUP (GF, DF)✨ Bright, cozy, and comforting—this lemony chicken soup is everything I want when I'm feeling run-down or just need something warm and protein-packed. It's made with rotisserie chicken, gluten-free stelline pasta (or you can use orzo), and finished with an egg-lemon mixture that gives it a silky, nourishing texture without any dairy. A cozy weeknight dinner or postpartum fridge staple that also happens to make incredible leftovers!

Ingredients:
4 tbsp olive oil
1 medium yellow onion, finely chopped
2 large carrots, peeled and diced
2 celery stalks, diced
3 cloves garlic, minced
2 tsp freshly grated ginger
1 tsp kosher salt (adjust to taste)
½ tsp black pepper
1 tsp Italian seasoning
8 cups chicken bone broth
1 cup uncooked stelline pasta (or orzo)
4 cups shredded rotisserie chicken
2 large eggs
Juice of 2 lemons (about 4 tbsp)

Instructions
In a large Dutch oven or heavy pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 4–5 minutes until softened and fragrant.
Stir in garlic and ginger, then season with salt, pepper, and Italian seasoning. Cook 1 more minute.
Pour in the chicken bone broth and bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the pasta and shredded rotisserie chicken. Simmer for 8–10 minutes, or until pasta is tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and lemon juice.
Temper the eggs by slowly ladling in about ½ cup of hot broth while whisking constantly.
Pour the tempered egg mixture back into the pot, stirring constantly to avoid curdling. Do not bring to a boil after this step.
Stir until silky and well combined. Garnish with parsley, or additional lemon zest before serving. Enjoy!
summary: A 20-minute Greek lemon chicken soup recipe made with rotisserie chicken, pasta, and an egg-lemon mixture for a silky, dairy-free texture.

caption:
🌶️ Fajita Chicken Casserole 🥩 Protein 48g

A sizzling, cheesy, flavor-packed dish that brings all the Tex-Mex goodness in one pan! This Fajita Chicken Casserole is loaded with juicy chicken, colorful peppers, and gooey melted cheese — the perfect high-protein comfort meal. 🧀🔥

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 500g chicken breast, sliced 
✦ 1 red bell pepper, sliced 
✦ 1 green bell pepper, sliced 
✦ 1 small onion, sliced 
✦ 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese 
✦ ½ cup shredded cheddar cheese 
✦ ½ cup Greek yogurt or light cream cheese 
✦ 2 tbsp olive oil 
✦ 1 tbsp fajita seasoning 
✦ 1 tsp paprika 
✦ ½ tsp garlic powder 
✦ Salt & pepper to taste 
✦ Fresh cilantro or parsley for garnish 

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣ - Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Lightly grease a baking dish with olive oil. 

2️⃣ - In a large bowl, toss chicken strips with fajita seasoning, paprika, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 

3️⃣ - In a skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil and sauté chicken until lightly browned (not fully cooked). 

4️⃣ - Add sliced peppers and onion to the skillet and cook for another 3–4 minutes until slightly tender. 

5️⃣ - Stir in Greek yogurt (or cream cheese) until everything is coated in a creamy sauce. 

6️⃣ - Transfer mixture to the baking dish, top with mozzarella and cheddar cheese. 

7️⃣ - Bake for 20–25 minutes until bubbly and golden. 

8️⃣ - Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve warm! 

✅ Macros Summary: 
✯ Protein: 48g 
✯ Carbs: 14g 
✯ Fat: 20g 
✯ Calories: 460 kcal 
✯ Time: 35 minutes 
✯ Serving: 2 

#HighProtein #TexMex #HealthyCasserole #ChickenFajita #ComfortFood
summary: A high-protein baked fajita chicken casserole with peppers, onions, and melted cheese, ready in 35 minutes.

caption:
One pot chicken hariyali pulav ✨ 

Ingredients 
500 gms chicken with bones (250 gms boneless)
1/2 cup curd
1/2 tsp black pepper powder 
1/2 tsp salt 
1 cup basmati rice 
2 cups water
1 med size chopped onion
1 tsp jeera
For making the paste
2 cups coriander leaves
1 cup mint leaves
1 inch ginger piece
7-8 garlic cloves
2-3 green chillies 
2 tbsp curd
Make a thick paste out of it. 
1 tsp garam masala powder
2-3 slit green chillies (for extra spice)
Salt as per taste 
4-5 tbsp Ghee/ Oil 
Fried onions for garnish 
Coriander leaves for garnish 
1/2 lemon

Marinate the chicken with curd, salt & pepper powder. 
Heat up the ghee/oil. Fry jeera and onions till it's golden brown. Add the green paste and cook for 1-2 minutes. 
Add the marinated chicken and cook for 3-4 minutes. Add the garam masala powder, salt and slit green chillies and further cook for a minute. 
Add 2 cups of water into the chicken. Let the water heat up. Add washed basmati rice. Squeeze half a lemon/lime. Give a light stir and cover the pan with foil and place the lid tightly so no air escapes. 
Cook this on a low flame for about 25-30 minutes.
summary: One-pot chicken hariyali pulav recipe with green herb paste, basmati rice, and aromatic spices.

caption:
Crispy supreme pizza rolls✨

These pizza rolls are crispy, cheesy, and perfect for a cozy movie night at home this holiday season. They're made with full flavor with @tillamook Farmstyle Whole Milk Mozzarella Shreds and nutty Parmesan Shreds. Recipe can be found below and on Tillamook.com 🤍 #tillamookpartner

Filling: ▪️1 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1/2 a Small Yellow Onion, diced small▪️1/2 a Small Green Bell Pepper, diced small▪️5 Medium Baby Bella Mushrooms, chopped small▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Black Pepper▪️1/2 tsp Italian Seasoning▪️1/4 tsp Crushed Red Pepper▪️1 Mild Italian Sausage, casing removed▪️1/3 Cup Sliced Pepperoni, chopped small▪️1/2 Cup Marinara▪️1+1/2 Cups Tillamook Farmstyle Whole Milk Mozzarella Shreds▪️1/4 Cup Tillamook Parmesan Fine Shreds▪️

Rolls: ▪️1 Package Wonton Wrappers (or egg roll wrappers cut into 3.5-inch squares)▪️Water, for sealing▪️~1/4 Cup Peanut Oil (or other neutral oil)▪️Marinara or Ranch, for serving▪️

1️⃣ Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add the onion, bell pepper, mushrooms, salt, pepper, crushed red pepper, and Italian seasoning. Cook 3-4 mins until softened. Add the sausage and cook, breaking it up into small pieces, until cooked through, 4-5 mins. Stir in the pepperoni. Transfer to a bowl and let cool slightly.
2️⃣ Stir in the marinara, mozzarella, and parmesan until evenly combined.
3️⃣ Place a wonton wrapper on a clean surface with a small bowl of water nearby. Using your finger or a pastry brush, moisten the edges of the wrapper. Spoon 1 Tbsp of filling into the center. Roll up from the bottom into a log to enclose the filling, pressing to seal at the top. Press down on the skin to the left and right of the filling to seal the edges. If desired, moisten the extra skin on the sides and fold them in. 
4️⃣ Heat a thin layer of oil in a large skillet over medium heat until shimmering. Add rolls in batches and cook 1-2 mins per side until golden brown and crisp. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain. (Or coat in oil and bake at 425F convection for 10-12 mins.)
5️⃣ Let cool for 5 mins, then serve hot with extra marinara or ranch on the side for dipping.

Makes ~35 pizza rolls
summary: Crispy pan-fried pizza rolls filled with sausage, pepperoni, mushrooms, and mozzarella cheese.

caption:
HIGH PROTEIN BREAKFAST SWEET POTATOES

Here's another healthy breakfast idea thats so easy and so good.

Its perfect for a health meal prep option, and a great way to stay full and satiated all day thanks to the protein, healthy fats, and slow digesting carbs.

This comes in at 405 calories, 35g protein, 29 net carbs, and will keep you full for hours.

And if you're watching your carbs, you can always use some spaghetti or delicate squash as your base.

Here is how I made it (makes 4 servings)

1.Pop 4 regular sized purple or orange sweet potatoes (about 125g each) into the oven at 350°F for 60-70 mins. The cook time depends on thickness, so if you're using a larger potato, just got a bit longer.

2.When your sweet potato is done, let them cool for a minute or two, then cut them down the middle and you can either mash the inside with a fork, or continue as is.

3.Top with 1.5 serving (about 1/2 cup) of cottage cheese

4.For each sweet potato, I whisked up 2 eggs well and cooked it on low-medium heat with a couple pinches of salt. Add cooked eggs on top of the cottage cheese.

5.You can garnish with your favorite salsa (I quartered up a handful of cherry tomato and tossed with fresh finely chopped mint & cilantro, 1/2 tsp olive oil, and some salt), you can also add fresh cilantro, hot sauce, or my everyday cilantro sauce from my Shredhappens cookbook, or any kind of sauce you like. I also topped it with everything bagel seasoning.

As always, feel free to modify any of the toppings or even the serving size based on your own goals. You can add more egg, more cottage cheese, use a smaller sweet potato, add avocado, or whatever feels right… you do you!

If you make this healthy breakfast, let me know what you think on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #healthybreakfast #goodmoodfood #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes
summary: A high-protein baked sweet potato breakfast topped with eggs, cottage cheese, and fresh salsa, designed for satiety and meal prep.

caption:
There are dishes that fill your stomach… and then there are dishes that fill your heart. For me, Methi Matar Malai is a mix of both. 💛
Every Bombay winter, my mom would make this cozy classic — and the aroma of fresh methi, sweet peas, and gentle spices would float through the whole house. It's a recipe that tastes like warmth, comfort, and childhood memories served in a bowl.

My version is completely vegan — no malai, only cashews for that rich, creamy texture — yet it tastes just as indulgent as the one I grew up eating. Some recipes just carry emotions in them… and this is mine.

If you love nostalgic, winter-perfect comfort food, you have to try this one.

caption:
🍛 Creamy Spiced Chicken with Basmati Rice 🍗
Craving something creamy, comforting, and just the right amount of spicy? What's your go-to spice when making chicken irresistible?

Ingredients:

2 tablespoons olive oil

1 medium onion, finely chopped

3 garlic cloves, minced

1 tablespoon fresh ginger, grated

1 pound boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into chunks

1 teaspoon ground cumin

1 teaspoon ground coriander

½ teaspoon turmeric

½ teaspoon chili powder

1 teaspoon smoked paprika

1 cup canned coconut milk (unsweetened)

½ cup plain Greek yogurt

Salt and pepper to taste

1 tablespoon fresh lemon juice

Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

1 cup basmati rice

2 cups water

Pinch of salt

Directions:

Rinse the basmati rice under cold water until the water runs clear. In a medium pot, bring 2 cups of water and a pinch of salt to a boil. Add the rice, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook for 15 minutes. Let it rest covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté onions until golden, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and ginger and cook for another minute.

Add chicken and stir in cumin, coriander, turmeric, chili powder, and smoked paprika. Cook for 6–8 minutes until the chicken is browned.

Reduce heat and stir in coconut milk and yogurt. Simmer gently for 10–12 minutes until the sauce thickens and the chicken is cooked through.

Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Serve over the fluffy basmati rice and garnish with fresh cilantro.

Nutritional Information:
Prep Time: 10 minutes | Cooking Time: 30 minutes | Total Time: 40 minutes
Kcal: 490 per serving | Servings: 4

#SpicedChicken #ComfortFood #CreamyCurry #BasmatiBliss #WeeknightDinner
summary: A creamy spiced chicken curry with coconut milk and yogurt served over basmati rice, ready in 40 minutes.

caption:
Reindeer Cookie Crunch Cups 🎄🦌 
Protein | 6g per cup

Adorable, fun, and irresistibly creamy! These layered cookie-crunch dessert cups come topped with the cutest chocolate reindeer — the perfect holiday treat for parties, kids, and festive nights in!

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 1 cup chocolate cookie crumbs 
✦ 2 tbsp melted butter 
✦ 1 cup chocolate pudding 
✦ 1 cup vanilla pudding or cheesecake pudding 
✦ 1 cup whipped cream 
✦ Chocolate sprinkles 

✦ **Reindeer Toppers:** 
✦ Chocolate truffles or cake balls 
✦ Mini pretzels (antlers) 
✦ Red candy for the nose 
✦ White mini candies for eyes 
✦ Chocolate chips (ears)

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣ - Mix cookie crumbs with melted butter and divide into small glass cups. 

2️⃣ - Add a layer of chocolate pudding over the cookie base. 

3️⃣ - Add a layer of vanilla pudding on top. 

4️⃣ - Sprinkle chocolate sprinkles around the upper pudding layer. 

5️⃣ - Pipe whipped cream into a tall swirl over each cup. 

6️⃣ - For each reindeer: press pretzels into a chocolate truffle for antlers, attach candy eyes, chocolate-chip ears, and a red nose. 

7️⃣ - Place each reindeer gently onto the whipped cream swirl. Chill until ready to serve!

caption:
Spicy, buttery, creamy Garlic Thecha Prawns — the quickest high-protein dinner you'll ever make. Ready in less than half an hour, loaded with flavour, and perfect with rice, rotis, or just on its own. Save this for days you want something indulgent but still macro-friendly. 🔥

Butter Garlic Thecha Prawns 
Makes 3 Servings - 247 kcal | 32 g protein | 6.7 g carbs | 11.7 g fat per Serving

Ingredients
For the Thecha:
	•	7–8 garlic cloves
	•	8–10 bird's eye chilies OR 4 regular green chilies
	•	2 tbsp roasted peanuts
	•	½ cup fresh coriander
	•	1–2 tbsp water only if needed

For the Prawns:
	•	500 g cleaned prawns
	•	1 tbsp light butter (7 g)
	•	1 medium onion, chopped (80 g)
	•	6–7 garlic cloves, chopped
	•	½ tsp turmeric
	•	½ tsp pepper
	•	¾–1 tsp salt
	•	3 tbsp fresh cream (30 g)
	•	¼ tsp chaat masala
	•	Spring onions + lime juice

Method
	1.	Make the Thecha:
Dry roast garlic + chilies until slightly blistered. Add peanuts and roast for 1 minute. Blend with coriander into a coarse paste.
	2.	Cook the Prawns:
Heat butter. Add chopped garlic + onion; sauté until golden.
Add prawns and cook 2–3 minutes.
Add turmeric, pepper, salt.
Add 2–3 tbsp thecha, mix well, cook 3–4 minutes.
Add fresh cream and simmer 1 minute.
Finish with chaat masala, spring onions, and lime juice.

(garlic thecha prawns, high protein Indian dinner, spicy prawns recipe, quick prawn stir fry, creamy garlic prawns, macro-friendly recipes, easy recipes, 15 minute dinner ideas)

caption:
Appe is a perfect after school snack tea time snack!🤎It's so simple to make, fun to eat and you can add any vegetables you have!

I made these using Vijay Home Foods Idli & Dosa Batter which I picked up from Costco!

Ingredients:
* 1 cup @vijayhomefoods dosa & idli batter 
* 1 tsp Ghee 
* 1/2 tsp cumin seeds  
* 1/2 tsp Rai(mustard seeds) 
* Pinch Hing 
* 1/2 tsp Chana dal 
* 1/2 tsp Urad dal 
* 1 tsp Curry patta, chopped  
* 1/2 tsp Ginger, chopped  
* 1/2 tsp Green Chillies, chopped  
* 1 Tbsp Grated coconut  
* 1 Tbsp Onions, finely chopped  
* 1/2 tsp Salt 
* 1 Tbsp Grated carrots  
* 1 Tbsp Bell Pepper, chopped  
* 1 Tbsp Tomato, chopped  
* Ghee to cook Appe 
 
Method  
 
* Heat 1 tsp ghee on low heat.  
* Add cumin and mustard seeds & stir 
* Add Hing, Chana & urad dal.  
* Stir for a minute or so until fragrant.  
* Add Ginger, green chili, coconut and onions  
* Add salt, carrots, bell peppers and tomatoes.  
* Stir for few minutes.  
* Let it cool down. Add this to the batter.  
* Heat Paniyaram pan on low medium heat.  
* Add 1/2t ghee in every cavity of the pan.  
* Pour 1T batter on each section.  
* Cover and cook for 2-3 minutes until light brown, flip it and let it cook on the other side.  
* Take it out and serve it hot with coconut chutney and cilantro/ mint chutney.  
* Garnish with coconut and cilantro.
* Enjoy!!🤎

#VijayHomeFoods #DesiMadeEasy #VijayIdliDosaBatter #SouthIndianFood #CostcoFinds

caption:
Easy 1-Pan Lemon Orzo Pasta with Shrimp in under 30 minutes! 😮‍💨 (recipe 👇🏽)

Full recipe is in my meal prep magic digital cookbook! ON SALE NOW 50% off ‼️ COMMENT "cookbook" and I'll send you the link 🤭

Macros (x3 servings): 455 calories |43g P | 54g C | 10g F

Ingredients :
1 lb. raw shrimp, peeled and deveined 
S&P, to taste
Paprika, 1/2 tsp 
Avocado oil spray, 3-4 seconds 
1 shallot, finely minced 
3-4 garlic cloves, minced
1  cup dry orzo pasta
2.5-3 cups lower-sodium chicken or vegetable broth
Fat free half n half, 1/4 cup 
Zest and juice of 1 lemon
Asparagus bunch, chopped
Parmesan cheese, 2-3 tbsp
1 Tbsp. butter 

Garnish (optional):
Chopped parsley 
Chili flakes 

Instructions:
1. Season shrimp with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika
2. In a pan, on medium high heat, spray avocado oil then sear the shrimp and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side. Remove shrimp and set to the side.
3. In the same pan, turn heat to medium low, add shallots and minced garlic and sauté until fragrant, about 1-2 minutes 
4. Stir in orzo pasta and let it toast for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
5. Gradually add broth to the orzo, stirring often, until the orzo is cooked and creamy, about 10-15 minutes. Add more broth as needed to keep the orzo from sticking.
6. Add lemon juice+zest, half n half, Parmesan cheese and the asparagus 
7. Garnish parsley and chili flakes then enjoy 😍 #healthyrecipes #easyrecipes #icekarim
summary: One-pan lemon orzo pasta with shrimp and asparagus, ready in under 30 minutes.

caption:
Packed with fresh, seasonal vegetables, ricotta cheese, and marinara sauce, this delicious and easy Veggie Lasagna will make even the most die-hard meat-lover ask for seconds.

1-15oz  whole milk ricotta cheese
1 cup each: mozzarella,colby jack + yellow cheddar cheese 
24oz pasta sauce – arrabbiata sauce 
2 cups broccoli florets finely chopped
1 bag fresh spinach finely chopped
1 bell pepper finely chopped
1 onion finely chopped
⅓ cup frozen peas
2 jalapenos finely chopped
5 cloves garlic finely minced
2 tbsp Italian seasoning
3 tbsp olive oil
salt and pepper to taste
1 box lasagna sheets 

◽️Preheat your oven to 350°F, cook pasta according to package instructions and drain well.
◽️In a large pan, heat some olive oil and add the onions, garlic and jalapeños. Cook for 2-3 mins and add the  bell peppers, broccoli and peas along with all the seasonings. Stir and cover the pan with a lid so broccoli and pea's can fully cook. About 4-5 mins on med – low heat.
◽️Add the spinach and cover the pan with a lid for 3 mins. Let the spinach wilt down. Once this mixture is cooked and cooled off, mix in the ricotta cheese in.
◽️Assemble the lasagna: Pour ⅓ of the sauce into the bottom of the baking pan/dish. Layer on 3-4 lasagna sheets. Top with veggie mixture, cheese. Repeat this: sauce, lasagna sheets, veggie filling and cheese. Top with another layer of lasagna sheets. Top with the additional 1 cup of sauce & the cheese.
◽️Cover the baking dish with foil and bake at 350°F for 20 mins. Serve while hot and enjoy with a side of garlic bread, salad or just as!

Full recipe is on:

caption:
Spicy, garlicky, hearty, nourishing — this Kohlapuri Style Sprouts Garlic Dal is proof that sprouts don't need to be boring or bland. 
I cooked it in my brass Lagan from @ptal.in — which offers even heat distribution, 93% nutrient retention, and natural antimicrobial properties that support better digestion and immunity.

Ptal's Black Friday sale is officially LIVE on their website, so this is the best time to grab their beautiful utensils. ✨

Full recipe
Servings: 3
Macros (per serving): ~215 kcal | 11g protein | 22g carbs | 8g fat

Ingredients
1 tbsp oil
1 bay leaf
1 medium onion, chopped
1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
1 medium tomato, chopped
1 cup green coriander leaves
2–3 green chillies
1 cup green mung sprouts
½ tsp red chilli powder
½ tsp turmeric powder
½ tsp garam masala
1 tsp chana masala
1 tsp coriander powder
Salt to taste
2 cups water (adjust)

Potli (tie in muslin cloth)
½ tsp cumin seeds
½ tsp black pepper
½ tsp fennel seeds
2 cloves
1 green cardamom
½ star anise
1 small piece cinnamon

Tadka
1 tbsp ghee
½ tsp cumin seeds
1 tbsp chopped garlic
½ tsp Kashmiri chilli powder

Method
	1.	Heat oil and add a bay leaf. Sauté onions and ginger-garlic paste till fragrant.
	2.	Add chopped tomatoes and a fresh paste of coriander leaves + green chillies.
	3.	Add red chilli, turmeric, garam masala, chana masala, coriander powder — sauté well.
	4.	Add green mung sprouts, salt, water, and the spice potli. Cover and cook till sprouts soften and gravy thickens.
	5.	Remove the potli. Make a tadka of ghee, cumin seeds, garlic, and Kashmiri chilli powder.
	6.	Pour over the dal, mix well, and serve hot with steamed rice or rotis.

.

(kolhapuri sprouts dal, garlic dal, mung sprouts recipes, healthy Indian meals, brass cookware, ptal utensils, traditional Indian recipes)
summary: A Kolhapuri-style spiced mung sprout dal recipe with detailed instructions and nutritional macros.

caption:
Indori Poha

A light breakfast from Indore known for its sweet-savory balance, crunchy toppings, and the signature Jeeravan Masala.

Ingredients
Poha
1 cup poha
1 onion, finely chopped
1 green chilli
1 teaspoon mustard seeds
1 teaspoon cumin seeds
½ teaspoon turmeric
½ teaspoon fennel seeds
Pinch of hing
½ teaspoon sugar
Salt
Lemon juice
2 tablespoons peanuts
Fresh coriander, chopped

Jeeravan Masala:

1 teaspoon fennel seeds
1 teaspoon coriander seeds
1 teaspoon cumin seeds
½ teaspoon black salt
½ teaspoon regular salt
½ teaspoon amchoor
8 dried red chillies
1 small mace
2–3 cassia leaves
2–3 cloves
Pinch of nutmeg
Pinch of hing
¼ teaspoon turmeric
¼ teaspoon black pepper

Method:

Jeeravan:
Roast fennel, coriander, cumin, red chillies, mace, cassia, cloves, nutmeg on low heat. Cool. Grind to a powder. Mix with salts, amchoor, turmeric, black pepper, hing.

Poha:
Rinse poha and drain.
Heat oil. Add mustard, cumin, fennel. When they splutter, add onion, chilli, hing.

Add sugar, salt, turmeric, lemon. Toss in the poha.
Cover and steam on low for 10 minutes.

Top with coriander, chopped onion, peanuts, sev, and a heavy sprinkle of Jeeravan.
summary: Recipe for Indori Poha, a sweet-savory Indian breakfast with homemade Jeeravan Masala spice blend and crunchy toppings.
