caption:
🍗 Garlic Herb Chicken with Crispy Potatoes & Green Beans 🥔🌿
Craving a one-pan meal with juicy chicken, golden potatoes, and garlicky green beans? What's your go-to seasoning combo for oven-roasted dishes?

Ingredients:

2 large boneless skinless chicken breasts

1 lb baby potatoes, halved

8 oz fresh green beans, trimmed

3 tablespoons olive oil

1 tablespoon Dijon mustard

3 cloves garlic, minced

1 tablespoon chopped fresh parsley (or 1 tsp dried)

1 teaspoon dried oregano

1 teaspoon smoked paprika

Salt and pepper to taste

Optional: grated Parmesan for finishing

Directions:

Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly grease a large baking dish or sheet pan.

In a small bowl, mix olive oil, Dijon mustard, garlic, parsley, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper.

Place chicken breasts in the center of the dish. Rub generously with half the herb mixture.

Toss potatoes in the remaining herb mix and scatter them around the chicken.

Bake for 25 minutes, uncovered.

Remove pan, add green beans, drizzle with a little olive oil and season lightly. Toss gently.

Return to oven and bake 15–20 minutes more, until chicken is cooked through (165°F) and potatoes are golden and tender.

Optional: sprinkle Parmesan on top before serving.

Nutritional Information:
Prep Time: 10 minutes | Cooking Time: 40 minutes | Total Time: 50 minutes
Kcal: 520 per serving | Servings: 4
#OnePanMeal #GarlicHerbChicken #CrispyPotatoes #EasyDinner #sheetpanrecipes
summary: Sheet pan recipe for garlic herb chicken with crispy potatoes and green beans, ready in 50 minutes.

caption:
🍗 Texas Roadhouse Smothered Chicken | Protein: 52g

A juicy, tender grilled chicken breast smothered in sautéed mushrooms, onions, and melted cheese — just like at Texas Roadhouse! This high-protein, low-carb comfort meal will make you feel like you're dining out right from your kitchen. 🤠🔥

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (8 oz each) 
✦ 1 tbsp olive oil 
✦ ½ tsp salt 
✦ ½ tsp black pepper 
✦ ½ tsp paprika 
✦ ½ cup sliced mushrooms 
✦ ½ cup sliced onions 
✦ ¼ cup low-sodium chicken broth 
✦ ¼ cup shredded mozzarella cheese 
✦ ¼ cup shredded Monterey Jack cheese 
✦ 1 tsp garlic powder 
✦ 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce 

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣ Season chicken breasts with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder. 

2️⃣ Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium heat and sear chicken for 5–6 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through (internal temp 165°F). Remove and set aside. 

3️⃣ In the same skillet, add onions and mushrooms. Sauté until softened and caramelized (about 5–7 minutes). 

4️⃣ Deglaze the pan with chicken broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping any brown bits for flavor. 

5️⃣ Return chicken to the skillet, top with the mushroom-onion mixture, and sprinkle both cheeses over the top. 

6️⃣ Cover and cook on low until cheese is melted and bubbly (about 2–3 minutes). 

7️⃣ Serve hot with a side of green beans or mashed cauliflower for a Texas-style feast! 🤩 

✅ Macros Summary:

✯ Protein: 52g 
✯ Carbs: 8g 
✯ Fat: 18g 
✯ Calories: 410 kcal 
✯ Time: 25 minutes 
✯ Serving: 2 

#️⃣ #HighProteinDinner #TexasRoadhouseCopycat #HealthyComfortFood #LowCarbRecipes #ChickenLovers
summary: A Texas Roadhouse-inspired grilled chicken breast topped with sautéed mushrooms, onions, and melted cheese.

caption:
Mike's viral homemade donner 

This is my take on @mezemike mum's donner hack! All I've done is changed the seasonings to suit my preference but full credit goes to him and his mum for the genius idea that I never knew existed before he posted it - now it's something I'll def be making again and again!

Recipe: this serves approx 6-8 people
- 1 kg lamb or beef mince
- 2 onions
- 4 tbsp yoghurt
- 2 tsp garlic puree
- 1 tsp garlic powder
- 2 tsp onion powder
- 2 tsp oregano
- 3 tsp paprika
- 3 tsp pul biber (Aleppo red pepper flakes)
- 1 tsp red chilli flakes 
- 2 tsp ground cumin
- 2 tsp salt
- 1 tsp black pepper
- 3 tbsp olive oil

For the garlic tahini sauce:
- 1 cup yoghurt
- 1/2 cup tahini
- 2 garlic cloves, minced
- 1/2 lemon, juiced

For the Salad:
- 2 red onions
- 3-4 tbsp chopped fresh coriander or parsley
- 1 tsp pul biber or sumac
- 1/2 lemon, juiced

For the butter drizzle:
- 3 tbsp butter
- 1 tsp pul biber

METHOD:
1. Blitz the onions into a paste, then add the meat, yoghurt, spices and oil to a food processor and mix well; if not using a processor, combine the blitzed onions with all the other ingredients and knead the meat until everything is fully incorporated.
2. Divide the mixture into four portions, flatten each one with your fingers onto a sheet of baking paper (wet your fingers to prevent sticking), place another sheet of baking paper on top and roll it out as thinly as possible, then roll or fold the sheet over and transfer it to a baking tray deep enough to catch the juices.
3. Bake at 200°C for 35 minutes, removing halfway to drain any liquid from the tray.
4. Remove the cooked donner, tear it into slices and either return it to the oven for 5 minutes for browning or sauté the slices in a pan with a little oil.

#doner #fakeaway #kebab #homemade #easyrecipes #donner
summary: Homemade oven-baked doner kebab recipe with spiced lamb mince, tahini sauce, and fresh salad.

caption:
Add these honey mustard brussels sprouts and butternut squash to your thanksgiving spread! 
I partnered with @sbrbbq to create a delicious and easy veggie side dish for the holidays! 
Ingredients:
1.5 lbs brussels sprouts, halved and trimmed
2 cups cubed butternut squash
2 tbsp olive oil
1/2 tsp nugmeg
Pinch of salt
1/2 cup raw pecan halves
1/3 cup sweet baby ray's honey mustard sauce
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (you can also make these in the air fryer to save oven space)
And lightly spray a large baking sheet or line with parchment paper.
Toss the brussels sprouts and squash with olive oil, nutmeg and salt then add to the baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake for about 18 minutes.
Remove from the oven and add the pecans then drizzle on the honey mustard sauce and toss to evenly coat.
Bake again for 10-12 more minutes.
Enjoy! #sbrbbqpartner

caption:
Sheet Pan Chicken Thigh Fajitas are a super easy and delicious dinner! 

INGREDIENTS
2 1/2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs 
1-2 tbsp oil 
1 tsp each salt and pepper
2 tsp each cumin, dried cilantro, garlic powder
4 tsp chili powder 
1 lime, juiced 
4 bell peppers, sliced 
1 large onion, sliced 
Salt, pepper, garlic powder 
Fresh cilantro 

INSTRUCTIONS 
First, slice all of the peppers and the onion. Spray a large sheet pan with cooking spray and add the peppers and onions. Drizzle them with a little bit of oil and season them with some salt, pepper, and garlic powder. 

Next, add about 2 1/2 lbs of boneless skinlesss chicken thighs to a bowl and add some oil. Season with salt, pepper, cumin, dried cilantro, garlic powder, and chili powder. Add the juice of one lime and stir everything together well until the chicken is well coated in seasoning. 

Add the seasoned chicken thighs to the sheet pan. I like to make little holes between the peppers and onions for each chicken thigh. 

Bake in the oven at 400 degrees F for about 35 minutes, or until the chicken reaches an internal temp of at least 165 degrees F. I personally like them cooked closer to 185 degrees F. 

Pour off any extra liquid (if needed) and broil for a couple of minutes if desired. We like to get a little char on the veggies under the broiler. 

Top the sheet pan with cilantro. Serve with tortillas, homemade guac, or some pico de gallo! This is also really good served over some Spanish rice if you aren't feeling tortillas. 

#easyrecipes #sheetpanmeal #chickenthighs #fajitas
summary: Sheet pan chicken thigh fajitas with peppers and onions, baked at 400°F for 35 minutes.

caption:
🔥 Garlic Butter Chicken Bites (Quick + Juicy!) 🍗🧈✨

This one is for my busy-night people — tender, golden chicken bites tossed in a rich garlic-butter sauce with fresh herbs. Easy, cozy, and SO delicious. Save this for dinner inspo tonight! 😍

---

🛒 Ingredients

1.5 lbs chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces

1 tsp salt

½ tsp black pepper

1 tsp paprika

1 tsp garlic powder

2 tbsp olive oil

4 tbsp butter

5–6 cloves garlic, minced

1 tbsp lemon juice

Fresh parsley, chopped

---

👩‍🍳 Instructions

1. Season chicken with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder.

2. Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook until golden and fully cooked. Remove from pan.

3. Lower heat, add butter and garlic. Sauté until fragrant.

4. Add chicken back into the pan, toss in the garlic-butter, and finish with lemon juice + parsley.

#dinnerinspo #easyrecipesathome #garlicbutterchicken #skilletmeals #weeknightdinnerideas

caption:
🔥🌶️ Get ready to spice up your Thanksgiving with this mouthwatering Hot & Spicy Enchilada Bean Dip! 🎉

Perfect dip that can be made in one skillet & under 30 mins!

INGREDIENTS
2 tbsp oil
1 small onion - diced
2 Anaheim peppers or poblano peppers - diced
2 tsp each: salt, ground cumin, oregano, smoked paprika
1 cup cream cheese
1/4 cup sour cream
1/4 cup Herdez salsa verde
1/3 cup enchilada sauce
1/2 cup pinto beans
1/2 cup black beans
1 cup Colby jack cheese
1 cup pepper jack cheese

INSTRUCTIONS
▫️Preheat oven to 350F. Saute onions and peppers for 2 mins.
▫️Add all of the seasonings followed by cream cheese and sour cream. Mix until cream cheese is fully incorporated.
▫️Add the salsa verde, enchilada sauce and beans. Mix until everything is nice and well incorporated. Turn off the heat and top it off with shredded cheese.
▫️Bake in the oven at 350F for 15 mins or until cheese is nice and melted. Garnish with cilantro, scallions and additional sauces if needed, Enjoy!

Full recipe can be found on:
https://thetableofspice.com/recipes/spicy-enchilada-bean-dip/

caption:
💥5 Minute Pomegranate Pistachio Feta Spread

This is probably the lowest effort, highest reward recipe I've made this year and it is SO good, especially if you love mediterranean food or mediterranean flavors.

It's bright, fresh, creamy, crunchy, tangy, sweet, and herby all at once. It's the kind of dish that people hover around

What makes it hit is the contrast. Cold whipped feta as the base, the burst of pomegranate, the crunch from pistachios, and the freshness from parsley and mint. Every bite has something different going on. It feels restaurant-level without the restaurant effort.

Serve it with whatever you have on hand. Pita, lavash, sourdough, crackers. It works for holidays, dinner parties, or those nights when you want something fast but elevated.

Ingredients
1 cup thick Greek yogurt
3 to 4 ounces feta
Zest and juice of 1 lemon
1 tablespoon honey (optional)
Seeds of 1 pomegranate
3 to 4 tablespoons crushed pistachios
2 to 3 tablespoons finely chopped parsley
2 to 3 tablespoons finely chopped mint
Pinch of sumac
Extra feta crumbles
Olive oil
Optional balsamic glaze

Here is how I made it:

1.Add the Greek yogurt, feta, lemon zest, lemon juice, and honey (if using) to a food processor and blend until smooth. Taste and adjust as needed.

2.Spread the whipped feta on a board or plate.

3.Add the pomegranate seeds evenly over the top.

4.Sprinkle the crushed pistachios, parsley, and mint across the surface.

5.Finish with a dusting of sumac, extra feta crumbles, a drizzle of olive oil, and optional balsamic glaze.

Serve with pita, lavash, sourdough, or your favorite crackers.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #mediterraneanfood #healthyfoodshare
summary: A 5-minute Mediterranean whipped feta and pomegranate spread with pistachios, herbs, and sumac.

caption:
Avocado cucumber wonton crisps✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Wonton Crisps:▪️~1 Cup Peanut Oil (or other neutral frying oil)▪️8 Wonton Skins, cut in half along the diagonal▪️Kosher Salt▪️

Avocado Cucumber Salad:▪️1 Small Persian Cucumber, diced small (3 oz)▪️1 Medium Avocado, diced small (6 oz)▪️1 Scallion, sliced▪️1 tsp Soy Sauce▪️1 tsp Rice Vinegar▪️1 tsp Toasted Sesame Oil▪️1 tsp Sesame Seeds▪️1 Pinch Salt▪️

Serving:▪️Chives, for garnish (optional)▪️Sesame Seeds▪️

1️⃣ Pour 1/4-inch of peanut oil in a large heavy bottom skillet. Heat over medium heat until it reaches 325F (check the temp with a meat thermometer or place a bit of a wonton skin in the oil and when it bubbles and sizzles it's ready). Line a baking sheet with paper towel. 
2️⃣ Working in batches, place the wonton skins in the skillet, leaving space in between each. Fry on the first side until golden brown around the edges (30 secs - 1 min), then flip and fry on the other side until the bottoms are deep golden brown (30 secs - 1 min). Transfer to the paper towel lined baking sheet to drain excess oil and sprinkle with kosher salt.
3️⃣ In a medium bowl, add the Persian cucumber, avocado, scallion, soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sesame seeds, and salt. Stir gently until all ingredients are evenly distributed. Taste and adjust seasoning if desired.
4️⃣ Line a white serving dish with chives (optional, but I think it looks pretty :). Spoon 1 Tbsp of avocado cucumber salad on each wonton crisp and place them on the serving platter. Sprinkle with sesame seed and enjoy!

⏲30 minute total prep + cook time

Recipe makes 16 bites

You can prep the wonton crisps a few hours in advance. However, I recommend waiting to toss the avocado cucumber salad and adding it to the crisps just before serving. Hope you love these!
summary: A recipe for crispy fried wonton crisps topped with avocado cucumber salad, ready in 30 minutes.

caption:
Taste and macros both equally crazy. 🔥
Made this spicy, garlicky Iffa Fish with boneless tilapia, a chatpata red marinade, and a silky smooth healthy mayo-ish sauce, and I have to admit, it really is the easiest high-protein meal prep or weeknight dinner.
Save this for your next quick protein-rich meal 🔥

Iffa Fish 
Makes 3 Servings. 
Macros (per serving): 197 kcal | 36 g protein | 1.5 g carbs | 6 g  fat

Ingredients:
For the marinade
	•	500g boneless tilapia (or any fish fillets)
	•	1 tbsp ginger–garlic paste
	•	1–2 green chillies
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri red chilli powder
	•	¼ tsp turmeric
	•	1 tsp coriander powder
	•	½ tsp cumin powder
	•	½ tsp garam masala
	•	1 tsp vinegar
	•	Salt to taste

For the white sauce
	•	2 boiled egg whites
	•	1 low-fat cheese slice
	•	¼ cup hot water
	•	2 garlic cloves
	•	1 tsp vinegar
	•	Pinch of salt
	•	½ tsp sugar (optional)
	•	1 tsp oil

For cooking
	•	1/2 tsp oil
	•	Coriander leaves
	•	Red chilli flakes

Method:
1. Marinate fish.
Coat the tilapia with the marinade ingredients evenly and rest for 15–20 minutes.

2. Make the white sauce
Blend egg whites, cheese, garlic, vinegar, hot water, salt, sugar, and oil until creamy and slightly thick.

3. Pan-fry the fish
Heat a nonstick pan and spray lightly with oil.
Place the marinated fillets and cook 2–3 minutes per side until just golden.
Lower heat, add a tbsp of water, cover for 1 minute and cook to keep the fish moist. 

4. Add the white sauce
Pour the sauce over the fish.
Cook covered on low heat for 30 seconds.

5. Garnish
Top with coriander leaves and serve hot.

.
(high protein fish recipe, healthy fish fry, macro friendly Indian meals, creamy fish recipe, grilled fish, weight loss dinner ideas, low carb fish meal, high protein, healthy Indian cooking, healthy mayo, nutritionist recipes, fitness friendly meals)
.

#HighProteinRecipes #healthyrecipes #iffafish
summary: High-protein Iffa Fish recipe with spiced tilapia fillets and creamy egg-white sauce, macro-optimized for fitness and weight loss.

Garlic Parmesan Chicken Wings 🐔😋🙌

What you need:
12 chicken wingettes
2-4 tbsp butter, melted
1/2 cup grated Parmesan
2 tsp minced garlic
Salt, pepper and paprika

How to make them:
1. Combine all ingredients until the wings are thoroughly coated. 
2. Air fry @ 375F for 12 mins, flip then air fry another 12 mins. Take them out, brush with garlic butter and enjoy!

To make garlic butter just mix 2 tbsp melted butter, 1 tsp minced garlic and 1 tsp parsley.

caption:
🥦 Broccoli & Cauliflower Cottage Cheese Bake | Protein: 32g

A golden, cheesy, high-protein veggie bake made with cottage cheese and loaded with tender broccoli and cauliflower. Creamy, comforting, and perfect for a nutritious meal or side dish! 

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 2 cups broccoli florets 
✦ 2 cups cauliflower florets 
✦ 1 cup low-fat cottage cheese 
✦ ½ cup shredded cheddar cheese 
✦ ¼ cup grated Parmesan cheese 
✦ 2 large eggs 
✦ ¼ cup milk (or unsweetened almond milk) 
✦ 1 tsp garlic powder 
✦ ½ tsp onion powder 
✦ ½ tsp salt 
✦ ¼ tsp black pepper 

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 190°C (375°F) and lightly grease a medium baking dish. 

2️⃣ Steam or microwave broccoli and cauliflower until slightly tender (about 3–4 minutes), then drain well. 

3️⃣ In a large bowl, whisk together cottage cheese, eggs, milk, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. 

4️⃣ Add the steamed vegetables and half of the cheddar cheese. Stir until well combined. 

5️⃣ Pour mixture into the baking dish and top with the remaining cheddar and Parmesan cheese. 

6️⃣ Bake for 25–30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and bubbly. 

7️⃣ Let cool for 5 minutes before serving. Enjoy warm! 

✅ Macros Summary: 
✯ Protein: 32g 
✯ Carbs: 10g 
✯ Fat: 12g 
✯ Calories: 270 kcal 
✯ Time: 35 minutes 
✯ Serving: 1 portion (makes 4 servings) 

#HighProteinMeal #CottageCheeseRecipe #HealthyBake #LowCarbComfortFood #BroccoliCauliflower
summary: A baked cottage cheese and vegetable casserole with broccoli, cauliflower, and cheese providing 32g of protein per serving.

caption:
📝 Ingredientes:
300 g de farinha de trigo
180 ml de leite
75 g de manteiga
2 g de fermento biológico seco
1 colher (chá) de fermento químico
7 g de sal
2 colheres (sopa) de gergelim torrado
Salsinha picada (ou outra erva fresca)

👩‍🍳 Modo de preparo:
Misture o leite com o fermento biológico e deixe ativar por 5 minutos.
Acrescente a manteiga, o sal e o fermento químico.
Adicione a farinha aos poucos até formar uma massa macia, junte o gergelim e a salsinha e sove levemente.
Deixe descansar 30 minutos.
Abra a massa fina, corte em quadradinhos e asse a 170 °C até dourar.
Ficam crocantes por dias se bem guardados!

✨ Bora fazer?

⸻

🍪 Crunchy Sesame & Herb Crackers

📝 Ingredients:
300 g all-purpose flour
180 ml milk
75 g butter
2 g instant yeast
1 tsp baking powder
7 g salt
2 tbsp toasted sesame seeds
Chopped parsley (or fresh herbs)

👩‍🍳 Instructions:
Mix milk and yeast, let rest for 5 minutes.
Add butter, salt, and baking powder.
Gradually add flour until soft dough forms, then mix in sesame and herbs.
Let rest 30 minutes.
Roll thin, cut into squares, and bake at 170 °C (340 °F) until golden.
Crispy, savory, and delicious for days!

caption:
Garlic Butter Cod Strips with Cilantro Lime Sauce 🐟🧈🌿 
Protein: 27 g

Perfectly seared cod strips with a golden garlic-butter crust, served over a zesty cilantro lime sauce—fresh, vibrant, and incredibly flavorful!

📝 Ingredients:
✦ 1 lb cod fillets (cut into strips) 
✦ 2 tbsp butter 
✦ 2 tbsp olive oil 
✦ 1 tsp garlic powder 
✦ 1/2 tsp paprika 
✦ 1/2 tsp black pepper 
✦ 1/2 tsp salt 
✦ 1 tbsp fresh parsley (chopped)

Cilantro Lime Sauce:
✦ 1/2 cup sour cream 
✦ 1 tbsp lime juice 
✦ 1 tsp lime zest 
✦ 2 tbsp cilantro (finely chopped) 
✦ 1 clove garlic (minced) 
✦ Pinch of salt & pepper 

👩‍🍳 Instructions:

1️⃣- Season cod strips with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper. 

2️⃣- Heat butter and olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. 
  Sear cod strips 3–4 minutes per side until golden with crispy edges. 

3️⃣- Whisk sauce ingredients in a bowl until smooth. 

4️⃣- Spread cilantro lime sauce on a plate and place the hot cod strips on top. 

5️⃣- Garnish with parsley and serve immediately. 

✅ Macros Summary:
✯ Protein: 27 g 
✯ Carbs: 4 g 
✯ Fat: 15 g 
✯ Calories: 240 
✯ Time: 15 minutes ⏱️ 
✯ Serving: 1 🍽️

caption:
Apple Mithai 🍎 
Such a simple recipe but super yummy!
Recipe; served 4
Ingredients;
4 apples
2 small cups almond flour 
1/2 cup mixed dryfruits diced 
Method;
Wash, peel & grate the apples. Put into a bowl & microwave bowl for 2 minutes. Remove mix and add in the almond flour & mix thoroughly. Microwave for 2 minutes again. Remove, mix and microwave for a minute again and it's ready to be garnished with the dryfruits, I have also garnished with some dry rose petals & a little varak which is optional!
You can always add a little sugar if you like it sweeter, I prefer not to 😂
Such a super fast & yummy recipe…
Do you agree ?

caption:
Got an old wrinkly aubergine? Don't bin it, man. @whatinthechef's gonna show you how to turn it into a banging dip.

Serves 4 
Total time: 45mins 

Ingredients 

1 large aubergine 
3 garlic cloves 
1 tbsp ghee 
0.5 tsp cumin seeds 
0.5 tsp mustard seeds (optional) 
0.5 small onion, finely chopped 
1 green or red chilli (optional, to taste) 
0.25 tsp turmeric 
Salt, to taste 
Fresh coriander (optional) 
Squeeze of lemon juice (optional) 

Method

1.  Preheat oven to 220°C (fan 200°C).

2.  Poke holes in the aubergine, then wrap it with the garlic cloves in foil. Roast on a tray or directly on the rack for 30 minutes, turning once, until completely soft 

3.  Unwrap the foil slightly and let it rest for 5 minutes to let the steam escape. Peel off the skin and mash the aubergine and garlic together

4.  Heat ghee in a pan, then add cumin seeds and mustard seeds. Cook for 30 seconds until they sizzle and pop

5.  Add chopped onion and cook until golden. Stir in turmeric, salt, and green chilli

6.  Add the aubergine mash and cook on medium for 3–5 minutes until combined

7.  Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander. Serve with warm pitta

#auberginebharta #bharta #eggplantdip #mashedAubergine #vegetarian
summary: Recipe for aubergine bharta, a roasted eggplant dip seasoned with cumin, mustard seeds, and turmeric.

▪️4 Slices Brioche Bread▪️2 oz. Gruyere, sliced thin▪️2 oz. Cheddar Cheese, sliced thin▪️2 Tbsp + 2 tsp Butter, divided▪️

2️⃣ Evenly divide the thinly sliced cheese between the two sandwiches. 
3️⃣ In a large pan over medium heat, add 2 Tbsp butter and cook until it melts and starts to bubble and foam (1-2 mins).
4️⃣ Add the sandwiches and cook until deep golden brown on the first side, about 1 min 30 seconds. (Lift a corner of a sandwich every 30 seconds to check the progress.) Flip and cook on the second side for 30-45 secs until deep golden brown. Transfer the sandwiches to a small sheet pan. 
5️⃣ Spread 1 tsp softened butter on the top of each sandwich, and bake for 2-5 mins until the cheese is fully melted. Slice and enjoy!

⏲15 minute total prep + cook time

Recipe makes 2 sandwiches

caption:
Chicken & Chorizo Paella

Not authentic but has all the Spanish flavours and great for meal prep and high in protein.

Serves 4. 47g protein per serving and 640 cals per serving 

You need:
-1 tbsp olive oil 
-550g chicken breast diced
-90g chorizo sliced 
-1 red pepper diced
-1 carrot diced
-1 onion diced 
-3 minced cloves garlic 
-300g paella rice 
-approx 1l chicken stock
-1 tbsp smoked paprika
-2 tbsp tomato purée 
-handful frozen peas
-handful fresh parsley
-fresh lemon to serve 

How:
1) in a pan add chorizo, chicken, carrot onion, garlic & smoked paprika to pan with oil and sauté for 5 mins.

2) after 5 mins add tomato purée, rice & a chicken stock cube. Add red pepper now too

3) add 600ml water and cook until absorbed (stir regularly). I ended up adding about 1l water total but this will depend on your brand of rice so look at quantity of water/rice ratio. 

4) when the rice is plump and drags from the bottom of the pan, add your frozen peas.. stir and turn off the heat for 10mins 

5) before serving squeeze over lemon and add fresh parsley and enjoy!

#paella #onepanmeal #onepotmeal #mealpreplife #mealprepideas
summary: One-pan chicken and chorizo paella recipe with 47g protein per serving, designed for meal prep.

caption:
Roasted tomato soup✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Soup:▪️2 lb. Ripe Vine Tomatoes, halved ▪️1 Medium Yellow Onion, cut in 8 wedges ▪️4 Garlic Cloves▪️1 Tbsp Fresh Oregano, chopped▪️3 Tbsp Olive Oil, plus more for serving▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt ▪️1/2 tsp Freshly Ground Black Pepper ▪️1 tsp Sugar ▪️1 Cup Whole Milk, warmed▪️Basil Leaves, for serving (optional)▪️

Cheese Crisps: ▪️6 Tbsp Shredded Cheddar Cheese▪️6 Tbsp Grated Parmesan▪️

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 425F. In a large baking dish, add the tomatoes, onion, garlic, and oregano. Drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle with salt, pepper, and sugar. Toss to evenly coat all veggies. Arrange the tomatoes so they are cut side up and roast for about 1 hour, or until soft and caramelized around the edges.
2️⃣ Meanwhile, line a baking sheet with parchment. Spoon six 1 Tbsp mounds of shredded cheddar onto the sheet, leaving space between each, and flatten slightly. Top each with 1 tablespoon of grated Parmesan and flatten again into thin rounds. Place on the rack below the vegetables and bake for 7-9 minutes, until golden brown and crisp. Let cool on the baking sheet for 10 mins until set, then transfer to a paper towel lined plate. 
3️⃣ Transfer the roasted tomatoes, onion, and garlic (with any juices) to a blender. Add the warm milk and blend until smooth and creamy. Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt or pepper if desired. (If your veggies cooled before blending, you can transfer the soup to a pot and gently simmer until heated to your liking.)
4️⃣ Ladle the soup into bowls, then add a cheese crisp (you'll have extras!) and drizzle with olive oil. Garnish with basil, if using, and enjoy!

⏲15 min prep time / 1 hour 15 min total time

Serves 4 as an appetizer
summary: Roasted tomato soup recipe with homemade cheese crisps, served with olive oil and basil.

caption:
Save my One Pan Marry Me Lentils Recipe Below! & If you love fast and simple comfort foods made healthier comment "BLUEPRINT" down below & I'll send you the link for the masterclass Dec 1st 

Recipe 
Vegan, GF
Serves 4

Ingredients 
* 1/2 cup sun-dried tomatoes in oil
* 4 garlic cloves minced
* 1 cup yellow onion finely diced
* 1 tbsp Italian seasoning
* 1 1/2 tbsp tomato pas
* 1½ cups red lentils
* 1 tbsp gf tamari 
* 2 1/2 cups vegetable broth
* 1 15oz can full-fat coconut milk
* 3 tbsp nutritional yeast
* 1 tsp sea salt
* 1/2 tsp ground black pepper
* 2 cups baby spinach 
* 1 tbsp lemon juice
* parsley for garnish 

1. Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add the sundried tomatoes with oil included. Add in the garlic and onion and sauté for 2-3 minutes until fragrant.
2. Add the Italian seasoning and tomato paste, and stir for about 30 seconds to caramelize the tomato paste.
3. Add the lentils, tamari, vegetable broth, coconut milk, and nutritional yeast and stir to combine. Increase heat to bring to a boil, and then reduce heat so it's at a gentle simmer. Cover and cook, stirring occasionally, for 15 - 20 minutes until the lentils have softened.
4. Stir in the lemon juice and spinach. Cook just until spinach is wilted. Top with chopped parsley right before serving.

caption:
It's soup season and I want to introduce you to one very close to my heart: Bieler Soup. 

Dr. Bieler, the physician who my dad credits with saving his life through nutrition, created this recipe and wrote the book "Food Is Your Best Medicine." 

My dad worked with him in the 1940s and followed his protocol religiously and I've carried on that ritual ever since! Dad credits this exact recipe with saving his life, rebuilding his health, and giving him the foundation for all of his success.

It's simple. It's powerful. It's love in a cup. I highly recommend you get it!

Bieler Soup Recipe

Ingredients:

6 cups of the cleanest water or broth (vegetable or meat-based — your choice)

1 large handful of green beans

3 zucchini (or 1 large)

3 celery stalks

1 big handful of parsley

Sea salt to taste

Instructions:

Bring water or broth to a boil.

Roughly chop all vegetables — no need to be precise, they'll be blended later.

Add vegetables to the boiling liquid, turn off the heat, and cover.

Let the vegetables sit until they turn a bright green and soften slightly.

Transfer to a blender (Vitamix preferred) and blend slowly, increasing to high until smooth.

Add a pinch of salt at the end.

Pour, sip, and feel your cells thank you.

#soupseason #recipe #wellness
summary: A simple blended vegetable soup recipe credited with healing benefits, made with green beans, zucchini, celery, and parsley.

caption:
Stuffed Pasta Shells - perfect to make ahead and bake when ready to eat!

1lb bag or count about 50 Jumbo Pasta Shells (depends on size of tray)
Pasta sauce
Ricotta filling:
1 lb (14oz) tub of whole milk Ricotta Cheese
10oz (1 bag) fresh blanched baby spinach
1/4 cup chopped parsley + 1 tbsp for topping
1.5 cups mozzarella cheese + more for topping
1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese + more for topping 
2 tbsp Italian seasoning
1 tsp red pepper flakes (optional)
1 tbs salt
2 tsp ground black pepper
2 tbsp fresh basil, chopped
2 crushed garlic cloves
2 eggs

Spread 1/2 -1 cup of pasta sauce to the bottom of your baking tray. 
Boil noodles until al Dante. Allow to cool. 
Separately, cook spinach in boiling water (I simply poured boiling water over the spinach in a pot and let it sit for 5 min until wilted). Drain water, then blanch in ice water to cool. Drain that water then squeeze as much water out of the spinach as you can. 

Mix all ricotta filling ingredients together in a bowl. Transfer to a gallon size ziplock bag or piping bag. Cut the corner. Begin filling noodle with ricotta mixture. Place on your baking tray. Do this until you have no more room in your tray or until you finish the ricotta. 

Top with more sauce, Parmesan cheese, mozzarella, parsley. 

At this point, you can cover and keep in the fridge until ready to bake. Or bake right away. 

Preheat oven to 375F. Bake covered for 20min (if it's cold) or 15min (if room temp), then uncovered for 10 more minutes. Allow to cool to warm temp and enjoy!

caption:
This is my new favourite high-protein comfort meal 🤤
Think chukauni… but with juicy golden chicken, smoky mustard oil tadka, garlic-y curd, and those Nepali-style flavours that just hit different.
It's rich, tangy, spicy, creamy — and honestly perfect with soft rotis or steaming hot rice.

Nepali Style Chicken Chukauni 
🥣 Serves: 3
🔥 Macros (per serving):
Calories: 374 kcal | Protein: 38 g | Carbs: 2.7 g | Fat: 22.3 g

Ingredients
For the Chicken
	•	400 g boneless chicken thigh (or breast), cut into small cubes
	•	2 tsp ginger-garlic paste
	•	½ tsp salt
	•	½ tsp pepper
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	½ tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp lemon juice
	•	Light brush or spray of oil for frying
For the Mix
	•	1 small onion, thinly sliced
	•	1–2 green chilies, chopped
	•	3 tbsp fresh coriander leaves, chopped (divided)
For the Yogurt Base
	•	1 cup thick curd (I used Amul High-Protein Curd) or Greek yogurt 
	•	1 tsp garlic paste
	•	½ tsp salt
	•	½ tsp sugar
For the Tadka
	•	1.5 tbsp mustard oil
	•	½ tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed

Method
1️⃣ Mix chicken with ginger-garlic paste, salt, pepper, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, and lemon juice.
Rest 10–15 minutes.

2️⃣ Brush or spray a pan with very little oil.
Pan-fry chicken on medium-high until golden brown.
Transfer to a bowl.

3️⃣ Add sliced onion, chopped chilies, and half the coriander.
Mix well and set aside.

4️⃣ In another bowl, whisk thick curd with garlic paste, salt, and sugar until smooth and creamy.

5️⃣ Heat mustard oil until smoking hot.
Turn off the heat, add turmeric, Kashmiri chili powder, and crushed kasuri methi.

6️⃣ Pour the hot tadka into the curd and swirl gently to keep the streaks.

7️⃣ Pour the yogurt mixture over the chicken mixture. Mix gently. Finish with more chopped coriander leaves.
Enjoy with soft rotis or steaming hot rice.

.

(chicken chukauni, Nepali chicken recipe, high protein Indian recipes, yogurt chicken salad, chicken curry, easy dinner, healthy chicken dinner, chukauni recipe)

.

#HealthyRecipes #NepaliFood #IndianCooking #ComfortFood #EasyDinners
summary: High-protein Nepali-style chicken chukauni with yogurt, mustard oil tadka, and aromatic spices.

caption:
EASY CHICKEN TIKKA & RICE PROTEIN BOWL

Makes 5 generous servings.

481 calories, 58g protein using Kaizen lowcarb rice, or
541 calories, 45g protein using regular rice

This is a super easy, healthy weeknight meal that tastes incredible. It's packed with colors, textures, tons of flavor, and works perfectly with regular rice, quinoa, or my high-protein, low-carb Kaizen rice. Eat it any night, or it's a great healthy high protein meal prep option.

Ingredients:

1.5-2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs (cut into chunks)
3 tbsp plain Greek yogurt
2 tbsp tomato paste
2 tbsp lemon juice
1 tbsp olive oil
4 garlic cloves, minced
1/2 tsp ground cumin
2 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp garam masala
1 tsp ground coriander
1/2 tsp turmeric
1/2 tsp chili powder or Aleppo pepper
Salt & Pepper to taste

1 red onion, sliced
1 red + green bell pepper, sliced

1 bag (8oz Kaizen protein rice), or 4 cups cooked white rice

Mint-Cilantro Yogurt Sauce:
1 cup Greek yogurt
1/2 cup cilantro leaves & mint chopped
1-2 garlic cloves
Juice of half a large lemon
1 small green chili
Pinch of salt

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ In a large bowl, combine all marinade ingredients and coat the chicken well. Marinate at least 2-6 hours (overnight is best).

2️⃣ Heat a large pan over medium. Add the marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through. Set aside. You can also use an air fryer or pop it into the oven at 400F for 35 mins.

3️⃣ When the chicken is done, remove it. Add the sliced onion and peppers to the same pan. Sauté until tender and coated in all the chicken drippings.

4️⃣ For the yogurt sauce, mix all ingredients. Taste and adjust for salt or lemon.

5️⃣ Prepare your favorite kind of rice according to packaging instructions. I used my high protein lowcarb @kaizenfoodco rice (20g protein, 6 net carbs a serving) but you can use any rice you like.

Build your bowl with rice, veggies, chicken and 2 tbsp yogurt. Top with chili flakes, fresh parsley or cilantro, and a squeeze of lemon.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #healthyfoodshare
summary: High-protein chicken tikka and rice bowl recipe with yogurt sauce, customizable with different rice options.

Honey butter corn cake✨

This corn cake is my mom's classic, reimagined to be melt-in-your-mouth tender and the star of your Friendsgiving table. Made with @vitalfarms eggs and butter, it's proof that high-quality ingredients can make even the simplest recipes shine 🤍 #vitalfarmspartner #ad

Cake:▪️1+1/2 Cups All-Purpose Flour▪️3/4 Cup Medium-Grind Yellow Cornmeal▪️1 tsp Baking Powder▪️1 tsp Baking Soda▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/3 Cup Sugar▪️1 Cup Buttermilk▪️1/3 Cup Honey▪️1/2 Cup Vital Farms Salted Butter, melted▪️1/4 Cup Neutral Oil (Avocado or Vegetable)▪️2 Vital Farms Pasture Raised Large Eggs▪️1 tsp Vanilla Extract▪️

Honey Butter:▪️4 Tbsp Vital Farms Salted Butter, plus more for on top▪️2 Tbsp Honey▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 350F and line an 8×8-inch (or 9x9-inch) baking dish with parchment.
2️⃣ In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, cornmeal, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and sugar.
3️⃣ In another bowl, whisk the buttermilk, melted butter, oil, eggs, honey, and vanilla until smooth.
4️⃣ Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and mix until just combined (the batter will be thick).
5️⃣ Pour into the prepared pan and bake 30-35 mins, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out mostly clean. (If using a 9x9-inch pan, bake 25-30 mins.)
6️⃣ Let cool in the pan for 10 mins, then slice and serve warm.
7️⃣ For the honey butter, melt the butter and whisk in the honey until combined. Drizzle over each slice and top with a small pat of butter if desired.

STORAGE: Store covered in the fridge for up to 4 days. Microwave individual slices for about 1 minute before serving.

⏲ 15 min prep time / 45 min total time

Recipe serves 9-12
summary: A honey butter corn cake recipe inspired by the poster's mom, made with quality eggs and butter.

caption:
Full recipe ⬇️

Soak 90g whole masoor dal for a few hours (or in hot water for a few minutes to save time) 🫘⏱️

Chop 6 garlic cloves, 2 green chillies, 1 small onion, 1 large tomato, & 100g spinach 🧄🌶️🧅🍅🌿

Heat a pressure cooker on low and add 1.5 tsp ghee, a bay leaf, 1/4 tsp cumin seeds, garlic, chillies & a pinch of hing.

Pair with a salad or 100g Greek yoghurt for extra protein 💪😋

caption:
Cheesy mashed potatoes au gratin✨

This dish definitely deserves a spot on your Thanksgiving table! I made the potatoes with whole milk instead of heavy cream which still feels rich and cozy, just a little lighter, and it adds some extra nutrients to a holiday that's all about indulging. @gonnaneedmilk #gonnaneedmilk #ad

Potatoes:▪️3 lb. Yukon Gold Potatoes, peeled and cut in large chunks▪️4 Tbsp Unsalted Butter, cut into cubes▪️1+1/4 Cups Whole Milk▪️1+3/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️ 3/4 tsp Freshly Ground Pepper▪️1/3 Cup Grated Parmesan Cheese▪️6 oz. Gruyere Cheese, shredded (1+1/2 Cups)▪️Chopped Parsley, for serving▪️

1️⃣Preheat the oven to 400F. 
2️⃣ Place the potatoes in a medium pot and cover with water by 1-2 inches. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium low. Simmer the potatoes until they can be easily pierced with a fork, 20-25 mins.
3️⃣ Drain the potatoes into a colander. Add the milk, butter, salt, and pepper to the hot pot and stir until the butter melts. Rice the potatoes back into the pot using a potato ricer (or mash directly with a masher). Add the Parmesan and stir until smooth and combined. Taste and adjust seasoning if desired.
4️⃣ Spread the potatoes into a baking dish and top with the shredded Gruyere.
5️⃣ Place on a baking sheet and bake until the top is golden and bubbling, 20-25 mins. Top with chopped parsley and enjoy!

⏲15 min prep time / 55 min total time

Recipe serves 6
summary: A cheesy baked mashed potato au gratin recipe made with whole milk, Gruyere, and Parmesan for Thanksgiving.

caption:
HIGH PROTEIN STUFFED BREAKFAST SWEET POTATOES

This is another healthy breakfast idea thats so easy and so good.

It comes in at 410 calories, 31g protein, 28 net carbs, and will keep you full for hours from the slow digesting carbs, fiber, and all the protein.

If you are watching carbs closely, feel free to use spaghetti squash as your base.

Here is how I made it (makes 4 servings)

1.Pop 4 regular sized sweet potatoes (about 125g each) into the oven at 350°F for 70-80 mins. The cook time depends on thickness, so if you're using a larger potato, just got a bit longer.

2.When the sweet potato is roasted, let them cool for a minute or so. Then cut them open. You can mash the inside with a fork, or you can eat it as is. This is where you can top it up with your favorite toppings.

3.I added 1.5 servings cottage cheese (2% Good Culture), half a thinly sliced avocado, and 1 egg on top of each sweet potato.

4.You can garnish with fresh cilantro, hot sauce, or my everyday cilantro sauce from my Shredhappens cookbook, or any kind of sauce you like. I added some fresh herbs (I love fresh mint and cilantro here), some everything bagel seasoning, and some garlic chili crisp.

As always, modify any of the serving sizes as you wish based on your own goals. You can add more egg, more cottage cheese, use a smaller sweet potato, not add avocado. You do you!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#highprotein #goodmoodfood #healthyrecipes #whatsonmyplate #lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes
summary: A high-protein roasted sweet potato breakfast bowl topped with cottage cheese, egg, and avocado.

caption:
recipe below 👇🏻 

Pumpkin Sabzi

✨ My Regional Cooking of India Cookbook is officially available. 130+ regional recipes and 20 lessons to improve your Indian home cooking. Comment "BOOK" and I'll send it instantly, or hit the link in bio.

Ingredients:

500 g pumpkin or squash, cubed (leave skin on if tender)
3 tbsp mustard oil
2 tbsp finely chopped ginger
2 dried red chillies
1 cassia leaf or bay leaf
1⁄8 tsp hing
1 tbsp panch phoran
1 tsp chilli powder
1⁄4 tsp turmeric
1 tbsp coriander powder
1 tbsp amchoor
Salt, to taste

Method:

1. Cut the pumpkin into cubes. Leave the skin on if the variety is soft.

2. Heat mustard oil. Add hing, dried chillies, and bay leaf. Let them sizzle briefly.

3. Add panch phoran and let it crackle.

4. Add the ginger and cook on low for a minute.

5. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and coriander powder with a small splash of water. Boil until the oil separates.

6. Add the pumpkin and salt. Cover and cook on low for 20 to 25 minutes until tender.

7. Finish with amchoor and serve with roti and pickle.
summary: Pumpkin sabzi recipe using mustard oil, spices, and amchoor, from a regional Indian cooking cookbook.

Foods That Change Moods 💫 | Episode 5: Biscoff Mysore Pak - Brand New Recipe!!

caption:
Crispy Chicken Caesar Smashed Tacos

Imagine combining crispy chicken nuggets, chicken burger or a crispy katsu cutlet with Caesar salad?? Well this is exactly what that these are and they were amazing!!! After seeing @tomjustcooks chicken katsu smashed tacos, I had to combine his genius idea with ceasar salad which is my latest obsession and it's fair to say I was not disappointed. Perfect for a quick lunch or dinner! 

Ingredients (makes 8-10)

Chicken Patties
* 500 g chicken breast mince
* 1 tsp dried oregano
* 1 tsp garlic powder
* 1 tsp onion powder
* ½ tsp black pepper
* 1 tsp salt (or to taste)
* 8–10 mini corn tortillas
* 200g panko breadcrumbs 
* 2 eggs, beaten
* Oil for frying

Caesar Salad
* 1 tsp Dijon mustard
* Juice of ½ a lemon
* 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce
* 1 garlic clove, minced
* 3 tbsp light mayonnaise
* ½ tsp black pepper
* Pinch of salt (or to taste) 
* 2-3 tbsp grated Parmesan
* 2 cups romaine lettuce, roughly chopped 

Method
1. Season chicken mince and mix well; marinate if time allows.
2. Place 1 heaped tablespoon of mince onto each mini tortilla and spread thinly with hands or a spoon. You can use more or less depending how many mini tacos you have or how thick you want the chicken layer to be. I made 8. 
3. Brush the meat side with beaten egg.
4. Coat or dip into panko breadcrumbs and gently press down.
5. Pan fry chicken side down in a little oil over medium heat for 3-4 minutes until golden.
6. Flip and cook the tortilla side for 1–2 minutes.
7. Mix romaine lettuce with the Caesar dressing (homemade or shop bought)
8. Top each taco with Caesar salad, extra Parmesan and enjoy immediately.

#chickentacos #mcchicken #chickennuggets #caesarsalad #chickenburger #easyrecipes #smashed #tacos #quicklunch
summary: A recipe for crispy pan-fried chicken patty smashed tacos topped with Caesar salad.

caption:
HIGH PROTEIN TOMATO BASIL SOUP 🍅

This full recipe has 79 grams of protein! Divide into however many servings you want. For 4 GENEROUS bowls, you'll get 20g protein with just 230 calories.

👉👉Comment "SLURP" and I'll send you a FREE EBOOK  with 3 of my favorite high protein soup recipes.

To make this super high protein, I added bone broth with 20g protein on its own, and to make it higher protein and creamy at the same time, some cottage cheese for another protein book.

This is loaded with veggies and SO good.

Here is how I made it:

1️⃣ Preheat your oven to 400F.

2️⃣ Add 4.5 pints cherry tomatoes to a large sheet pan.

3️⃣ Roughly chop or dice 1 large yellow onion & 1 large bell pepper. Add to the try.

4️⃣ Smash and peel 6-8 garlic cloves and toss into the tray.

5️⃣ Drizzle with 2 tbsp olive oil and add 1 tsp sweet paprika, 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp chili flakes, salt & pepper. Toss well to coat evenly.

6️⃣ Pop the tray in the oven at 400F for 60-70 mins until tomatoes are blistered and roasted perfectly.

7️⃣ Transfer everything a blender. Add 1 full container chicken bone broth, 3/4 cup (3 servings) of cottage cheese, and a large handful of fresh basil.

8️⃣ Blend or use an immersion blender until silky smooth. Taste and adjust however way you wish with more salt, pepper, fresh basil, or chili flakes. Remember, salt is key here to bringing out the flavor.

9️⃣ Pour into your favorite cup or bowl and you can enjoy this right way, or this is perfect for meal prep.

For toppings, I like a drizzle of hot sauce, cream, olive oil, extra chili flakes, or fresh basil. 🌿
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #soup #souprecipe #goodmoodfood #healthyrecipes
summary: A high-protein tomato basil soup made with roasted tomatoes, bone broth, and cottage cheese that delivers 20g protein per serving at 230 calories.

caption:
🍫🥜PROTEIN CHOCOLATE PB BROWNIE

Crumbl just dropped their highest protein menu item… a peanut butter brownie. Sure its got 20g protein, but it comes in at 1200 calories for a single brownie.

This healthier brownie version? The same rich, chocolate-PB flavor for under 200 calories and 20g of protein per square. You could eat all of these for 1200 calories.

Each piece: 170 calories and 20g protein

These are dense yet light, and really scratch the itch

INGREDIENTS:

Brownie Base
16 oz 2% cottage cheese (blended smooth)
4 eggs
5 tbsp cacao powder (4 for a lighter flavor)
6-8 tbsp powdered monkfruit (or your favorite sweetener)
Nice splash of vanilla extract
2 servings Lily's or low sugar chocolate chip

Protein Frosting
1/2 cup thick 0% Greek yogurt
2 tbsp whipped cream cheese
5-6tbsp powdered PB2
1-2 tsp powdered sweetener (optional)
Dust with dark chocolate or add peanut butter M&Ms (optional)

1️⃣ Blend the cottage cheese, eggs, cacao, sweetener, and vanilla until smooth. I used a small food processor.

2️⃣ Grab a baking tray line with parchment paper. Pour in the batter, and toss in the chocolate chips. These add a nice texture

3️⃣ Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes until set. Remove, and let this cool COMPLETELY then pop into the fridge for 5-6 hours or ideally overnight. This part is IMPORTANT. Do not under any circumstance skip this part.

4️⃣Make the pb frosting by whisk everything together until thick and creamy. Taste and adjust with more PB, powdered monk fruit or vanilla.

5️⃣Spread over the cooled brownie and ideally chill before slicing. Top with optional peanut M&Mss or a thin dusting of shaved dark chocolate like me.

If you make this, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #keto #ketorecipes #healthyfoodshare
summary: High-protein, low-calorie chocolate peanut butter brownies with frosting—170 calories and 20g protein per square.

caption:
Healthier sheet pan nachos✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Recipe inspired by the viral sheet pan nachos trend 😁 

Baked Chips:▪️20 Corn Tortillas▪️4 Tbsp Avocado Oil (or other neutral oil), divided▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt, divided▪️

Toppings:▪️1 Cup Green Salsa▪️1 x 15 oz. Can Pinto Beans, drained and rinsed▪️2 Cups Shredded Mexican Blend Cheese▪️Pico de Gallo▪️Sour Cream▪️Pickled Jalapeños▪️Sliced Avocado▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 400F convection (or 425F regular bake).
2️⃣ Stack the tortillas and cut into triangles (6 per tortilla). Divide between two half sheet pans (18 x 13-inch). Drizzle each pan with 2 Tbsp oil and sprinkle with 1/4 tsp salt, tossing to coat evenly.
3️⃣ Bake for 8-12 mins, rotating the pans from top to bottom and turning each pan front to back halfway through, until the chips are deep golden brown and crisp. Let cool slightly.
4️⃣ Meanwhile, on a quarter sheet pan (9 x 13-inch), spread the green salsa in an even layer. Add the pinto beans in one layer and sprinkle evenly with the shredded cheese.
5️⃣ Bake for 8-10 minutes, until the cheese is fully melted and bubbling.
6️⃣ Arrange the baked tortilla chips on another quarter sheet pan (or a similar sized platter or tray). Slide the cheesy bean mixture over the top.
7️⃣ Finish with pico de gallo, sour cream, pickled jalapeños, and sliced avocado. Serve immediately and enjoy!

⏲ 15 min prep time / 30 min total time

Recipe serves 6-8
summary: A healthier sheet pan nachos recipe with homemade baked tortilla chips, beans, cheese, and fresh toppings.

caption:
HEALTHY GREEK CHICKEN & RICE BOWL

52G protein and under 500 calories a serving if you use my @kaizenfoodco high protein rice, or just about 40g protein and 500 calories if you use regular or brown rice!

I've lost over 130lbs eating healthy food like this thats high-protein, low-carb and most importantly, tastes INCREDIBLE.

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ First, the chicken. I placed 2 lb boneless chicken thighs to a bowl. Mix with 2 tbsp Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp olive oil, juice and zest of 1 lemon, 3 minced garlic cloves, 1 tbsp oregano, 1 tsp sweet paprika, 1 tsp garlic powder, salt & pepper. Marinate at least 2 hrs, but preferably overnight.

2️⃣ You can grill, airfare, or make this on your stovetop. Just heat a pan over medium heat. Drizzle 1/2 tbsp olive oil, and cook your chicken about 4-5 mins per side with the lid on. Flip, then cook for 1 minute on each side again or until cooked through.

3️⃣ Make your simple Greek salad… Just finely dice 2 Persian cucumbers, 1 bell pepper, & 1/2 small red onion. Add 1/2 cup sliced cherry tomatoes, 2 tbsp diced black olives & 1oz crumbled feta. Drizzle with olive oil, a splash of red wine vinegar (or lemon), a small pinch oregano, salt, pepper, and a handful of chopped flat-leaf parsley.

4️⃣ For the tzatziki sauce, grate 1 cucumber and squeeze out excess liquid. Mix with 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp olive oil, juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 grated garlic clove, 2 tbsp fresh dill, salt and pepper until creamy.

5️⃣ Cook or prep your base. I cooked up my Kaizen lowcarb rice here, it has just 6 net carbs but packs 20g protein. You can use regular rice, brown rice, or quinoa, too.

6️⃣ Assemble the bowl: rice on the bottom, 4-5oz chicken, a big scoop of the Greek salad, and a generous dollop of tzatziki. Sprinkle extra parsley or dill to finish.

If you make it, be sure to let me know what you think!!

ENJOY! 🙌

#lowcarb #highprotein #keto #greekfood #healthyrecipes #mediterraneanfood
summary: A high-protein Greek chicken and rice bowl with marinated chicken thighs, Greek salad, and tzatziki sauce.

caption:
🍁High Protein Butternut Squash Feta Pasta

Serves 4 - 340 calories, 33g protein per serving

➡️Comment "PROTEINPASTA" and I will send you a message with details on my Kaizen pasta. 

Olive Garden is running endless pasta right now and it is INSANE to see how many bowls of pasta people are going for. I used to be that guy too… at one point 350lbs, high cholesterol, pre diabetic, and eating way too much food.  If you're craving pasta, but you want to keep it healthy, filling, and without the carb crash, this is an amazing healthy pasta dish you can make. True comfort food without the slump. I used my high protein lowcarb Kaizen pasta, but use any high protein or lowcarb pasta you like.

 
Ingredients
1 small to medium butternut squash
1 small red onion, halved
5oz feta cheese
1/2 tbsp olive oil
Salt & pepper
1 tbsp sweet paprika
1 tbsp thyme
8 oz bone broth
1 serving (1/3 to 1/2 cup cottage cheese
1 box high protein low carb pasta (8 ounces, I use @kaizenfoodco 
Fresh herbs, chili flakes, parmesan (optional)

Instructions

1.Preheat oven to 400F.

2.Cut the butternut squash in half, scoop the seeds, score it, and place it in an oven safe dish with the red onion and feta.

3. Drizzle with olive oil and season with salt, pepper, paprika, and thyme.

4.Roast 45-55 mins until tender.

5.Transfer squash, onion, and feta to a blender. Add bone broth and cottage cheese. Blend until creamy.

6.Cook your high protein pasta in generously salted water. I used my kaizen low carb pasta because it has 20g protein per serving with just 6 net carbs. Use what you like!

7.Drain, add sauce, and stir until glossy and creamy.

8.Season to taste, top with parmesan, fresh chopped herbs or chives, and chili flakes.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarb #healthyrecipes #proteinrecipes #goodmoodfood #whatsonmyplate
summary: High-protein, low-carb butternut squash and feta pasta recipe with 33g protein per serving.

caption:
Greek-style chicken thighs.

Full written recipe with macros, a grocery list, and more can be found at the link in my bio.

This week I'm bringing y'all a recipe that's been in my dinner rotation for years: broiled Greek-inspired chicken thighs. It's zippy from the lemon zest, the cloves add a little backbone to it, and it's an absolute banger.

I mainly wanted to share the chicken recipe itself so you can add it to your protein prep rotation, but I also included a quick rundown of the sides I made in the video at the link in my bio in case you want to try them out too.

* 3 lb boneless, skinless chicken thighs (1,300g)
* 3/4 cup 0% fat Greek yogurt (180g)
* 1 tbsp mayonnaise (15g)
* 3–4 grated garlic cloves (12–16 g)
* 1 1/2 tbsp dried oregano (XL pinch)
* 1/2 tsp ground cloves (teeny pinch)
* Zest from 1 lemon, juice from half
* 1 tbsp olive oil (15ml)
* Lots of fresh cracked black pepper
* * 3-second spray cooking oil (about 1 g)

1. Add the yogurt, mayo, olive oil, garlic cloves, oregano, cloves, lemon zest/juice, salt, and black pepper in a bowl. Stir to combine, thinning with a small splash of water if needed. You want this to have a paste-like consistency.
2. Add in your chicken thighs and toss with the marinade, ensuring all sides are thoroughly covered.
3. Spray a large baking sheet with oil, lay your chicken in an even layer, then generously spray the chicken with additional oil.
4.  Broil on high for about 12-15 minutes or until charred and the chicken is cooked through, keeping an eye on it while it cooks as oven broiling temperatures vary widely. Alternatively air fry at 425°F for ~15 minutes, shaking the basket every 5 minutes.

#easyrecipe #mealprep #chicken
summary: Greek-style broiled chicken thighs with yogurt-herb marinade and macros for meal prep.

caption:
Sheet Pan Chicken Kabob Wraps

Forget takeout! These are so easy and so good! An easy healthy weeknight meal, perfect for the family any day of the week or for meal rep. Plus, you can customize them however way you want.

Ingredients:

4-6 Lowcarb Lavash, Pita, or Bread 
2 lbs ground chicken
1 small red onion
3-4 garlic cloves
Large handful fresh parsley
1 jalapeño
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp sumac
1 tsp Aleppo pepper (or chili flakes)
1/2 tsp ground cumin
1 tbsp smoked paprika
1/4 tsp turmeric
Salt & Pepper
1 tbsp olive oil

Here is how I made it:

1️⃣ Preheat your oven to 425°F.

2️⃣ In a small food processor, process 1 small red onion, 3-4 garlic cloves, and some parsley. Pulse until finely minced.

3️⃣ In a large bowl,add 2 pounds ground chicken, the mix from step 2, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 finely chopped deseeded chili pepper, and the following spices: 1 tbsp sumac, 1 tsp Aleppo pepper or chili flakes, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, 1/4 tsp turmeric, and season with salt and pepper. Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil. 

4️⃣ Mix gently until everything is evenly combined. Do not overwork this- just mix well. Add to a sheet pan, and spread out evenly.

5️⃣ Run your knife or spatula along the edges to smooth and round them out, and cut long vertical lines through the chicken to make single kabobs with space between them.

6️⃣ Bake for 18-22 mins. Then put on the top rack on Broil for 3 mins.

7️⃣For the spread/sauce: mix ½ cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1-2 small grated garlic clove,  1 tbsp fresh dill, and a pinch of salt. Stir until smooth & creamy.

8️⃣ For the salad: Combine 1 diced cucumber, 1 chopped tomato, thinly sliced red onion, a handful of chopped parsley or dill, the juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 tsp sumac, salt, and pepper mix well.

9️⃣I used a lowcarb wrap, you can use lavash, pita, tortilla, or any regular type of bread depending on your goals. You can also use my high protein lowcarb Kaizen rice or regular rice, too!

Then assemble: wrap, some spread, lettuce, 2 chicken skewers, salad, and top with more sumac and fresh parsley.

ENJOY!
.
.
.
#lowcarb #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #mediterraneanfood #healthyfoodshare
summary: Sheet pan ground chicken kabob wraps with yogurt sauce and cucumber-tomato salad, customizable for low-carb or regular bread.

caption:
318 Kcal for a LARGE Serving 💪🏼
A creamy paneer curry that actually fits your macro goals 😌✨
This Paneer Changezi has all the richness  in flavour— but with smart swaps: low-fat paneer, NOT deep fried onions, minimal butter and skim milk. 
To make comparison a little easier, I'm using 1 tsp + 2 sprays oil + 1 tbsp light butter + 1/2 cup skim milk for the whole dish - whereas a normal restaurant version uses nearly 2 cups oil (deep frying) + 4-5 tbsp butter + 1/2 cup cream (if not more). 
But I can assure you, this has the same comfort level, just better macros 🤍🔥

Light Paneer Changezi

Makes 4 servings
Macros for the whole dish: ~955 kcal | ~79g protein | ~33.2g fat | ~77g carbs
Per serving (3 servings):
~318 kcal | ~26g protein | ~11g fat | ~26g carbs

Ingredients

Brown onion paste:
	•	4 medium onions, thinly sliced
	•	1 tsp oil spray + pinch salt
	•	15g soaked cashews
	•	3 tbsp thick curd

Paneer marinade:
	•	300g low-fat paneer, cubed
	•	Turmeric, chilli, coriander powder, garam masala
	•	1 tsp mustard oil
	•	Pan-sear with minimal/no oil

Gravy:
	•	2 tsp light butter
	•	Whole spices — black + green cardamom, bay leaf, cinnamon
	•	1 tsp ginger garlic paste
	•	Purée of 3 tomatoes
	•	Brown onion paste
	•	Hot water as needed
	•	1/2 cup skim milk
	•	Kasuri methi + fresh coriander
	•	Drizzle of fresh cream (optional)

⸻

Method
	1.	Air-fry onions mixed with a pinch of salt and a spray of oil at 190°C for 8–9 mins (shake every 2 mins)
(Microwave option: HIGH for 12–14 mins, stir every 1–2 mins)
	2.	Blend onions + soaked cashews + curd → paste.
	3.	Marinate paneer with spices + mustard oil → pan-sear golden.
	4.	Heat butter → add whole spices → sauté ginger-garlic paste.
	5.	Add tomato purée → simmer → add brown onion paste.
	6.	Thin with hot water → cook till glossy + oil separates.
	7.	Add skim milk, simmer → mix paneer gently.
	8.	Finish with kasuri methi, coriander & a tiny cream drizzle.
.

(healthy paneer changezi, low fat paneer curry, high protein Indian veg, light m curry, macro friendly paneer, Indian weight loss recipes)

.

#PaneerRecipes #HealthyIndianCooking #HighProteinVegetarian #MacroFriendlyMeals
summary: A macro-friendly paneer changezi curry recipe with 318 calories per serving, using low-fat paneer and smart ingredient swaps to reduce oil and butter.

caption:
We were blown away by your amazing feedback on our viral Christmas Olympics video! So many of you asked for a supply list and  how to play... and guess what? We've put it all together in one new video just for you! Type 'play games' in the comments and we'll send you the supply list. 

How to play: 
Bow Toss: Whoever gets the most bows in their bowl in wins. 

Fill the Cup: Blow up a balloon and use it to move a plastic cup without using your hands. The person who finishes in the shortest amount of time wins.

Reindeer Toss: Whoever gets the most rings on the antler ears within the allotted time wins. 

Christmas Balls: The person who knocks all of the puffs off their cup in the shortest amount of time wins.

Reindeer Hooves: Whoever gets the most marshmallows in their plate wins.

Candy Cane Fishing: The person who gets the most candy canes out of their cup in the allotted time wins.

Saran Wrap Gift Ball: Place your grand prize in the middle, wrapping it with Saran Wrap, cutting the wrap after each prize to make it more challenging. One person unwraps the ball while another rolls the dice. When a double is rolled, the ball passes to the next player. They get to keep any prizes they won while unwrapping the ball.

🥳🎅✨

#ChristmasOlympics #HolidayFun #DIYChristmas #FestiveGames #PartyTime #Christmas2025 #never_done_diy
summary: A collection of seven DIY Christmas Olympics games with rules and supply instructions for holiday parties.

caption:
Save the recipe below and comment "Healthy" if you wanna be the first to know what I'm planning! Hint: it's going to help you reach your health & fitness goals while eating deliciously! 😋 💚

Chili Stuffed Peppers 

Ingredients 
1 cup dry quinoa 
4 large multi-color bell peppers (tops sliced off & deseeded) 
1 14oz can fire-roasted diced tomatoes 
1 Tbsp nutritional yeast 
1 oz spicy chili seasoning 
1 tsp sea salt 
1 15-ounce can black beans, drained 
1 cup whole kernel corn
1/2 cup tomato basil tomato sauce
Olive oil, for brushing 
Dairy-free yogurt or sour cream for topping 
Chopped cilantro for topping 

Method 
1. Cook quinoa according to package instructions. 
2. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F and lightly grease a large baking dish or cast iron skillet. 
3. Brush peppers with olive oil. 
4. Add cooked quinoa to a large mixing bowl and add the tomatoes, nutritional yeast, chili seasoning, sea salt, black beans, and corn. Mix to combine thoroughly. 
5. Generously stuff the peppers with quinoa mixture until all peppers are full. Pour tomato sauce into bottom of the pan. 
6. Bake for 25 minutes covered. Then remove foil, increase heat to 400 degrees F and bake for another 25-30 minutes, or until peppers are soft and slightly browned. 
7. Serve with plant-based yougut or sour cream and cilantro. 

#plantbasedrecipe  #easyrecipe  #healthyrecipe  #glutenfree  #vegan  #dairyfree  #meatlessmondays  #recipe  #quickrecipe  #plantbased  #fitness  #abs  #nutrition  #stuffedpeppers
summary: A vegan chili-stuffed bell pepper recipe with quinoa, black beans, and corn, baked until tender.

caption:
Spiced Cauliflower with Chickpeas & Couscous
Serves 4
Ready in 30 minutes

Olive oil
1 pound frozen cauliflower florets
1 (15 ounce) can chickpeas, drained
1 to 3 tablespoons harissa
½ teaspoon ground allspice
½ teaspoon ground turmeric
Salt and pepper, to taste 
1 ½ cups broth
1 cup couscous
8 ounces feta

Heat a medium skillet over medium-high until very hot (a splash of water should instantly sizzle away). Add enough oil to generously coat the bottom of the pan, then add the cauliflower. Cook, stirring occasionally, until browned and tender, about 10 minutes. 

Add the chickpeas, harissa, allspice, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Cook until the spices are aromatic and the harissa caramelizes, about 1 minute. 

Add the broth, taste and adjust seasoning as needed, and bring to a simmer. Add the couscous, and stir well. Cover, turn off the burner, and steam until tender, about 10 minutes. 

Stir to fluff, crumble over the feta, and serve.
summary: 30-minute spiced cauliflower and chickpea couscous with feta recipe from Sohla El-Waylly.

caption:
#ShopPartner Cheesy veggie crostini✨

With the holidays around the corner, I ordered new serving dishes and utensils from @shopapp, where I've been curating all my kitchen must-haves this season. Close your tabs and keep your wish list organized in one place with Collections on Shop.

Cheesy Veggie Topping:▪️2 Tbsp Butter▪️1/2 a Medium Sweet Yellow Onion, diced small▪️3 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️4 oz. Baby Bella Mushrooms (8 mushrooms), chopped▪️1/4 tsp Red Pepper Flakes▪️3/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/2 tsp Black Pepper▪️10 oz. Fresh Baby Spinach▪️3 Tbsp Chopped Fresh Parsley, plus more for garnish▪️1/4 Cup Italian Breadcrumbs▪️1/4 Cup Finely Shredded Mozzarella▪️1/4 Cup Grated Parmesan▪️4 oz. Cream Cheese, softened▪️

Crostini:▪️6 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1 Baguette, sliced in 1/2-inch rounds▪️2/3 Cup Finely Shredded Mozzarella▪️3 Tbsp Grated Parmesan▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F.
2️⃣ In a large pan, melt the butter and add the onion. Cook for 2-3 mins until it starts to soften, then add the garlic, mushrooms, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Cook another 2-3 mins until the mushrooms are tender.
3️⃣ Add the spinach (in batches if needed) and cook until it is wilted and excess moisture cooks off, about 3-4 mins.
4️⃣ Transfer the veggie mixture to a bowl and let it cool slightly while you slice your baguette. Then, add the parsley, breadcrumbs, mozzarella, parmesan, and cream cheese to the bowl with the veggies. Mix until combined.
5️⃣ Coat a half sheet pan (18x13-inches) with the olive oil. Working with one baguette round at a time, spread a layer of the cheesy veggie mixture on top and place on the prepared sheet pan. Rub the underside of the bread in the oil to ensure it is nicely coated. Once all of the crostini are on the pan, top with the remaining shredded mozzarella and parmesan.
6️⃣ Bake for about 12-15 mins, until the bread is golden and crispy. Sprinkle with extra parsley and serve warm. Enjoy!

⏲ 20 min prep time / 45 min total time

Recipe makes 36 bites
summary: Cheesy vegetable crostini recipe with spinach, mushrooms, and cream cheese topping.

caption:
Baked Curry Rice with Hanging Chicken Skewers 🥘✨
Juicy spiced chicken skewers roast right over golden curry carrot rice, the flavor drips right into every grain. A complete family meal, easy and delicious😋
Comment "pan" and I'll send the link for the enamel baking pan over to your inbox. 

Ingredients
Chicken Skewers
* 2 lbs boneless chicken (thighs/breast), cut in chunks
* 4 tbsp olive oil
* 1 tbsp curry powder
* 1½ tsp salt
* 1 tsp black pepper
* 1 tsp turmeric
Rice
* 2 cups basmati rice, washed & drained
* 1 large onion, chopped or sliced
* 1 large carrot, grated
* ¼ cup oil
* 1½ tbsp curry powder
* 2 tsp salt
* 1 tsp black pepper
* 2 tsp turmeric
* 3½ cups water
Salad
* Cherry tomatoes, halved
* 3–4 cucumbers, diced
* 1 red pepper, chopped
* 1 bunch parsley, chopped
* Juice of 1 lemon
* Olive oil, salt & pepper to taste

Instructions
1. Cut chicken into chunks and mix with oil & spices. Let marinate.
2. In a 9x13" baking dish, add onion, carrot, rice, oil, spices & water. Mix well.
3. Skewer chicken (about 6 skewers, 7 pieces each).
4. Lay skewers across the pan so they rest on the edges, hanging above the rice.
5. Cover with parchment + foil (to prevent foil contact with food).
6. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 30 minutes.
7. Remove cover and broil 10 minutes until chicken is golden.
8. Meanwhile, mix salad ingredients with lemon, olive oil, salt & pepper.
9. Serve the rice and skewers with salad & pita or flatbread.
✨ One-pan, full of flavor, done in about an hour!

#OnePanDinner #CurryRice #EasyMeals #FamilyFavorite #HomeCooking #sivanskitchen #explorepage
summary: One-pan baked curry rice with hanging chicken skewers that drip flavor into the rice as they cook.

caption:
450 Kcal & 43 g Protein 💪🏼 
This Green Chicken Coconut Pulao is fragrant, juicy, packed with protein and made for both weekdays and weeknights. Pair with raita and thank me later 💚🥥🍗

🌿 Green Chicken Coconut Pulao
Makes 4 servings
Macros per serving: ~450 kcal | ~43g protein | ~10.6g fat | ~42g carb

Ingredients
For green paste:
	•	Mint leaves, handful
	•	Coriander leaves, handful
	•	2–3 green chillies
	•	1" ginger
	•	4–5 garlic cloves
	•	Salt & lemon juice

For chicken:
	•	500g boneless chicken (or curry-cut)
	•	Salt & lemon juice
	•	1 tsp turmeric
	•	1 tsp red chilli powder
	•	1 tsp coriander powder
	•	1/2 tsp garam masala
For pulao:
	•	1 tbsp ghee
	•	2 bay leaves
	•	1 small cinnamon stick
	•	2–3 cardamom pods
	•	2 medium onions, thinly sliced
	•	1 tsp ginger garlic paste
	•	1 cup (180g) basmati rice, washed & soaked
	•	1 cup thin coconut milk
	•	1 cup water
	•	1 chicken stock cube - I've used the Zero Salt one from Knorr (available on Amazon)
Method:
	1.	Blend mint, coriander, green chilli, ginger, garlic, lemon juice & salt into a paste.
	2.	Marinate chicken with this paste, salt & lemon juice for 15 minutes.
	3.	Sauté: Heat ghee, add whole spices, then onions. Cook till golden.
	4.	Add ginger garlic paste & sauté.
	5.	Add marinated chicken, turmeric, chilli, coriander powder & garam masala. Cook 3–5 mins.
	6.	Add soaked rice, thin coconut milk, water & stock cube. Season if needed.
	7.	Cover & cook on low 15–20 mins till rice is fluffy & chicken cooked.
	8.	Rest 5 minutes, fluff & serve hot.

Creamy, aromatic, high-protein comfort food in one pot ✅

⸻

(chicken rice, one pot meal, coconut chicken pulao, high protein Indian meal, lean one pot chicken rice, healthy Indian dinner, weeknight pulao recipe, macro friendly chicken rice)

.

caption:
🔥Quick Lunchbox Hack – Spinach Egg Cheese Quesadilla!🌿

Ingredients:

1 almond flour tortilla

1 egg

1 cup spinach (chopped)

1 tsp butter

Salt and pepper – to taste

¼ tsp chili flakes

¼ tsp garlic powder

¼ tsp onion powder

¼ tsp Italian seasoning

¼ cup cheddar cheese (shredded)

Method:

1. In a pan, add butter. Sauté chopped spinach with salt, chili flakes, garlic powder, onion powder, and Italian seasoning until all moisture evaporates.

2. In a bowl, beat the egg with a pinch of salt and pepper. Add the sautéed spinach and mix well.

3. Lightly heat the almond flour tortilla on a pan for a few seconds.

4. Spread the egg–spinach mixture over the tortilla. Sprinkle some cheddar cheese, then place another tortilla on top (or fold it in half if using one).

5. Cook on medium heat, flipping carefully. Add remaining cheese on one side, fold and press gently.

6. Apply butter on both sides and cook until light golden and crispy.

7. Slice into halves and pack warm for a protein-rich, cheesy lunchbox.

#spinachcheesequesadilla #cheesequesadilla #almondflourtortilla #easylunchrecipe #lakshquesadillarecipe  #quicklunchboxideas
summary: A spinach, egg, and cheddar cheese quesadilla made with almond flour tortillas, cooked in a pan until crispy.

caption:
SKILLET CHICKEN THIGHS WITH MUSHROOM GRAVY✨ warm, cozy, and comforting - this was my most popular recipe of 2023 for good reason! Its so easy and requires such minimal cleanup that its even worthy of being a weeknight meal. I served it was my dairy-free mashed potatoes (recipe on my blog) and it was "eat it while standing up before its remotely cooled" good 🤣

Ingredients: 
6 chicken thighs, boneless and skinless 
4 tbsp avocado oil
1 onion, diced
1 lb baby bella mushrooms, sliced thick 
2 cloves of garlic, minced
2-3 tbsp fresh thyme leaves, roughly chopped
2 cups chicken broth
2 tbsp tamari
2 tbsp arrowroot starch (or thickener of choice)
salt and pepper to season 

Directions: 
Heat a large skillet over medium high for a few minutes then add 1-2 tbsp of avocado oil 
Add your chicken thighs and cook for about 5 minutes on each side until golden brown 
Remove from heat and add 2 more tbsp of avocado oil 
Add your mushrooms and onions, spreading them out as much as possible and allow them to cook, stirring infrequently, for about 7 minutes until the onions are softened and the mushrooms are mostly golden (note: do not salt them at this point! They will sweat and steam and not get as golden brown)
Stir in your garlic, thyme, and 1 tsp of salt and allow to cook for 1-2 more minutes 
Combine your chicken broth and tamari in a glass or bowl and pour 3/4 of it over your mushrooms, stirring to deglaze any bits that have stuck to the bottom
Add your arrowroot starch to your remaining broth and tamari and whisk until no lumps remain 
Pour your starch mixture over your mushroom and stir to combine 
Let simmer for 2-3 minutes while the mushroom gravy thickens then add your chicken thighs
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook for 10 more minutes 
Serve over mashed potatoes (I served mine over dairy-free mashed potatoes), quinoa, or pasta, garnish with parsley, and ENJOY! 

Follow for more simple, healthy recipes! 
.

caption:
Broccoli parmesan pastina✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

▪️2 Tbsp Butter, plus more at the end if desired▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️4 Cups Chicken Bone Broth▪️1 Cup Pastina (or other tiny pasta like acini di pepe, stelline, or orzo)▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Black Pepper▪️1+1/2 Cup Finely Chopped Broccoli Florets▪️1/2 Cup Freshly Grated Parmesan Cheese, plus more for serving▪️1/2 tsp Finely Grated Lemon Zest▪️High Quality Olive Oil, for finishing▪️

1️⃣ In a large pot, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the garlic and cook until fragrant and just turning golden, about 1 minute.
2️⃣ Pour in the chicken bone broth and bring to a gentle boil over high heat.
3️⃣ Stir in the pastina, salt, and pepper and cook, stirring frequently, until mostly tender but not fully done, about 5-6 mins.
4️⃣ Add the chopped broccoli and cook another 3-4 mins until the broccoli is tender and the pastina has soaked up most of the broth. It should be loose enough to ladle but not soupy.
5️⃣ Remove from heat and stir in parmesan and lemon zest. Add another small pat of butter, if desired, to make it glossy.
6️⃣ Ladle into bowls and top with a drizzle of high quality olive oil and more freshly grated Parmesan. Enjoy!

⏲️5 min prep time / 20 min total time

Serves 2-3 as a main, or 4 as a side
summary: A simple broccoli and parmesan pastina soup recipe made with butter, garlic, bone broth, and lemon zest.

caption:
POWER & GLOW IMMUNITY SOUP 

👉👉Comment "SLURP" and I'll send you a FREE EBOOK  with 3 of my favorite  soup recipes. 

This SWEET POTATO CHIPOTLE SOUP not only delivers big on protein, but does some serious work for your skin, gut, and immunity with a powerful punch of retinol.

For the whole pot: 1,235 calories, 82g protein
For 5 servings: ~245 calories, 17g protein 

Split it into 4 or 5 filling servings, and you have plenty as a super filling complement to any meal and great healthy meal prep option.

Ingredients:

2 large sweet potatoes (about 2 lbs), peeled and chopped
1 yellow onion, roughly chopped
6 garlic cloves
1–2 chipotle peppers in adobo (start with 1 for mild, 2 for spicy)
1 tbsp tomato paste
2 tsp smoked paprika
1/2 tsp cumin
1/4 tsp cayenne or chili powder
Salt & black pepper to taste
1 tbsp olive oil
4 cups bone broth (or vegetable broth)
1 cup water 
1 block soft or silken tofu or 2 servings (1/2 cup) cottage cheese
Juice of ½ large lime

Toppings:
Greek yogurt to swirl
Cilantro or parsley
Crumbled feta or cotija
Drizzle of chili oil or olive oil
Lime wedge

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 400°F.

2️⃣ Add the peeled, chopped sweet potatoes, onion, and garlic cloves to an oven safe tray. Drizzle with olive oil and season with smoked paprika, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Toss to coat evenly.

3️⃣ Roast for 55-60 mins until tender and caramelized.

4️⃣ Transfer everything to a blender or pot. Add bone broth, chipotle pepper(s), tomato paste, cottage cheese (or tofu), and lime juice. Blend until completely smooth and creamy.

5️⃣ Taste and adjust with more salt, lime juice, or chipotle if needed. Salt is KEY here. And if you want to water it down a bit, start with 1/2 cup water and slowly work your way to 1 cup. It depends how thick you want it.

6️⃣ Pour into bowls, top with a swirl of Greek yogurt, a drizzle of chili oil, crumbled feta or cotija, and lots of fresh cilantro.

It's smoky, slightly sweet, tangy, and comforting, but with protein, skin benefits, and gut benefits.

If you make this SHREDHAPPENS soup, let me know what you think!

ENJOY!!
.
.
.
#souprecipe #healthyrecipes #healthyfoodshare #proteinrecipes
summary: A high-protein sweet potato chipotle soup with roasted vegetables, tofu or cottage cheese, and toppings for skin and immunity support.

caption:
One-skillet baked ziti✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍

Spinach & Ricotta:▪️1 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️5 oz. Fresh Baby Spinach▪️ Kosher Salt▪️Freshly Ground Black Pepper▪️1 Cup Ricotta▪️

Ziti:▪️1 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1/2 a Yellow Onion, diced small▪️3 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️1 lb. Hot Italian Sausage, casing removed (or Mild Italian Sausage, Ground Beef, Ground Turkey etc.)▪️1 tsp Italian Seasoning▪️1/4 tsp Red Pepper Flakes (optional)▪️1/2 tsp Sugar▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1 x 24 oz. Jar Marinara Sauce▪️1 lb.  Mezzi Rigatoni, Ziti, or Penne, uncooked▪️3+1/2 Cups Water▪️2 Cups Shredded Mozzarella▪️Grated Parmesan, for serving▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F.
2️⃣ Make the spinach & ricotta: Heat the olive oil in a 12-inch oven-safe skillet (it needs to be at least this big; otherwise use an oven-safe pot instead) over medium heat. Add the spinach and garlic, and a sprinkle of salt and pepper. Sauté until wilted, 2-3 mins. Transfer to a bowl and stir in the ricotta, 1/4 tsp kosher salt, and a few grinds of pepper, then set aside in the fridge.
3️⃣ Make the ziti: In the same skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened, 3-4 mins. Add the garlic and cook 30 seconds, then add the sausage. Cook, breaking it into small pieces with your spatula, until browned, about 5-6 mins.
4️⃣ Stir in the Italian seasoning, red pepper flakes (if using), sugar, and salt. Stir in the marinara sauce, then stir in the uncooked pasta. Add the water.
5️⃣ Bring to a simmer, then reduce the heat to medium-low and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the pasta is tender and the sauce thickens, 12-15 mins. (You can add more water if you'd like the pasta more tender once the excess liquid is absorbed, but I didn't have to.)
6️⃣ Spoon dollops of the spinach ricotta mixture evenly across the top, then sprinkle with shredded mozzarella.
7️⃣ Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 15-20 mins, until the cheese is melted, golden brown, and some of the pasta edges are crisped. Serve warm with grated parm on top and enjoy!

⏲5 min prep time / 45 min total time

Recipe serves 4-6
summary: One-skillet baked ziti with spinach and ricotta filling, cooked on stovetop then finished in the oven.
