caption:
EASY CHICKEN TIKKA SHEET PAN DINNER

43g protein a serving!

This is an incredible easy weeknight meal, big on flavor, no fuss, and the juicy chicken tikka is roasted right in your oven with no grill, no skewers, and no mess. It's also an amazing healthy recipe, loaded with protein, packed with fun flavors and colors, and perfect over some regular rice, quinoa, or my high-protein, low-carb Kaizen rice.

Ingredients:
1.5-2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs (cut into chunks)

3tbsp plain Greek yogurt
2 tbsp tomato paste
2 tbsp lemon juice
1 tbsp olive oil
4 garlic cloves, minced
1/2 tsp ground cumin
2 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp garam masala
1 tsp ground coriander
1/2 tsp turmeric
1/2tsp chili powder (or Aleppo pepper)e
Salt & pepper to taste

1 red onion, cut into wedges
1 red bell pepper, sliced
1 green bell pepper, sliced

Mint-Cilantro Yogurt Sauce:
1 cup Greek yogurt
1/2 cup cilantro leaves
1/2 cup mint leaves
1-2 garlic cloves
Juice of half large lemon
1 small green chili (optional)
Pinch of salt
1–2 tbsp water to thin

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ In a large bowl, combine all marinade ingredients and coat the chicken well. Marinate at least 2–6 hours (overnight is best).

2️⃣ Cut the onion and peppers and set aside.

3️⃣ Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a sheet pan with parchment paper, spread out the chicken and veggies evenly, and drizzle lightly with olive oil. Roast for 30 minutes, then broil for 2-3 minutes to get that perfect char.

4️⃣ While it roasts in the oven mix together all the yogurt sauce ingredients. Then taste and adjust for salt or lemon.

5️⃣ When the chicken is done, remove, top with chili flakes, fresh chopped cilantro and mint, and strategically place dollops of the yogurt spread around it. You can serve this as is, with some rice, or if you are trying to eat high protein, my high protein kaizen rice.

If you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS.

ENJOY!
.
.
.
#lowcarb #ketorecipes #lowcarbrecipes #healthyrecipes #chickenrecipes #keto
summary: Sheet pan chicken tikka marinade recipe with 43g protein per serving, roasted in the oven with bell peppers and onions, served with mint-cilantro yogurt sauce.

caption:
📍Protein TruFru bites

I took a pint of fresh organic blueberries, poked them with a toothpick, and gave them the TruFru treatment, but cleaner.

If you're looking for an easy high-protein summer snack or summer dessert that's refreshing, lower in sugar, and tastes like dessert, these frozen chocolate-covered protein TruFrus with blueberries are it. They're first dipped in a protein batter made with greek yogurt and vanilla protein, then dipped in dark chocolate for the perfect balance of creamy, crunchy, and sweet. This is a healthy frozen snack you'll actually look forward to. No blender. No baking. Just a handful of simple ingredients. And LOTS of antioxidants from the blueberries and dark chocolate. The perfect frozen summer snack, but high protein.

Here is how to make it:

1.Mix together 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1/2 scoop vanilla protein powder, a small splash of vanilla extract, and 2-3 tbsp milk of your choice. Add the milk slowly so it's not too thick or too watery. If its too thick, add a tiny more until you reach the consistency you see in my video.

2.Place a toothpick through each of the blueberries. Then dip each blueberry to coat the bottom, place on a parchment-lined tray, and freeze for 2 to 3 hours.

3.After this time, remove the blueberries from the freezer so you can dip them in melted chocolate. I used 85 percent dark chocolate to keep it lower sugar, but use whatever chocolate you like. Just set a heat-safe bowl over simmering water and stir until smooth.

4.Dip the frozen blueberries in chocolate, place back on the tray, and freeze for another 5 to 10 minutes. You want the chocolate to set, but not freeze them. This is important!

5.Remove the toothpicks, toss in a bowl, and enjoy.

These are my kind of homemade TruFrus. Protein-packed. Low sugar. Gluten-free. And so satisfying.

Perfect for:
A high-protein dessert
A healthy frozen snack
Low-carb sweet cravings
Summer snack boards
A fun nighttime treat

Send to someone who should make them for you!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.

summary: A recipe for homemade protein-coated chocolate-covered frozen blueberries made with Greek yogurt and vanilla protein powder.

caption:
My FAVE Starbucks breakfast — made at home!! ✨ comment RECIPE and I'll send you the link, or screenshot below!

Meet your new favorite high-protein breakfast! This one took a bit of workshopping but I am very happy to report it is justttt like the Starbucks version. Except way cheaper, higher protein, and JUST as easy. Here's how to make it!

EQUIPMENT
Large non-stick pan, about the size of your tortilla

INGREDIENTS
▢cooking spray or butter
▢⅔ cup chopped baby spinach, lightly packed
▢2 Tbsp sun dried tomatoes in oil, patted dry and diced small
▢½ cup egg whites
▢¼ tsp Italian seasoning
▢salt, to taste
▢1 large whole wheat tortilla
▢¼ cup shredded mozzarella cheese
▢2 Tbsp crumbled feta cheese

1. Heat your pan to medium. Spray with a thin and even layer of cooking spray.
2. Add baby spinach to the pan. Stir and cook until wilted, about 2-minutes.
3. Stir in sun dried tomatoes. Spread the ingredients evenly throughout the pan.
4. Pour in egg whites. Sprinkle with Italian seasoning and salt.
5. Immediately press your tortilla on top of the egg whites. Press down gently with your hands or the back of a spatula to ensure they stick together.
6. Cover and cook for about 2-minutes. You'll know you're ready for the next step when the tortilla and eggs are stuck together, and can easily slide around the pan.
7. Carefully flip. Now, your tortilla should be on the bottom and the egg whites on top.
8. Sprinkle with mozzarella and feta. Cover and let the cheese melt for 1-minute.
9. Use a flipper to fold in both sides of your tortilla in a letter fold. Brown the bottom of your folded tortilla for 1-2 minutes, flip, and repeat on the other side.
10. Remove from the pan and cool for 5-minutes on a cutting board. Slice in half, and enjoy!

Is you need another visual, I have step-by-step photos on the blog! https://reallifenutritionist.com/starbucks-spinach-feta-wrap/
summary: A homemade high-protein Starbucks-inspired spinach and feta egg wrap made with egg whites, sun-dried tomatoes, and melted cheese.

caption:
How to do an EASY CARD TRICK for your friends and family!!🃏🎩 This is one of the best self working card tricks you can possibly do. It's extremely powerful and super convincing!

This card trick works because when the red ace is placed onto the dealt packet, you place the rest of the deck on top of it. This puts the bottom card of the deck directly next to the red ace. You start with one black ace on the bottom and the other on top. The first red ace will end up next to the black ace that starts on the bottom of the deck, and the second red ace will end up next to the black ace that started on top (this black ace, at the beginning of the trick, was the first card dealt down to the table, which puts it on the bottom for the second round of dealing).
summary: A step-by-step tutorial for performing an easy self-working card trick with aces.

caption:
SHEET PAN QUESADILLAS // comment SHEET PAN for details on everything I used in this video sent to your DMs, SAVE this video to come back later and make it yourself!  The perfect lazy Sunday appetizer or dinner to throw together for football watching & cozy fall lounging! https://liketk.it/5t4p8 #quesadillas #easyrecipe #appetizer #whatsfordinner 

INGREDIENTS:
3 cups shredded rotisserie chicken
3 cups Monterey Jack cheese
14 burrito sized flour tortillas
1 stick butter, melted
1 can black beans, drained and rinsed
1 can corn, drained and rinsed
1 can rotel tomatoes and chiles
About 1 cup chopped green onion (I eyeballed it)
1/2 red onion, diced
1 cup sour cream
1 oz packet taco seasoning
TOPPINGS - sour cream, avocados, salsa, limes, cheese and cilantro

INSTRUCTIONS:
1) Preheat oven to 450.  In a large bowl, mix together chicken, cheese, beans, corn, rotel tomatoes & chiles, red onion, green onion, sour cream and taco seasoning. Set aside

2) Brush melted butter on cookie sheet, then layer on tortillas, overlapping sides + 2 in the center. Evenly distribute quesadilla mixture on top of tortillas. Place 2 tortillas in the center, on top of mixture then fold over tortillas on the edges. Brush on the rest of your melted butter. Place in oven with second cookie sheet on top of quesadilla. Cook for 30-35 minutes. 

3) Remove from oven & using a pizza cutter, cut quesadilla into slices. Add desired toppings, serve and enjoy!
summary: Sheet pan quesadillas with rotisserie chicken, cheese, beans, and rotel tomatoes baked until crispy and served with toppings.

caption:
Greek chicken kebabs 🇬🇷
Serves: 3 | Prep: 5-10 min | Cook: 20 min
Macros (per serving): 364 kcal | 52g protein 

Ingredients:
- 500g 5% fat chicken mince
- 60g cup cottage cheese
- Bunch of chopped parsley
- One small onion
- 20g tomato puree
- 4-5 garlic cloves
- One lemon
- 1tsp salt, pepper, cumin, chili flakes & garlic powder 

Directions:
1. Preheat oven to 200 degrees.
2. Chop up your onion, garlic, and parsley. 
3. In a large bowl mix your chicken mince, cottage cheese, juice of 1/2 lemon, chopped parsley, chopped garlic and onion. Dont forget your seasoning here!! Get stuck in with your fingers for best flavours 🥲
4. On parchment paper or foil lined oven tray, spread the chicken mix flat, press with your fingers to spread. Use a spatula to slice into 1-inch pieces
5. Bake for 15–20 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
6. While it cooks, throw together your high-protein tzatziki 
7. 0% greek yoghurt, dill, garlic powder & juice of lemon. Mix it up, mix it up.
8. Serve in a flat bread with the tzatziki and any other toppings you like! 

High-Protein Greek Yogurt Tzatziki
- 375g 0% Greek yogurt
- 1/2 shredded cucumber
- Juice of 1/2 lemon
- Galic powder
- Chopped dill
- 1 tsp salt + pepper

Mix it up, mix it up.

The recipe above is for three servings 
The calories and protein are for one servings of the chicken kebabs and the tzatziki together! … I know it's good 🤤

#mealprepmadeeasy #highproteinmeals #highproteindiet 

Bon apetit!
summary: Greek chicken kebab recipe with high-protein tzatziki sauce, 52g protein per serving, ready in 30 minutes.

Roasted Carrots 

10 peeled carrots cut into chunks
1 onion cut into chunks
Season with Olive Oil, Garlic Powder, Salt, Pepper
2 'glugs' of Maple Syrup

caption:
Chicken broccoli cheddar soup with crispy cheese toast✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Soup:▪️4 Tbsp Butter▪️1 Medium Onion, diced small▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️1/4 Cup Flour▪️3+1/2 Cups Chicken Stock▪️1 lb. Boneless Skinless Chicken Breasts or Thighs▪️1 Large Yukon Gold Potato (6 oz.), peeled and diced▪️2 Cups Whole Milk▪️2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1 tsp Freshly Ground Black Pepper▪️1 Dash Nutmeg▪️1 Dash Cayenne Pepper▪️12 oz. Broccoli Florets (4 Cups), roughly chopped▪️3 Cups Shredded Sharp Cheddar Cheese▪️

Crispy Cheese Toast:▪️2 Slices Sourdough Bread▪️1 Tbsp Butter, room temp▪️1/2 Cup Shredded Sharp Cheddar Cheese▪️

1️⃣ In a large pot over medium heat, melt the butter, then add the onion. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 3-5 mins, until the onion begins to soften.
2️⃣ Add the garlic and flour. Cook, stirring, for 1 min, then gradually add the stock 1 cup at a time, stirring to incorporate after each addition. 
3️⃣ Add the chicken and diced potato. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-18 minutes, until the chicken is cooked through (internal temp reaches 165F). Remove the chicken to a plate and let cool.
4️⃣ Add the milk, salt, pepper, nutmeg, cayenne, and broccoli. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer 5 mins, stirring occasionally, until the broccoli is tender. Push some of the potatoes against the side of the pot with the back of a wooden spoon to encourage them to break down. Meanwhile, shred the chicken with your fingers or two forks.
5️⃣ Turn off heat and stir in the cheddar and shredded chicken. Cover to keep warm.
6️⃣ Butter one side of each slice of sourdough. Heat a nonstick skillet over medium heat and sprinkle a thin, even layer of shredded cheddar onto the pan, about the same size as one slice of bread. Working one slice at a time, place the bread butter-side up on the cheese and cook until the cheese is golden and crisp, about 1-2 mins. Carefully slide a spatula under the toast, flip, and cook the other side for 1-2 mins, until golden brown and crisp. Cut the toast into smaller pieces, and serve with the soup. Enjoy! 

Serves 4
summary: Creamy chicken broccoli cheddar soup recipe served with crispy cheese toast.

caption:
Creamy chicken enchilada skillet✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Skillet:▪️2 Tbsp Avocado Oil▪️1 lb. Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast▪️2 Tbsp Butter▪️1/2 of a Medium Yellow Onion, diced small▪️1 Small Jalapeño, seeds removed and diced small▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1 tsp Chili Powder▪️1 tsp Cumin▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️1 Tbsp Flour▪️1 Cup Whole Milk▪️10 oz. Can Mild Green Enchilada Sauce▪️4 oz. Can Mild Diced Green Chilis, with their juices▪️15 oz. Can White Beans, drained and rinsed▪️6 Corn Tortillas, each sliced in 8 triangles▪️1 Cup Shredded Mexican Blend Cheese▪️

Serving:▪️Fresh Cilantro, chopped▪️Sour Cream▪️Avocado, sliced▪️Pickled Jalapeños▪️Lime Wedges▪️Tortilla Chips▪️

1️⃣ Season both sides of the chicken generously with salt and pepper.
2️⃣ In a large oven safe skillet, heat the avocado oil over medium heat, then add the chicken breasts. Cook for 6-8 mins per side (internal temp 165F). Remove the chicken from the skillet and set aside to cool. If the skillet is super hot and smoking, turn off the heat and let it cool down for a couple mins before proceeding. 
3️⃣ Turn the heat on the skillet to medium and add the butter, onion, and jalapeño. Cook while stirring until the onions are starting to become tender and translucent (3-4 mins), then add the salt, chili powder, cumin, garlic, and flour.
4️⃣ Cook while stirring for 1 minute, then start gradually adding the milk 1/4 cup at a time, stirring to fully incorporate it before adding more. Once all the milk is incorporated, add the green enchilada sauce and diced green chilis. Mix and simmer uncovered over low heat for about 5 minutes while you shred the chicken. 
5️⃣ Shred the chicken into small pieces with your fingers (or two forks) and add it to the skillet along with the white beans. Mix. Add the tortillas, mix, and cook for 2-3 more mins until heated through. Turn off the heat. 
6️⃣ Spread everything in the skillet into one even layer and top with shredded cheese. Transfer the skillet to the oven and broil for 2-3 mins until the cheese is melted and has golden brown spots. Sprinkle with fresh cilantro and enjoy with toppings :)

Serves 4
summary: One-skillet creamy chicken enchilada recipe with green sauce, white beans, and cheese topping.

caption:
Orange Pepper Chicken Rice Bowls🍗🍊🍚

Perfect sweet and savoury balance, chicken is crispy & juicy with that signature orange pepper flavour, paired with garlic herb infused rice and a tangy cucumber salad🤌🏽

Macros Per Serving (5 Total)

518 Calories | 49g Protein | 55g Carbs | 12g Fat

Ingredients (5 Servings)

Orange Pepper Chicken
- 1100g Raw Boneless Skinless Chicken Thighs, trim any excess fat
- 1 Tsp Salt
- 2.5 Tsp Black Pepper
- 2.5 Tsp Garlic powder
- 30g Honey
- 25g Sriracha
- 1 Large Orange Juiced + Orange Zest. I like to add bits of the pulp in the marinade for extra caramelized texture
- 25g Light Soy Sauce

• Marinate chicken for 30 mins or overnight for maximum depth of flavor
• Spread evenly on a lined sheet pan, squeeze extra orange juice and zest. This will caramelize while cooking and create a sticky glaze.
• Oven bake or air fry for 25-27 mins at 200C/400F. To achieve a good crust, you can broil for 2-3 mins at the end

Crunchy Cucumber Salad
- 250g Mini Cucumbers, thinly sliced
- 100g Red Onion, thinly sliced
- 120g Carrots, grated
- 100g Red Bell Pepper, chopped
- 50g Green Onion/Scallion
- 25g Sesame Seeds
- 15g Honey
- 20-25g Rice Vinegar

💡Meal Prep Tip
Keep the salad in separate meal prep containers and combine right before eating to maintain crunch. The vinegar base helps preserve the texture for 2-3 days

Garlic Butter Herb Rice
- 220g Uncooked Basmati Rice 
- 20g Light Butter
- 30g Garlic, finely chopped
- 1.5 Tbsp Fresh Parsley, chopped
- Pinch of Salt & Chilli Flakes

Let the rice cool slightly before folding in the herbs to keep them vibrant and fresh. You could also top each container with a sprinkle of fresh herbs right before serving to brighten it up

caption:
Made this twice in three days. 😋
Such an easy recipe with basic household staples but the result is just so APPETISING! Perfect for any meal of the week because it's basically macro friendly comfort food. 🔥

Iffa Chicken
Makes 3 servings - 335 kcal | 39.7g protein | 2.0g carbs | 17.7g fat per Serving
Ingredients
For the marinade:
	•	543g boneless chicken thighs (or chicken breast)
	•	½ cup + 1½ tbsp (100g) whisked low-fat curd
	•	1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	¼ tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp coriander powder
	•	½ tsp pepper powder
	•	½ tsp garam masala
	•	1 tsp vinegar
	•	Salt to taste

For the white sauce:
	•	2 boiled egg whites
	•	1¼ low-fat cheese slices (≈25g)
	•	¼ cup + 1 tbsp (60ml) skim milk
	•	1 tsp vinegar
	•	2 garlic cloves
	•	A pinch of salt
	•	½ tsp sugar (optional)

For cooking:
	•	1 tsp total oil spray (≈5g)
	•	Chopped coriander leaves, for garnish

Method
	1.	Marinate the chicken:
	•	In a large bowl, mix curd, ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, coriander, pepper, garam masala, vinegar, and salt.
	•	Add chicken and mix until well coated.
	•	Let it marinate for at least 1 hour, or overnight for best flavor.
	2.	Prepare the white sauce:
	•	Blend together egg whites, cheese, skim milk, vinegar, garlic, salt, and sugar.
	•	Blend until smooth, creamy, and slightly tangy. Set aside.
	3.	Cook the chicken:
	•	Heat a nonstick pan and spray with oil.
	•	Add marinated chicken and cook on medium-high heat for 4–5 minutes each side until golden brown.
	•	Lower the heat, cover, and cook for another 4–5 minutes until the masala thickens and forms a rich gravy base.
	4.	Add the white sauce:
	•	Pour the sauce evenly over the chicken.
	•	Cover and cook on low heat for 2–3 minutes, allowing the sauce to gently coat and thicken.
	5.	Finish:
	•	Garnish with chopped coriander.
	•	Serve hot with millet rotis, red rice, or sautéed vegetables.

.

.
#healthyrecipes #iffachicken #healthydinner
summary: High-protein Iffa Chicken recipe with marinated chicken thighs and creamy egg-cheese sauce, macro-friendly and ready in under 30 minutes.

caption:
POWER & GLOW IMMUNITY SOUP🥕🔥

👉👉Comment "SLURP" and I'll send you a FREE EBOOK  with 3 of my favorite high protein soup recipes. 

This not only delivers big on protein, but does some serious work for your skin, gut, and immunity with a powerful punch of retinol.

For the whole pot: 885 calories,71g protein, 77 net carbs
For 4 servings: 221 calories, 18g protein, 19 net carbs

It's cozy, easy to make, and super filling. Split it into 3 or 4 servings, and you have plenty as a super filling complement to any meal.

I used soft tofu here instead of cottage cheese, but 3 servings of cottage cheese works great too!  The curry, ginger, roasted garlic, and smoked paprika bring the flavor, and the shaved parmesan at the end adds the perfect umami

Here's how to make it:

1️⃣ Preheat your oven to 400°F.

2️⃣ Peel and chop 2 pounds of carrots. Roughly chop 1 white onion. Add to a lined sheet pan with 10-12 garlic cloves.

3️⃣ Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil and season with 1 tsp curry powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, a couple generous pinches of salt, and cracked black pepper. Salt is key here!

4️⃣ Roast for 45–50 mins until everything is tender and golden.

5️⃣ Transfer the roasted carrots, onion, and garlic to a blender or large pot.

6️⃣ Add 4 cups bone broth, 1 block of soft or silken tofu (or 3 servings cottage cheese), 1 tbsp fresh grated ginger, the juice of half a lemon, and some chili flakes for a little heat.

7️⃣ Blend until creamy and smooth. Taste and adjust with more salt, curry powder, or lemon juice as needed.

8️⃣ Pour into bowls and top with freshly grated parmesan, some fresh basil, a squeeze of lemon juice, and toasted seeds for the perfect finish.

If you make it, be sure to let me know what you think on SHREDHAPPENS!

Enjoy!!
.
.
.
.
#souprecipe #healthyrecipe #goodmoodfood #healthyfoodshare
summary: A high-protein roasted carrot and ginger soup recipe made with tofu or cottage cheese, designed for immunity and skin health.

caption:
HIGH PROTEIN STUFFED SWEET POTATOES

This is another healthy lunch idea thats so easy and so good.

It comes in at 478 cals, 41 grams protein, and will keep you full for hours from the slow digesting carbs, fiber, and all the protein.

If you are watching carbs closely, feel free to use spaghetti squash, zucchini, or your favorite vessel.

Here is how I made it (makes 4 servings)

1.Place 4 sweet potatoes (~125g each) in the oven at 350°F for 75 mins. Time depends on thickness, so if your potatos are larger, go a little longer. These hold up great in the fridge all week, so feel free to make more.

2.Cook 1 lb lean ground beef (I use 93/7). Season with salt, pepper, and 2 tbsp taco seasoning (or just season with 1tbsp garlic powder, 1 tbsp oregano, 1btsp smoked paprika, & 1/2 tsp cumin). Lightly spray a pan with avocado oil, add seasoned ground beef, and continuously mince until fully cooked through.

3.For the protein queso process 1 cup cottage cheese or Greek yogurt with ½ cup shredded cheddar, ½ tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp onion powder, ¼ tsp smoked paprika, and a splash (1–2 tbsp) milk or water until smooth. Warm gently on LOW heat until melted and creamy, then season with salt to taste before serving.

4.For the guac, just mash 1 avocado with the juice of 1 lime, a large handful of freshly chopped cilantro, 1/4 small white onion, a couple pinches of salt, and pepper.

5.For the pico or tomato salad, finely dice 1/3 red onion, add half a pint of quartered cherry tomato, a handful of finely chopped fresh cilantro, 1 finely diced and deseeded jalapeño, the juice of 1 lime, and a generous pinch of salt.

6.When the sweet potato are done, just let them cool for a minute or two, then cut them open, mash the inside with the fork, then fill them up with the following: 1 quarter of the protein queso, 4oz of cooked beef, a quarter of the mashed avocado, pico, garnish with fresh cilantro, and a light drizzle of sour cream.

As always, if you make it, be sure to let me know on SHREDHAPPENS!

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#proteinrecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood #healthyfoodshare #whatsonmyplate #lowcarb
summary: A high-protein stuffed sweet potato recipe with seasoned ground beef, cheese sauce, guacamole, and pico de gallo.

caption:
3 PROTEIN POWER BAKES 💥 that hit the spot

➡️ Comment "BAKE" and I'll DM you the full recipes for each one.

Fall is a busy season with school, work, and less time to cook, but I promise you can make simple healthy meals to stay on track. These easy, meal prep worthy high protein bakes are filling, comforting, and so good. 

I used my high protein low carb @kaizenfoodco pasta and rice with 20g protein per serving, but you can use whatever pasta or rice you prefer.

1.HIGH PROTEIN CHICKEN ENCHILADA BAKE

This is creamy, garlicky, and ridiculously good. Each serving has 360 calories and 29g protein, and you can always add chicken or shrimp for more protein. All the flavors of stuffed peppers in one easy bake. Each serving has 420 calories, 49g protein, and 15 net carbs. caption:
Crispy chicken spiedini with garlic butter✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

Chicken:▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1 Tbsp Lemon Juice▪️2 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️1/4 tsp Red Pepper Flakes▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/2 tsp Freshly Ground Black Pepper▪️2 Chicken Breasts (~1 lb.), cut into 1-inch cubes▪️1/4 Cup Flour▪️2 Eggs▪️1/2 Cup Panko Breadcrumbs▪️1/2 Cup Italian Breadcrumbs▪️1/2 Cup Grated Parmesan▪️Avocado Oil Spray or Olive Oil Spray▪️Wooden Skewers▪️Lemon Wedges, for serving▪️

Garlic Butter:▪️3 Tbsp Salted Butter▪️1 Clove Garlic, finely chopped▪️1 Tbsp Finely Chopped Fresh Parsley▪️1/2 tsp Lemon Zest▪️

1️⃣ In a medium bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, red pepper flakes, salt, and pepper. Add the chicken cubes and toss to coat. Marinate for at least 30 mins or up to 4 hours in the fridge.
2️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F convection (or 450 regular bake). Set a wire rack on top of a sheet pan and spray the rack with oil to prevent sticking. (If you don't have a rack, lightly oil the sheet pan instead.)
3️⃣ Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten egg, and one with the panko, Italian breadcrumbs, and Parmesan combined.
4️⃣ Dredge each piece of chicken in flour, then egg, then breadcrumb mixture, pressing it on with your fingers to adhere. Thread the breaded chicken pieces onto skewers, about 4-5 pieces per skewer. Arrange skewers on the wire rack and spray with oil.
5️⃣ Bake for 12-15 mins, until golden and cooked through (165F internal temperature). Option to broil for 1-2 mins at the end to get the tops extra golden brown.
6️⃣ Meanwhile, melt the butter in a small skillet over medium-low heat. Add garlic and cook 30–60 secs until fragrant. Transfer into a small bowl and stir in the parsley and lemon zest.
7️⃣ Drizzle or brush the chicken with the garlic butter and serve warm with lemon wedges. Enjoy!

⏲25 min prep time / 1 hour 10 min total time (includes a 30 min marinade)

Recipe serves 4
summary: Crispy chicken spiedini skewers with garlic butter, baked and finished with a lemon-herb compound butter.

caption:
Here's how you can train your upper body in under 15 minutes.

Grab a pair of dumbbells and perform:
• 10 Hammer Curls – focus on keeping your elbows tucked in and squeeze your biceps at the top.
• 10 Lateral Raises – lift only to shoulder height and control the way down to target your delts.
• 10 Overhead Presses – keep your ribs down and drive the weights straight up, not forward.
• 10 Upright Rows – pull the weights toward your chest while keeping your shoulders relaxed.

Repeat for 3–4 rounds, resting 30–60 seconds between rounds.

This simple combo hits your biceps, shoulders, and upper back, improving strength, posture, and shoulder stability. Perfect for days when you're short on time but still want to feel strong.

caption:
SUPER CRISPY POTATOES + BRAVAS SAUCE

This potato cooking technique is so key, and I love using this types of potatoes for hashes, fancy brunch or breakfasts, any time you want a super crispy potato. I learned this lesson from @kenjilopezalt from his video on @seriouseats on YouTube.
For the full ratio, check out the recipe on the YouTube video. Let me know if you want it and I can DM the link to the video

ingredients: water, salt, baking soda, potatoes

Notes: 
-I used Yukon gold potatoes, russets also work nicely 
-boiling potatoes will release some of the moisture from the potato which is what we want, before we bake em 
-I added microplaned Parmesan cheese, this is optional, but it's more flavour and more surface area that can potentially crisp up
-Baked in the oven at 425°F (convection on) - this circulates hot air better

PATATAS BRAVAS SAUCE [👇🏼RECIPE]

My non-traditional spin on a bravas sauce. I'm using korean gochugaru as the source of heat, it's mild, slightly sweet and smokey, and plays well with the spanish paprika. Feel free to add more chilli flakes or powder to your own liking.

1.5 tbsp neutral oil (I use avocado oil)
3 cloves of garlic (minced)
1 tsp sweet smoked paprika
1 tsp gochugaru (korean chilli powder)
2 tbsp tomato paste
3-4 oz water
a dollup of mayo (optional - I used avocado oil mayo)
1/2 tsp salt to taste
1 tsp apple cider vinegar

Method:

1. on medium heat, sweat the garlic in neutral oil (20 seconds)
2. add paprika and gochugaru and coat evenly into the oil (20 seconds)
3. add tomato paste, mix evenly and cook out tomato sauce. Move frequently to prevent burning
4. add water, incorporate evenly into the sauce
5. season with salt
6. remove from heat and cool to room temp
7. once cooled, add apple cider vinegar, and mayo, and mix thoroughly
summary: Crispy oven-baked potatoes with a Korean-Spanish fusion bravas sauce using gochugaru and smoked paprika.

caption:
@recipeincaption 's Sheet Pan Chicken Kefta Wraps 🔥

For the Chicken Kefta:
-1.5–2 lbs ground chicken (700-900 g)
-2 tbsp tomato paste
-8 garlic cloves, minced
-2 tsp smoked paprika, 2 tsp cumin, 2 tsp salt, 2 tsp black pepper, 1 tsp turmeric, 2 tsp garlic powder
-1/3 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1/3 cup fresh cilantro, chopped (optional)
-1 large or 2 medium yellow onions, grated + squeezed dry*
-On top before baking: 4 tbsp olive oil
For the Roasted Garlic:
-2 garlic heads, 1 tbsp olive oil and 1 tsp salt
For the Fresh Salad:
-1/2 large red onion, thinly sliced
-1 cup cherry tomatoes, quartered
-1/4 cup fresh parsley, chopped
-1 tsp salt, 1 tsp pepper & 2 tsp sumac
-Juice of 1/2 lemon
Roasted Garlic Paprika Sauce:
-Roasted garlic cloves, 1/4 cup mayo, 1/4 cup Greek yogurt, juice of 1/2 lemon, 1 tsp paprika + 1 tsp pepper

1. Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Slice the tops off two garlic heads, drizzle with olive oil and salt, wrap in foil, and roast for 45–60 minutes until golden and soft. While the garlic roasts, prep the kefta.
2. Grate the onion, squeeze out all liquid with paper towel or cheesecloth. (This prevents watery kefta.) In a large bowl, combine chicken, tomato paste, minced garlic, spices, parsley, cilantro (if using), and the grated onion. Mix with your hands for 2–3 minutes until sticky and well combined.
3. Spread onto the sheet pan in a 1/2-inch (1.25 cm) thick rectangle. With a knife, score into 1-inch strips (2.5 cm). Drizzle evenly with 4 tbsp olive oil.
4. Bake for 12–15 minutes, then broil 2–5 minutes until golden and slightly crisp on top.
5. While baking, toss onion, tomatoes, parsley, salt, pepper, sumac, and lemon juice in a bowl.
6. Squeeze roasted garlic cloves into a bowl, mash, then mix with mayo, yogurt, lemon juice, paprika, and pepper until smooth.
7. On lavash or pita, slather sauce, add 1–2 strips of chicken kefta, top with salad. Roll tight. For extra magic, crisp the wrap in a lightly oiled skillet for 1-2 mins per side.
summary: Sheet pan chicken kefta wraps with roasted garlic sauce, fresh salad, and optional pan-crisping.

caption:
If you haven't tried egg chutney yet — you're missing out! A higher protein, South Indian-style chutney that's rich, nutty, and packed with flavor. Perfect with dosa, idli, or even as a dip for your evening snack

Spicy Garlic Egg Chutney
Makes 2 Servings 
~138 kcal | 10.9 g protein | 7.4 g fat | 6.8 g carbs per Serving

Ingredients
For the chutney:
	•	2 tsp (10 g) coconut oil
	•	10 garlic cloves, sliced
	•	1 medium onion (50 g), sliced
	•	1 sprig curry leaves
	•	1 medium tomato (60 g), chopped
	•	½ tsp chili powder
	•	¼ tsp turmeric powder
	•	Salt to taste
	•	1 tbsp (10 g) roasted peanuts
	•	4–5 boiled egg whites (~160 g)

For the tadka:
	•	1 tsp coconut oil
	•	½ tsp mustard seeds
	•	2 dried red chilies
	•	Few curry leaves

Instructions
	1.	Sauté the aromatics:
Heat 2 tsp coconut oil in a pan. Add sliced garlic, onion, and curry leaves. Sauté until the onions turn light golden.
	2.	Add flavor:
Add chopped tomatoes, chili powder, turmeric powder, salt, and roasted peanuts. Cook until the tomatoes soften and the oil begins to separate.
	3.	Blend:
Turn off the heat. Once slightly cooled, transfer to a blender along with 4–5 boiled egg whites. Add a splash of water to reach your preferred consistency and blend into a smooth chutney.
	4.	Tadka (tempering):
Heat 1 tsp coconut oil. Add mustard seeds, dried red chilies, and curry leaves. Let them splutter. Pour this over the chutney.
	5.	Serve:
Mix well and enjoy with dosa, idli, or as a dip for vegetable sticks or crackers!
.

(egg chutney, high protein chutney, South Indian recipe, healthy Indian dip, macro-friendly chutney, low-calorie Indian side, boiled egg recipe, peanut chutney, protein-packed breakfast)

.

#HighProteinIndianRecipes #HealthyChutney #SouthIndianFood #MacroFriendlyMeals #easyproteinrecipes
summary: A spicy garlic egg chutney recipe with 10.9g protein per serving, served with dosa or idli.

caption:
🌿 Mutter Pilao🌿

A simple, comforting classic. Perfectly fluffy basmati rice cooked with peas and warm spices.

Ingredients:
1/4 cup oil
1 medium onion finely chopped
1 tbsp ginger and garlic (chopped or paste)
1 tbsp cumin seeds
4 cloves
10 black peppercorns
1 inch cinnamon stick
2 black cardamoms
1.5 cups petit pois peas
2 tsp salt or to taste
4 cups basmati rice washed and soaked
1 chicken stock cube diluted in 1 glass water
7 cups water

Method:
	1.	Fry onions until deep golden.
	2.	Add ginger and garlic and cook for 2 minutes. Add whole spices and cook for another 2 minutes. Add a splash of water if it starts to burn — it also helps release the colour.
	3.	Add peas, stock and salt. Boil for 5 minutes until peas are tender.
	4.	Add rice and all the water. Water should sit about 1 inch above the rice. Cook on high until the water is absorbed.
	5.	Top with chillies and place a paper towel under the lid. Move the pot to a smaller hob, lowest flame, and steam for 10 to 15 minutes.
	6.	Turn off the heat, fluff the rice, and serve hot.

#MutterPilao #PeaPilaf #PakistaniRecipes #RiceLovers #WeeknightDinner
summary: A recipe for mutter pilao, a Pakistani rice dish with basmati, peas, and warm spices.

caption:
Oil separation ✅ No chopping ✅ One pot cook ✅ - this recipe has everything, cheers @whatinthechef! 

Serves 2-4 
Total time: 1hr 30 mins (plus marinating time) 
 
Ingredients 

4 tbsp ghee 
2 tbsp natural yogurt 
6-8 garlic cloves, grated 
1 white onion, grated 
3/4 tbsp ginger paste 
2 tbsp tomato puree 
1/2 tbsp turmeric 
1/2 tbsp cumin powder 
1 tbsp coriander powder 
1/2 tbsp paprika 
1/2 tbsp garam masala 
1/2 tbsp red chilli flakes 
2 handfuls of crispy fried onions 
large pinch of mint, chopped 
large pinch of fresh coriander, chopped 
4 chicken thighs (bone in) - skinless 
4 chicken drumsticks - skinless 
2 handfuls of baby potatoes, quartered 
large pinch of salt 
sprinkle of black pepper 

Method

1. In a bowl combine the ghee, yogurt, garlic, ginger, tomato puree, the spices, crispy fried onions, mint, and coriander. This is your marinade

2. Place the chicken pieces (slit them before hand), grated onion and potatoes (cut them small first) in the bowl. Add over a large pinch of salt and pepper. Then mix the marinade into the chicken and potatoes. Once done, you can set this aside in the fridge to marinade, ideally for at least 2 hours or overnight

3. Pre-heat the oven to gas mark 6 (200°C). Remove the bowl at least 30 minutes from the fridge before you're ready to cook them. Add the chicken and potatoes to a baking tray. Cover the tray with foil, then place it in the oven for 30 minutes

4. Once done, remove the foil and use a basting brush to brush the juice over the chicken. Give the potatoes a toss and shimmy the tray a few times

5. Place the tray back in the oven for a further 30 minutes with the foil off. After the 30 minutes, remove it and give it a final shake and brush the juices over the top of the chicken pieces once more. Then place it back in the oven for a final 10-15 minutes

6. Remove the tray from the oven and sprinkle over some fresh coriander and a squeeze of lemon juice. Allow the chicken to rest for a few minutes. Serve over some white rice

#masala #traybake #chickenrecipe #easyrecipe
summary: One-pot Indian masala chicken and potato traybake with ghee and yogurt marinade, served with rice.

caption:
Comfort food maxxx 🫠
A cozy bowl of Sri Lankan-style coconut spinach dal 🥥🌿 — creamy, garlicky and hits all the right spots.
Made with masoor dal, coconut milk, and spinach, it's that perfect mix of homely and tropical — pair it with rice or rotis for the ultimate comfort meal 💛
🥥 Sri Lankan-Style Coconut Spinach Dal
Serving Size: 3 servings
Macros (per serving): ~230 kcal | 10g protein | 18g carbs | 12g fat

🌿 Ingredients
	•	1 cup masoor dal (red lentils), washed and soaked 10–15 mins
	•	1 medium onion, finely chopped
	•	1 green chilli, slit
	•	A few curry leaves
	•	1 tsp grated ginger
	•	1 tsp grated garlic
	•	1 medium tomato, chopped
	•	½ tsp turmeric powder
	•	½ tsp red chilli powder
	•	¼ tsp fenugreek (methi) powder
	•	¾ cup coconut milk (divided — ½ cup for cooking + ¼ cup later)
	•	1½ cups water (adjust consistency)
	•	Salt to taste
	•	1 handful fresh spinach leaves, roughly chopped
	•	1 tsp coconut oil
For tempering (tadka):
	•	½ tsp mustard seeds
	•	1 dried red chilli
	•	A few curry leaves
	•	1 tsp coconut oil
To finish:
	•	Chopped coriander leaves
	•	A squeeze of lemon juice

🫕 Method
	1.	In a kadai, add the soaked masoor dal, onion, chilli, curry leaves, ginger, garlic, tomato, turmeric, chilli powder, fenugreek powder, coconut milk (½ cup), water, and salt.
	2.	Mix well, cover, and cook for 10 minutes on medium flame.
	3.	Open the lid, stir well, and continue cooking uncovered for another 10 minutes — keep stirring occasionally so it doesn't stick.
	4.	Add the remaining ¼ cup coconut milk and spinach leaves. Cook till spinach softens.
	5.	In a small pan, heat coconut oil and add mustard seeds, dried red chilli, and curry leaves. Let them splutter.
	6.	Pour this tadka into the dal, turn off the flame, and finish with coriander leaves and lemon juice.
.

caption:
NRIs lose crores of inheritance in India every year... and are unable to claim ownership on their parents assets in India...

That's because the moment they go to claim the property and get it registered in their name... they are asked for a document - the "Succession Certificate"

And to get this is one of the hardest things on the planet if their parents did not leave a Registered Will mentioning them as the legal heir.

The Succession Certificate is a document issued by the district courts... It grants authority to manage the deceased's bank accounts, investments, and financial assets. 

The process requires filing a petition in the court where the deceased resided or where assets are located, followed by a mandatory 45-day public notice period allowing for objections.

And till the time these documents are not obtained, banks freeze the accounts, not letting anyone use the account, draw money, or make payments from them... Same with Demat accounts and shares held in parents name.

So the NRI has to get all their foreign documents apostilled in the foreign country, and make a Power of Attorney to appear in court, file several affidavits and indemnities.

And this entire process of obtaining this certificate takes anywhere between 6 to 18 months...

The stamp duty could be anywhere between 2-3% of the asset value... and some lawyers charge a % of the asset value, as high as 5%...

In Delhi, the Court Fee itself is 4% of the Estate Value.

For a professional to charge a % of such value may actually not be permitted under some regulations, but it is a market reality.

And this entire process can cost no less than Rs 10-20 lakhs and a couple of years.

To avoid all of this and to ease the process, I would recommend each NRI to do two things -

First, please update your names under nomination at every bank, insurance policy, mutual fund, and demat account. It may sound boring, but should be a priority.

And second, get your parents to make a Registered Will specifying the assets, the beneficiaries, and the identifying details therein.

#casarthakahuja
summary: Explanation of the succession certificate process for NRIs claiming inherited assets in India, including costs, timeline, and recommended preventative measures.

caption:
💥Sheet Pan Chicken Kabobs

This entire plate was 550 calories and 71g protein! I served two skewers over my high protein, low carb @kaizenfoodco rice with a dollop of garlic yogurt sauce and a simple salad, and it hit every craving. High protein food middle eastern / Mediterranean food that tastes SO good.

Ingredients:

2 lbs ground chicken
1 small red onion
3–4 garlic cloves
Large handful fresh parsley
1 jalapeño
2 tbsp tomato paste
1 tbsp sumac
1 tsp Aleppo pepper (or chili flakes)
½ tsp ground cumin
1 tbsp smoked paprika
¼ tsp turmeric
Salt and pepper to taste
1 tbsp olive oil

Here's how I made it:

1️⃣ Preheat oven to 425°F.

2️⃣ In a small food processor, add 1 small red onion, 3 to 4 garlic cloves, and a handful of parsley. Pulse until finely minced, or finely chop by hand.

3️⃣ In a large bowl, combine 2 lbs ground chicken, the onion-garlic-parsley mix, 2 tbsp tomato paste, 1 finely chopped and deseeded jalapeño, and the spices (1 tbsp sumac, 1 tsp Aleppo pepper, ½ tsp cumin, 1 tbsp smoked paprika, ¼ tsp turmeric, salt, & pepper). Drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil.

4️⃣ Mix gently until evenly combined. Do not overwork it. Spread the mixture evenly onto a sheet pan.

5️⃣ Use a knife or spatula to score long vertical lines through the meat so each strip looks like an individual kabob & leave a bit of space between them.

6️⃣ Bake for 18–22 mins, then move to the top rack & broil for 3 minutes until golden.

7️⃣ Garlic Yogurt Sauce: Mix ½ cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1–2 small grated garlic cloves, 1 tbsp chopped fresh dill, and a pinch of salt until smooth.

8️⃣ Salad: Combine 2 diced cucumber, 1 chopped tomato, 1/2 thinly sliced red onion, a handful of chopped parsley, juice of ½ lemon, olive oil, 1 tsp sumac, salt & pepper. Toss well.

9️⃣ Get your fav rice ready. I used Kaizen high protein rice, but any rice works. Using my rice added 20g protein.

Plate it up with Kaizen rice, 2 chicken skewers, the salad, and garlic yogurt sauce. Finish with a sprinkle of sumac and fresh dill.

If you make it, tag me on @shredhappens

ENJOY!
.
.
.
.
#lowcarbrecipes #lowcarb #keto #ketorecipes #healthyrecipes #goodmoodfood
summary: Sheet pan chicken kabobs with garlic yogurt sauce, cucumber salad, and high-protein rice—550 calories and 71g protein per serving.

caption:
Who needs calcium tablets when your kitchen has this? 🥄✨

A simple roasted mix of methi seeds (fenugreek for bones), flax seeds (richest omega 3 vegetarian food), fennel seeds (cooling & digestive), black pepper (absorption booster) in a spoon of ghee/coconut oil is literally kitchen medicine 
Ayurveda.

🔬 Science says:
Fenugreek preserves calcium & supports hormone balance, thanks to good phytoestrogens. Hot in potency so should never be taken alone. 

Flax adds plant-based omega 3 for bone density & joint care. So rich in ALA & lignans = great joint health. Also, nourishes tissues, builds Shukra (reproductive) & Asthi dhatu, keeps dryness (Vata) in check.

Fennel cools digestion + adds natural phytoestrogens + antioxidants → supports bone strength, improves digestion. Did you know, it pacifies methi's heat, balances Pitta, improves Agni (digestion) gently without aggravating acidity.

Black pepper Piperine boosts bioavailability of nutrients like calcium, magnesium & Omega-3 → so your body actually absorbs what you eat.

Ghee helps fat-soluble vitamins (A, D, K) reach your bones. Act as Anupana (carrier), taking herbs/nutrients into deeper dhatus. Ghee is tridoshic; coconut oil is cooling and good for Pitta.

This is such a gorgeous nuskha for your bone and joint health — it blends both Ayurvedic and modern nutritional science beautifully.

📏 Ratio 
For a small batch (say 50 - 60g mix):
Methi seeds: 2 tbsp (20 g)
Flax seeds: 2 tbsp (20 g)
Fennel seeds: 1 tbsp (10 g)
Black pepper: ½ tsp (2–3 g)
Ghee / Coconut oil: 1 tsp (just enough to roast lightly)

Together → They strengthen Asthi dhatu (bones), reduce joint pain, retain calcium and support overall health naturally.

caption:
Healthy chicken tikka masala 
Feeds 2
 
1 tbsp olive oil 
1 thumb sized piece of ginger, finely grated
2 garlic cloves, minced
1 onion, finely diced
1 heaped tsp ground coriander
1 tsp ground cumin
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp turmeric
1/2 tsp chilli powder
2 tbsp tomato purée
300 chicken breast or thigh, diced
300ml chicken stock
Salt and pepper to taste
2 heaped tbsp fage 0% Greek yoghurt (stir in at the end)
1 tsp garam masala 
Squeeze of lemon to finish
Fresh coriander to serve 

Heat the oil in a pan over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger. Cook for 3–4 mins until softened and fragrant.

Stir in your spices: coriander, cumin, paprika, turmeric, and chilli powder. Let them bloom for 30 seconds.

Add the tomato purée. Cook it out for 1–2 mins until it darkens slightly.

Add your chopped chicken, season with salt and pepper, and stir to coat in the spice base.

Pour in the chicken stock. Simmer gently for 12/14 mins or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce thickens- you don't want this watery 

Turn the heat down, then stir in the garam masala & Greek yoghurt until creamy. Don't boil it once the yoghurt is in.

Finish with a squeeze of lemon and fresh coriander
summary: A healthy chicken tikka masala recipe for two using yogurt instead of cream.

This Diwali season, you JUST HAVE TO try this green chutney. Serve it with your starters and let this be a highlight on your table! 

Do not miss any of the ingredients please. 

Coriander leaves 
Mint leaves 
(Ratio of coriander to mint is 2:1)

Blend the leaves with:

2-4 pieces of ginger
Handful of cashews 
2 green chillies 
Half a raw mango 
Apple slices (peeled) 
2 tbsp black salt 
1 tbsp cumin powder 
1 tbsp mango (aamchur powder)
Ice cubes 
Lemon juice 
1/2 cup of water 

Adjust the consistency with adding water little by little. 

💡Pro tip: please please use ice cubes and cashews for this consistency and colour! 

💡Pro tip: you can make it in bulk and store it in the freezer for upto 6-8 months. Defrost it and it'll be ready to use 💚

If you're trying this, don't forget to tag me!

caption:
Spinach & Feta Pastries RECIPE & Freezing instructions ⬇️ 

To see how I make the filling check the "spinach rolls tutorial" 1st post at the top of my page 

1 x sheet puff pastry 

Filling:
120g frozen spinach 
1 x small onion (half large)
3 x garlic cloves 
Mixed herbs, salt & pepper 
1/2 x cup water 

200g feta cheese crumbled 
50g grated mozzarella 

1 x egg separated (white will mix into filling, yolk to brush the pastry) 

Sesame seeds 

Pre heat oven to 180C 

Making filling: 
Fry onion and garlic for a few minutes till tender, add in spinach, herbs, salt and pepper and the water and fry until the water has evaporated. Cool down, for quickest cooling spread on a large plate and put in the freezer, should take 5 min this way. Once cooled add in the cheese and egg white and mix well. 

Spread the mixed filling over the pastry sheet and roll it up, once rolled press down to flatten slightly and slice, I did these fairly thin so I made 11 and they were the perfect size and easy for my kids to hold. 

Brush with egg yolk mixed with a tsp of milk, add sesame seeds and bake for 30-32 minutes. Don't be tempted to take it out earlier, puff pastry gets golden quickly but it doesn't mean it's flakey all the way through, hang tight for a better bake.

Leave to cool for 5-10 min and eat warm or cool.

To freeze these slice, brush with egg wash, add seeds and freeze on a tray before cooking, once hard (a few hours) add to a zip lock bag and bake straight from frozen. 

Enjoy D 🫶

#spinach #feta #puffpastry #spanakopita
summary: Recipe for spinach and feta puff pastry rolls with detailed freezing instructions.

caption:
Aloo Gobi - recipe below 👇

Ingredients: @riverford 

½ head cauliflower or romanesco, cut into florets
2–3 medium potatoes, cut into small wedges
Thumb-sized piece of ginger, finely chopped
2–3 green chillies, finely sliced
4–5 curry leaves (optional)
1 tsp cumin seeds
1 tsp mustard seeds (optional)
⅛ tsp hing (asafoetida)
1 cassia or bay leaf
1 tsp red chilli powder
¼ tsp turmeric
1 tbsp coriander powder
1 tsp amchur (mango powder)
Handful of chopped coriander
Salt, to taste
Oil, for cooking

Method:

1. Cut cauliflower into florets and potatoes into wedges. Parboil the potatoes (and cauliflower if you prefer) in salted turmeric water, then drain.

2. Heat oil in a kadai or wok. Add mustard and cumin seeds, then hing, cassia leaf, ginger, and chillies. Fry a few seconds, then add curry leaves if using.

3. Add cauliflower and cook on low heat 5–6 minutes until lightly golden.

4. Add potatoes and a splash of water. Cover and cook 4–5 minutes until tender.

5. Stir in salt, amchur, and chopped coriander.

6. Serve hot with roti, yogurt, and pickle. Enjoy.

And if you want loads more regional Indian recipes just like this, you can grab a copy of my e-book comment the word "BOOK" and I'll send it over to you instantly ✨
summary: Step-by-step recipe for Aloo Gobi, an Indian potato and cauliflower curry with spices.

caption:
Lentil Spinach Rice! I make this weekly and it's a great way to add fiber and nutrients to rice if that's a staple in your meals (it is for us!). You can add so much more to this like herbs, different greens and veggies and pair it with just about anything ! So delish and simple :)

Here's how to make it:

1 tbsp butter
1/4 tsp turmeric
1 large clove garlic, grated
1 cup cooked green lentils
2 cups cooked basmati rice
1 cup chopped kale or spinach
3/4 tsp Garam Masala
*because my rice and lentils were well salted I did not add additional salt here but adjust and add as needed*

In a pan over medium heat, melt the butter and add the garlic and your choice of greens. Saute for a couple minutes then reduce the heat slightly and add the lentils, spices, salt and cook for about a minute. Add 1 tbsp of water at a time if your pan is dry. Add the rice, stir gently to mix well. Enjoy! Optional to garnish with cilantro and squeeze of lemon!

To cook the lentils:

Rinse 1/2 cup of green lentils (I like the Aurora brand from Whole Foods)  well then add to a pot of cold water with a 1 tsp of salt. Bring the lentils to a rolling boil then partially cover the pot, reduce the heat all the way to low and cook for about 20 minutes. Check often and remove from heat when the lentils are cooked, but still holding their shape. Drain the water completely and bring them to room temperature to store in an airtight container in the fridge if not using immediately.
.
.
.

caption:
Making a perfect "batter" at home for pillowy soft idli  involves six important steps :
 
Step 1 - Washing Rice:
 
Take a vessel  and wash multiple times :
 4 cups of Idli Rice 

Step 2– Washing Dal
  
In another vessel, wash together:
- 1 cup urad dal/ husked black gram
- 3/4 tsp fenugreek seeds

Wash them at least 3-4 times

 
Step 3– Soaking Right
 
- Soak the washed rice and Urad Dal mixture 
- Important tip: When you soak the rice and dal , you should be able to see the dal and rice clearly through the water.
- Soak them overnight or for minimum of 4 hours.
 
Step 4 –Grinding Urad Dal
 
- Grind both Rice mixture and Urad Dal separately. 
Grind Urad dal by adding little water at a time and till the dal turns light and fluffy. In a stone grinder grinder Urad dal takes around 40-45 minutes. Transfer the urad dal batter into a vessel

Step 5–Grinding Rice

Now add rice into the grinder. Grind Rice by adding little water at a time. Some like to grind rice coarsely and some smoothly. I like to grind it to an almost smooth paste like consistency. Grinding rice in a stone grinder takes around 30 minutes. You can add 1/2-1 glass of water while transferring the ground rice mixture if it is too thick. 
 

Step 6 – Mixing Right
 
- Transfer the ground urad dal batter and rice batter into a large vessel.
- Add sea salt ( or regular salt) - 1.5 tsp ( you can adjust later if required) and 2 tsp sesame oil. Adding sesame oil is optional. It enhances the taste of the idlis.
- Mix the batter using your hand for at least 3-4 minutes. This is a very important step.
- Once you have mixed well , cover and allow it to rest for 6-8 hours. It will take longer in colder climates.
- Chef tip: I cover the vessel completely with a Stoll or a shawl in colder months.
 
After the resting time, the batter would have risen beautifully. Your perfect idli batter is now ready!!
 
Hope these steps are helpful 

. If you find the video useful, do share it with your friends and family 💕🙏🏻
 
#howtogrindidlibatter #idli #idlibatter #glutenfreerecipes #veganrecipes #idlilove
summary: Step-by-step guide to making homemade idli batter from scratch by grinding rice and urad dal separately then fermenting.

caption:
Comfort food at its very best. 🫶🏼😋
After Aloo Chukauni, this spicy, nutty Alu Ko Achar is my latest obsession. Gonna try a lot more of these incredible recipes now. 🔥

Nepali Alu ko Achar
Macros (per serving – serves 3):
🍽️ 205 kcal | 4.5 g protein | 25.6 g carbs | 10.1 g fat

Ingredients
	•	3 medium potatoes, boiled & cubed (300 g)
	•	1 cucumber, sliced (100 g)
	•	1 small carrot, sliced (optional, 50 g)
	•	1 medium onion, thinly sliced (100 g)
	•	3 tbsp toasted white sesame seeds, crushed (use more if you like)
	•	2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves
	•	Juice of ½ lemon (15 g)
	•	½ tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	Salt to taste

For the tadka
	•	1 tbsp mustard oil (15 g)
	•	½ tsp methi seeds
	•	1 green chili, sliced
	•	Pinch of turmeric powder

Method
	1.	Toast sesame seeds until golden and crush coarsely.
	2.	Mix potatoes, cucumber, carrot, onion, coriander, sesame powder, lemon juice, chili powder & salt in a bowl.
	3.	Heat mustard oil till smoking, add methi, turmeric & green chili.
	4.	Pour the hot tadka over the salad, mix well.
	5.	Serve immediately with steaming rice or hot rotis.

✨ Nutty, spicy, tangy — and macro-friendly too.

⸻

(healthy Nepali recipe, sesame potato, macro-friendly Indian food, vegetarian recipes, 15 minute meals, healthy food)

.

#healthyrecipe #nepalifood #vegeterianfood #aloo #easysidedish
summary: A spicy and nutty Nepali potato salad (Alu Ko Achar) with sesame seeds, cucumber, and a hot mustard oil tadka.

caption:
Easy Sheet Pan Chicken Kebabs

Or should us Brits be calling them Traybake Kebabs??? (As we call them baking trays not sheetpan lol) Ah well we might as well go by the viral name. These could easily be made with lamb or beef mince instead - I just prefer the chicken ones as they are leaner and juicier!! This recipe isn't a traditional kebab recipe rather somewhere inbetween an Adana kebab and a seekh kebab, combining the best of both! 

Ingredients: makes 8-10
* 1 kg chicken mince (50:50 thigh to breast)
* 3 small onions
* 4 cloves garlic
* 1 red pepper
* 4 tbsp chopped fresh coriander (or parsley)
* 1 tbsp ground cumin
* 1 tbsp ground coriander
* ½ tbsp Kashmiri red chilli powder (use less if using regular chili powder)
* 1 tsp red chilli flakes 
* ½ tbsp garam masala
* 2 tsp salt (or to taste)
* 1 tsp black pepper
* 1 tsp garlic granules

Method: 
1. Finely chop the onions, garlic and red pepper, then squeeze out the excess liquid so the mixture doesn't loosen.
2. Add the chopped vegetables to the chicken mince along with the coriander and all the spices and seasonings.
3. Mix everything very well until fully combined.
4. Let the mixture marinate for at least one hour if possible (overnight for best results) or cook straight away if needed.
5. Spread the mixture evenly over a baking tray and use a knife or spatula to separate into portions.
6. In a preheated oven, bake at 180°C for 20 minutes. Remove halfway, drain any liquid that collects and redefine the separations.
7. Return to the oven and bake at 200°C for 5-6 minutes until golden and slightly crisp on top.
8. Serve hot with naan and salad or as a wrap or even over rice.

#kebabs #kebab #traybake #easyrecipe #highprotein #mealprep #adanakebap #chickenrecipes
summary: Easy sheet pan chicken kebabs combining Adana and seekh kebab styles, baked in the oven with aromatic spices.

caption:
Chole Masala with Coconut Milk ! It's so hearty, satisfying and the perfect weeknight meal that uses pantry friendly ingredients and won't take you a ton of time ! 

Here's the recipe:

2 tbsp oil
2 tsp cumin seeds
3 thai green chilis, finely diced
1 bay leaf
1 large yellow or white onion, finely diced
2 tbsp tomato paste (double concentrated if you can find it)
1 tsp salt
8 large cloves of garlic, grated 
1 tsp Garam Masala
1.5 tbsp chole masala like Badsha Punjabi Chana Masala
2 cans of chickpeas, drained and rinsed
1 can of full fat, unsweetened coconut milk 
1 handful of finely chopped cilantro
squeeze of lemon 

Heat a large pot over medium heat and add the oil. Once the oil is hot, add the cumin seeds and as they begin to sizzle, toss in the green chilis along with the bay leaf. After a few seconds, add the diced onions and cook while stirring occasionally, lowering the heat as necessary, until the onions are translucent and slightly browned (about 6-8 minutes).

Add the tomato paste, salt, garlic and cook until the tomato paste turns a deeper color and garlic is fragrant (about 2-3 minutes). Add the garam masala, chole masala and stir. Add ¼ cup of water slowly while stirring as the spices will begin to stick and clump together. Use a spatula to scrape up any browned bits. Add the chickpeas, along with the coconut milk and stir well. Reduce the heat to a slow simmer and place a lid on the pot. Cook for about 8-10 minutes. Remove the lid, turn off the heat and toss in the cilantro and squeeze of lemon. Adjust salt if necessary. 

This will thicken slightly as it sits so you can add a splash of water as needed (just be sure to adjust the salt if you add a lot more liquid). Serve with rice, naan, and salad (I like to toss onions, tomatoes and cucumbers with chat masala for a quick bright salad) or scoop this up with toasty sourdough bread!
summary: A recipe for chole masala with coconut milk, an Indian chickpea curry made with pantry-friendly ingredients and served with rice, naan, or bread.

caption:
Comfort food never disappoints. 😋
Recipe adapted from one of my FAVOURITE creators of all time - thank you @samayalwithsharon - you are AMAZING. ❤️❤️❤️
Never thought I could put my air fryer to even better use than before - don't worry if you don't have one, I've mentioned stove and oven instructions below too. 🫶🏼

Air Fryer Chicken Bharta
Macros (per serving, serves 3): ~135 kcal | 19.3g protein | 5g carbs | 4.1g fat

Ingredients
	•	250g boneless chicken cubes
	•	1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
	•	1 tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	Salt to taste
	•	2 tsp mustard oil (for chicken) + 1 tsp (for tempering)
	•	6–7 garlic cloves
	•	1 medium onion, sliced (about 50g)
	•	1-2 fresh green chili
	•	2–3 dry red chilies
	•	Few curry leaves
	•	1 small onion, finely chopped (for mixing at the end)
	•	2 tbsp coriander leaves, chopped
	•	Juice of ½ lemon
	•	1 tsp chaat masala

Method
	1.	In a bowl, marinate the chicken with ginger-garlic paste, salt, turmeric, Kashmiri chili powder, and 2 tsp mustard oil.
	2.	Air fry at 200°C for 15 minutes, shaking halfway, until golden brown and crispy.
	3.	Add garlic cloves, sliced onion, green chili, dry red chilies, curry leaves, and 1 tsp mustard oil into the air fryer. Mix well and cook for another 6–7 minutes.
	4.	Transfer everything to a plate and allow to cool slightly. Using your hands (wear gloves if needed), crush the chicken and aromatics into fine shreds.
	5.	Mix in chopped onion, coriander leaves, lemon juice, and chaat masala.
	6.	Serve hot with steamed rice and papad.

Alternative cooking methods - 
	•	Oven: Bake the marinated chicken at 200°C for 18–20 minutes, then roast the aromatics for 9-10 minutes until crisp.
	•	Stovetop: Pan-fry the chicken in a skillet until golden, then sauté the aromatics in mustard oil (try not to deep fry) until fragrant before mixing.

Serving Suggestions:
	•	Mix with thick yogurt or mayo for a sandwich spread.
	•	Use as a filling for wraps or rolls.

.
(Chicken Bharta, Air fryer chicken, Healthy Indian chicken recipe, High protein Indian recipe)
.
#HighProteinMeals #AirFryerRecipe #aathirafriedchicken
summary: Air fryer chicken bharta recipe with macros and alternative cooking methods for stovetop and oven.

caption:
A homestyle Kerala favourite – pan roast chicken cooked low and slow with fiery spices, caramelised onions, curry leaves, and a touch of oil. The chicken is seared to perfection, soaking up all the flavours of black pepper, ginger, garlic, and aromatic Kerala masalas. It's rustic, bold, and deeply comforting – the kind of dish that pairs beautifully with hot parottas, fluffy appams, or just plain steamed rice. Every bite tells the story of Kerala's spice-rich cuisine, smoky, spicy, and absolutely irresistible.

Recipe Ingredients 
Serves 2-3
400g boneless chicken 
4 tbsp ginger and garlic paste 
1 tsp turmeric powder 
1 tsp Kashmiri red chili powder 
1 tsp fennel seed powder 
3 tsp home-made garam masala
2 tsp cracked black pepper 
1 tsp salt
10 fresh curry leaves 
1/2 lemon

5-6 tbsp vegetable oil/coconut oil 
3 medium-sized onions
2 mild green chillies 
10 fresh curry leaves 
Julienned ginger
Salt to taste

#chicken #chickenrecipes #indianfood
summary: Pan-roasted Kerala-style chicken recipe with spices, caramelized onions, and curry leaves, served with parottas or rice.

caption:
Sheet pan chicken kebabs with lemon-shallot yogurt✨

Recipe inspired by @sivanskitchen 😘

Lemon-Shallot Yogurt:▪️1 Cup Greek Yogurt▪️2 Tbsp Fresh Lemon Juice▪️2 Tbsp Finely Chopped Fresh Shallot▪️1 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️1/4 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1 Tbsp Finely Chopped Fresh Mint▪️

Kebabs:▪️1 lb Ground Chicken (93/7 or similar, not breast only)▪️1/2 Cup Yellow Onion, finely chopped▪️1/4 Cup Parsley, finely chopped▪️2 Tbsp Mint, finely chopped▪️3 Cloves Garlic, finely chopped▪️1 Egg▪️1 Tbsp Coriander▪️1+1/2 tsp Cumin▪️1/2 tsp Cinnamon▪️1/4 tsp Cayenne Pepper▪️1 Pinch Cloves▪️1/4 tsp Black Pepper▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil, for cooking the kebabs▪️

Serving:▪️Pita Bread▪️Olive Oil▪️Sliced Cucumber▪️Sliced Onion▪️Sliced Tomato▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F. In a medium bowl, stir together the yogurt, lemon juice, shallot, olive oil, salt, and mint until smooth. Chill in the fridge while you prepare the kebabs.
2️⃣ In a separate medium bowl, mix together the ground chicken, onion, parsley, mint, garlic, egg, coriander, cumin, cinnamon, cayenne, cloves, black pepper, and salt until just combined.
3️⃣ Add 1 Tbsp olive oil to a quarter baking sheet, then spread the chicken mixture into a rectangle about 1/2-inch thick, flattening evenly. With a knife or bench scraper, separate into kebabs (about 1+1/4-inch wide), then make indents horizontally across the kebabs every 3/4-inch or so. Drizzle the remaining 1 Tbsp olive oil over the top of the kebabs and brush to coat evenly. 
4️⃣ Bake on the top rack for 10 mins, then switch the oven to broil on high and broil for 5-7 mins until browned and slightly charred on top.
5️⃣ Lightly brush pita with olive oil and toast in a hot pan for 1–2 minutes per side until warm and golden.
6️⃣ Serve the kebabs with the lemon shallot yogurt, warm pita, sliced cucumber, onion, and tomato, and enjoy! You can either eat everything deconstructed on a plate, or make wraps with the pita. For wraps, spread some of the yogurt sauce inside the pita before adding the kebabs and vegetables.

⏲ 30 min prep time / 45 min total time

Serves 4

caption:
This sheet pan chicken shawarma is done before the takeout place even starts cooking your order… and honestly, it tastes better too. Juicy, spiced, and loaded with all the fixings—tell me why you're still waiting on delivery? 🤤🔥🔥

Sheet Pan Chicken Shawarma
Inspo: @shredhappens 

Ingredients

For the Chicken:
2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into strips or chunks
½ Tbsp olive oil
3 Tbsp plain Greek yogurt
2 Tbsp tomato paste
Juice of 1 large lemon
8 garlic cloves, minced
1 Tbsp dried thyme or oregano
1 Tbsp sweet paprika
⅓ tsp ground cinnamon
1 tsp ground cumin
Salt and black pepper, to taste

For Roasting:
2 medium red onions, cut into wedges
1–2 Tbsp olive oil (for drizzling)
½ Tbsp ground sumac

For Serving:
Chopped parsley
Tzatziki sauce 
Extra sumac, for dusting
Fresh lemon juice

Instructions

Preheat the oven to 400F. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.

Place chicken in a large bowl. Add olive oil, Greek yogurt, tomato paste, lemon juice, garlic, thyme, paprika, cinnamon, cumin, salt, and pepper. Mix until evenly coated.

Arrange chicken pieces on the prepared baking sheet, leaving space between each piece. Scatter red onion wedges around the chicken. Drizzle with additional olive oil and sprinkle with sumac.

Bake for about 40 mins, or until chicken is cooked through and slightly charred at the edges.

Top with fresh herbs, diced cucumber, extra sumac, and a squeeze of lemon juice. Serve warm.

#chicken #shawarma #lowcarb #keto #food #foodies #foodstagram #instafood #healthyrecipes #recipe
summary: A quick sheet pan chicken shawarma recipe with spiced chicken thighs, roasted onions, and tzatziki served on the side.

caption:
recipe below👇

Moong Dal Chilla with Seasonal Vegetables

A chilla is a savoury Indian pancake made from lentils high in protein, naturally gluten-free, and endlessly adaptable. This version uses sweetcorn and cavolo nero (black kale), but you can fold in whatever vegetables are fresh and in season.

✨ If you enjoyed this recipe, check out my ebook: 130+ regional recipes + 20 lessons to improve your Indian home cooking - Link in bio

Ingredients:

200g yellow moong dal
150g sweetcorn, boiled and kernels sliced off
100g cavolo nero or kale, stems removed and leaves blanched
1 small onion, finely chopped
2 green chillies, finely chopped
Handful of fresh coriander leaves, chopped
¼ tsp turmeric
⅛ tsp hing (asafoetida)
Salt and black pepper, to taste
Oil for cooking

Method:

1. Wash and soak the moong dal for 20 minutes. Drain, rinse, then blend with a little water until smooth and creamy.

2. Stir in turmeric, hing, salt, onion, chillies, and coriander. Loosen with water until the batter is like pancake batter.

3. Heat a non-stick pan and brush lightly with oil. 
Pour in a ladle of batter and spread into a circle.

4. Cook gently on the first side, then drizzle with a little oil, flip, and cook the other side until golden.

5. Serve hot with butter or a green chutney. Enjoy!

#dal #indianfood #seasonal
summary: Recipe for moong dal chilla (Indian savory lentil pancake) with seasonal vegetables like sweetcorn and kale.

caption:
"LAZY GIRL" CHICKEN THIGHS✨ use this foolproof technique for tender and juicy chicken thighs every time! Whenever a recipe calls for cooked chicken, 90% of the time this is how I'm going to prepare it. No oil, 2 ingredients (four if we count S&P but I don't think those count), under 30 minutes. You're going to LOVE this recipe!

Ingredients: 
1 1/2 lbs of chicken thighs, boneless and skinless
salt and pepper to season (about 1/4 tsp salt per thigh, and 1/8 tsp pepper)
about 1/2 cup of low sodium chicken broth or bone broth

Directions: 
Heat a large cast iron skillet over medium high heat
While the skillet heats up, season the chicken thighs generously on both sides with salt and pepper 
Add the chicken thighs to the dry skillet, making sure not to overcrowd the pan, and cook, undisturbed, for 10-12 minutes or until they are white around the edges and are no longer sticking to the pan. It's important not to move them around while cooking because you want a nice golden crust to form. 
Flip the chicken once it can easily release from the pan and cook for another 5-7 minutes.
Lower the pan heat to medium low and add 1/4 cup of chicken broth, scraping up any chicken bits as the broth deglazes the pan, then add another 1/4 cup to cover the bottom on the pan in a thin layer.
Allow the sauce to reduce until there is only a thin, sticky glaze over the bottom of the pan, tossing the chicken in the sauce as you go, for another 3-5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and allow to rest for 5 minutes before slicing. 
Slice into your juicy chicken thighs, serve (with the pan drippings), and ENJOY! 

Follow for more simple, healthy recipes!🤍
summary: A simple cast iron skillet method for tender, juicy chicken thighs with minimal ingredients and under 30 minutes.

caption:
Crispy cheesy skillet potatoes✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now. Link in bio 🤍.

▪️2 lb. Yukon Gold Potatoes, peeled and sliced into 1/4-inch rounds▪️1 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/2 tsp Black Pepper▪️2 Tbsp Olive Oil▪️3/4 Cup Water▪️1 Cup Shredded Cheddar Jack Cheese (or cheese of choice)▪️1 Tbsp Chopped Fresh Chives▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 425F. Place the sliced potatoes in a bowl and toss with the salt and pepper to evenly coat.
2️⃣ Heat a large ovenproof nonstick or seasoned cast-iron skillet over medium heat and add the olive oil. Arrange the potato slices in an overlapping spiral pattern, starting at the center and working your way out. 
3️⃣ Pour in the water. Cover with a lid (any lid that fits, it doesn't need to match the pan exactly) and cook over medium heat for 12-15 mins  until the water has evaporated and the potatoes are tender and pierce easily with a fork. 
4️⃣ Uncover and continue cooking 2-3 mins, pressing gently with a spatula, until the bottom is golden and crisp in spots. Sprinkle the cheese evenly over the top, making sure to go all the way to the edge of the pan (that's how you'll get those crispy cheesy bits!).
5️⃣ Transfer the skillet to the oven for 8-10 mins, until the cheese is melted and bubbling with golden brown spots. Sprinkle with chives, serve from the skillet, and enjoy!

⏲ 10 minute prep time / 40 minute total time

Recipe serves 4 as a side
summary: Crispy cheesy skillet potatoes recipe with Yukon Gold potatoes, cheddar jack cheese, and fresh chives.

caption:
Because the Indo in Indo-Chinese was not enough. 😬
Golden, garlicky, buttery paneer tikka under 400 calories per serving 🤌 

✨ Butter Garlic Paneer ✨
Makes 2 Servings ~ 383 kcal | 23 g protein | 28 g fat | 13 g carbs per Serving

Ingredients
	•	200 g paneer, cubed 
	•	1/4 cup Greek yogurt or thick curd
	•	1 tbsp garlic paste
	•	2 tbsp chopped spring onion
	•	2 tbsp finely chopped onion
	•	2 tbsp chopped coriander leaves
	•	1/2 tsp chili flakes
	•	1/2 tsp pepper
	•	Salt, to taste
	•	1/4 tsp cardamom powder or garam masala
	•	1 tbsp almond flour / powdered cashew / almond meal
	•	1 tbsp light butter
	•	1 tsp chopped garlic (for cooking)

Method
	1.	In a bowl, whisk together yogurt, garlic paste, spring onion, onion, coriander, chili flakes, pepper, salt, cardamom/garam masala, and almond flour/powder.
	2.	Add paneer cubes, coat well, and marinate in the fridge for 30 minutes.
	3.	Heat butter in a pan, add chopped garlic, and let it sizzle.
	4.	Add the marinated paneer along with all the marinade.
	5.	Cook on medium heat, flipping until the paneer turns golden brown with a buttery garlic crust.
	6.	Serve hot as is, or use in wraps, rolls, or bowls.

caption:
Shredded Chicken Sliders – perfect for a crowd! 

SAVE this for your next family get together!

The chicken recipe is form my cookbook BISMILLAH, Let's Eat! These sliders are packed with flavour and come together easily in the Instant Pot.

Chicken Filling:
* 4 large skinless chicken breasts
* 1 cup crushed tomatoes
* 2–4 chipotle peppers in adobo sauce
* 2 garlic cloves
* 2 packets fajita or taco seasoning
* 1 tsp ground cumin
* 1 tsp chili powder
* 2 tbsp salsa
* Juice of half a lime
Mix all ingredients in an Instant Pot. Cook on manual for 20 min, then slow release for 10–15 min. Shred the chicken and set aside.

To Assemble:
* Brioche slider buns
* Mayonnaise
* Cheese, avocado, jalapeños, sour cream
* Melted butter + dried parsley

Spread buns with mayo, layer with shredded chicken, cheese, avocado, jalapeños, and sour cream. Top with bun lids, brush tops with melted butter + parsley. Bake at 350°F for 10–15 min, then broil 2–4 min to crisp tops (watch carefully!).
Serve and enjoy!

caption:
One-Pan Thai Coconut Chicken & Rice 

Chicken:
4 lbs skin-on chicken thighs
4 tsp kosher salt
1 tsp black pepper
Zest of 3 limes
⅓ cup neutral oil

Rice:
¾ cup sliced shallot
10 garlic cloves, smashed
3 tbsp minced (or grated) ginger
4 cups jasmine rice, rinsed
10 green onions, thinly sliced
Juice of 3–4 limes
2 cups chicken broth
4 cups full-fat coconut milk
4 tbsp fish sauce
1 cup chopped cilantro

How to make it:
Preheat oven to 350°F. Pat chicken dry and rub with salt, pepper, and lime zest. Heat oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium heat. Sear chicken skin-side down for 6–7 minutes until golden and crisp. Remove and set aside.

In the same skillet, reduce heat to medium-low. Sauté shallot, garlic, ginger, and chilies for about 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in rice and green onions; toast for 2–3 minutes. Add lime juice, chicken broth, coconut milk, and fish sauce. Bring to a simmer.

Nestle chicken back into the rice, skin-side up. Cover and bake for 25 minutes. Uncover and bake another 10 minutes, until chicken is cooked through and skin is crisp. Fluff the rice, top with cilantro, and serve hot.
** original recipe by @kalejunkie

caption:
Bringing back these melting sweet potatoes — so soft, so flavourful, and perfect for fall. They make a great nutritious side dish, and they never last long at the table!🍠

Recipe ⬇️

INGREDIENTS 
2 ½ lb sweet potatoes 
¼ cup olive oil 
1/8th tsp salt 
1 cup vegetable or chicken stock

INSTRUCTIONS 
1. Cut sweet potatoes into 1" rounds. Add to bowl with olive oil and salt.
2. Place on parchment paper and roast at 500F for 15 minutes.
3. Turn over and roast another 15 minutes.
4. Add stock and cook another 10 minutes of just until almost all the stock is absorbed.
5. Garnish with fresh herbs of choice. Enjoy!
.
.

caption:
Over 300 macro-friendly paneer recipes ❤️
Just scroll down on my feed to meet your macro goals while satisfying your cravings. 

Paneer Ghotala - Bhurjee 🤓
✨ Serving Size: 3 servings
✨ Macros (per serving, approx) - 260 kcal, 21 g P, 12 g C and 12 g F

📝 Ingredients
	•	Curd/Yogurt base:
	•	1 cup (240 g) high-protein Greek yogurt
	•	1 tsp heavy cream (optional)
	•	Spice mix:
	•	2 tsp Kashmiri chili powder
	•	½ tsp turmeric powder
	•	1 tsp coriander powder
	•	1 tsp pav bhaji masala
	•	1 tsp garam masala
	•	1 tsp peri peri seasoning (optional)
	•	1 tsp kasuri methi (crushed)
	•	Salt to taste
	•	Aromatics:
	•	1-inch piece ginger, grated
	•	3 cloves garlic, crushed
	•	2 green chilies, chopped
	•	2 tbsp chopped fresh coriander
	•	Veggies:
	•	1 medium onion, finely chopped
	•	1 small capsicum, finely chopped
	•	2 medium tomatoes, finely chopped
	•	Protein:
	•	250 g low-fat homemade paneer, grated
	•	For cooking:
	•	½ tsp light butter or ghee
	•	Finishing:
	•	More kasuri methi (crushed)
	•	Fresh coriander leaves, chopped
	•	Lemon wedges, onion slices, and green chili (for serving)

👩🏻‍🍳 Method
	1.	Prepare yogurt-spice base:
	•	Whisk Greek yogurt with 1 tsp heavy cream until smooth.
	•	Add spices, ginger, garlic, chilies, and coriander. Mash well.
	2.	Cook the masala:
	•	Heat ½ tsp butter/ghee in a non-stick pan.
	•	Add the yogurt-spice mixture and cook on low-medium heat, stirring continuously, until thickened and oil separates.
	3.	Add veggies:
	•	Mix in onion, capsicum, and tomato with a pinch of salt.
	•	Cook until soft, mashed, and gravy-like.
	4.	Add paneer:
	•	Stir in grated paneer.
	•	Add ¼–½ cup hot water for saucier consistency. Cook 2–3 minutes.
	5.	Finish:
	•	Sprinkle more kasuri methi and coriander.
Serve hot with roti, pav or paratha, along with chopped onion, chilli, and a squeeze of lemon juice.

.

caption:
Sheet pan chicken and broccoli stir-fry✨

My debut cookbook Maxi's Kitchen is available for pre-order now! Link in bio 🤍.

Chicken + Marinade:▪️1+1/4 lb. Boneless Skinless Chicken Breast, sliced in thin bite-size pieces▪️1 Egg White▪️1 Tbsp Cornstarch▪️1/2 tsp Kosher Salt▪️1/4 tsp Pepper▪️1 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️1 Tbsp Sesame Oil▪️

Stir Fry:▪️2 Tbsp Sesame Oil▪️1 lb. Broccoli, cut into small florets (~5 Cups)▪️3 Tbsp Finely Chopped Fresh Ginger (or less if you like a milder flavor)▪️3 Scallions, sliced▪️1 Tbsp Sesame Seeds▪️Cooked White or Brown Rice, for serving▪️

Sauce:▪️3/4 Cup Chicken Stock (or water)▪️3 Garlic Cloves, finely chopped▪️2 Tbsp Cornstarch▪️2 Tbsp Soy Sauce▪️2 Tbsp Oyster Sauce▪️1 tsp Sesame Oil▪️1 Tbsp White Vinegar▪️1/2 tsp Sugar▪️1/2 tsp Black Pepper▪️

1️⃣ Preheat the oven to 375F.
2️⃣ In a medium bowl, add the chicken, egg white, cornstarch, salt, pepper, soy sauce, and sesame oil. Mix to combine and marinate for at least 15 mins at room temp (or up to 4 hours in the fridge).
3️⃣ Drizzle 2 Tbsp sesame oil onto a sheet pan and add the broccoli, tossing to coat. Arrange the chicken around the broccoli, spreading it into an even layer so it touches the pan. Sprinkle with the chopped ginger and bake for 15 mins.
4️⃣ In a medium glass measuring cup or heatproof bowl, whisk together the chicken stock, garlic, cornstarch, soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, white vinegar, sugar, and pepper until combined. Microwave for 2-3 mins, pausing once or twice to whisk, until the sauce is bubbling and thickened (or cook in a small saucepan over medium heat, whisking constantly, until bubbling and thickened, 3-5 mins).
5️⃣ Spoon the sauce onto the chicken and broccoli and toss to coat. Sprinkle with the scallions and sesame seeds and return to the oven for 5 more mins. 
6️⃣ Serve over rice and enjoy!

⏲20 minute prep time / 45 minute total time

Recipe serves 4
summary: Sheet pan chicken and broccoli stir-fry with ginger-garlic sauce, served over rice.
